observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) MA to (i) reclassify the erroneously recorded CAs totaling ₱8,387,500.00 from Advances to Officers and Employees account to Advances to SDO; (ii) assign one of her personnel to monitor CAs, ensuring timely liquidation within the prescribed period; (b) designate additional SDO to prevent the granting of multiple CAs to a single AO; (c) require the MT to (i) increase the fidelity bond of SDO to an amount sufficient to cover her maximum accountability; (ii) cease the practice of granting CAs for special purposes to officials not designated as SDO and not bonded except for traveling expenses; and (iii) direct the payment of expenses to suppliers/creditors and refrain from processing transactions through CAs except for petty expenses; (d) instruct the MA and MT to require the AOs to submit lacking supporting documents in the liquidation of their CAs and limit the use of RER in the payment of goods and services; (e) require the MA to (i) submit the LRs that were already recorded in the books of accounts; and (ii) ascertain the completeness of LRs before recording them in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) establish an IC tasked with conducting the annual physical count of PPE and rendering the RPCPPE based on the results; (b) require the MA to (i) prepare PPELCs and regularly update records for adequate monitoring of PPE; (ii) reconcile records with the Municipal Assessor and make representations with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) for the titling of the 40 parcels of land with TDs under the Municipality’s name for proper valuation in the books; (iii) adjust semi-expendable properties to their respective expense accounts; and (iv) reconcile records with the MTO for the recognition of 11 unaccounted transportation equipment in the books of accounts; (c) require the MTO to (i) maintain PCs for each class of PPE and prepare ARE prior to their issuance to end-user to ensure accountability over Municipality-owned assets; and (ii) implement a system to actively monitor and facilitate the timely renewal of motor vehicle registrations; and (d) require the DAC to expedite the preparation of relevant documents to cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MBO Chief to monitor the appropriations vis-à-vis obligation to avoid the recurrence of the same deficiency; and (b) MA in coordination with the SDO, submit the (i) certified true copies of requisite documents for CAs for CF; (ii) DV in an enclosed envelope to the AT within seven (7) days after the release of check; and (iii) LR to ICFAU within the prescribed period to ensure the availability of necessary documents for audit scrutiny.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to (i) give preferential attention and expedite the processes enumerated in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-05 for the proper write-off of dormant accounts; (ii) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs accounts to adequately monitor the accounts; and (iii) refrain from the inappropriate use of RER as proof of payment and comply with the rules and regulations relative thereto; and (b) concerned Municipal officials to identify and prioritize projects to be funded by the DOE funds for immediate implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) SB members prioritize the updating of the decades-old LRC; and (b) LCE require the BPLO Chief and MT to work towards achieving the projected income outlined in the appropriations ordinance by (i) maintaining reliable and complete business taxpayers’ ledger cards to monitor collections and identify delinquent taxpayers; (ii) conducting random examinations of business taxpayers’ books of accounts; (iii) ensuring that all essential documents for the computation of business taxes are submitted by business taxpayers upon registration and renewal; and (iv) levying business taxes in accordance with the provisions of the LRC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the MDC to (i) aim for full implementation of PPAs at the set time to ensure the continuity of the LGU’s development efforts towards the attainment of targets; and (ii) stop the practice of transferring funds appropriated for the 20% DF to different barangays as the Fund is intended for development projects to be implemented by the Municipality; (b) require the MA to (i) record all fund transfers to appropriate account, make the necessary adjustments in the books, and demand the submission of liquidation reports with complete required supporting documents from the recipient barangay; and (ii) ensure that supporting documents are sufficient and valid before payments are made; (c) require the Municipal Budget Office (MBO) to stop charging ineligible expenses from the DF; (d) require the BAC to strictly observe the requirements for the alternative mode of procurements and/or subject all procurements to public bidding; and (e) require the ME to (i) to cause the contractor to repair the portion of the project found defective at no additional cost by the Municipality; and (ii) prepare a Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure project implemented by administration as basis for the issuance of Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require (a) the LDRRMO Head to (i) stop the practice of implementing PPAs beyond their appropriated amounts; and (ii) optimize the utilization of the fund to lessen the socio economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community; (b) the BAC to (i) post in the PhilGEPS the RFQ and Award for the procurements covered by the AMP – Shopping; and (ii) issue BAC Resolutions recommending the use of alternative mode of procurement for approval of the LCE; (c) the MA to (i) immediately submit the requisite supporting documents for the AT’s verification; and (ii) ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing claims for payments to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements; and (d) the MA and MBO to prepare and submit the required reports within the prescribed period to ensure the availability of information on the receipts and utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairperson of the SWMB in coordination with the MENRO and barangays, to exert more efforts in their campaign towards safe and sanitary SWM by (a) requiring business establishments to (i) put preference in using biodegradable packing materials; (ii) install different waste receptacles and eco-friendly signages; (b) discourage littering and open burning by imposing penalties against violators; (c) address the issue on the delayed to non-collection of garbage in both Barangay and Municipal levels to encourage stakeholders to actively participate in the LGU’s initiatives towards safe and sanitary SWM; and (d) focus on the repair and maintenance of damaged/idle machinery and/or installation of new machinery and equipment to improve its recycling performance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the books and make the necessary adjustments to properly account for the prior years’ non-moving balance; and (b) strictly comply with RA No. 9679 and on the withholding and full remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: 1.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the (a) MA and the MT to continuously (i) strengthen cash management controls through compliance with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting of collections and deposits; and (ii) coordinate and reconcile their cash records for early detection and correction of errors; and (b) MT to ensure the timely submission of the monthly CRAAF, including details of check receipts, issuances, and balances.

2.13 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MT and MA to confirm the
existence of the bank account with a reported balance of ₱44.19 in the books with the
concerned bank, and take appropriate corrective action; (b) MA to (i) prepare an
adjusting entry to correct the book reconciling items that were incorrectly treated as
bank reconciling items in the BRSs, ensuring the accurate reflection of the CIB-
LCCA account balance; and (ii) ensure the prompt submission of DVs to the AT for
post-audit; and (c) MT to provide the MAO with a list of unreleased checks at year
end to facilitate adjustment in the books moving forward.

3.4 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MT to: (i) furnish the MA with a duly certified list of taxpayers indicating the account due and collectible for the year; and (ii) ensure the accurate computation of RPT and SET receivable to reflect the full amounts due, in compliance with applicable regulations; and (b) MA to: (i) record the RPT and SET receivables based on the 1% of the assessed value for each, ensuring complete and accurate reporting; and (ii) maintain subsidiary ledgers for both RPT/SET receivables, reconcile these records with the MT’s data, and make the necessary adjusting entries to ensure that the financial statements fairly reflect the municipality’s financial position.

4.3 We recommended that the LCE (a) direct the AOs to immediately liquidate
their outstanding CAs and instruct the MA to assign one of her personnel to monitor
the liquidation of CAs, ensuring timely liquidation within the prescribed period; (b)
designate additional SDO to prevent the granting of multiple CAs to a single AO; (c)
require the MT to process payments directly to suppliers/creditors and limit the use
of CAs to petty expenses; (d) instruct the MA and MT to require the AOs to submit
lacking supporting documents in the liquidation of their CAs and limit the use of RER
for the payment of goods and services; and (e) require the MA to (i) submit the LRs
that were already recorded in the books of accounts; and (ii) ascertain the
completeness of liquidation reports before recognition in the books of accounts.

5.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to (i) adopt the PIM in
recording inventories; (ii) record the cost of issued inventories based on the SSMI
prepared and submitted by the SO; and (iii) maintain SLCs to support the Inventory
account balances; and (b) SO to (i) conduct physical count of inventories on a
semestral basis and submit the RPCI to the MA and AT; (ii) prepare SSMI and
submit to the MAO; (iii) ensure that all maintained SCs are updated; and (iv)
diligently notify the AT of all inventory deliveries within 24-hours from receipt by
submitting the IAR to facilitate inspection and to secure compliance with the
applicable provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.

6.5 We recommended that the LCE (a) prioritize the creation of an IC that will be
tasked to conduct a complete inventory of all Municipality’s assets and prepare the
RPCPPE for the results of the undertaking, providing copies to the MAO, MTO, and
AT for verification; (b) direct the following actions: (i) the MT and MA to prepare
complete property records and documents for proper monitoring and accountability,
and to reconcile the ₱203,136,203.06 discrepancy between their records; (ii) the MA
to reclassify the semi-expendable properties totaling ₱35,666,271.13 that are still
recorded under the PPE account; and (iii) the MT and MA to investigate the
recognition of parcels of land valued at ₱48,410,255.29 that lack TCTs under the
Municipality’s name and take appropriate action; (c) form a special committee to
investigate the status of land donations to the Municipality and facilitate proper
titling; (d) establish an appraisal committee to assess the value of three parcels of land
with TCTs under the Municipality’s name but not recognized in the books, and
furnish the MAO with the report for proper recognition; (e) instruct the MA and MT
to review the prior years’ records to determine the details of the Transportation
Equipment account; (f) require the MT to ensure the timely registration of all
Municipal motor vehicles with the LTO; and (g) create a disposal committee to
undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties.

7.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to affect the necessary
adjustments in the CIP account to accurately report the cost of on-going projects as
of year-end 2024; and (b) MA and ME to closely coordinate and monitor the status of
project completion to ensure proper recognition in the books of account.

8.8 We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to (a) prioritize and expedite
the processes enumerated in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular 2016-05 for proper
write-off of dormant accounts; and (b) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for
the Due to NGAs account to adequately monitor and enable regular aging of the
accounts.

9.4 We recommended that the (a) SB members prioritize the enactment of the
revised LRC to replace the decades-old version and ensure updated and effective tax
policies; and (b) LCE require the BPLO Chief and MT to work towards achieving the
projected income outlined in the appropriations ordinance by (i) ensuring that all
essential documents for the computation of business taxes are submitted by business
taxpayer upon registration and renewal and that proper approval from authorized
personnel is obtained before processing payments; (ii) maintaining reliable and
complete business taxpayers’ ledger cards to accurately monitor collections and
identify delinquent taxpayers for appropriate action; and (iii) levying business taxes
in accordance with the provisions of the LRC to uphold fairness, accuracy and
transparency in tax assessments and collections.

10.4 We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson of the LSB, instruct the LSB
Secretary to provide (a) the Municipal officials concerned with a copy of the SEF
budget within the prescribed period; and (b) the Local Sanggunian, and the Offices
of the DBM and DILG with the quarterly and annual utilization of SEF in accordance with
the Item 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s of 2017.

11.3 We recommended that the LCE (a) propose to the SB the establishment of
additional plantilla positions across various offices to address current staffing
deficiencies and to enhance LGU’s capacity to operate as a responsive, efficient and
service-oriented institution; and (b) instruct the (i) LSB members to prepare and
submit a more comprehensive and detailed PPAs; ((ii)) MBO to conduct a thorough
review of fund availability before approving disbursements to prevent budget
overdrafts; (iii) BAC to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR
of RA No. 9184 in procuring goods and services through public bidding; (iv) DS to
ensure that seminar and training are cost-efficient venues, unless substantial
justification is provided to support the necessity of an out-of-town location for
achieving the seminar’s objectives; (v) SDO to immediately submit the lacking
documents under her custody and provide the detailed explanation and reconciliation
of the ₱190,500.00 discrepancy in the liquidation of per diem for athletes and
coaches; (vi) MA to (a) exercise vigilance in reviewing and verifying the completeness
and validity of the supporting documents before processing DVs and LRs; (b)
discontinue the practice of paying through cash advances for the transactions that
should be settled directly with suppliers; and (c) submit all lacking supporting
documents outlined in the audit findings.

12.19 We recommended that LCE (a) as head of the MDC (i) aim for full
implementation of PPAs at the set time to ensure the continuity of the LGU’s
development efforts towards the attainment of targets; (b) require the MA to (i)
deduct the corresponding retention money from every progress payment; (ii) record
all fund transfers to appropriate account, make the necessary adjustments in the
books, and demand the submission of liquidation reports with complete required
supporting documents from the recipient barangay; and (iii) ensure that supporting
documents are sufficient and valid before payments are made; and (c) require the
BAC to ensure strict compliance with the pertinent provisions of the Procurement
Law, specifically on the review of bid documents and the prescribed periods for each
procurement phase, to enhance transparency and efficiency in the procurement
process.

13.20 We recommended that the LCE, (a) as head of the MDRRM Council, (i) direct
the Council to prioritize eligible PPAs under the LDRRMF; (ii) require the MDRRM
and MBO Officers to monitor the implementation of DRRMF PPAs and ensure that
expenditures remain within the approved appropriation; otherwise, prepare a
supplemental budget; and (iii) appropriate the STF for disaster preparedness and
mitigation PPAs and include them in the LDRRMFIP; (b) require the MA, MBO and
MDRRM Officers to observe the maintenance of records and timely reporting of
LDRRMF appropriations and utilizations; and (c) direct the BAC, Technical
Working Group, and MA to (i) post in the PhilGEPS website the RFQ and Award
for procurements conducted through alternative modes of procurement – Shopping
and Emergency Cases; (ii) scrutinize eligibility documents before awarding contracts;
and (iii) submit the required supporting documents.

14.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) HRMO Officer to ensure that
JO contracts are duly signed by both the Municipality and JO personnel, explicitly
stating duties, responsibilities, terms, and conditions; and (b) HRMO Officer and MA
to immediately discontinue the practice of paying the salaries and wages, as well as
granting overtime pay and bonuses to bookkeepers from barangay funds due to the
lack of legal basis, until the execution of MOA between the LCE, for and in behalf of
the Municipality, and the Barangays.

15.5 We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) coordinate with the
HRMO to review the amounts of GSIS contributions recorded in the books to trace
the noted discrepancies and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b)
strictly comply with the GSIS Act of 1997 in the timely and full remittance of the
withheld contributions and loan payments.

16.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to (a) coordinate with the HRMO
to resolve the prior years’ unaccounted balance by thoroughly reviewing the
contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the previous years’ records; and (b)
strictly comply with RA No. 9679 by ensuring the withholding and full remittance of
Pag-IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

17.5 We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) continue to comply with
the withholding and timely remittance of PhilHealth contributions; and (b) facilitate
reconciliation of records with the HRMO and the PHIC to address the unaccounted
prior years’ balance of ₱83,326.85.

18.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA and Municipal Assessor
to: (i) expedite the preparation and submission of necessary documents to facilitate the
payment of the outstanding CGT to the BIR; and (ii) ensure that any penalties and
charges incurred due to the late remittance of CGT are charged to the personal accounts
of the responsible Municipal employees; and (b) MA to strictly comply with the
remittance of withheld taxes within the prescribed period to avoid penalties and
surcharges from the BIR.

19.6 We recommended the LCE require the GFPS to: (a) continue its faithful
compliance with the GAD requirements particularly in the provision of adequate
budget, establishment of institutional mechanisms, preparation of necessary reports,
and full implementation of planned PPAs; and (b) exert effort in mobilizing its
proponent offices to provide complete, accurate and updated data for the GAD
database as a tool for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy
formulation.

1.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a. Personnel concerned to complete the physical count of PPE and consider the
one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-
006;
b. Municipal Accountant and GSO designate to update and prepare the
PPELC and PC, respectively, for each class of PPE; and
c. Accounting Office and General Services Office to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their PPE records and reconcile their records with the
RPCCPE and to immediately effect necessary adjustments, if any.

2.14 We recommended and the Municipal Vice Mayor agreed to:
a. Require all accountable officials and employees concerned to immediately
liquidate or refund the long overdue cash advances, otherwise the necessary
sanction for its non-settlement such as withholding of salary should be
enforced;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that additional cash advances
are not granted to officials and employees with previous unliquidated cash
advances in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations, and to
attach a certification on the grant of a new cash advance that previous cash
advances of the concerned accountable officers were liquidated; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify, analyze and validate the
unliquidated cash advances of officials and employees who are already
separated from the service and recommend for write-off upon sufficient
proof that collection thereof is no longer feasible, provided that the
procedures under COA Circular 2016-005 is strictly adhered to.

3.9 We recommended and the Municipal Vice Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular
reconciliation of records of Cash in bank balances and promptly resolve
variances thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and provide copy of the list
of unreleased checks to the Municipal Accountant at the end of the year; and
c. Henceforth, Municipal Accountant to revert back to the Cash in Bank and
recognize the corresponding Payable accounts the amount of unreleased
checks at year-end.

4.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace the
unreconciled balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds
and make necessary adjustments, where appropriate, to ensure fair presentation
of reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

5.8 We recommended and the Municipal Vice Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to refrain from treating the procured inventory items/goods as
outright expense to prevent misstatement of both the inventory and expense
accounts, as well as, strengthen the inventory control and accountability
mechanism.

6.8 We recommended and the Municipal Vice Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to regularly prepare the certified list of RPT and SET
due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables; and
b. Municipal Accountant to reflect the correct balance of receivables in the
financial statements based on the certified list of all RPT Delinquencies
provided by the Municipal Treasurer, and henceforth, recognize in the books
the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the
certified list of due and collectible RPT as submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer.

7.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to record the Disaster
Response and Rescue Equipment and Equity accounts, and henceforth ensure
that the accounting and reporting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund’s (LDRRMF) transactions are in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

7.10 We also recommended and the Municipal Vice Mayor agreed to require the
DRRM Officer/focal person to submit the letter requesting financial assistance
and other pertinent documents relative to the purchase of two units watercraft
to the Audit Team for verification and evaluation.

1.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
c. Verify the cause/s of the difference between the Subsidiary Ledgers
and the Bank Statements totaling ₱18,251,269.38 as of December 31,
2024, and prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts
to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts and other
affected accounts, when warranted.
d. Perform regular Bank Reconciliation for all bank accounts within the
prescribed period to enable the identification of errors or deficiencies
in the recording of cash and ensure its timely correction, in order to
achieve accuracy and reliability of the balances per books and per
bank.

9.8 We recommended that the GSO-Designate:
c. prepare and maintain complete and updated property records and
reports, including the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
form and physical inventory reports
d. monitor the receipts and issuances of inventory items through proper
documentation and reconciliation
9.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
c. establish and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each inventory account
to accurately record and track inventory movements.
d. reconcile inventory balances regularly with the records of the GSO to
ensure reliability and fair presentation of the Inventories account in
the financial statements.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a written directive,
including specific timelines and applicable sanctions, directing the Inventory
Committee to carry out the following actions and ensure strict compliance:
a.) Conduct and complete a comprehensive physical count of all properties
owned by the Municipality within CY 2025;
b.) Prepare the RPCPPE using the format prescribed by existing guidelines.
Each PPE item should be properly classified as serviceable or
unserviceable, with clear identification of the accountable personnel,
status of serviceability, and location. The completed report should be
submitted to the Municipal Accountant prior to the year-end closing of
the LGU’s books of accounts; and
c.) investigate any non-existing or missing PPE items and prepare a separate
report for immediate submission to the Municipal Accountant, as a
prerequisite for the filing of a request for derecognition of the said items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the (a) MA and the MT to continuously (i) strengthen cash management controls through compliance with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting of collections and deposits; and (ii) coordinate and reconcile their cash records for early detection and correction of errors; and (b) MT to advise personnel responsible for recording barangay collections to thoroughly review the Abstract prepared by BTs to prevent erroneous recording of collections and deposits.

We recommended that the LCE require the SO in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items to comply strictly with the submission of delivery documents to the AT within 24 hours upon acceptance thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the amounts of GSIS contributions recorded in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly comply with the GSIS Act of 1997 in the timely and full remittance of the withheld contributions and loan payments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly comply with BIR No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended by BIR RR No. 11-2018 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the GFPS to (i) continue its faithful compliance with the budgetary requirement for the GAD Fund; and (ii) exert effort in mobilizing its proponent offices to accomplish all the planned PPAs and fully utilize the GAD budget in the succeeding years; and (b) the MPDO to spearhead the setting up, maintenance, and updating of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the LCE:

a.	direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and the Municipal Engineer to evaluate the status of the rehabilitated FMR and come up with an action plan, if necessary, to render it usable;

b.	require the MPDO and the Municipal Engineer to prepare a comprehensive set of plans, specifications, and a program of works encompassing the road networks of the barangays of San Miguel specifying the activities to be done, target dates of completion and the funding requirements for by phase implementation for incorporation in the Local Development Plan of the Municipality to comply with Sections 3.2.3 and 4.0 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. 

We also recommended that Management, through the Local Development Council, carefully and judiciously plan the developmental projects to be charged to the 20% DF to ensure optimization of resources and for maximum benefit of the target beneficiaries as provided for in Section 2 of PD 1445 and Section 305g of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to prepare a comprehensive action plan
observations.reason: Management did not prepare a comprehensive action plan

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE cause the immediate refund of honoraria granted to COS totaling ₱36,000.00 contrary to the relevant regulations; Sections 2.0 and 3.0 of DBM National Compensation Circular No. 75 dated March 1, 1995 and Section 2.0 of Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007. 

We also recommended that grant of honoraria and the like be in consonance with existing rules and regulations so that the payment thereof would be considered as regular and valid.
observations.managementAction: To continue collection of refund in CY 2024
observations.reason: Full refund was not made

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	strictly enforce the laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;


b.	direct the Municipal Accountant to suspend processing of additional cash advances of officials and employees unless settlement of previous advances is made; and


c.	require the Municipal Accountant to send notices 10 days before expiration of the 30-day period to concerned personnel to settle their accounts or face possible administrative and legal sanctions for non-compliance despite notice.
observations.managementAction:

To fully commit adherence to the rules and regulations on cash advances
observations.reason:

Processing of cash advances proceeded despite written objection from the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

a) provide a stock room for proper storage of items of supplies and materials and require coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the GSO towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials; or if impracticable, ensure the cooperation of the end-users and the GSO to conduct an inventory of supplies and materials issued to their offices at year-end and forward the schedule of inventory on hand to the Municipal Accountant for adjustment of the inventory balances and expense accounts; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipality exercise due diligence and care over the government properties owned and entrusted to them to avoid any liability occasioned by negligence in the safekeeping or use of such property;

2. The   immediate filing of the notice and Request for Relief from Accountability of the totally damaged Municipal Ambulance by the person/s responsible with a written satisfactory explanation or the reason(s) for such delay to the Regional Director thru the audit team to facilitate proper accounting of the property.  Hereunder are the documents for the Request for Relief from Accountability:

Basic notice of loss/damage

Affidavit or Sworn Statement of the proper accountable officer on the facts and circumstances surrounding the said loss/ damage, supported by the affidavit of two (2) disinterested persons who have personal knowledge of such fact/loss/damage

Comment and/or recommendation of the Agency Head concerned on the request

Exact or accurate amount of government cash or book value of the property subject of the request

Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR)/ Memorandum Receipts (MR) covering the properties subject of the request

Report of Damaged/Destroyed Property 

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to apply accounting policies relative to the subject matter and record the accountability of the accountable officer.

The Municipality hold the person/s concerned liable for the value of the damaged property.
