observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee to expedite the preparation and consolidation of all the necessary reports relative to the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to enable the counter-checking and verification of reports by the Accounting Office and Inventory Committee and the subsequent submission of reports to the Audit Team, for review and evaluation, in order to effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE accounts and fairly present the balances in the LGU books.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue making representations with the banks in order to secure copies of the Credit and Debit Memo, especially for those newly incurred credits and charges in CY 2022, so as to obtain proper bases and valid supporting documents to record the afore-cited reconciling items in the LGU books, and that the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account will be reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government. Likewise, it is recommended that Management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to immediately submit liquidation reports for fund transfers which are already utilized and implemented, so as to timely determine proper utilization and implementation of funded barangay projects.

We recommended that Management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in the timely implementation of projects funded so as to ensure that funds received are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and that expected services and benefits which could be derived therefrom are enjoyed by the public at the most opportune time.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor continue to prioritize the processing of titles so as to acquire adequate and legal proof of ownership over these parcels of land pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and in order not to disrupt normal LGU operations and prevent loss of structures already built thereon. It is also recommended that Management provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of certificates of title.

We recommended that Management ensure the full utilization of the unexpended balance of MMDA financial assistance the soonest possible time in order to optimally serve the purpose thereof which is to extend assistance to the LGU in carrying out its task to alleviate the effects of Typhoon Odette within the municipality. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the corresponding Liquidation Report to the Audit Team pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of this account and regularly monitor the changes of Agricultural Produce for Distribution to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management: (1) return the unexpended balances from fund transfers to the source agency; (2) immediately remit the share of other LGUs on various collections and; (3) instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reclassification entries to properly identify those amounts which are due to NGAs and immediately remit the same.

We recommended that management create and Inspection and Appraisal Committee as well as Disposal Committee which shall take charge over the inspection, appraisal and disposal procedures in accounting for unserviceable and lost properties and follow the procedures and secure documents set forth in COA Circular Nos.2019-003 and 2020-006 and COA Memorandum No.92-751.

We recommended that henceforth, Management observe the provisions of COA Circular 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other LGUs so as to ensure that funds received are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and those services and benefits due to the public are not delayed.

We recommended that the Management initiate the conduct of an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by Management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer (MSWO) conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and that the MSWO thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible while for accounts deemed unrecoverable,  a request for authority to write-off accounts shall be made with COA, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability thereof.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify excess funds which are due for remittance to source agencies; (b) identify excess of fund transfers which are to be requested with approval to be used for other useful programs/projects and; (c) prepare the necessary reclassification entries to properly identify those accounts which are due to LGUs and NGAs and those which are to be recorded using the account “Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share”.

We recommended that management establish an accounting and operating system in running Masanao operations to have a system of internal control and proper administrative practices installed, to ensure compliance with laws and regulations and to provide for efficient, economical and effective operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a review on the collectability of the dormant receivable accounts, as well the validity and complete documentation for the payable accounts, and take appropriate action pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2016-005, in the event that write-off of such accounts are warranted, in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

a) The Management assign personnel to exert efforts specifically in tracing back prior years’ transactions and records, and identify the unidentified reconciling items to reconcile the balances between the book and the bank pertaining to the LBP account nos. 0522-1114-30 and 0522-1145-29;

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to record the identified book reconciling items; and

c) Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the balances of cash in bank accounts which still remained recorded in the books of accounts of the LGU, but for which the corresponding bank accounts are already closed; and prepare the necessary adjustments, as warranted.

Further, we recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, make representations with concerned depository banks relative to the unrecorded balances of DBP account no. 5539-840-6/0840034563082 and LBP account no. 0522-1133-28, conduct reconciliation of balances with the bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 1)

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations:

a) The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts;

b) . . .

c) . . .

We further recommended that:

a) The Municipal Accountant submit to the Office of the Auditor the Journal Entry Voucher to set up the RPT and SET Receivables together with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and

b) The Municipal Treasurer conduct proper and timely monitoring and reporting of the list of tax delinquencies as basis for reconciliation with the receivables recorded by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 3)
(This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 6)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a) The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the costs of donated farm machineries and equipment and its accumulated depreciation, taking into consideration the date of receipt of the same;

b) The Municipal Accountant make representation with the DA-PhilMECH relative to the deed of donation including cost information of the three units hand tractor as basis for the recording of the same in the books of accounts of the LGU; and

c) The Municipal Accountant make representation with the DPWH relative to the project turnover documents including cost information of the newly rehabilitated municipal hall building as basis for the recording of the same in the books of accounts of the LGU.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 7)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a) . . .; 

b) . . .; and

c) The Management cause the reversion of accounts which remained outstanding for over two (2) years and for which no actual claim has been filed, or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record, or for which the purpose of the trust fund has already been accomplished.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 11)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a) Process immediately the return of the unutilized balances of fund transfers to concerned source agencies and/or to the National Treasury, as applicable; and

b) Determine the accuracy and validity of the unremitted dues and payables to the BIR; reclassify the same from the Due to NGAs account to the Due to BIR account pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; and cause the immediate remittance, if warranted.

observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 13)

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 53 of IPSAS 3.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to prior period errors and adjustments pursuant to Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 14)

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management take full opportunity on the application of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and fully comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and the recording of accounting entries and disclosures in the notes to financial statements, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We further recommended that Management reconstitute its Inventory Committee through a Memorandum or Executive Order and indicate therein the specific duties and responsibilities of its members pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

After reconstitution, the Inventory Committee prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, to be approved by the local chief executive and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 17)

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer, as General Services Officer, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the subject parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant determine the unidentified balance of P8,880.00 recorded under the Land account, and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:

a) Prepare and submit the PIF as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and

b) Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

We recommended that the LDRRMC, thru its LDRRM Officer:

a) Ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the LGU’s disaster risk reduction and management activities; and

b) Identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended balances of LDRRMF which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and incorporate the same in the AIP for approval by the local sanggunian.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2023 accounts as AO No. 22)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management, thru its Local Finance Committee, revert the unobligated/unexpended balances of all MOOE items under the current year appropriations to the surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year pursuant to Section 322 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management:

a) Grant contract time extensions due to unfavorable weather conditions after careful examination of the complete and sufficient documents such as, but not limited to, weather reports from PAGASA and Monthly Time Suspension Report as provided under DPWH DO No. 42 s.2020 and Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;

b) Require contractors to post performance securities with complete details as to its period of validity and/or expiry; and

c) Require contractors to cause the extension of their performance securities corresponding the contract time extensions granted to them.

We recommended that Management adopt strategies such as tax campaign and information drive on remedies that can be enforced to improve collection efficiency. Also, request the assistance of the Barangays for the Municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of Notices of Delinquencies to the delinquent real property owners within their territorial jurisdiction.

We further recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer immediately cause the posting of Notice of Tax Delinquency at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay concerned; and service of the same to real property owners.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the (a) MA
and the MT to continuously (i) strengthen cash management controls through
compliance with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting of collections and
deposits; and (ii) coordinate and reconcile their cash records for early detection and
correction of errors; and (b) MT to advise personnel responsible for recording
barangay collections to thoroughly review the Abstract prepared by BTs to prevent
erroneous recording of collections and deposits.

2.4. We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the MA to (i) reclassify the
erroneously recorded CAs totaling ₱8,387,500.00 from Advances to Officers and
Employees account to Advances to SDO; (ii) assign one of her personnel to
monitor CAs, ensuring timely liquidation within the prescribed period; (b) designate
additional SDO to prevent the granting of multiple CAs to a single AO; (c) require the
MT to (i) increase the fidelity bond of SDO to an amount sufficient to cover her
maximum accountability; (ii) cease the practice of granting CAs for special
purposes to officials not designated as SDO and not bonded except for traveling
expenses; and (iii) direct the payment of expenses to suppliers/creditors and refrain
from processing transactions through CAs except for petty expenses; (d) instruct the
MA and MT to require the AOs to submit lacking supporting documents in the
liquidation of their CAs and limit the use of RER in the payment of goods and
services; (e) require the MA to (i) submit the LRs that were already recorded in the
books of accounts; and (ii) ascertain the completeness of LRs before recording them
in the books of accounts.

3.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to (i) record all acquisitions
of inventories to their appropriate Inventory account and recognize related expense
account upon receipt of SSMI supported with RIS from the SO; and (ii) maintain
updated SLCs; and (b) SO to (i) conduct a physical count of inventories on a semi-
annual basis and submit the RPCI to the MA and AT for reference in audit; and (ii)
prepare and maintain SCs, RIS, and SSMI to secure compliance with the applicable
provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.

4.4 We recommended that the LCE (a) establish an IC tasked with conducting the
annual physical count of PPE and rendering the RPCPPE based on the results; (b)
require the MA to (i) prepare PPELCs and regularly update records for adequate
monitoring of PPE; (ii) reconcile records with the Municipal Assessor and make
representations with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) for the titling of the 40
parcels of land with TDs under the Municipality’s name for proper valuation in the
books; (iii) adjust semi-expendable properties to their respective expense accounts;
and (iv) reconcile records with the MTO for the recognition of 11 unaccounted
transportation equipment in the books of accounts; (c) require the MTO to (i)
maintain PCs for each class of PPE and prepare ARE prior to their issuance to end-
user to ensure accountability over Municipality-owned assets; and (ii) implement a
system to actively monitor and facilitate the timely renewal of motor vehicle
registrations; and (d) require the DAC to expedite the preparation of relevant
documents to cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets.

5.6 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MBO Chief to monitor the
appropriations vis-à-vis obligation to avoid the recurrence of the same deficiency; and
(b) MA in coordination with the SDO, submit the (i) certified true copies of requisite
documents for CAs for CF; (ii) DV in an enclosed envelope to the AT within seven
days after the release of check; and (iii) LR to ICFAU within the prescribed period.

6.16 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to (i) give preferential
attention and expedite the processes enumerated in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular
No. 2016-05 for the proper write-off of dormant accounts; (ii) prepare and maintain
subsidiary ledgers for the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs accounts to adequately
monitor the accounts; and (iii) refrain from the inappropriate use of RER as proof of
payment and comply with the rules and regulations relative thereto: and (b)
concerned Municipal officials to identify and prioritize projects to be funded by the
DOE funds for immediate implementation.

7.4 We recommended that the (a) SB members prioritize the updating of the
decades-old LRC; and (b) LCE require the BPLO Chief and MT to work towards
achieving the projected income outlined in the appropriations ordinance by (i)
maintaining reliable and complete business taxpayers’ ledger cards to monitor
collections and identify delinquent taxpayers; (ii) conducting random examinations
of business taxpayers’ books of accounts; (iii) ensuring that all essential documents
for the computation of business taxes are submitted by business taxpayers upon
registration and renewal; and (iv) levying business taxes in accordance with the
provisions of the LRC.

8.39 We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the MDC to (i) aim for full
implementation of PPAs at the set time to ensure the continuity of the LGU’s
development efforts towards the attainment of targets; and (ii) stop the practice of
transferring funds appropriated for the 20% DF to different barangays as the Fund
is intended for development projects to be implemented by the Municipality; (b)
require the MA to (i) record all fund transfers to appropriate account, make the
necessary adjustments in the books, and demand the submission of liquidation
reports with complete required supporting documents from the recipient
barangay; and (ii) ensure that supporting documents are sufficient and valid
before payments are made; (c) require the Municipal Budget Office (MBO) to
(ii) stop charging ineligible expenses from the DF; (d) require the BAC to strictly
observe the requirements for the alternative mode of procurements and/or subject
all procurements to public bidding; and (e) require the ME to (i) to cause the
contractor to repair the portion of the project found defective at no additional cost
by the Municipality; and (ii) prepare a Status of Work Accomplished for every
infrastructure project implemented by administration as basis for the issuance of
Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of the agency.

9.8 We recommended that the LCE require (a) the LDRRMO Head to (i) stop the
practice of implementing PPAs beyond their appropriated amounts; and (ii) optimize
the utilization of the fund to lessen the socio economic and environmental impacts of
disasters in the community; (b) the BAC to (i) post in the PhilGEPS the RFQ and
Award for the procurements covered by the AMP – Shopping; (ii) issue BAC
Resolutions recommending the use of alternative mode of procurement for approval
of the LCE; (c) the MA to (i) immediately submit the requisite supporting documents
for the AT’s verification; (ii) ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and
proper documentation before processing claims for payments to establish the
validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements; and (d) the MA and MBO to
prepare and submit the required reports within the prescribed period to ensure the
availability of information on the receipts and utilization of DRRMF.

10.5 We recommended that the LCE require the SO in charge of accepting deliveries of
procured items to comply strictly with the submission of delivery documents to the AT
within 24 hours upon acceptance thereof.

11.10 We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairperson of the SWMB in
coordination with the MENRO and barangays, to exert more efforts in their campaign
towards safe and sanitary SWM by (a) requiring business establishments to (i) put
preference in using biodegradable packing materials; (ii) install different waste
receptacles and eco-friendly signages; (b) discourage littering and open burning by
imposing penalties against violators; (c) address the issue on the delayed to non-
collection of garbage in both Barangay and Municipal levels to encourage
stakeholders to actively participate in the LGU’s initiatives towards safe and sanitary
SWM; and (d) focus on the repair and maintenance of damaged/idle machinery
and/or installation of new machinery and equipment to improve its recycling
performance.

12.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the
amounts of GSIS contributions recorded in the books to trace the noted discrepancy
and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly comply with the
GSIS Act of 1997 in the timely and full remittance of the withheld contributions and
loan payments.

13.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the
contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the books and make the necessary
adjustments to properly account for the prior years’ non-moving balance; and (b)
strictly comply with RA No. 9679 and on the withholding and full remittance of Pag-
IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

14.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) continue to
comply with the withholding and timely remittance of PhilHealth contributions and
(b) facilitate reconciliation of records with the Human Resource Management Office
and the PHIC to address the recurring observation on the unaccounted/unremitted
balance of Due to PhilHealth account and effect the necessary adjustments in the
books, if warranted.

15.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the
amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted
discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly
comply with BIR No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended by BIR RR No. 11-2018
and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of
the same within the prescribed period.

16.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the GFPS to (i) continue
its faithful compliance with the budgetary requirement for the GAD Fund; and (ii)
exert effort in mobilizing its proponent offices to accomplish all the planned PPAs
and fully utilize the GAD budget in the succeeding years; and (b) the MPDO to
spearhead the setting up, maintenance, and updating of the GAD database to serve
as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly enforce the laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to suspend processing of additional cash advances of officials and employees unless settlement of previous advances is made; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to send notices 10 days before expiration of the 30-day period to concerned personnel to settle their accounts or face possible administrative and legal sanctions for non-compliance despite notice.

We recommended that the LCE cause the immediate liquidation of the CA for CF amounting to ₱83,000.00 through the submission of the required documentation to the COA Central Office in accordance with COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that management ensure that only eligible PPAs are included in the POPS Plan so that computation of the 30% allocation for CF using the POPS Plan as basis is accurate to comply with Sections 4.1.3 and 5.1.3.1 of Joint Circular No.2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of the COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND.

We recommended that Management ensure that the CF will only be allocated if the current peace and order situation in the municipality is really a priority concern as certified and evaluated by the Local PNP Chief, whose certification justifies the necessity of the fund allocation.

We commended the Management for the substantial implementation of development projects programmed for CY 2023 as provided under JMC No. 2020-1 dated November 4, 2020. Nonetheless, for the project FTMR at Barangay Siay to Pacogon, we recommended that the Management fast track the implementation of the related infrastructure projects so that said FTMR can be used the soonest time. Likewise, the Management shall fast track the necessary process of operationalizing the TCA at Barangay Siay so that the use of the access road therein will be maximized.
We recommended that the Management ensure in the succeeding years that transfer of actual cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of current year LDRRMF be made immediately at year end.

We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare and submit immediately to the Audit Team the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF to comply with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 and Item 24, Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written justification for the inconsistency noted on the computation of mobilization/demobilization cost, OSH component, and other indirect costs in the ABC with the provisions of DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016;
Should after the Audit Team’s evaluation the justification is untenable, require the contractor to refund the excess amount paid or deduct from remaining claim/s as necessary; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure the reasonableness of the computation of the ABC for all infrastructure projects by adhering to the provisions of applicable laws, rules and regulations to avoid excessive computation of the ABC.

We recommended that Management:
Require the contractor to immediately rectify the defects/deficiencies noted considering that it is still within the defects-liability period. Otherwise, Management shall take appropriate actions to prevent further damage and deterioration of the structure; and
Direct the Municipal Inspectorate Team to be cautious at all times in the conduct of inspection of LGU projects to ensure that visible defects/deficiencies are reported and recommended for rectification to ensure that completed projects comply with the specifications of the approved plans indicated in the contract.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written justification for the incongruency noted between the construction quantities in the detailed estimates and plans/drawings for the project; otherwise,require the contractor to refund the excess amount paid; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be circumspect in the preparation of detailed estimates which shall properly reflect the correct measurements of the area in the plans/drawings.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that project signboards/ billboards are properly installed and conform with the prescribed guidelines of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
Instruct the Municipal Inspectorate Committee to monitor the delivery of the required quantity of project signboards/billboards as stipulated in the contract. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance will be issued for non-compliance/violation of the afore-cited rules and regulations;
Notify the COA Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted for validation; and
Require the BAC and the TWG to carefully evaluate all bid proposals such that detailed breakdown of estimates and/or unit cost analysis/derivation for each work item expressed in volume/area/lump sum/lot, particularly for project signboards and billboards, will be strictly required in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the LCE, as the Head of the Procuring Entity, direct the BAC to strictly observe the provisions of the RIRR on inviting observers in all stages of procurement undertaken through competitive bidding. Likewise, the BAC shall send out invitation to a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and to an NGO, both registered with SEC or CDA and meet the criteria set forth in Section 13.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the LCE, as the Head of the Procuring Entity, direct the BAC to strictly observe the provisions of the RIRR on inviting observers in all stages of procurement undertaken through negotiated procurement.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the BAC ensure that the validity of the Performance Security of the projects provided by the winning bidder extends up to the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance in accordance with Sections 39.1, 39.3, and 62.2 of RIRR of RA 9184; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to validate the Performance Security attached to the claims for payment if its validity covers up to final acceptance of the project.
For projects not yet issued a Certificate of Final Inspection, management should require the winning bidder to extend the validity of the Performance Security covering the one-year period from date of completion.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the BAC to conduct careful evaluation of all procurement transactions and recommended the appropriate mode of procurement. The BAC shall be guided by the definition of “regular and ordinary office supplies” in resorting to shopping per Sec. 52.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184.
Likewise, in resorting to any alternative mode of procurement, the LCE shall direct the BAC to refrain from splitting of government contracts and ensure compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.
We recommended that Management prepare an annual comprehensive and systematic plan specifying PPAs and target outcomes under the SWM Program to comply with Section 305 (g) of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management exercise prudence and judgment in the conduct of programs and activities and ensure that all expenses incurred therein are necessary in the delivery of basic services to the constituents of the Municipality. It shall refrain from incurring unnecessary expenses to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the LCE cause the immediate refund of claims for hotel accommodation in excess of the prescribed rates totaling ₱30,230.00.
Prospectively, the LCE shall ensure strict compliance with EO No. 77, particularly on the grant of claims for hotel accommodation, to avoid the incurrence of excessive expenditures.

1.12 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the following:
a.	Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to establish the reliability of the conduct of actual physical count of PPEs in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. 	Accounting Office and General Services Office to determine the unreconciled balances and make necessary adjustments if any; and
c. 	Personnel concerned to consider the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006, and reclassify the PPE accounts below the ₱50,000.00 threshold to its appropriate semi-expendable property account and adjust the beginning balance in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity in order to conform with COA Circular No. 2022-004.

2.15 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to send demand letters to require all accountable officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate or refund the overdue cash advances granted to them, otherwise the necessary sanction for its non-settlement such as withholding of salary should be enforced.
2.16 	We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that additional cash advances are not granted to officials and employees with previous unliquidated cash advances in accordance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations and to attach a certification on the grant of a new cash advance that previous cash advances of the concerned accountable officers were liquidated.
2.17 	We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish to the Audit Team a copy of the demand letters issued to accountable officers with outstanding cash advances for review, verification and appropriate action.
2.18 	We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the unliquidated cash advances of officials and employees who are already separated from service and recommend for write-off upon sufficient proof that collection thereof is no longer feasible, pursuant to existing guidelines issued by the Commission on Audit

3.12 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
Personnel concerned to submit the aforementioned lacking documentary requirements to support and justify the payments made and avoid suspension or disallowance in audit;
b. 	NGO concerned to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them so that the Municipal Accountant can submit the lacking documents and liquidation reports to the Audit Team for verification and post audit; and
c. 	Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries, in order to present the accurate balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

4.12 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw JEVs to record the book reconciling items that require adjustments and corrections in the general ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011.
4.13 We also recommended and the Local Chief Excutive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. 	Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks;
b. 	Enforce the sending of written notices to the payees at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
c. 	Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

We recommended that:
The MSWDO to submit to the Municipal Accountant all the pertinent forms, documents and reports relative to the distribution of welfare goods to the fire victims in November 2023;
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the journal entry recognizing the issuance of welfare goods in order to properly reflect the Welfare Goods for Distribution account in the financial statements.
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the following journal entry to properly recognize the receipt of donations in CY 2023, as follows:
Government Equity			₱827,900.00
Prior Period Adjustment			₱827,900.00

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance granted by the municipal government.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Engineer make a follow-up with the contractor and cause the immediate completion of the project;
The Municipal Engineer examine and/or conduct an investigation as to the reason/s for suspension of work, variation order and/or time extension as requested by the contractor, duly supported with proper documents, taking into consideration Items 10.1, 10.2, 11.1, 11.3 and 9.1 of Annex E of the R-IRR of RA 9184, in order to justify the grant of Suspension Order, Variation Order and/or Time Extension to represent days with justifiable delays.
The Municipal Accountant compute for liquidated damages on the contractor for those days which are found to be of no justifiable cause/s and do not properly merit the grant of Suspension Order, Variation Order and/or Time Extension, in order to cover for the cost of delayed project implementation which consequently caused the delayed delivery of services to the public.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to properly record transactions by recognizing the appropriate expense accounts to fair fairly present expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record liquidation of cash advance for confidential/intelligence expenses only upon receipt of a copy of the transmittal of documents duly acknowledged by the ICFAU pursuant to Item 6.3.3.e of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in order to fairly present the Advances to Officers and Employees and Confidential Expenses in the financial statements.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a written request to
the depository banks for the release of the LGU’s monthly bank statements
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.

1.10 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly the
Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts within the
prescribed period to determine the causes of the difference between the
Subsidiary Ledgers and the Bank Statements; prepare the necessary
adjustments in the LGU books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in
Bank accounts then submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.

2.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a written directive, with
timelines and sanctions, compelling the Inventory Committee, to perform the
following tasks, and monitor their compliance thereon:
a.) Accomplish and complete in CY 2024 the comprehensive physical count
of all properties owned by the Municipality;
b.) Prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form, with each PPE properly
classified as to serviceable and unserviceable, with proper identification
of persons accountable, status of serviceability and location for immediate
submission to the Municipal Accountant before the yearend closing of the
LGU books of accounts; and
c.) conduct an investigation on non-existing/missing PPEs and prepare a
separate report thereon for immediate submission to the Municipal
Accountant as a pre-requisite for the filing of the request for
derecognition.

2.16 Also, we recommended that the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant
maintain, and update monthly, Property Stock Cards and PPE Ledger Cards,
respectively, for each category of assets.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to re-classify the total costs of
completed projects from Construction in-Progress account to the
appropriate PPE account based on the report of the Municipal Engineer;
and
b. Compute and record the related depreciation of each completed PPEs.

3.12 We likewise recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit a report to the
Municipal Accountant immediately upon completion of each infrastructure
project supported with the Certificate of Completion for immediate recording
of the asset.

4.15 We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Verify the discrepancies between the Notes to Financial Statements,
the GL Balance of Trust Liability-DRRM account and the Utilization
Report and make the necessary corrections, where necessary;
b. Tally the amounts and balances of LDRRM Fund presented in the
Notes to Financial Statements and ensure that it is consistent with the
source documents/registries.

4.16 During the exit conference, the representative from the Municipal Accountant’s
Office agreed to the audit recommendation.

5.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Municipal
Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the MDRRM Officer, to set a cut-off
date every December for the processing of transactions to be charged to
LDRRMF in order to determine before yearend the unexpended QRF and
DRRMF- MOOE due for transfer to the Special Trust Fund.

5.7. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant notify, before yearend,
the Municipal Treasurer in writing of the amount of unexpended QRF and
DRRMF- MOOE to be deposited to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund before the
last working day of each year.

6.16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer
discontinue the implementation of projects by administration UNTIL such
time that the LGU has procured the needed equipment and machineries, or
borrowed/leased from other government agencies; otherwise, implement
projects wholly by contract.

7.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a written directive to the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group, to
submit a written justification, within thirty (30) days, on their failure to
declare AVANTRACT Heavy Machinery, Inc. ineligible despite the
information that it was in default to deliver a previous obligation.

7.13 We recommended that the BAC and TWG initiate, in the ensuing biddings,
the suspension and blacklisting proceedings of bidders who committed any of
its grounds during the competitive bidding stage and consider the following
information submitted by them:
a. Red flags/history of delays on the completion of projects disclosed in
the Technical Proposals;
b. Availability of the supplies in its site/place of business and/or its
ability to acquire or made available the supplies/goods contracted.

7.14 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the
liquidated damages based on the maximum allowable extension of not longer
than the initial delivery period of 30 days as stated in the original contract.

8.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the Disbursement Voucher/s, and the Municipal Treasurer to
issue the corresponding check/s, for those amounts under the Due to
NGAs account which remain idle, even without details, especially those
covered with Demand Letters from the various NGAs and remit the
same to the Bureau of Treasury, informing the Source Agencies of such
return; and
b) determine the source and balances of the unknown fund transfers
amounting to ₱224,612.39, otherwise, return the funds to the Bureau of
Treasury.

9.4. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Budget Officer
and the Municipal DRRM Officer:
a.) Replenish the amount of LDRRM Fund improperly utilized in CY
2023 totaling ₱541,500.00 through a re-alignment from the CY 2024
MOOE appropriations in the General Fund to restore the cost of the
ineligible and irregular expenses; and
b.) Stop using the LDRRM Fund for operational and administrative
expenditures of the MDRRM Office, instead, provide sufficient budget in
the General Fund for its operations.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Budget
Officer allocate funds for Confidential Funds at an amount not exceeding
30% of the total annual amount appropriated for the Peace and Order
Program (POP) of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer properly acknowledge all receipt of funds thru the issuance of official receipt particularly those received in the last working day of the calendar year; and
The Municipal Accountant carefully analyze and record transactions in the books of accounts of the GF and TF pertaining to the transfer of unexpended balances of the LDRRMF incurred at the last working day of the calendar year to reflect the accurate account balances in the consolidated financial statements as at year-end.

We recommended that Management:
Request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts in accordance with existing COA rules and regulations;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Schedules of Dormant Accounts quarterly and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor or before the 15th day of the ensuing month; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose comparative information in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to the dormant accounts of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more efforts to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze and record transactions affecting or involving the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, ensure that the corresponding entries are made in all books of accounts of the LGU, to avoid the incurrence of discrepancies in the consolidated account balances.
Further, for the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries for discrepancies which were already identified and furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).

We recommended that:
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
The Municipal Accountant:
Present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks; and
Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road
The General Services Officer:
Prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network; and
Prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.
The Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical inventory of the LGU’s local roads, prepare the RPCLRN with complete details as to road network ID, road components, construction dates and conditions of each road network, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments to reclassify the carrying amounts of the subject issued semi-expendable properties from the respective PPE accounts into the Prior Period Adjustments account;
The Municipal Accountant observe the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 for PPE items, and recognize tangible items with unit cost below P50,000.00 as semi-expendable property pursuant to COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; and
Management keep abreast with the latest guidelines affecting PPE and semi-expendable properties, and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 particularly with the preparation and maintenance of prescribed forms, registries, and reports to strengthen control over the LGU’s semi-expendable properties.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant conduct analysis of the Accounts Payable, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify subject accounts payable into its appropriate liability accounts;
The Management determine the validity of accounts particularly those pertaining to the aid to barangays, livelihood grants, financial assistance and payables to officers and employees for their salaries, benefits, emoluments and other allowances, prioritize and/or cause the immediate payment or release of funds, as warranted; and
The Management cause the reversion of accounts which remained outstanding for over two (2) years and for which no actual claim has been filed, or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record, or for which the purpose of the trust fund has already been accomplished.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the noted variances between the balances per books and per bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 53 of IPSAS 3; and
Prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to prior period errors and adjustments pursuant to Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fairly present the Statement of Cash Flows taking into consideration the actual inflows and outflows of cash transactions incurred during the calendar year. Further, consider the upgrading/improvement of the existing accounting computer system for the generation of a more accurate and fairly presented complete set of financial statements.

We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, make representations with concerned source agencies, determine the necessity of maintaining the subject cash in bank accounts with unused/idle funds and return such funds to the source agencies and/or revert the same to the National Treasury or the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other priority government programs, projects and activities.
After determining that the subject cash in bank accounts are no longer necessary, Management thru a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution apply with the concerned depository banks for the closure of the subject bank accounts.

We recommended that the General Services Officer:
Process immediately the renewal of registration of all motor vehicles owned by the LGU with expired registration, the penalty for the late registration thereof be shouldered by the person responsible; and
Exert efforts and/or make representations with the concerned supplier and the LTO to obtain the Certificates of Registration of the 1 unit Ford Raptor pick-up vehicle and 1 unit six-wheeler dump truck.

We recommended that that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:
Determine the validity of the balances of barangay and provincial shares from RPT collections and initiate the immediate release thereof and/or effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted;
Ensure the timely release of barangay shares on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter; and
Make representations with the Provincial Government for the refund and/or application for advance payment relative to the over-remittance of provincial shares from RPT collections.

We reiterated our recommendations that the LDRRM Council, thru its LDRRM Officer:
Ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the LGU’s disaster risk reduction and management activities; and
Identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended balances of LDRRMF which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and incorporate the same in the AIP for approval by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately process the transfer of the unexpended balance of the MF-MOOE amounting to P15,091.16 to the Special Trust Fund; and
Direct the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to carefully review and monitor the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF and ensure that the unexpended balances of the QRF and MF-MOOE are transferred to the STF, while unexpended balances of the MF-CO be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed and any savings thereof be made available for use in other disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that Management comply with the requirement under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1 relative to the composition of mandatory positions under LDRRM Office to be composed of the LDRRM Officer and three LDRRM staff responsible for administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning, respectively.

We recommended that Management, in close coordination with the PSA, establish, maintain and institutionalize a CBMS as a tool for the formulation and implementation of poverty alleviation and development programs.

We recommended that Management:
Correct the noted deficiencies and comply with the fire safety measures prescribed under Rule 10, Chapter 2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9514 to ensure the safety of the occupants of government buildings and structures and to prevent the risks of damage or loss of life and property; and
Comply with the submission of plans/designs of government buildings to the BFP for review, evaluation and assessment and their issuance of FSEC as a pre-requisite for the issuance of the building permits by the Office of the Building Official.

We recommended that the Local Development Council include in its AIP, specific and detailed programs, projects and activities, such that all the Local Chief Executive (LCE) needs to do is to execute the contract, and no further approval or authorization by the sangguniang bayan is required. Otherwise, the LCE secure the sangguniang bayan’s resolution authorizing the former to enter into a contract, in the implementation of its programs, projects and activities described in generic terms in the LGU’s AIP.

We recommended that Management institutionalize a policy relative to the preparation of invitation/notice of meetings and/or activity/program design together with the agenda of the meeting or objectives of the activity/program, list or number of target participants and minutes of the meetings to establish the reasonableness and appropriateness of expenditures for procured meals and snacks during meetings, activities, trainings and other events. In line with this, the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements for meals and snacks are supported with complete supporting documents including the aforesaid documents.

We recommended that Management properly accomplish its purchase orders and indicate the relevant information as prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-010 to ensure that the items delivered are in accordance with the terms and conditions as intended by Management.

We recommended that Management, through its GFPS, endorse to the SB, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313, which if passed, be disseminated or posted in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance the following: (a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; (b) imposition of fines/penalties for violation thereof; and (c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
(a) instruct the MT and MA to
(i) continuously comply with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting of collections and deposits pursuant to existing regulations; and
(ii) coordinate and reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors; and
(b) designate a personnel in-charge of recording the ORs and CTC issued by market collectors and barangay treasurers, respectively, to permit complete and reliable information generated by the System.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the
(a) MA to
(i) make the necessary adjustments in the books to properly recognize inventories on-hand at year-end and recognize as expense the distributed stocks accordingly;
(ii) maintain SLCs; and
(iii) consistently apply the PIM in the recording of inventories upon purchase;
(b) GSO Officer to
(i) prepare and maintain SCs and SSMI for control and monitoring of inventories; and
(ii) conduct physical count of inventories on a regular basis and submit the results thereof to the MAO and AT for reference in audit; and
(c) GSO Officer and MA to
(i) reconcile inventory records; and
(ii) establish an Inventory Management process indicating the required documents to be prepared upon receipt/delivery, issuance, and at month-end, including the deadline for the submission of reports, and to whom the reports are to be submitted as prescribed under the MNGAs for LGUs to ensure accuracy of records, promote operational efficiency, and encourage adherence to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
(a) relieve the Inventory Committee members from their regular duties and require them to devote full time in the conduct of physical inventory taking until the same is completed;
(b) require the MA to
(i) take immediate steps in accounting for PPE accounts and derecognition of semi-expendable, unserviceable and non-existing/missing PPE and reconcile their records with the GSO, Municipal Assessor, Inventory Committee, and MTO and take up the differences based on the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
(ii) maintain complete PPELCs for monitoring of PPEs; and
(c) require the GSO Officer to
(i) conduct physical count of PPE at least once a year to establish the accuracy and existence of PPEs;
(ii) maintain PCs for each class of PPE and ARE; and
(iii) renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the Municipality with the LTO to avoid unnecessary expenses from payment of damages to third person liabilities and penalties in case of vehicular accident.

We recommended and the LCE, as head of the MDC agreed to
(a) continue to improve and enhance the Municipality’s development planning and project prioritization to ensure that its manner of appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is invariably compliant with the prescribed criteria thereon;
(b) address the issue on timely completion of projects; and
(c) aim full implementation of PPAs at the set time to ensure the continuity, consistency, and strength of the LGU’s development efforts towards the attainment of targets over the long run.

We recommended and LCE agreed to
(a) aim for the full utilization and implementation of the identified development projects under the trust fund for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund;
(b) require the ME to
(i) ensure that the necessary documents for civil works and in case of temporary work suspensions are prepared, duly approved and submitted to the AT within the prescribed period; and
(ii) check the progress of the construction at all reasonable times during the project implementation stage to avoid negative slippage; and
(c) require the MA to
(i) compute for the liquidated damages to be charged to the Contractors concerned for not satisfactorily completing the work within the specified contract time; and
(ii) in coordination with the ME, BAC Chairperson, and MSWDO to submit the documents with noted deficiencies to the AT.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to
(a) exert efforts in identifying the source of funds for the unused balances amounting to ₱979,121.29 and ₱780,243.25 recognized under the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs accounts, respectively;
(b) if warranted, file the request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers duly supported with relevant documents; and
(c) prepare SL for each fund transfers and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of fund transfers to source NGAs, GOCCs, and other agencies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to
(a) review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
(b) strictly comply with BIR No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended by BIR RR No. 11-2018 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended and LCE agreed to require the MA to
(a) review the amounts of GSIS contributions withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted;
(b) ensure that contributions and payments for loans deducted from employees reconcile with GSIS records; and
(c) update the information of employees submitted to the GSIS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to
(a) review the contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
(b) strictly comply with RA No. 9679 and its IRR on the withholding and full remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to
(a) review the amounts of PhilHealth contributions withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
(b) check the amounts withheld and remitted in the books to ensure correctness of data and avoid discrepancies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
(a) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the rationalization and revision of the Local Revenue Code to generate more revenues for the local government; and
(b) identify the problems that face real property tax collection of the Municipality and enforce the procedures and remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided under Section 174 of RA No. 7160 to improve revenue collection.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD Focal Point Person to
(a) continue to comply with the budgetary requirement of at least 5% of the Municipality’s total budget appropriation for GAD PPAs;
(b) prioritize the implementation of the identified PPAs as per GPB to achieve and promote desirable gender responsive society; and
(c) utilize fully the GAD budget in order to attain the objective for which the funds were appropriated.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MIC, in coordination with the ME, to make an updated assessment of the value of subject PPE items as of December 31, 2022. Any reduction in value shall be reflected in the RPCPPE. Afterwards, the Municipal Accountant shall draw a journal entry to record the impairment loss to bring the PPE to its proper valuation for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management:

a.	require the Municipal Health Officer to render a report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines directly received from outside sources; and 

b.	direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer, to determine the full cost of ending inventory of vaccines as at year end, if any and henceforth recognize and record future receipts and issuances of the same.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to determine the proper disposition of the balances of idle fund transfers totaling ₱424,660.37 by reviewing and evaluating the original intended projects and/or by conferring with the Source Agencies, in the absence of a MOA. After careful evaluation, the LFC shall recommend to the LCE whether to continue utilizing the fund or return the same to the Source Agencies.

We recommended that the LCE:

a.	direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the E-TRACS software provider and request for assistance on the generation of RPT/SET receivables data as of December 31, 2022 so this could be duly recorded in the books of the LGU and reported in the FS accordingly; and

b.	require proper coordination with the offices responsible for the preparation of RPTAR as basis for RPT collection and its recording in the books; namely the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure adherence to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on the proper accounting and reporting of RPT and SET in the books of the agency and attain the objectives of consistency and reliability of the FS as enunciated in Section 111 (2) of PD 1445 and Appendix B of PPSAs No. 1.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that PPAs prepared and funded from the LDRRM Fund, particularly the 30% QRF, are for relief and recovery programs in consonance with Section 21 of the IRR of RA No. 10121 and Sections 4.0 and 5.3 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan exercise fully its review function on budgets submitted by the executive department and the details of which, in this case the QRF budget emphasizing compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations relative to disaster response.

We reiterated our recommendation that management faithfully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively control FOL by implementing the following corrective measures:

a. exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved serially numbered DTTs and RIVs;

b. submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit and verification purposes; and

c. The Municipal Accountant to be vigilant in processing claims for payment of FOL, suspending payment unless necessary and documents are attached.
observations.managementAction: GSO commit to prepare the required reports
observations.reason: GSO not fully aware of the preparation of the monthly reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that LCE cause the immediate refund of the U/CA granted to employees who are ineligible to receive the benefits totalling ₱66,000.00 otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit.

Prospectively, Management shall ensure strict compliance with laws, rules, and regulations, particularly DBM BC No. 2018-1 on the grant of U/CA to its officials and employees.

We recommended the following:

1. The LCE to require all officials, employees, and JO/COS personnel granted with travel allowances to submit to the Municipal Accountant the lacking documents for their respective travel as discussed previously, as follows:

a. Certificate of Travel Completed for travel and training expenses totaling ₱2,412,003.69 and ₱568,450.00
b. Certificate of Appearance for expenditures totaling ₱141,868.00
c. Documentation on the results of travel without specific purpose totaling ₱109,419.85
d. Certification that the incurrence of excess hotel accommodation expense totaling ₱117,163.96 was absolutely necessary for the employee to complete their assignment
e. For travels of JO/COS, Certification from the Head of Agency or any authorized approving official that: (a) the official missions/tasks cannot be performed by/or assigned to any other regular/permanent official and/or employee; and (b) the tasks/activities are necessary to fulfill the obligations as contained in his/her contract of service 

The Municipal Accountant shall then submit these documents Audit Team for verification.

2. The LCE shall submit to the Audit Team the authority to travel duly approved by the Provincial Governor for his travel incurring travel expenses totaling ₱304,378.85 and training expenses totaling ₱32,000.00.

3. The LCE shall cause the refund of claims for hotel accommodations in excess of the prescribed rates totaling ₱73,513.96 and transportation expenses where service vehicle was granted totaling ₱20,752.00

4. Lastly, the LCE shall ensure strict compliance with rules and regulation on travel such as EO No. 77, COA Circular No. 2012-001, COA Resolution No. 2021-044 dated December 28, 2021 to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditures.

We recommended that LCE direct all requisitioning departments to only include the technical specifications of items requested and exclude the brand names in the PRs prepared.

Likewise, the LCE shall require an explanation from the BAC for procuring the drone with reference to a specific brand name.

Prospectively, the Management shall ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184 particularly under Section 18 on the prohibition of brand names.

We recommended that the LCE require all payees of internet reimbursement to submit proofs of actual usage of internet connectivity and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for submission to the Audit Team.
	
Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all claims for internet connectivity are supported with adequate documentation before processing the disbursement.

Lastly, the LGU should also consider application from internet service provider/s for a centralized internet connection which could serve all departments instead of allowing internet reimbursements per Department/Office.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Local Development Council to review the PPAs chargeable against the 20% DF and ensure that the same are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready so that their actual completion would contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the collection of tax delinquencies through publication, demand letters and massive tax campaigns in accordance with Sections 254, 255 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to avail of the civil remedies for the collection of real property tax, if warranted by circumstances.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned personnel to perform the following:

Prepare an Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan, and formulate policies and guidelines so that the necessity and propriety of hiring job order employees are justified; 

Incorporate in the contract the specific services to be rendered and the number of man hours, and quantity and duration of the works to be performed by the job order personnel and require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports; and

Instruct the HRMO and all the department heads to review the current manpower matrix to ensure that the hiring and engagement of job orders are in accordance with the needs of the Municipality.

We likewise recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make sure that necessary supporting documents were submitted before the payment of wages/salaries of job hire employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to make representation to the Municipality’s legislative body to ratify an ordinance pertaining to the collection of garbage fees from households in compliance with RA No. 9003, and its IRR.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to stop the subscription of the additional mobile post-paid plan, Unli All Net Text, International call and text, and Netflix subscription and set reasonable limits on use of mobile phones to the entitled officials. Any excess on the limit set shall be for the personal account of the user and will be subject to disallowance.

We also recommended and Management agreed to establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of communication allowances to officials and employees and coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to adopt and implement control measures to ensure that the amount granted for cellphone allowance is reasonable and used for official business only.

We further recommended that the GSO Designate renew the ARE every three years if there is no change in property custodian and likewise issue new ARE whenever there is change in custodianship.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality shall draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withheld the appropriate taxes due the government.
