observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the MDRRMO to make the necessary and constant communication with the donor to secure the proper supporting documents in order to facilitate the recognition of these donated PPEs in the LGU books pursuant to Item IV-C of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and make use thereof for the MDRRMO’s disaster prevention and response activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the recording of the accounts Discount on Real Property Tax – Basic and Discount on Special Education Tax upon collection of Real Property and Special Education Tax which are paid at a discount pursuant to Section 22 of the Manual on NGAS, in order to fully derecognize at gross amount, the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables, as well as the Deferred Real Property Tax and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts, and fairly present these items in the financial statements. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the following adjusting journal entries:

We recommended that Management instruct the MDRRMO to make the necessary and constant communication with the donor to secure the proper supporting documents in order to facilitate the recognition of these donated PPEs in the LGU books pursuant to Item IV-C of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and make use thereof for the MDRRMO’s disaster prevention and response activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce more stringent measures and avail of other remedies in order to collect delinquent taxes pursuant to Sections 256 and 247 of R.A. 7160, to facilitate implementation of programs and projects of the LGU which shall be financed out of collections from tax delinquencies.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Engineer, Program Supervisor and MDDRMO to immediately submit the pertinent document or report which would serve as basis in determining the nature, quantity and total cost of items procured out of the financial assistance from the OP in observance of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately return the excess amount representing the unutilized portion of the financial assistance to the OP for its immediate use and submit the corresponding Liquidation Report to COA for review, in keeping with the provisions of COA Circular No.94-013 and Item 4 of the MOA between OP and LGU.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that only PPAs which are ready for implementation are included in the Annual Investment Plan and that procurement timelines are followed so as to avoid delays in project implementation and make certain that all PPAs are fully implemented at year-end pursuant to the intents of DBM-DILG-DOF JMC No. 1 which is the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, enforce immediate and regular remittance of daily collections by the Collecting Officer to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS in order to fairly present collections of the LGU in a given period, avoid possible loss or misuse of funds and facilitate immediate use of collections for LGU operations and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the Liquidating Officer to observe Section 32 of Manual on NGAS and ensure the immediate and intact deposit of remitted collections in order to prevent misappropriation or loss of public funds and facilitate smooth accounting and recording of collections and deposits.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Assessor to exert efforts in order to facilitate the conduct timely general or particular revision of real property assessments as required under Section 219 of R.A. 7160 and Section I, Chapter III of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations so as to enable collection of additional real property taxes which could be derived from the revised real property assessments.

We recommended that management revisit the current regular plantilla items of the LGU and propose/create another plantilla position when necessary to avoid hiring of contract of service and job order personnel to perform the duties and responsibilities of a regular employee, in compliance with Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of 1998 and Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of Items 3.0 and 4.2 of NDDRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.2014-1 as to the complete creation of the four (4) mandatory positions in the LDRRMO so as to ensure that sufficient number of competent personnel are employed who shall be responsible in areas of (1) administration and training, (2) research and planning and (3) operations and warning.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the account balances per fund and review the recorded transactions for possible errors in recording and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted. When found correct, we also recommend the immediate remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR within the reglementary period as required under Section8 (b) of BIR Revenue Regulation No.14-2002 to avoid incurrence of penalties/surcharges and fines.

We recommended that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the creditable and percentage taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor allocate time to accurately compute the RPT and SET receivables for the year and submit the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1, so as fairly present these receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that: (a) the concerned requisitioning officer or department head, in coordination with the BAC, monitor the delivery of procured goods in accordance with the delivery term as stated in the Purchase Order, to avoid delays in future procurement of goods; (b) the Municipal Engineer apply strict monitoring of project accomplishment in accordance with the terms of the contract, to avoid further delays on-going contracts, and prevent incurrence thereof in the future; (b) the BAC ensures giving emphasis on the importance of timely delivery of goods as well as project completion to the participating bidders during the entire procurement process, as can be documented in the BAC minutes of meeting, so as to improve their consciousness on complying with the terms of the Purchase Order and contracts and give the public reasonable assurance of timely attainment of benefits that can be derived from these programs/projects.

We recommended that management immediately create a database of all constituents in the municipality, and conduct periodic updating thereof so as to have readily available data to be used in cases of emergency, disaster or pandemic and submit to the Audit Team, the complete distribution lists; Instruct the LGU personnel designated to perform the specific tasks on the inventory and distribution of relief packs, keep a uniform and proforma distribution list which shall be updated upon determination of the exact nature and quantity of items to be distributed, and maintain a complete and accurate record of food packs on hand, for transparency purposes. Likewise, we recommended proper monitoring of relief goods so as to ensure timely distribution thereof to constituents and prevent spoilage thereof and wastage of funds, in order to attain the purposes of DBM LBC Nos. 124 and 125 and DILG-DBM JMC No.2020-001.

We recommended that: the Municipal Mayor, with the  assistance of the Municipal Agriculturist, make representation with DARFO7, and ask for copies of donation papers and turn-over documents, duly signed and acknowledged received by concerned parties, in order to provide proof of concrete ownership and control over these donated PPEs, in accordance with IPSAS 17, 23, 29 and 33 and Section 63 of P.D.1445; the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of proper and complete copies of donation papers and turn-over documents, record the donated PPEs in the LGU books at its fair value pursuant to Item 42 of IPSAS 23.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Local Finance Committee and Motorpool In-Charge focus on the afore-cited income accounts and formulate schemes and apply dynamic and systematic measures to maximize revenue generation efficiency rate in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160.

We recommended that the: Municipal Accountant review the composition of subsidiary ledgers for all Construction in Progress accounts so as to provide accurate supporting data for the corresponding general ledger account balances; Municipal Engineer assist the Municipal Accountant by providing copies of Program of Works, Statement of Work Accomplished, Certificates of Completion and Turn-over of all infrastructure projects in the current and prior years, when needed, so as to supply data required in the reconstruction of the CIP account SLs; Municipal Accountant conduct a periodic reconciliation of inventory of completed projects with the records of the Municipal Engineer so as to promptly recognize completed projects and drop the corresponding amount in the related CIP accounts.

We recommended that Management to fast track the implementation of programs/ projects/ activities identified in the budget and ensure inclusions of the projects enumerated under RA 9003 to serve the purpose of the created funds.

We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

a) Send demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees with unsettled accounts for its immediate settlement. In case of non-settlement of employees who are still connected with the LGU, cause the withholding of salaries or any money claims due them;

b) Send demand letters and/or take appropriate actions on the unsettled accounts of accountable officers who are no longer connected or not in active service with the LGU, and of suppliers for their tax deficiencies; and

c) . . .

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the GF and SEF books of accounts to properly recognize the proper receivable and payable accounts, respectively, relative to the erroneous charging of funds in the GF instead of the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a) Request for copies of loan agreements with the concerned depository bank and submit copies of the same to the Office of the Auditor; 

b) . . .; and

c) Present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as applicable, and carefully review and record financial transactions using the appropriate accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer coordinate in the preparation of an accurate and complete SCBAA, taking into consideration the original budgeted amounts in the annual budget, the amounts of supplemental budgets, if any, the amounts of final budget and the balances of all continuing appropriations. Also, the Municipal Accountant present proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for any changes/material differences between the original and final budget.

We recommended that Management:

a) . . .; 

b) . . .; and

c) Relieve the accountable officers from their duties and responsibilities as Collecting Officers.

We recommended the following:

a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer identify the discrepancies between the ledger and cashbook balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in their records to arrive at reconciled balances;

b) The Municipal Treasurer ensure that cashbooks are regularly updated and all financial transactions are properly recorded; and

c) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of balances between the accounting’s ledgers and treasury’s cashbooks at least quarterly.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the subject unexpended balances of the LDRRMF to the STF and recognize the same as “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books of LGU.

We recommended the following:

a) The Management, thru its Human Resource Management Officer, consider adding a stipulation in the contracts of concerned JO/COS personnel their authority to go on official travel and claim traveling expenses;

b) The Local Chief Executive issue a certification pursuant to Item B.5 of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 for official travel of JO/COS personnel, in the absence of stipulation in their contracts authorizing them to go on official travel and claim traveling expenses; and

c) The Municipal Accountant carefully review the supporting documents pertaining to claims of JO/COS personnel for traveling expenses and ensure that such claims are supported with the required certification from the Local Chief Executive or his authorized representative.

We recommended that Management direct the Local Finance Committee to issue a certification certifying the actual sources of funds and indicating therein the specific appropriations affected or new revenue sources, as applicable, to support the realignment of funds for new PPAs and/or objects of expenditures or to augment the same.

We reiterated the following recommendations that Management:

a) Require contractors to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or surety bond to support all requests for advance payment for mobilization fees; and

b) Adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the posting of warranty securities for all completed and accepted infrastructure projects, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects;

c) Submit immediately the lacking documents to support the disbursements relative to the implementation of subject infrastructure projects; and

d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review, examine and ensure that disbursement vouchers for infrastructure projects are supported with complete and adequate supporting documents before such claims are processed and released for payment. Also, the Municipal Accountant consider preparing a checklist of all documentary requirements relative to the disbursements for infrastructure projects.
We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Engineer, exercise due prudence in the preparation of the ABC and POW of infrastructure projects.

We recommended the following:

a) Management set up control mechanisms so that economical, efficient and effective repairs of government property and equipment are undertaken, which include, but not limited to, the following procedures:

• Identification of property or equipment subject for repair and maintenance;
• Conduct of pre-repair inspection and preparation of Pre-Repair Inspection Report;
• Replacement of damaged/worn-out parts and preparation of Report of Waste Materials; and
• Conduct of post-inspection and preparation of Post-Inspection Report; and

b) The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements relative to repairs and maintenance of government property and equipment are duly supported with Pre-Repair Inspection Reports, Post-Inspection Reports and Reports of Waste Materials.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management prepare its APP using the form prescribed by the GPPB to cover all procurements including those items in the APP-CSE; revise and update the APP as needed, and submit the same to the GPPB and the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed deadlines. Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken without an approved APP.

 We further recommended that the BAC prepare its PMR to include not only those procurement activities undertaken thru competitive bidding but also those undertaken thru alternative modes of procurement, as well as the APCPI Results, and submit these reports to the GPPB within the prescribed deadlines. Furthermore, copies of these reports be furnished the Office of the Auditor for monitoring and as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Engineer:

a) For ongoing infrastructure projects without project signboards, immediately post the project signboards in the project sites/locations. Henceforth, comply with the posting of project signboards for all infrastructure projects; and

b) Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards are already posted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a) Devise a tool/mechanism to eliminate the cause of delay in the submission of periodic reports and transaction documents and effectively speed up the processing, segregation and compilation of pertinent documents; and

b) Strictly observe the timely submission of periodic reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management, thru the Municipal Treasurer:

a) Furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders relative to the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the same; and

b) Submit notice of deliveries/completion to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured goods/infrastructure projects within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

We reiterated that Management require the heads/representatives of the implementing offices to ensure that GAD-related activities are implemented as planned, and maximize the utilization of the GAD funds in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided. 

Further, we recommended that the GAD Focal Person adhere to the prescribed format of GAD Accomplishment Report and its timely submission in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that Management, thru its GAD Focal Point System, update its GAD database to include data and other gender-information relating to the institutional sector to better identify organization-focused gender issues and concerns. Further, we recommend that Management include sourcing of data and GAD related information from local offices of NGAs to have a more comprehensive GAD Database. In compliance thereto, Management may consider the allocation of budget in its GAD Plan and Budget for the collection and processing of data requirements of the GAD Database.

We recommended that Management include in its GAD Plan and Budget the attendance of the members of the GAD Focal Point System to capability trainings/seminars to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of various gender assessment tools. Henceforth, the GFPS institutionalize the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of gender assessment tools.

We recommended that Management:

a) . . .

b) Direct the BAC to post the Notice of Award, Purchase Order, including Notice to Proceed, if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the LGU website, if available, and at any conspicuous places in the premises of the LGU, for procurement of goods and services with ABC of above P50,000.00.

We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the imposition of warranty securities in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee for the delivered and accepted goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer/GSO-Designate prepare and issue the AREs to concerned end-users and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, AREs shall be prepared for all property and equipment issued to officials and employees. Also, the Municipal Accountant require the submission of ARE and attach the same to disbursement vouchers as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended the following:

a)	The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer identify the discrepancies between the ledger and cashbook balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in their records to arrive at reconciled balances;

b)	The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of balances between the accounting’s ledgers and treasury’s cashbooks at least quarterly; and

c)	The Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain separate cashbooks for each depository account of the LGU and regularly update the same for proper control and monitoring of funds.

We recommended that Management determine the necessity and authority of maintaining the dormant/non-moving cash-in-bank accounts and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other local government programs and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more efforts to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts. 

We further recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with an accurate and proper balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts; and exert effort to identify the discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts,  reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Require the Municipal Accountant to observe the Perpetual Inventory Method by initially recording the purchases of medical supplies, materials, and relief goods as Inventory, and subsequently recognizing the same as expenses upon issuance/distribution to end-users/intended beneficiaries, and maintain Supply Ledger Cards; and

b)	Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer/GSO to take control and custody of supplies and materials, comply with the prescribed procedures in the requisition and issuance of inventory items, and maintain stock cards.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of local road networks and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network not later than January 31 of each year;

b)	Require the Municipal Accountant to present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks, and to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road; and

c)	Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of a designated General Services Officer, to prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network, and to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer coordinate in the preparation of the SCBAA, taking into consideration the amount balances in the Registries and SAAOB. Also, the Municipal Accountant adhere to the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 24 particularly on the proper presentation of Reconciliation Statement between the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance as disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements and the proper disclosure for any changes between the original and final budget.

We recommended that Management take full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We further recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, to be approved by the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that that the Municipal Assessor and the Acting Municipal Treasurer, as General Services Officer, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the subject parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the unidentified balance of P8,880.00 recorded under the Land account, and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Prepare and submit the Property Inventory Form as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and

b)	Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

We recommended that Management adopt strategies such as tax campaign and information drive on remedies that can be enforced to improve collection efficiency. Also, request the assistance of the Barangays for the Municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of Notices of Delinquencies to the delinquent real property owners within their territorial jurisdiction.

We further recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer immediately cause the posting of Notice of Tax Delinquency at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay concerned; and service of the same to real property owners.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant determine the validity of the prior years’ balances of barangay shares from RPT collections, initiate the immediate release thereof, and henceforth ensure the timely release of barangay shares which should be on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter. Also, make representations with the Provincial Government for the refund and/or application for advance payment relative to the over-remittance of provincial shares from RPT collections.

We recommended that Management optimize the utilization and implementation of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning of project including timing of execution; coordinate action of LGU officials for better result; and strict monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely accomplishment of the project. We recommend further that the MPDC in coordination with the Municipal Development Council (MDC) ensure that the identified projects to be included in the AIP for the 20% Development Fund are properly selected, planned and evaluated.

We recommended that Management prepare its APP using the form prescribed by the GPPB to cover all procurements including those items in the APP-CSE; revise and update the APP as needed, and submit the same to the GPPB and the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed deadlines. Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken without an approved APP.

We further recommended that the BAC prepare its PMR to include all procurement activities undertaken thru competitive bidding and other alternative modes of procurement, as well as the APCPI Results, and submit these reports to the GPPB within the prescribed deadlines. Further, copies of these reports be furnished the Office of the Auditor for monitoring and as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that Management:

b)	Direct the BAC to post the Notice of Award, Purchase Order, including Notice to Proceed, if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the LGU website, if available, and at any conspicuous places in the premises of the LGU, for procurement of goods and services with ABC of above P50,000.00.

We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the imposition of warranty securities in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee for the delivered and accepted goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects.

b)	The Municipal Engineer submit the approved plans/drawings, Statement of Work Accomplished, Certificate of Completion, Certificate of Acceptance, As-built plans and other pertinent documents of the subject infrastructure projects. b)	Submit notice of deliveries/completion to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured goods/infrastructure projects within the prescribed period to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

We recommended that Management:

b)	Adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the posting of warranty securities for completed and accepted infrastructure projects, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects; and

We recommended the following:

a)	The Local Chief Executive secure the sangguniang bayan’s resolution authorizing the former to enter into a contract, in the implementation of its programs, projects and activities described in generic terms in the LGU’s Annual Investment Program (AIP). Also, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation; and

b)	The Local Development Council, if possible, include in its AIP, specific and detailed programs, projects and activities, such that all that the LCE needs to do is to execute the contract, and no further approval or authorization by the sangguniang bayan is required.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer/GSO-Designate prepare and issue the AREs to concerned end-users and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, AREs shall be prepared for all property and equipment issued to officials and employees. Also, the Municipal Accountant require the submission of ARE and attach the same to disbursement vouchers as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer determine and resolve the causes of delay in the submission of periodic reports and transaction documents relative to the receipts and disbursements of government funds, and strictly observe the timely submission of the same within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) instruct the MT and MA to (i) continuously comply with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting of collections and deposits pursuant to existing regulations; and (ii) coordinate and reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors; and (b) designate a personnel in-charge of recording the ORs and CTC issued by market collectors and barangay treasurers, respectively, to permit complete and reliable information generated by the System.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) send demand letters to AOs and employees concerned requiring them to refund/liquidate their outstanding cash advances; (b) require the Human Resources and Management Office (HRMO) to withhold the payment of salary/benefits due the active AOs concerned; (c) institute available legal remedies and sanctions that may apply pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and (d) require the MA to (i) submit subsidiary records for the unaccounted cash advances amounting to ₱200,000.00; and (ii) strictly comply with the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to (i) make the necessary adjustments in the books to properly recognize inventories on-hand at year-end and recognize as expense the distributed stocks accordingly; (ii) maintain SLCs; and (iii) consistently apply the PIM in the recording of inventories upon purchase; (b) GSO Officer to (i) prepare and maintain SCs and SSMI for control and monitoring of inventories; and (ii) conduct physical count of inventories on a regular basis and submit the results thereof to the MAO and AT for reference in audit; and (c) GSO Officer and MA to (i) reconcile inventory records; and (ii) establish an Inventory Management process indicating the required documents to be prepared upon receipt/delivery, issuance, and at month-end, including the deadline for the submission of reports, and to whom the reports are to be submitted as prescribed under the MNGAs for LGUs to ensure accuracy of records, promote operational efficiency, and encourage adherence to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) relieve the Inventory Committee members from their regular duties and require them to devote full time in the conduct of physical inventory taking until the same is completed; (b) require the MA to (i) take immediate steps in accounting for PPE accounts and derecognition of semi-expendable, unserviceable and non-existing/missing PPE and reconcile their records with the GSO, Municipal Assessor, Inventory Committee, and MTO and take up the differences based on the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and (ii) maintain complete PPELCs for monitoring of PPEs; and (c) require the GSO Officer to (i) conduct physical count of PPE at least once a year to establish the accuracy and existence of PPEs; (ii) maintain PCs for each class of PPE and ARE; and (iii) renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the Municipality with the LTO to avoid unnecessary expenses from payment of damages to third person liabilities and penalties in case of vehicular accident.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Management assign personnel to exert efforts specifically in tracing back prior years’ transactions and records, and identify the unidentified reconciling items to reconcile the balances between the book and the bank pertaining to the LBP account nos. 0522-1114-30 and 0522-1145-29;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to record the identified book reconciling items; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the balances of cash in bank accounts which still remained recorded in the books of accounts of the LGU, but for which the corresponding bank accounts are already closed; and prepare the necessary adjustments, as warranted.
Further, we recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, make representations with concerned depository banks relative to the unrecorded balances of DBP account no. 5539-840-6/0840034563082 and LBP account no. 0522-1133-28, conduct reconciliation of balances with the bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts;
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts; and
The Municipal Accountant exert effort to identify the discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
We further recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant submit to the Office of the Auditor the Journal Entry Voucher to set up the RPT and SET Receivables together with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
The Municipal Treasurer conduct proper and timely monitoring and reporting of the list of tax delinquencies as basis for reconciliation with the receivables recorded by the Municipal Accountant.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
Send demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees with unsettled accounts for its immediate settlement. In case of non-settlement of employees who are still connected with the LGU, cause the withholding of salaries or any money claims due them;
Send demand letters and/or take appropriate actions on the unsettled accounts of accountable officers who are no longer connected or not in active service with the LGU, and of suppliers for their tax deficiencies; and
Request for write-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the GF and SEF books of accounts to properly recognize the proper receivable and payable accounts, respectively, relative to the erroneous charging of funds in the GF instead of the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more efforts to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the costs of donated farm machineries and equipment and its accumulated depreciation, taking into consideration the date of receipt of the same;
The Municipal Accountant make representation with the DA-PhilMECH relative to the deed of donation including cost information of the three units hand tractor as basis for the recording of the same in the books of accounts of the LGU; and
The Municipal Accountant make representation with the DPWH relative to the project turnover documents including cost information of the newly rehabilitated municipal hall building as basis for the recording of the same in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Municipal Accountant:
Present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks; and
Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road
The General Services Officer:
Prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network; and
Prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.
The Inventory Committee prepare the RPCLRN with complete details as to road network ID, road components, construction dates and conditions of each road network; and
The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Inventory Committee reconcile the balances of their respective accounting records, property records and the result of the physical count relative to road networks.
We further recommended that:
The Inventory Committee, after its creation, prepare the RPCLRN with complete details as to road network ID, road components, construction dates and conditions of each road network; and
The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Inventory Committee reconcile the balances of their respective accounting records, property records and the result of the physical count relative to road networks.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer conduct reconciliation of documents/records pertaining to the Construction in Progress accounts;
The Municipal Engineer furnish the Municipal Accountant copies of the Certificate of Completion, approved program of works and other pertinent documents as basis for the reclassification of accounts for completed projects; and
The Municipal Accountant identify all costs incurred for each project based on the approved program of works and other relevant documents, and make necessary adjustments to reclassify the completed projects into their appropriate accounts, as warranted.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant conduct analysis of the Accounts Payable, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify some accounts payable into its appropriate liability accounts;
The Management determine the validity of accounts pertaining to aid to barangays, prioritize and/or cause the immediate payment or release of funds, as warranted; and
The Management cause the reversion of accounts which remained outstanding for over two (2) years and for which no actual claim has been filed, or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record, or for which the purpose of the trust fund has already been accomplished.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Request for copies of loan agreements with the concerned depository bank and submit copies of the same to the Office of the Auditor;
Reconcile the noted variances between the balances per books and per bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted; and
Present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Process immediately the return of the unutilized balances of fund transfers to concerned source agencies and/or to the National Treasury, as applicable; and
Determine the accuracy and validity of the unremitted dues and payables to the BIR; reclassify the same from the Due to NGAs account to the Due to BIR account pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; and cause the immediate remittance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as applicable, and carefully review and record financial transactions using the appropriate accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 53 of IPSAS 3.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to prior period errors and adjustments pursuant to Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer coordinate in the preparation of an accurate and complete SCBAA, taking into consideration the original budgeted amounts in the annual budget, the amounts of supplemental budgets, if any, the amounts of final budget and the balances of all continuing appropriations. Also, the Municipal Accountant present proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for any changes/material differences between the original and final budget.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned accountable officer to immediately settle the cash shortage amounting to P183,382.08;
Require the concerned accountable officers to present the subject accountable forms, and/or settle the equivalent money value of the same, as applicable; and
Relieve the accountable officers from their duties and responsibilities as Collecting Officers.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer identify the discrepancies between the ledger and cashbook balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in their records to arrive at reconciled balances;
The Municipal Treasurer ensure that cashbooks are regularly updated and all financial transactions are properly recorded; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of balances between the accounting’s ledgers and treasury’s cashbooks at least quarterly.

We recommended that Management determine the necessity and authority of maintaining the dormant/non-moving cash-in-bank accounts and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other local government programs and projects. After determining that the subject dormant bank accounts are no longer necessary, Management thru a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution apply with the concerned depository banks for the closure of the subject bank accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management take full opportunity on the application of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and fully comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and the recording of accounting entries and disclosures in the notes to financial statements, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.
We further recommended that Management reconstitute its Inventory Committee through a Memorandum or Executive Order and indicate therein the specific duties and responsibilities of its members pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006. After reconstitution, the Inventory Committee prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, to be approved by the local chief executive and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer, as General Services Officer, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the subject parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant determine the unidentified balance of P8,880.00 recorded under the Land account, and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
The Municipal Accountant trace prior years’ records to determine the cost and carrying amounts, if any, of the subject unserviceable heavy equipment and motor vehicles;
The Appraisal Committee conduct appraisal of unserviceable property and equipment reported in the IIRUP, whose appraisal shall be considered as the floor price in the case of disposal thru sale at public auction; and
The Disposal Committee to dispose of the unserviceable property and supplies in accordance with the rules and regulations on supply and property management in local government units.
We further recommended that Management reconstitute its Appraisal and Disposal Committee through a Memorandum or Executive Order and indicate therein the specific duties and responsibilities of its members.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Prepare and submit the PIF as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and
Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

We recommended that the LDRRMC, thru its LDRRM Officer:
Ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the LGU’s disaster risk reduction and management activities; and
Identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended balances of LDRRMF which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and incorporate the same in the AIP for approval by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the subject unexpended balances of the LDRRMF to the STF and recognize the same as “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books of LGU. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant observe annually the transfer of unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the STF to be used for LDRRM programs, projects and activities within the next five years in accordance with the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended the following:
The Management, thru its Human Resource Management Officer, consider adding a stipulation in the contracts of concerned JO/COS personnel their authority to go on official travel and claim traveling expenses;
The Local Chief Executive issue a certification pursuant to Item B.5 of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 for official travel of JO/COS personnel, in the absence of stipulation in their contracts authorizing them to go on official travel and claim traveling expenses; and
The Municipal Accountant carefully review the supporting documents pertaining to claims of JO/COS personnel for traveling expenses and ensure that such claims are supported with the required certification from the Local Chief Executive or his authorized representative.

We recommended that Management, thru its Local Finance Committee, revert the unobligated/unexpended balances of all MOOE items under the current year appropriations to the surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year pursuant to Section 322 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management direct the Local Finance Committee to issue a certification certifying the actual sources of funds and indicating therein the specific appropriations affected or new revenue sources, as applicable, to support the realignment of funds for new PPAs and/or objects of expenditures or to augment the same.

We reiterated the following recommendations that Management:
Require contractors to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or surety bond to support all requests for advance payment for mobilization fees; and
Adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the posting of warranty securities for all completed and accepted infrastructure projects, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects;
Submit immediately the lacking documents to support the disbursements relative to the implementation of subject infrastructure projects; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review, examine and ensure that disbursement vouchers for infrastructure projects are supported with complete and adequate supporting documents before such claims are processed and released for payment. Also, the Municipal Accountant consider preparing a checklist of all documentary requirements relative to the disbursements for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management:
Grant contract time extensions due to unfavorable weather conditions after careful examination of the complete and sufficient documents such as, but not limited to, weather reports from PAGASA and Monthly Time Suspension Report as provided under DPWH DO No. 42 s.2020 and Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
Require contractors to post performance securities with complete details as to its period of validity and/or expiry; and
Require contractors to cause the extension of their performance securities corresponding the contract time extensions granted to them.
We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Engineer, exercise due prudence in the preparation of the ABC and POW of infrastructure projects.

We recommended the following:
Management set up control mechanisms so that economical, efficient and effective repairs of government property and equipment are undertaken, which include, but not limited to, the following procedures:
Identification of property or equipment subject for repair and maintenance;
Conduct of pre-repair inspection and preparation of Pre-Repair Inspection Report;
Replacement of damaged/worn-out parts and preparation of Report of Waste Materials; and
Conduct of post-inspection and preparation of Post-Inspection Report; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements relative to repairs and maintenance of government property and equipment are duly supported with Pre-Repair Inspection Reports, Post-Inspection Reports and Reports of Waste Materials.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management prepare its APP using the form prescribed by the GPPB to cover all procurements including those items in the APP-CSE; revise and update the APP as needed, and submit the same to the GPPB and the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed deadlines. Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken without an approved APP.
We further recommended that the BAC prepare its PMR to include not only those procurement activities undertaken thru competitive bidding but also those undertaken thru alternative modes of procurement, as well as the APCPI Results, and submit these reports to the GPPB within the prescribed deadlines. Furthermore, copies of these reports be furnished the Office of the Auditor for monitoring and as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Engineer:
For ongoing infrastructure projects without project signboards, immediately post the project signboards in the project sites/locations. Henceforth, comply with the posting of project signboards for all infrastructure projects; and
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards are already posted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Devise a tool/mechanism to eliminate the cause of delay in the submission of periodic reports and transaction documents and effectively speed up the processing, segregation and compilation of pertinent documents; and
Strictly observe the timely submission of periodic reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management, thru the Municipal Treasurer:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders relative to the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the same; and
Submit notice of deliveries/completion to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured goods/infrastructure projects within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

We reiterated that Management require the heads/representatives of the implementing offices to ensure that GAD-related activities are implemented as planned, and maximize the utilization of the GAD funds in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.
Further, we recommended that the GAD Focal Person adhere to the prescribed format of GAD Accomplishment Report and its timely submission in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that Management, thru its GAD Focal Point System, update its GAD database to include data and other gender-information relating to the institutional sector to better identify organization-focused gender issues and concerns. Further, we recommend that Management include sourcing of data and GAD related information from local offices of NGAs to have a more comprehensive GAD Database. In compliance thereto, Management may consider the allocation of budget in its GAD Plan and Budget for the collection and processing of data requirements of the GAD Database.

We recommended that Management include in its GAD Plan and Budget the attendance of the members of the GAD Focal Point System to capability trainings/seminars to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of various gender assessment tools. Henceforth, the GFPS institutionalize the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of gender assessment tools.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) Municipal Accountant (MA) to allow/approve only disbursement claims that are supported by appropriately filled-out documents necessary to prove the validity and propriety of the transactions; and (b) Supply Officer to submit a comprehensive report on the receipt, distribution and remaining inventory of welfare goods to the AT for verification.
observations.managementAction: Management committed compliance with submission of the required documents to the Audit Team. As of November 30, 2022, Management submitted to the Audit Team the documents necessary to prove the validity and propriety of the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) and MA to (a) submit the approved estimates of petty expenses for one month as additional documentary requirement for the initial set-up the PCF; (b) ensure that payments out of the PCF is in accordance with the nature of expenses intended for the fund; (c) assess the necessity of maintaining the amount of the established PCF and to possibly reduce to the average level of actual requirement; and (d) strictly comply with the limit of ₱1,000.00 to be paid from the PCF.
observations.managementAction: Management reduced the PCF from ₱100,000.00 to ₱50,000.00, committed to comply with the rest of the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE institute appropriate action on long overdue cash advances by (a) sending demand letters to AOs to refund/liquidate their long outstanding cash advances
observations.managementAction: (b) withholding the payment of salary/benefits of the AOs concerned that are still in service
observations.reason: Management informed the AT that demand letters were already sent to the respective AOs to remind them to liquidate/settle their cash advances. Management also committed to implement the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to adopt the PIM in the recording of inventories purchased and record in the books the unused supplies in the custody of the GSO at year-end; and (b) Supply Officer to (i) conduct physical count of inventories and furnish the AT with results of actual count; (ii) prepare the SSMI for all inventories issued supported with RIS; and (iii) submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the AT duly supported with Acknowledgment Receipt of the Municipality from the donor, proof of receipt by the beneficiaries, and inventory of remaining undistributed in-kind donations, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

Lack of
competent
personnel to
update the
records of the
Supply Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Assessor to make a representation with the Land Registration Authority for the titling of the 38 parcels of land with TDs under the name of the Municipality for proper valuation in the books; (b) MA and MT to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELCs and PCs, respectively; (c) MT as the General Services Officer/Property Officer/Supply Officer to (i) prioritize the conduct of complete physical inventory and the reconciliation of the results thereof with the MAO records; and (ii) identify the items for disposal to facilitate the derecognition from the books of the carrying values of unserviceable PPE by the MAO; (d) MA to (i) establish the cost of PPE items which are part of a set or recorded in bulk to facilitate proper recognition in the books; and (ii) effect the adjustment in the books of small value items and inventories for distribution that were inappropriately recognized under the PPE accounts; and (e) Inventory Committee to investigate the incident of unaccounted communication equipment with total carrying value of ₱744,900.00 for appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed and committed to comply with the audit recommendations. It also committed to initiate efforts to (a) identify the unserviceable items in order to initiate the proper disposal procedures and derecognition in the books
observations.reason: (c) locate the missing PPE items

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to (a) analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding liabilities in the books, otherwise, request the write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and (b) revert to Government Equity the recorded obligations without supporting documents and/or which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed.
observations.managementAction: The MA committed to complete the documentary requirements needed to file a request for write-off before the Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the (a) ME and implementing officials to utilize optimally the 20% DF in accordance with the development plan of the Municipality by implementing and monitoring the completion of all the planned PPAs for the year; (b) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office to constantly monitor the efficient and effective implementation of the priority development projects particularly by the recipient Barangays; (c) ME to monitor the works of contractors before acceptance of the completed projects and enforce the warranty security, if needed, on defective projects; and (d) BAC Members, Secretariat and TWG to submit the lacking documents to the AT and henceforth comply strictly with the required documents in the implementation of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations, viz:
(a) Prioritize the implementation of the planned PPAs to optimally utilize the 20% DF in accordance with the development plan of the Municipality.
(b) Regular monitoring of the implementation of the priority development projects by the recipient Barangays by the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office.
(c) To conduct final inspection of all completed projects before acceptance thereof and enforce the warranty security, if needed, on defective projects.
(d) To submit to the Audit Team the documentary deficiencies noted during the audit and observe strict compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) to comply with the budgetary requirement of at least 5% of the Municipality’s total budget appropriation for GAD PPAs
observations.managementAction: (b) prioritize the implementation of the identified PPAs as per GPB to achieve and promote desirable gender responsive society
observations.reason:
Management committed to continue addressing gender inequality in the locality and committed to improve the implementation of GAD PPAs. Specifically, Management’s action plan consists of:
(a) Prepare GAD plan and budget that are focused on addressing gender issues and concerns and prioritized their implementation;
(b) Conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments using identified performance indicators to ascertain that all activities/projects are accomplish as planned; and
(c) To revise the plan as warranted by current prevailing circumstances with clear objective of addressing gender issues and concerns for a more responsive and efficient government operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE continue to be compliant with the existing regulation on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from the payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the MA and the HRMO Officer to (a) continue working back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱844,544.33; and (b) ensure that the contributions and loan amortizations are remitted intact to avoid instances of under/over remittances to GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The MA commented that she will continue exerting effort in reconciling the prior years’ balance of Due to GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the MT to continue tracing the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱85,873.91 and to strictly observe the full remittance of the amount due to PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed and committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA and the MT to work back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱ 139,362.49; and (b) direct the MT to ensure that the amount due to the Pag-IBIG are remitted intact and on time to avoid instances of under/over remittances.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: b) unremitted balances of mandatory obligations
observations.managementAction: and c) abnormal/negative balances per supporting schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines on the transfer of unexpended balances of QRF to the Special Trust Fund. Further, for the Municipal Accountant to carefully review and record financial transactions in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that Management determine and resolve the causes of delay in the submission of year-end financial statements, and strictly observe the timely submission of the same on or before February 14 of each year.

We recommended that Management refrain from granting special cash advances for operating expenses to officers and employees who are not duly appointed or designated as disbursing officers and who are not properly bonded, and strictly abide with the proper granting of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Observe and implement the correct employer’s share on the monthly premium contributions to the HDMF, which should not exceed P100.00 per month per employee; and

b)	Make representations with the HDMF to consider the over-remittance of employer’s share as advance payment on the premium contributions of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRM Officer/LDRRM Council to prepare a supplemental investment program for any realignment of funds affecting the LDRRM Fund or for any changes made thereto for COVID-related PPAs and to submit the same to the local sanggunian for approval.
We recommended that Management stop the operation of the open dumpsite for its garbage disposal and properly use the MRF and RCA so that the intended purpose for such establishment will be achieved.

We recommended that the BAC comply with existing rules and regulations of RA No. 9184 particularly on the preparation of resolutions recommending to the HoPE the use of alternative methods of procurement and the subsequent resolutions recommending award of contacts to winning bidders. Also, the Municipal Accountant thoroughly check the supporting documents for every transaction, and refrain from processing of claims in cases where no proper documentary requirements were attached.

We recommended that Management adopt competitive bidding as the general method of procurement and only resort to shopping and/or other alternative methods of procurement if and when the conditions are met as provided under Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Legislative Department coordinate for the formulation and establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of burial, medical and other financial assistance for the proper and efficient utilization of the AICS Program. We further recommend that Management ensure that grants of financial assistance are supported with complete and necessary documents to ascertain its legality, validity and propriety.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the prescribed policies and guidelines on the grant of overtime services and overtime pay to government employees as provided under the CSC and DBM JC No. 1, s.2015 to avoid the incurrence of unauthorized, irregular and/or excessive overtime pay.

We recommended that Management comply with the prescribed threshold of P5,000.00 for the minimum net take home pay that each employee should receive; refrain from approving additional loans which may reduce the net take home pay of employees below said threshold; and orient all employees of the provision on the net take home pay as provided every year in the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act.
and (b) non-implementation of GAD activities as planned and underutilization of the GAD Budget.

observations.reason: No update and revision

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the concerned accountable officers to cause the immediate submission of all lacking DVs with original supporting documents to the Auditor's Office and to follow strictly the regulations of the submission of accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Assign personnel to exert effort specifically in tracing back of prior years’ transactions and records, and identify the unidentified reconciling items to reconcile the balances between the book and the bank pertaining to the LBP account no. 0522-1114-30; and

b)	Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to record the identified book reconciling items to reflect the correct Cash in Bank account balances.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2021 accounts as AO No. 1)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a)	The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer identify the discrepancies between the ledger and cashbook balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in their records to arrive at reconciled balances;

b)	The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of balances between the accounting’s ledgers and treasury’s cashbooks at least quarterly; and

c)	The Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain separate cashbooks for each depository account of the LGU and regularly update the same for proper control and monitoring of funds.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2021 accounts as AO No. 2)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management determine the necessity and authority of maintaining the dormant/non-moving cash in bank account and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other local government programs and projects especially those for the response to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2021 accounts as AO No. 3)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2021 accounts as AO No. 4)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a)	Create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of local road networks and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network not later than January 31 of each year;

b)	Require the Municipal Accountant to present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks, and to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road; and

c)	Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of a designated General Services Officer, to prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network, and to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2021 accounts as AO No. 9)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management determine and resolve the causes of delay in the submission of year-end financial statements, and strictly observe the timely submission of the same on or before February 14 of each year.
observations.reason: (This is reiterated in the audit of CY 2021 accounts as AO No. 15)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer determine and resolve the causes of delay in the submission of periodic reports and transaction documents relative to the receipts and disbursements of government funds, and strictly observe the timely submission of the same within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.
observations.reason: (Not reiterated in Part II of this AAR)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare its APP using the form prescribed by the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) to cover all procurements including those items in the APP-CSE; revise and update the APP as needed, and submit the same to the GPPB and the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed deadlines.
