observations.recommendation:
1.10	We recommended that Management direct the following:
Municipal Treasurer:
To prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant;
Ensure proper reporting on cancellation/replacement of check;
Municipal Accountant:
To post adjustment in the books of accounts that correspond to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the reporting period; and,
Make necessary adjusting entry to effect the reversion of previously restored cash due to cancellation of stale checks.

We recommended that Management  direct the following:
Municipal Agriculturist to strictly monitor the implementation of the project based on the purpose of the grant and coordinate with the Accounting Office the actual status and submission of documentary requirements for the liquidation of the grant;
Municipal Accountant to require the timely submission of the liquidations and ensure that expenses are recorded in the books on the period of its incurrence. Further, prepare adjusting journal entries, if necessary.
Moreover, exert effort to determine the persons/Officials of the Cooperatives responsible for the grant and send the demand letters. Otherwise, we recommend that Management comply with the guidelines and procedures provided in Section 7.4 of COA Circular 2016-005 in requesting authority to write-off the affected account.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a Report on Physical Count of PPE to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Inventory Committee to cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable items and the Municipal Accounting Office to record the dropping from the books of the items disposed of; and
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards and ensures reconciliation against the Accounting ledgers cards.

Recommendation:


    10. We recommended that Management:


          a) Assign personnel to exert effort specifically in tracing  back
             prior  years’  transactions  and  records,  and  identify  the
             reconciling items to reconcile the balances between  the  book
             and the bank pertaining to the LBP account  nos.  0522-1114-30
             and 0522-1145-29. Also,  make  representation  with  concerned
             depository banks for copies of  bank  statements  particularly
             those of previous years;


          b) Require the Municipal  Accountant  to  prepare  the  necessary
             journal entries to  record  the  identified  book  reconciling
             items;


          c) Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the balances  of
             cash-in-bank accounts which still  remained  recorded  in  the
             books of accounts of the LGU, but for which the  corresponding
             bank accounts are already closed; and  prepare  the  necessary
             adjustments, as warranted; and


          d) Make representation with concerned depository  banks  for  the
             correction of identified bank errors/reconciling items.

Recommendation:

     7. We recommended the following:


          a) The Municipal Accountant and the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer
             identify the discrepancies between  the  ledger  and  cashbook
             balances,  and  effect  the  necessary  adjustments  in  their
             records to arrive at reconciled balances;


          b) The Municipal Accountant and the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer
             conduct  periodic  reconciliation  of  balances  between   the
             accounting’s  ledgers  and  treasury’s  cashbooks   at   least
             quarterly; and


          c) The Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare and  maintain  separate
             cashbooks for each depository account of the LGU and regularly
             update the same for proper control and monitoring of funds.

Recommendation:

     7. We recommended that Management determine the necessity and authority
        of maintaining  the  dormant/non-moving  cash-in-bank  accounts  and
        revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the  general  fund,
        if warranted, to be made  available  for  appropriation  to  support
        other local government programs and projects.

Recommendation:

     6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more  efforts  to
        identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from
        Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same,  and  effect
        the necessary adjustments, as  warranted,  to  reflect  the  correct
        account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the  financial
        statements.

Recommendation:

     9. We recommended that  the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer  furnish  the
        Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a  duly  certified
        list  showing  the  name  of  taxpayers  and  the  amount   due   and
        collectible for the year which will serve as basis of the latter for
        the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts.


    10. We further recommended  that  the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer  and
        Municipal  Accountant  undertake  periodic  reconciliation  of   the
        balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of  the  RPT
        and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up
        with an accurate and  proper  balance  of  RPT  and  SET  Receivable
        accounts; and exert effort to  identify  the  discrepancies  of  the
        reciprocal accounts,  reconcile the same, and effect  the  necessary
        adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct  account  balances
        for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:

     8. We recommended that Management:


          a) Send demand letters  to  concerned  accountable  officers  and
             employees  with   unsettled   accounts   for   its   immediate
             settlement. In case of non-settlement  of  employees  who  are
             still  connected  with  the  LGU,  cause  the  withholding  of
             salaries or any money claims due them;


          b) Send demand letters and/or take  appropriate  actions  on  the
             unsettled accounts of accountable officers who are  no  longer
             connected or not in  active  service  with  the  LGU,  and  of
             suppliers for their tax deficiencies;


          c) Immediately settle/liquidate the cash  advance  pertaining  to
             the Intelligence Fund amounting to  P20,000.00  following  the
             guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2003-
             003 dated July 30, 2003  as  amended  by  COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND
             Joint Circular 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015; and


          d) Request for write-off of  dormant  accounts  pursuant  to  COA
             Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:

     7. We recommended that Management:


          a) Require the Municipal  Accountant  to  observe  the  Perpetual
             Inventory Method  by  initially  recording  the  purchases  of
             medical supplies, materials, and relief  goods  as  Inventory,
             and  subsequently  recognizing  the  same  as  expenses   upon
             issuance/distribution to end-users/intended beneficiaries, and
             maintain Supply Ledger Cards; and


          b) Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer/GSO to take control and
             custody of supplies and materials, comply with the  prescribed
             procedures in the requisition and issuance of inventory items,
             and maintain stock cards.

Recommendation:

    20. We recommended the following:


          a) The Inventory Committee conduct annual physical count  of  PPE
             to facilitate the preparation and  submission  of  RPCPPE  not
             later than January 31 of each  year,  complete  with  all  the
             required information, which shall serve as the basis  for  the
             reconciliation  and  updating  of  accounting   and   property
             records;


          b) The Municipal Accountant and the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer
             prepare and maintain complete PPE Ledger  Cards  and  Property
             Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of  their
             records and update the  same  based  on  the  results  of  the
             physical inventory;


          c) The Municipal Accountant transfer the completed infrastructure
             projects recorded in the books of accounts of the  Trust  Fund
             to the General Fund, effect the necessary adjustments for  the
             recognition  of  the  prior  years’   depreciation   and   the
             corresponding    accumulated    depreciation    taking    into
             consideration    the    costs,    year    and     month     of
             purchase/completion, and useful life, and recognize the annual
             depreciation of PPE items in the books of accounts; and


          d) The Municipal Accountant determine the accuracy  and  validity
             of the public infrastructure assets reported in  its  Registry
             of Public Infrastructures, and make necessary  adjustments  in
             the LGU’s books of accounts to record the same, if warranted.

Recommendation:

     5. We recommended that Management:


          a) Create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count
             of local road  networks  and  to  submit  the  Report  on  the
             Physical Count of Local Road Network not later than January 31
             of each year;


          b) Require the Municipal Accountant to present in  the  Notes  to
             Financial Statements the required disclosure  for  local  road
             networks, and to  prepare  and  maintain  Local  Road  Network
             Ledger Cards for each local road; and


          c) Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer, in the  absence  of  a
             designated General Services Officer,  to  prepare  the  annual
             Report of Local Road Network, and to prepare and maintain  the
             Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.

Recommendation:

    11.  We recommended the following:


          a) The Municipal Accountant and the  Municipal  Engineer  conduct
             reconciliation  of   documents/records   pertaining   to   the
             Construction in Progress accounts;


          b) The Municipal Engineer furnish the Municipal Accountant copies
             of the Certificate of Completion, approved  program  of  works
             and   other   pertinent   documents   as   basis    for    the
             reclassification of accounts for completed projects;


          c) The Municipal Accountant identify all costs incurred for  each
             project based on the  approved  program  of  works  and  other
             relevant  documents,  and  make   necessary   adjustments   to
             reclassify  the  completed  projects  into  their  appropriate
             accounts, as warranted; and


          d) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer  establish
             a  proper  monitoring   system   particularly   for   projects
             undertaken by  administration  in  order  for  the  former  to
             completely record all costs incurred thereto as  CIP  accounts
             during  construction,  and  to  reclassify  the  total   costs
             incurred  into  the  appropriate   PPE   account   after   its
             completion.

Recommendation:

     9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


          a) Exert extra effort to trace  prior  years’  records  including
             financial   statements,   trial   balances,   ledgers   and/or
             supporting schedules to determine the  unidentified  beginning
             balances of subject liability accounts;


          b) Determine the validity of unremitted balances  to  GSIS,  Pag-
             IBIG and PhilHealth and  cause  the  immediate  remittance  or
             adjustments of the same, as warranted;


          c)  Make  representations  with  concerned  government   agencies
             relative to the  outstanding  balances  of  subject  liability
             accounts and the refund and/or other appropriate  actions  for
             over-remittances made;


          d) Properly monitor the withholding and remittance  of  mandatory
             contributions/obligations so as to avoid under/over-remittance
             of the same; and review thoroughly the  schedule  of  payables
             due to concerned agencies during the  month  and  ensure  that
             remittances  are  based  on  actual  contributions   vis-a-vis
             payroll deduction.

Recommendation:

     6.  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  adhere  to   the
        accounting and reporting guidelines on the  transfer  of  unexpended
        balances of  QRF  to  the  Special  Trust  Fund.  Further,  for  the
        Municipal  Accountant  to  carefully  review  and  record  financial
        transactions in accordance with the Revised Chart  of  Accounts  for
        Local Government Units.

Recommendation:

     5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly present  prior
        period errors and adjustments by restating the prior  year  balances
        of the affected accounts shown  as  corresponding  figures  for  the
        current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs
        46 to 54 of IPSAS 3.

Recommendation:

     9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  and  Municipal  Budget
        Officer coordinate in the preparation  of  the  SCBAA,  taking  into
        consideration the amount balances in the Registries and SAAOB. Also,
        the Municipal Accountant adhere to the pertinent provisions of IPSAS
        24  particularly  on  the  proper  presentation  of   Reconciliation
        Statement  between  the  SCBAA  and  the  Statement   of   Financial
        Performance as disclosed in the Notes to  Financial  Statements  and
        the proper disclosure for any changes between the original and final
        budget.

We recommended the following:
a. 	The LCE require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. The progress of the inventory taking should be monitored by the LCE;
b. 	Thereafter, the Committee, the GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts; and
c. The LCE require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that all transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the BAC, and a corresponding resolution thereto on the mode of procurement to be adopted shall be prepared for approval of the LCE. On the acquisition of food items for the SFP, the Management may consider segregating the non-perishable and perishable items during the preparation of work and financial plan. The non-perishable items shall be acquired thru public bidding or any alternative mode of procurement (with emphasis on negotiated procurement through community participation) in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and terms and conditions of the MOA. On the other hand, perishable goods and other items where application of procurement laws under RA 9184 may not be feasible and for justifiable reasons, may be acquired by way of special purpose cash advances following usual budget, accounting and auditing rules and DSWD AO No. 4 Series of 2016.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant (MA) to ensure that all liquidations of funds released to special disbursing officers are supported with the necessary documents such as official receipts or its equivalent to substantiate the validity and propriety of the expenditures in accordance with Section 4.6 of PD 1445.
Lastly, we recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with the MOA for all fund transfers received from the DSWD, as well as all National Government Agencies. All procurements shall be in accordance with procurement laws, rules and regulations and budgeting, accounting and auditing rules and regulations, as applicable.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to determine the proper disposition of the fund transfer from the CO. If after careful scrutiny, continuing the project is more advantageous for the Municipality, the Management shall determine the source of additional funding which may be appropriation of funds from the general fund. Likewise, if the Management determines that the project implementation is no longer feasible, the LCE shall cause the immediate return of the fund to the source agency.
Moreover, the Municipality shall ensure that all fund transfers from NGAs are covered with a MOA.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that all government funds released to NGOs must be supported with complete documentary requirements such as the MOA, application for funding, and other documents enumerated in COA Circular No. 2007-001. Further, the LCE shall require the MA to record the grant of financial assistance to NGOs under the Due from NGOs/POs Account and require the liquidation thereof.

We recommended that the Management ensure that fund transfers to implementing agencies are covered by a MOA setting forth the terms and conditions of transfer, submission of required reports, specific period to liquidate the funds and the roles and responsibilities of the contracting parties for the proper management and effective utilization of funds.
We also recommended that the MA prepare an adjusting entry to record the fund transfer to barangays under the “Due from LGU’s” account instead of an expense account.

We recommended that Management ensure that future transfer of funds which are considered as realignment be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.

We recommended the following:
The LGU shall appropriate funds for the concreting of approach so that the footbridge located at Mabato, San Miguel can be used as intended;
The waste management operations of the MRF at Mabato, San Miguel shall be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized in the earliest possible time; and
The Municipal Planning and Development Officer, in coordination with the project implementing departments of the LGU, shall judiciously plan the PPAs under its local budget to ensure that funds are effectively and efficiently utilized.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of developmental projects as provided under JMC No. 2020-1 dated November 4, 2020. Likewise, we recommended that Management to strive for the total implementation of all projects embodied in the 20% Development Plan.

We recommended that the LCE direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit supplemental LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP for all PPAs to be charged to LDRRMF which were not yet included in the annual budget such as the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF under the special trust funds, funds received from other LGUs and sources, and changes in CY appropriations such as realignments. Thereafter, the LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP shall be submitted to the MDC to be incorporated in the AIP for approval of the SB.
Moreover, the LCE shall ensure that all changes in the CY appropriations of the LDRRMF are covered with supplemental budget.

We recommended that the LCE direct all concerned employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances for travel in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.  See to it that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to process disbursements for advance payment to contractors only upon submission of security in the form of irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission, duly accepted by the LGU, in accordance with Section 4.2. of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of supplies and materials and require coordination between the MA and the GSO towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials; and
Require the GSO to prepare the SSMIs weekly supported with RIS for supplies and materials issued and the MA to prepare JEV as supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the GSO to record issuance of supplies and materials

We recommended that the LCE ensure that the procurement of FOL shall be done by way of public bidding, or any alternative mode of procurement as provided under R.A. 9184. All transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the BAC, and a corresponding resolution thereto should be prepared and attached to the corresponding DV.

We recommended that the management faithfully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively control FOL by implementing the following corrective measures:
Exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved serially number DTTs and RIVs; and
Management submit to the AT the Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit and verification purposes.

We recommended that the BAC observe the disclosure of the ABC in the RFQ in accordance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to obtain the most advantageous price for its procurement transactions.
We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to ensure compliance with posting requirements for procurement under emergency cases pursuant to Section 54.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the GSO to submit promptly to COA copies of contracts, POs and notices of deliveries pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to  require  the  Municipal
   Accountant and Municipal  Treasurer  to  conduct  periodic  and  thorough
   reconciliation of the Cash in Bank balances per books and  per  cashbooks
   at least once at the end of each quarter, so that discrepancies  will  be
   immediately traced and necessary adjustments made.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct all accountable
   officials and employees concerned to comply with the  provisions  of  COA
   Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and immediately  settle
   their unliquidated cash advances, otherwise the  necessary  sanction  for
   its settlement such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the
   unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced.
   We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that no
   additional cash advances should be given  to  any  official  or  employee
   unless the previous cash advance given to  him  is  first  settled  or  a
   proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct  the  Municipal
   Accountant and the Chairman of the  Inventory  Committee  to  submit  the
   RPCPPE in conformity with Section 124 and Annex 49 of the Manual  on  the
   New Accounting System for  Local  Government  Units,  Volume  I  and  II,
   respectively.  Likewise, a reconciliation of the actual physical count of
   property  with  the  accounting  records  be  undertaken  to  ensure  the
   reliability of PPE accounts as presented in the financial statements.


   We further recommended and the GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant
   agreed to prepare the property cards and ledger cards  and  to  reconcile
   the postings/records of their PPE thereon.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct  the  concerned
   personnel to secure supporting documents evidencing the donation  of  the
   abovementioned motor vehicles.


   We also recommended and the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  make  the
   necessary adjusting entry to record the cost of motor vehicles  based  on
   the fair value as at the date of acquisition.

We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided for in the same circular, to afford management with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of this account and regularly monitor the changes of Agricultural Produce for Distribution to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate recording/adjustment of the book reconciling items in the Cash in Bank accounts, giving priority to long outstanding prior years reconciling items based on valid supporting documents in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements. Make another representation with the bank in order to secure copies of the Credit and Debit Memo, copy furnish the Audit Team, so as to obtain proper basis in recording the reconciling items in the LGU books.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to continue tracing the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and             Due to Other Funds accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at accurate balances of the financial statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purpose, if any.

We recommended that management: (1) return the unexpended balances from fund transfers to the source agency; (2) immediately remit the share of other LGUs on various collections and; (3) instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reclassification entries to properly identify those amounts which are due to NGAs and immediately remit the same.

We recommended that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 30% Quick Response Fund programs pursuant to Item 4.0 of the NDDRMC-DBM, DILG Joint Circular No.2013-1 so that intended purpose is attained/achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, in order to fairly present the account Due from Local Government Units in the financial statements and provide proof that the purpose for the fund transfer has already been attained. Also, refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the account Trust Liabilities in recognizing collections from sale of bid documents as well as payment of BAC honoraria pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units. Also, reclassify the balance using the following journal entry:
Debit		Credit
Due to LGUs			       ₱119,830.00
Trust Liabilities				      ₱119,830.00

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the use of correct account titles as provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units so as to properly record transactions and prepare accurate financial statements.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the journal entry debiting the account Bail Bonds Payable and crediting the account Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable in order to accurately present these two accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases using the perpetual inventory method for the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicine, Medical, Dental and Laboratory and Agricultural and Marine Supplies pursuant to Section 114 of the NGAS Manual and the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant which shall be used as basis in recording the issuances/expense account as required under Section 121 of the same manual.
b. Coordinate directly with the concerned departments for the submission of duly acknowledged Requisition and Issue Slips for the withdrawal/issuance of supplies and the Monthly Report of the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicines, and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies, Agricultural and Marine Supplies and Office Supplies issued and its supporting documents, in order to establish inventories on hand at year-end.

We recommended that management: (a) instruct the officials involved in the project implementation to stop the practice of granting time extension due to the same reason of unworkable field condition caused by heavy rains in the locality. Also, require the inclusion of the estimated number of rainy/unworkable days which was not made known to all participating bidders before the bidding date pursuant to Item 3.1 of Annex A of the IRR of R.A. 9184; and (b) the BAC Chairman consider disqualification and/or blacklisting of contractors with repetitive project completion delays pursuant to the applicable provisions of Appendix 17 of RA 9184.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to provide explanation on the afore-cited deficiencies and submit documents that would address the noted deficiencies and facilitate comprehensive evaluation of the project implementation.

We recommended that the MSWDO and MDRRMO submit to the Audit Team the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances Donations and its supporting documents for the received donations in-kind pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2020-009.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the account balances per fund and review the recorded transactions for possible errors in recording and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted. When found correct, we also recommend the immediate remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR within the reglementary period as required under Section 8(b) of BIR Revenue Regulation No.14-2002 to avoid incurrence of penalties/surcharges and fines.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit to the BIR the taxes due on the honoraria of BAC members, TWG and BAC Secretariat whose total annual taxable income exceed the P250,000.00 exemption threshold, in accordance with pertinent BIR laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the creditable and percentage taxes.
We recommended that management observe the rules and regulations in the hiring of consultancy services pursuant to the provisions of Item V – Specific Guidelines per Annex H of R.A.9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare the correct lapsing schedule as the basis in recording their
monthly depreciation expense; and
b) Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect in the books the correct
carrying amounts of PPEs.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer submit
immediately to the Municipal Accountant any check that will not be claimed
by the payees beyond six months, together with the related Disbursement
Vouchers for cancellation in the books of the LGU.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal
Entry Voucher to restore in the Cash in Bank-LCCA account and set-up the
corresponding liability account whenever stale checks are submitted by the
Municipal Treasurer with proper documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the amount of
₱611,612.77 from Due to PhilHealth account to Trust Liabilities
account in the Trust Fund; and
b) inform the Municipal Health Officer of the funds available for the
improvement of the facilities of the LGU’s birthing center.

We recommended that the Officer In-Charge of the Municipal Health Office:
a) Request from LPH, or other source agency, the actual information on
the unit cost and quantities of vaccines that the LGU will receive in the
future in relation to its vaccination program;
b) Submit to the Municipal Accountant, at the end of each month, the
complete documents from the source agency pertaining to the receipt
and utilization of the vaccines;
c) Maintain appropriate logbook to account for the receipt and the
utilization of the vaccines.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record using the
perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received by the LGU and the doses
dispensed, using as reference the report/documents submitted by the MHO.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the total unpaid
obligations and the unutilized funds from NGAs and reconcile said amounts
with the records of the Municipal Engineer as to the status of projects, and
with the Municipal Treasurer as to cash balances, to ensure that obligations
of the LGU has sufficient cash backup for its settlement. Return to the
Bureau of Treasury the unutilized funds from NGAs once reconciled.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer monitor closely the obligations and disbursements under the Trust
Fund to prevent incurrence of deficits or irregular use of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer:
d) implement all priority projects in the AIP during the budget year;
e) review and reprogram, if necessary, the unimplemented projects to
those which are vital and urgent; and
f) provide sufficient appropriation to each project.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant consolidate the remaining balances from the
completed projects and revert the amount to the General Fund for
subsequent reappropriation for other basic services upon the approval of the
Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the Bids and Awards
Committee to follow strictly all the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184, or
the Procurement Reform Law and its 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulation to avoid penal sanctions.

We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, its
Secretariat and the members of the Technical Working Group:
a.) Use the 6th Edition of PBDs and fill out completely the clauses contained
in the BDS and SCC, and incorporate the Specifications, Drawings and
Bill of Quantities (Infrastructures), Schedule of Requirements and
Technical Specifications (Goods) in the preparation of PBDs to provide
prospective bidders all the necessary information that they need to
prepare their bids;
b.) Invite three observers in all stages of the procurement activities;
c.) Prepare the Checklist of Technical and Financial Component using the
prescribed format indicated in the 6th Edition of PBDs;
d.) Affix the BAC’s initials in the original copy of Technical Documents,
including the Eligibility and Financial Documents as evidence of
authentication of submitted bids;
e.) Require the BAC-TWG to prepare Bid Evaluation Report;
f.) Prepare a notice to the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid to submit its
latest income and business tax returns, and other appropriate licenses and
permit required by law;
g.) Require the BAC-TWG to state clearly in their Post Qualification Report
that they have verified and validated all necessary eligibility
requirements mentioned in Sections 23.1 and 25.2.b of the 2016 RIRR of
R.A. 9184; and that they have confirmed the LRCB’s compliance with the
requirements and conditions stated in the Bidding Documents;
h.) Require the winning bidder to post the required Performance Securities as
provided in Section 39.2 of the RIRR otherwise forfeit the bid security and
implement appropriate sanctions as mentioned in Section 69 of the RIRR of
RA No. 9194;
i.) Require the winning bidder to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP before
approval of the contracts; and
j.) Post the NOA and NTP, together with the approved contract, in the PhilGEPS
website within three (3) and fifteen (15) calendar days from issuance,
respectively.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the
contractors of the Project Nos. 1 and 2 in Table 1, and all contractors in
future procurements of the LGU, to post Warranty Security to protect the
interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal DRRM
Officer:

c. Implement during the budget year the projects, programs and activities
identified in the LDRRM Investment Plan, and give the highest
priority, in terms of urgency and/or impact, the four thematic areas of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management to enhance preparedness and
response capabilities of the Muncipality’s constituents; and

d. Include in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the
Municipal Development Plan, all programs, projects and activities
(PPAs) to be funded from prior years unexpended DRRMF in the
Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize the utilization of the
LDRRMF only for disaster risk reduction and management activities to
avoid audit disallowances.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM Officer,
identify specific and time-bound PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
where expected outputs are readily measurable.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal
Accountant submit the following supporting documents to prove the
eligibility and entitlement of the claimants and to support the validity of the
disbursements:

a) Liquidation Report on the P271,363.63 Cash Advance together with the DOH
validated payrolls amounting to P1,164,681.69;
b) Duly accomplished and approved Daily Time Records of all recipients of
COVID-19 AHDP and SRA; and
c) Certification of the Municipal Health Officer that the claimants were
assigned at the Rural Health Unit or at the LGU’s quarantine facility.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the
books of the LGU the Report of Disbursement to liquidate a cash advance
only if supported with pertinent necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) Municipal Accountant (MA) to allow/approve only disbursement claims that are supported by appropriately filled-out documents necessary to prove the validity and propriety of the transactions; and (b) Supply Officer to submit a comprehensive report on the receipt, distribution and remaining inventory of welfare goods to the AT for verification.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) and MA to (a) submit the approved estimates of petty expenses for one month as additional documentary requirement for the initial set-up the PCF; (b) ensure that payments out of the PCF is in accordance with the nature of expenses intended for the fund; (c) assess the necessity of maintaining the amount of the established PCF and to possibly reduce to the average level of actual requirement; and (d) strictly comply with the limit of ₱1,000.00 to be paid from the PCF.

We recommended that the LCE institute appropriate action on long overdue cash advances by (i) sending demand letters to AOs to refund/liquidate their long outstanding cash advances; (ii) withholding the payment of salary/benefits of the AOs concerned that are still in service; and (iii) institute other legal remedies and sanctions that may be applied to two separated personnel of the Municipality with outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to adopt the PIM in the recording of inventories purchased and record in the books the unused supplies in the custody of the GSO at year-end; and (b) Supply Officer to (i) conduct physical count of inventories and furnish the AT with results of actual count; (ii) prepare the SSMI for all inventories issued supported with RIS; and (iii) submit the  Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the AT duly supported with Acknowledgment Receipt of the Municipality from the donor, proof of receipt by the beneficiaries, and inventory of remaining undistributed in-kind donations, if any.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Assessor to make a representation with the Land Registration Authority for the titling of the 38 parcels of land with TDs under the name of the Municipality for proper valuation in the books; (b) MA and MT to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELCs and PCs, respectively; (c) MT as the General Services Officer/Property Officer/Supply Officer  to  (i) prioritize the conduct of complete physical inventory and the reconciliation of the results thereof with the MAO records; (ii) identify the items for disposal to facilitate the derecognition from the books of the carrying values of unserviceable PPE by the MAO; (d) MA to (i) establish the cost of PPE items which are part of a set or recorded in bulk to facilitate proper recognition in the books; and (ii) effect the adjustment in the books of small value items and inventories for distribution that were inappropriately recognized under the PPE accounts; and (e) Inventory Committee to investigate the incident of unaccounted communication equipment with total carrying value of ₱744,900.00 for appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to (a) analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding liabilities in the books, otherwise, request the write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and (b) revert to Government Equity the recorded obligations without supporting documents and/or which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) ME and implementing officials to utilize optimally the 20% DF in accordance with the  development plan of the Municipality by implementing and monitoring the completion of  all the planned PPAs for the year; (b) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office to constantly monitor the efficient and effective implementation of the priority development projects particularly by the recipient Barangays; (c) ME to monitor the works of contractors before acceptance of the completed projects and enforce the warranty security, if needed, on defective projects; and (d) BAC Members, Secretariat and TWG  to submit the lacking documents to the AT and henceforth comply strictly with the required documents in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit the same to the AT for evaluation; and (b) ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DV prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE continue (a) to comply with the budgetary requirement of at least 5% of the Municipality’s total budget appropriation for GAD PPAs; (b) prioritize the implementation of the identified PPAs as per GPB to achieve and promote desirable gender responsive society; and (c) utilize fully the GAD budget in order to attain the objective for which the funds were appropriated.

We recommended that the LCE continue to be compliant with the existing regulation on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from the payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA and the HRMO Officer to (a) continue working back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱844,544.33; and (b) ensure that the contributions and loan amortizations are remitted intact to avoid instances of under/over remittances to GSIS.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the MT to continue tracing the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱85,873.91 and to strictly observe the full remittance of the amount due to PhilHealth.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA and the MT to work back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱ 139,362.49; and (b) direct the MT to ensure that the amount due to the Pag-IBIG are remitted intact and on time to avoid instances of under/over remittances.

We recommended the following:

a)	The Management create an Inventory Committee responsible for the conduct of annual physical count of the LGU’s PPEs.

b) . . .

c)	The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and update the same based on the results of the physical inventory;

d)	The Inventory Committee, after its creation, conduct annual physical count of PPEs to facilitate the preparation and submission of RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year, complete with all the required information, which shall serve as the basis for the reconciliation and updating of accounting and property records; 

e)	The Municipal Accountant transfer the completed infrastructure projects recorded in the books of accounts of the Trust Fund to the General Fund, effect the necessary adjustments for the recognition of the prior years’ depreciation and the corresponding accumulated depreciation taking into consideration the costs, year and month of purchase/completion, and useful life, and recognize the annual depreciation of PPE items in the books of accounts; and

f)	The Municipal Accountant examine and re-establish the actual costs of PPE items purchased and/or completed in CY 2019 and below, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to fairly present the actual costs of PPE items and its accumulated depreciation in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the relevant documents/reports to prove the complete distribution of relief/welfare goods including a summary report showing the total quantity of goods available before the distribution, the total quantity of goods distributed and balances on hand, if any. Further, the Municipal Accountant to carefully review financial transactions and record such expenses only when supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the Notes to Financial Statements to provide users of the same relevant and adequate information and a clearer understanding of the financial statements.

We recommended that the LGU create and maintain a special account in the general fund for the receipt and utilization of the BGCM with complete and separate ledgers, trial balance and financial statements.

We recommended that the BAC comply with the guidelines and procedures of Negotiated Procurement through Emergency Cases set forth in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, as amended. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of supporting documents of every claim before payment is made to ensure legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Submit immediately the lacking documents and the unsubmitted copies of contracts of infrastructure projects as indicated above, together with all the supporting documents, to facilitate the audit team’s contract review and the technical review to be conducted by the COA Regional Technical Services Office; and

b)	Furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Furnish the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of every calendar year a list of all its on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;

b)	Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and

c)	Prepare and maintain a complete monthly Report of Publicized Government Projects/Programs/Activities containing not only infrastructure projects but also non-infrastructure PPAs.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management through the Local School Board prepare and approve the SEF Budget in accordance with the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 to attain the goals and objectives and observe transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that management require the accountable officers to liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible misuse and losses of funds. On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every time collections are turned over to him, all accountable forms whether issued or not must be presented and/or accounted for, and issuance of another accountable forms must be made only after liquidation/remittance of all collections. To ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.
We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prioritize the attendance to seminar on GMEF to capacitate itself in the conduct of gender audit for CY 2020.
We recommend that the LGU will investigate the matter, make the necessary adjustments in the book, and liquidate the funds received to the DOST.

observations.recommendation:
6. We recommended that the LCE direct the MA the MT and the Implementing Departments to confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds.  The MA and the MT shall likewise determine if the unexpended balances accrue to the National Government or the LGU in case of counterpart funds. If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, the LGU shall take the following actions:

7. For funds accruing to NGA’s, return immediately the unexpended balance to SAs.  If the SAs cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury; or

8. For counterpart funds, the Local Finance Committee (LFC) shall conduct the necessary evaluation and thereafter give recommendations on the proper disposition of the funds in relation to the priority projects of the Municipality.

9. Lastly, initiate the immediate closure of the bank accounts once the balance is returned to the Bureau of Treasury (for Due to NGAs) or transferred to the general fund (for counterpart funds).

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:

10. Strictly enforce the full settlement of all unliquidated CAs as of December 31, 2020;

11. Stop granting additional CAs to officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and

12. Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring AOs/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs, if warranted.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% DF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.

26. We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject JMC pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.   Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials but only to those permanent and designated disbursing officers in order to ensure accountability of funds pursuant to Items 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No.97-0002.

b) Cash advance granted for Social Pension of Senior Citizens was recorded under the account Advances to Officers and Employees which is used to record cash advances granted only for official travel, instead of the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer thus, may affect monitoring of cash advances for liquidation of special purpose/time-bound undertakings.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements.

c) Discrepancies noted on the paid payrolls against the summary list for liquidation relative to unpaid senior citizen beneficiaries affected the propriety of the distribution of social pension.

We recommended that the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer henceforth, be more mindful in distributing the social pension to the senior citizens and carefully review the paid payroll so as to immediately determine discrepancy, if any, and take appropriate measures in order to correct any deficiency.

d)Documents required for the authorized representatives to claim the Social Pension, as well as other requirements to support validity of non-payment thereof to the senior citizens or his/her authorized representative/s, were not attached to the liquidation report contrary to Items D.4, D.5, and Item E of DSWD Memorandum Circular (MC) No.04, series of 2019 thus, affecting the propriety of distribution of social pension to the affected senior citizens.

We recommended that the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer ensure submission of required documents to prove validity of payment to authorized representatives, as well as validity of non-payment of Social Pension to senior citizens or his/her representatives, pursuant to Items D.4, D.5, and Item E  of DSWD Memorandum Circular (MC) No.04, series of 2019. We also recommended that MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer provide clarification on the noted deficiencies in the distribution process of Social Pension. Lastly, We recommended that the Internal Audit Service institute measures to ensure observance of the procedures as provided for in DSWD MC No.04, s.2019.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2020 AAR | The practice was stopped when the Municipal Treasurer reported back to office. The reason for this was that the Municipal Treasurer was on leave of absence since February 2020 and was supposed to be back to work on April 2020. 







The Municipal Accountant agreed with the audit recommendation and used the account for similar transactions.



Management has been more careful in the preparation of the summary list of liquidation.






On March 2021, the MSWDO submitted to the Audit Team, copies of death certificate of deceased social pensioners whose pensions were claimed by their representatives. Copies for certification for deceased pensioner and photocopies of claimants of pension were also submitted to prove the validity of the claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than those stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, in order to ensure proper segregation of duties between custody of fund and authorization on the granting of cash advances and establish full accountability for public funds.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | The practice was stopped when the Municipal Treasurer reported back to work on November 2020 after the leave of absence since February 2020.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was already complied with by the management. There was no noted cash advance for special purposes that was granted to elected officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management henceforth, stop the practice of premature reversion of unspent LDRRMF to the General Fund and adhere to the provisions of Items 4.4 and 5.1.13 COA Circular No.2012-002 in order to guarantee availability of funds and ensure implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management agreed with the audit recommendation and already stopped the practice of premature reversion of unspent LDRRMF to the General Fund. The transaction of this nature only happened once. It was used as  a source for the slope protection of Hagbuyo-Cambangay Norte Bridge. The project on Hagbuyo-Cambangay Norte Bridge which was funded by the national government, did not include the slope protection in the Program of Work. LGU needed an amount to finance the said project to prevent disaster such as flash floods.
observations.reason: The Audit Team did not note transaction of this nature in CY 2021.

observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | All items procured were already received and checked as to completeness. An Activity Design is prepared and attached as supporting document to the procurement of the inventory. List of actual beneficiaries was submitted as evidence of attainment of the project.
observations.reason: Pertinent documents were already submitted by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Hospital Administrator and Municipal Health Officer, henceforth, confirm the existence of a municipal ordinance and verify the rates indicated therein so as to ensure valid, legal and accurate imposition and collection of any income derived from hospital services rendered.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | A journal entry was prepared at adjusted rates of the overcharged rapid tests.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was complied with by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure accuracy of generated RCDs by reminding the Liquidating Officer to properly and immediately review the RCDs submitted by collecting officers to timely correct any deficiencies noted in the reporting of collections. Likewise, We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the collecting officers to stop altering ORs so as not to affect the integrity of source documents as well as affect the propriety of reporting of collections.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Attention of the collectors was already called upon and collectors were already reminded that the OR is an accountable form and everything that is written can no longer be changed. It was explained that the alteration was done due to an honest mistake. There shall be an ongoing monitoring by the department to address the deficiency and stop alteration of ORs.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was complied with by the management. Management was already mindful of the accuracy of generated RCDs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Internal Audit Service, in coordination with the Hospital Administrator and Municipal Treasurer, formulate internal control measures on hospital collection system specifically at nighttime, to ensure safeguarding of collections against inaccurate and incomplete reporting. Likewise, We recommended that management consider the installation of proper LGU personnel to handle collections at nighttime when warranted, after conducting a cost-benefit analysis. Otherwise, a stronger internal control on collection needs to be in place. Also, We recommended that hospital tariff rates be posted at the hospital cashier’s office for transparency purposes, especially due to varying consultation fee.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Tariff rates are already posted at the cashier’s office as compliance. A collecting officer was already appointed specially during nighttime to collect payments and issue ORs to the patients to ensure proper documentation and recording.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was complied with by the management. Tariff rates were already posted in the hospital premises.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the afore-cited laws and rules on bonding requirement for all accountable officers and make sure that only those designated collecting officers handle the collecting functions in the MTO in order to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | The MTO has successfully added additional Revenue Collection Officer and bond application is already applied currently awaiting approval. It is expected that the bonding requirement will be complied with in the next few months.
observations.reason: It was confirmed by the management that the MTO now has six (6) regular employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide explanation on the collection of consultation fee in the San Miguel Hospital in the absence of an ordinance. We also recommend that management stop collecting consultation fee in the San Miguel Hospital due to absence of a legal basis.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | An ordinance was enacted on January 2021 and collection has continued in accordance with the ordinance.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was complied with by the management thru the passage of an ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct all department heads to observe Section 141 of NGAS Manual in taking up and recording of payable accounts, so that only valid payable accounts are recognized. It is also recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from booking up payables which still lack incomplete documentation so as to fairly present the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Management made sure that payables are booked up with proper supporting documents.
observations.reason: Management has already practiced requiring the attachment of pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide explanation on the: (a) issuance of 13 Suspension Orders equivalent to 155 calendar days due to the same reason of unworkable field condition caused by heavy rains in the locality and (b) non-inclusion of the estimated number of rainy/unworkable days which was not made known to all participating bidders before the bidding date. 

	Likewise, we recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer and MPDC observe Item 3.J of Annex A and Item 1.3 of Annex E of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184, respectively, by preparing a more comprehensive study of the program/project so as to come up with a Program of Work already which reflects the most accurate contract duration date possible, covering all the necessary items of work, to expedite project implementation in accordance with the terms of the contract and timely render the services and benefits that can be derived from the programs to the public.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | These 3 infrastructure projects are now 100% completed. The issuance of suspension orders and time extensions were appropriate considering circumstances. Preparation of succeeding Program of Works are now are now being carefully studied.
observations.reason: These projects were verified to have been fully completed per submitted reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the MAO to gather data on the whereabouts of the animals comprising the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and verify existence thereof thru conduct of physical inventory. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant be also furnished with the same report in order to reconcile with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA Circular No.2012-001. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management create and Inspection and Appraisal Committee as well as Disposal Committee which shall take charge over the inspection, appraisal and disposal procedures in accounting for unserviceable and lost properties. 

	Likewise, We recommended that the created Inspection and Appraisal and Disposal Committees follow the procedures and secure documents set forth in COA Circular Nos.2019-003 and 2020-006 and COA Memorandum No.92-751 so as to facilitate: (a) determination of the condition and location of unserviceable and/or lost properties, (b) identification of the mode of disposal deemed appropriate for the properties, (c) proceed with the chosen mode of disposal in order for the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the amount of unserviceable properties in the LGU books and recognize any amount of income which can be possibly derived from sale thereof and (d) proceed with the preparation and submission of documents relative to request for relief from property accountability, when warranted.

Likewise, We recommended proper monitoring of relief goods so as to ensure timely distribution thereof to constituents and prevent spoilage thereof and wastage of funds, in order to attain the purposes of DBM LBC Nos. 124 and 125 and DILG-DBM JMC No.2020-001.

No.7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct all the department heads who have had programs and projects implemented in CYs 2016 to 2019, with fund source other than LGU funds, to facilitate representation to their complementary national government agencies, and ask confirmation on the presence of fund transfers which have remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2019, being the person directly spearheading the program/project implementation. Only in the absence of confirmation, after exertion of all possible efforts to establish the transfer of funds from a specific government agency or entity, can the management opt to record and declare these unaccounted funds as income, which may then be used to finance beneficial programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor continue to prioritize the processing of titles so as to acquire adequate and legal proof of ownership over these parcels of land and in order not to disrupt normal LGU operations and prevent loss of structures already built thereon. observations.managementAction: CY 2019 & 2018 AARs | Necessary steps has been continually done to secure certificate of title over these lots and fully comply with the audit recommendation. As of October 2021, eight (8) titles are already transferred in the name of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management initiate the conduct of an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Huge amount of this account pertains to the disallowance of previous municipal treasurer which is under litigation. The LGU will just have to wait for the judgment in order to act on this matter. For those accounts of employees, salary deductions are imposed and demands were made. Thus, there is a decrease of this account.
observations.reason: There were still accounts which remained uncollected as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for: (1) debtors who are still alive and within the locality and (2) debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. Consequently, We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, We recommended that management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write-off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | The MSWDO has exerted efforts to send demand letters. There is also a collection from other debtors in CY 2019. The MSWDO is yet to submit list of debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.
observations.reason: Accounts already due from the previous years remained uncollected as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC coordinate with the Local Finance Council to evaluate and assess the actual necessity of these unimplemented PPAs. Otherwise, management may consider                 re-programming the funds so as to include only those PPAs really found viable and responsive to the actual and urgent need of the constituents, in order to ensure full attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes pursuant to the intents of Item 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1. Likewise, We recommended that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the BAC to fast track implementation of on-going PPAs. We also recommended that henceforth, management include in the formulation of the Annual Budget, only those PPAs which are actually deemed significant and doable, and avoid inclusion of PPAs with generic description.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019           &                2017 AARs | The management is now more prudent with regard to appropriation for Barangay Development. This is in addition to requiring them to liquidate previous financial assistance before granting another.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate new pricing/costing schemes to come up with more relevant rates and fees for Masanao Hilltop Resort and Restaurant to ensure that goods and services are offered to the public at competitive prices without sacrificing the target operating income that can be derived therefrom.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of properties and continue to identify in the other departments/offices those PPEs and inventories which are already unserviceable or non-functional and facilitate disposal thereof in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting and property records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the assets of the municipality in the financial statements.
