observations.reason: The inventory reports prepared by the GSO still cannot be relied upon as some PPE/inventories were no longer in the custody of the department head indicated in his report. Moreover, the GSO still needs to conduct a complete count of the PPE/inventory in all the department offices, and not just merely rely on the old reports with updates for current year purchases, also in consideration of the aftermath of Typhoon “Odette.”

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record purchases of supplies and materials to the corresponding inventory account and compute inventory balances as prescribed under Sections 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock. Likewise, We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office  (a) maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; (b)furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances. Furthermore, We recommended that the Accounting Unit record issuances based on the SSMI submitted. Consequently, We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: CY              2012 AAR | GSO is taking the control of the receipts and release of supplies to different departments. Starting the year 2020, inventory is being recorded upon purchase and the corresponding departments submitted inventory balances as of period end to correct inventory accounts and take up expense. Inventory for agricultural produce was adjusted on January 2021.
observations.reason: Maintenance of Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock is still not followed. Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials, were also still not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant   draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and not by the Municipal Assessor and make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance. Lastly, We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR | The Municipal Treasurer’s Office in coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, are now starting to extract copies of Assessed Value per Property Owners to set the lists of Accounts Receivables beginning for CY 2021 plus tax delinquent of previous year.
observations.reason: Balances of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable accounts are still rendered inaccurate since the set-up amounts were only  estimates and are not representing the actual amount receivable from all of the taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2015 AAR | The ECG machine became operational in CY 2020 while the ultrasound machine was not used, especially on the onset of COVID-19 pandemic.

Management is still awaiting instructions as to hiring of personnel relevant to this function.
observations.reason: The ultrasound machine was confirmed to still has not been used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the (a)  LDRRMO through the LDRRM Council and Local Development Council (LDC), to revise and update the LDRRMFIP in the event that there are realignments made for the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) for proper monitoring of  fund availability and as basis for reporting  the accomplishments of  the Municipality; (b) Municipal Accountant to allow/ approve only disbursement claims which are supported by appropriately  filled-up documents necessary to prove the validity, propriety, and legality of the transactions; (c) require the Supply Officer to submit a comprehensive report on the receipt, distribution and remaining  inventory of  welfare goods purchased for distribution  and the receipt of donations in-kind coming from different sources  in response to Covid-19 for verification by the audit team; and (d) require the BAC Secretariat to comply with the required posting and reporting requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  the (a) Municipal Treasurer together with the authorized LGU representative, the DSWD Officer and other concerned officials  to (i) submit the liquidation documents for the payrolls with unsigned  proof of receipts; (ii) impose measures in profiling the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities such as utilizing  the existing database of the LGU;    (iii) introduce paperless technology in every Barangay to obtain more reliable information in implementing the prescribed  regulations; and  (iv) strictly comply with the existing regulations with the objective of providing fair and equal opportunity to the most eligible beneficiaries in the implementation of programs, projects, and activities; and (b) Municipal Accountant to   submit complete  and valid  documentary requirements to support all  fund disbursements.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU was able to comply with the distribution of ₱110,000,000.00 to 17,000 beneficiaries. The documents were completed and submitted to proper agencies.

The barangays had been continuously monitoring and listing residents who are eligible for assistance during calamities. This will be consolidated at the municipal level upon completion.

Computerization of each barangay is almost fully complied as a way of adopting paperless technology.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) install an effective control system over the handling of collections and  deposits  by the Municipal Treasurer in order to safeguard assets and promote efficient operations in consonance with  the fiscal responsibility of the  Agency Head  pursuant to  Section 123 of P.D. No. 1445; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) direct the LO to closely verify the RCDs submitted particularly the details of accountable forms issued and balances to ensure that all issued accountable forms are correctly reported in the RCD; and (ii) affix her signature on the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued; and (iii) designate an accountable forms custodian that will focus on the (1) control and monitoring of all accountable forms released to collecting officers/accountable officers; and (2) preparation of a complete RAAF to include all types of accountable forms in  sequential order in order to permit  close and easy monitoring on the completeness of accountable forms issued.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) institute appropriate action  to the five resigned / retired  accountable officers with long overdue cash advances such as: (i) sending demand letters  to require  the refund/liquidation of the long outstanding cash advances; (ii) withholding the payment of salary/benefits of the concerned accountable officers; and (iii) institute  other legal remedies and sanctions that may apply; and (b) require the accountable officers with current cash advances to strictly comply with the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Accountant to recognize the purchases   under  the Asset account, Inventories,  instead of the expense  account;(b) Supply Officer to submit the  Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances duly supported with (1) acknowledgment receipts of the donations in-kind; (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form; (3) inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and (c) Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the procedures embodied in Chapter 6,  of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I so that the requisition, receipt, and issuance of inventories could be accurately recognized in the books of accounts and in the property records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) Municipal Accountant to exert further efforts to establish the ownership of  the 38 parcels of land with total carrying value of ₱18,481.53 for proper recognition in the books pursuant to Paragraphs 26 and 27 of IPSAS No.17; (b) Municipal  Treasurer as the General Services Officer/ Property Officer/ Supply Officer   to facilitate the immediate transfer of the land title under the name of the Municipality upon fulfillment of the contract and to submit the deficient documents noted on lot registrations and other expenses capitalized in the Land account; (c) General Services Officer/ Property Officer/   Supply Officer (SO) and  the Municipal  Accountant to identify the items for disposal and perform the required procedures to derecognize the carrying amounts of the unserviceable properties in the Accounting and Property Records; (d) Municipal Accountant  to establish the cost of items which are part of a set or recorded in bulk to facilitate proper recognition in the books; (e) Municipal Treasurer to  (i) locate the PPE items which were not accounted for; and (ii) if these cannot be located anymore, require the accountable officers and end-users  to request for relief of property accountability for the lost items; and (f) require the Municipal Treasurer as the General Services Officer/ Property Officer/ Supply Officer  to  prioritize the conduct of the complete physical inventory and the reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; and (g) require the Municipal Accountant  and Municipal Treasurer  to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The land title of the newly procured land was already registered under the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal  Accountant to (a) analyze  and  ascertain  the  validity  of  long  outstanding  liabilities  in  the  books, otherwise, request the write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;  and (b)  revert to Government Equity the recorded  obligations  without  valid  documentation  and/or  which  have  been  outstanding  for  two  years  or  more  and  against  which  no  actual  claim  has  been  filed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Engineer and Implementing Officials to utilize optimally the 20% DF in accordance with the  development plan of the Municipality by implementing and monitoring the completion of  all the planned PPAs for the year; (b) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to constantly monitor the efficient and effective implementation of the priority development projects; (c) BAC Members, Secretariat and TWG  to  (i) to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the conduct of bidding, including the proper mechanism on the use of the PhilGEPS;  and (ii) submit advance copies of contracts and their supporting documents  to the Auditor’s Office; and (d) Municipal Accountant to (i) work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit the same to the Audit Team; and  (ii) ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers prior to payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Accountant to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit the same to the Audit Team for evaluation; (b) ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/ attached to the disbursement vouchers prior to payment; and (c) utilize the LDRRMF in accordance with laws, rules, and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant worked on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and had already submitted some of the required documents needed to support the disbursement vouchers.

Instructed the Municipal Accountant to check and double check all supporting documents necessary before effecting payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended that (a) the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management (HRM) Officer to continue working back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱849,312.20; and (b) ensure that the contributions and loan amortizations are remitted intact to avoid instances of under/over remittances to GSIS.

a)  The Municipal Accountant had traced some miscoded remittances and is now preparing the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to work back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱137,936.90; and (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the amount due to the Pag-IBIG are remitted intact and on time to avoid instances of under/over remittances.

We  recommended that the Local Chief Executive   (a) instruct the Municipal  Accountant to  analyze  and  ascertain  the  validity  of  long  outstanding  liabilities  in  the  books, otherwise, request the write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;  and (b)  revert to Government Equity the recorded  obligations  without  valid  documentation  and/or  which  have  been  outstanding  for  two  years  or  more  and  against  which  no  actual  claim  has  been  filed.

observations.recommendation: 1We recommended that management assign personnel to specifically to reconcile the balances in the books and that the balances in the bank with Account Account:0522-1114-30.

We recommended that the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer of the municipality maintain and update stock cards and the Municipal Accountant maintain ledger cards to facilitate the conduct of the inventory taking and render the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PRCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the adjustments to reflect the correct account balances to present reliable data on the agency’s financial position as of a given period of time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and look into the matter in order to determine the causes of the delay and to direct the prompt submission of the required reports in compliance with their duties and responsibilities and as prescribed by existing rules and regulations.  Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of their salaries, until they shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item No. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that management require the accountable officers to liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible misuse and losses of funds. On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every time collections are turned over to him, all accountable forms whether issued or not must be presented and/or accounted for, and issuance of another accountable forms must be made only after liquidation/remittance of all collections. To ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the BAC prepare the PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required under Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies in compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency on government procurement. Further, we suggested that COA be furnished with a printed copy of the said monitoring report as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council establish sex disaggregated data for its own personnel and from there conduct gender analysis to identify gender issues and appropriate organization-focused PPAs to address the gender issues and incorporate them in the GPB for costing.
We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prioritize the attendance to seminar on GMEF to capacitate itself in the conduct of gender audit for CY 2020.
We recommend that the LGU will investigate the matter, make the necessary adjustments in the book, and liquidate the funds received to the DOST

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend management to issue the notice to liquidate to the concerned AOs of all the unliquidated cash advances and enforce the suspension of salary of the concerned active AO until the compliance thereof. In the event that the AO is already separated from the LGU, the notice be send to the last known address of the concerned AO. Lastly, for AOs who were already deceased, prepare the list of the deceased AOs with the corresponding Death Certificate for a request to COA for write-off of the accounts.

We recommended that management establish an accounting and operating system in running Masanao operations to have a system of internal control and proper administrative practices installed, to ensure compliance with laws and regulations and to provide for efficient, economical and effective operations.

observations.recommendation:

through the LDRRM Council and Local Development Council (LDC) to revise and
update the LDRRMFIP in the event that there are realignments made for the

Accountant to allow/approve only disbursement claims which are supported by

report on the receipt, distribution and remaining inventory of welfare goods
purchased for distribution and the receipt of donations in-kind coming from different
sources in response to Covid-19 for verification by the audit team; and (d) require
the BAC Secretariat to comply with the required posting and reporting requirements.

2.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal
Treasurer together with the authorized LGU representative, the DSWD Officer and
other concerned officials to (i) submit the liquidation documents for the payrolls with
unsigned proof of receipts; (ii) impose measures in profiling the beneficiaries
specifically in times of calamities such as utilizing the existing database of the LGU;
(iii) introduce paperless technology in every Barangay to obtain more reliable
information in implementing the prescribed regulations; and (iv) strictly comply

documentary requirements to support all fund disbursements.
1We recommended that management assign personnel to specifically to reconcile the balances in the books and that the balances in the bank with Account Account:0522-1114-30.

We recommended that the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer of the municipality maintain and update stock cards and the Municipal Accountant maintain ledger cards to facilitate the conduct of the inventory taking and render the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PRCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect the adjustments to reflect the correct account balances to present reliable data on the agency’s financial position as of a given period of time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and look into the matter in order to determine the causes of the delay and to direct the prompt submission of the required reports in compliance with their duties and responsibilities and as prescribed by existing rules and regulations.  Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of their salaries, until they shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is processed and approved as required under existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance to ensure efficient and effective control of the cash advance transactions.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item No. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the issuance of Principal and/or Supplementary Receipts/Invoices in the ordinary course of business and the consequent examination of evidence of receipt of payments thereof as referred under existing BIR rules and regulations.
We recommended that the above Accountable Officers be relieved from their respective post until they will render accounting on the above accountable forms.

We recommended that management require the accountable officers to liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible misuse and losses of funds. On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every time collections are turned over to him, all accountable forms whether issued or not must be presented and/or accounted for, and issuance of another accountable forms must be made only after liquidation/remittance of all collections. To ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the BAC prepare the PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required under Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies in compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency on government procurement. Further, we suggested that COA be furnished with a printed copy of the said monitoring report as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that the LGU thru its GAD Council convene, draw out the calendar of activities for the information drive to the different sectors of the Municipality and implement its plan to include the LGU’s employees including the Casuals, Job Orders and the Contract of Service.

We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council establish sex disaggregated data for its own personnel and from there conduct gender analysis to identify gender issues and appropriate organization-focused PPAs to address the gender issues and incorporate them in the GPB for costing.
We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prioritize the attendance to seminar on GMEF to capacitate itself in the conduct of gender audit for CY 2020.

We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prepare the GPB strictly in accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01. Ensure that organization-focused PPAs do not undergo HGDG. In case of attribution of PPAs budget to GAD Budget, use HGDG sectoral checklist for specific sector PPA, if not applicable, use the HGDG generic checklist. The attribution must be duly supported with means of verification per element of the HGDG checklist.

We recommend that the Accountant be directed to make the necessary adjustments in the books, the source of fund cannot and will not be a factor in the classification of accounts, and proper coordination of the Accounting Office and Treasury to avoid recurrence of similar error in the future.
We recommend that the LGU will investigate the matter, make the necessary adjustments in the book, and liquidate the funds received to the DOST

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend management to issue the notice to liquidate to the concerned AOs of all the unliquidated cash advances and enforce the suspension of salary of the concerned active AO until the compliance thereof. In the event that the AO is already separated from the LGU, the notice be send to the last known address of the concerned AO. Lastly, for AOs who were already deceased, prepare the list of the deceased AOs with the corresponding Death Certificate for a request to COA for write-off of the accounts.

We recommend that: 

The Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures;

 Concerned officials with government vehicles and the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit regularly thru the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets; and

 The Municipal Accountant be required to look into the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the DVs. Claims with incomplete supporting documents must be returned to the claimant before payment be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all the department heads who have had programs and projects implemented in the current and previous years to facilitate representation to their complementary national government agencies, and ask confirmation on the presence of fund transfers which have remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2020, being the person directly spearheading the program/project implementation.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant make representation with the bank on the nature of Debit and Credit Memo and ask copies thereof, so as to record these amounts in the books and fairly present the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended that management create and Inspection and Appraisal Committee as well as Disposal Committee which shall take charge over the inspection, appraisal and disposal procedures in accounting for unserviceable and lost properties.

Likewise, we recommended that the created Inspection and Appraisal and Disposal Committees follow the procedures and secure documents set forth in COA Circular Nos.2019-003 and 2020-006 and COA Memorandum No.92-751 so as to facilitate: (a) determination of the condition and location of unserviceable and/or lost properties, (b) identification of the mode of disposal deemed appropriate for the properties, (c) proceed with the chosen mode of disposal in order for the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the amount of unserviceable properties in the LGU books and recognize any amount of income which can be possibly derived from sale thereof and (d) proceed with the preparation and submission of documents relative to request for relief from property accountability, when warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor allocate time to accurately compute the RPT and SET receivables for the year and submit the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1, so as fairly present these receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials but only to those permanent and designated disbursing officers in order to ensure accountability of funds pursuant to Items 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No.97-0002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer henceforth, be more mindful in distributing the social pension to the senior citizens and carefully review the paid payroll so as to immediately determine discrepancy, if any, and take appropriate measures in order to correct any deficiency.

We recommended that the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer ensure submission of required documents to prove validity of payment to authorized representatives, as well as validity of non-payment of Social Pension to senior citizens or his/her representatives, pursuant to Items D.4, D.5, and Item E  of DSWD Memorandum Circular (MC) No.04, series of 2019. We also recommended that MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer provide clarification on the noted deficiencies in the distribution process of Social Pension. Lastly, we recommended that the Internal Audit Service institute measures to ensure observance of the procedures as provided for in DSWD MC No.04, s.2019.

We recommended that Management discontinue granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than those stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, in order to ensure proper segregation of duties between custody of fund and authorization on the granting of cash advances and establish full accountability for public funds.

We recommended that management henceforth, stop the practice of premature reversion of unspent LDRRMF to the General Fund and adhere to the provisions of Items 4.4 and 5.1.13 COA Circular No.2012-002 in order to guarantee availability of funds and ensure implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Hospital Administrator and Municipal Health Officer, henceforth, confirm the existence of a municipal ordinance and verify the rates indicated therein so as to ensure valid, legal and accurate imposition and collection of any income derived from hospital services rendered.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure accuracy of generated RCDs by reminding the Liquidating Officer to properly and immediately review the RCDs submitted by collecting officers to timely correct any deficiencies noted in the reporting of collections. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the collecting officers to stop altering ORs so as not to affect the integrity of source documents as well as affect the propriety of reporting of collections.

We recommended that the Internal Audit Service, in coordination with the Hospital Administrator and Municipal Treasurer, formulate internal control measures on hospital collection system specifically at nighttime, to ensure safeguarding of collections against inaccurate and incomplete reporting. Likewise, we recommended that management consider the installation of proper LGU personnel to handle collections at nighttime when warranted, after conducting a cost-benefit analysis. Otherwise, a stronger internal control on collection needs to be in place. Also, we recommended that hospital tariff rates be posted at the hospital cashier’s office for transparency purposes, especially due to varying consultation fee.

We recommended that Management adhere to the afore-cited laws and rules on bonding requirement for all accountable officers and make sure that only those designated collecting officers handle the collecting functions in the MTO in order to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the government.

We recommend that management provide explanation on the collection of consultation fee in the San Miguel Hospital in the absence of an ordinance. We also recommend that management stop collecting consultation fee in the San Miguel Hospital due to absence of a legal basis.

We recommended that management immediately create a database of all constituents in the municipality, and conduct periodic updating thereof so as to have readily available data to be used in cases of emergency, disaster or pandemic and submit to the Audit Team, the complete distribution lists. Also, we recommended that henceforth, management, especially the LGU personnel designated to perform the specific tasks on the inventory and distribution of relief packs, keep a uniform and proforma distribution list which shall be updated upon determination of the exact nature and quantity of items to be distributed, and maintain a complete and accurate record of food packs on hand, for transparency purposes. Likewise, we recommended proper monitoring of relief goods so as to ensure timely distribution thereof to constituents and prevent spoilage thereof and wastage of funds, in order to attain the purposes of DBM LBC Nos. 124 and 125 and DILG-DBM JMC No.2020-001.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MT to observe the prescribed rules and regulations for all collections and deposits.  The COs shall remit all collections in tact to the MT and prepare the corresponding RCD on daily basis.  Consequently, the MT shall deposit all collections turned over to him in tact on daily basis or on the next banking day.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA the MT and the Implementing Departments to confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds.  The MA and the MT shall likewise determine if the unexpended balances accrue to the National Government or the LGU in case of counterpart funds. If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, the LGU shall take the following actions:
For funds accruing to NGA’s, return immediately the unexpended balance to SAs.  If the SAs cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury; or
For counterpart funds, the Local Finance Committee (LFC) shall conduct the necessary evaluation and thereafter give recommendations on the proper disposition of the funds in relation to the priority projects of the Municipality.
Lastly, initiate the immediate closure of the bank accounts once the balance is returned to the Bureau of Treasury (for Due to NGAs) or transferred to the general fund (for counterpart funds).

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:
Strictly enforce the full settlement of all unliquidated CAs as of December 31, 2020;
Stop granting additional CAs to officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and
Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring AOs/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA, the MT and the Implementing Departments to confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds. If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, return immediately the unexpended balance to SAs. If the SA cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury.
For the prior year unreconciled balances, the MA shall continue to conduct reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments in the FS, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE task the Local DRRM Officer to ensure that the required information are filled out/supplied in the RDS particularly the kind and volume of relief goods received in order to provide necessary data in determining if the relief goods were received by intended beneficiaries, and establish accountability of concerned municipal officials/employees in cases of lapses in relief distribution.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to strictly ensure that documents required by procurement laws are complete before processing claims for payment to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the recording of the remaining balance of cash donations to Trust Liabilities – DRRM Fund account.
Likewise, succeeding cash donations received intended for the DRRM activities should be properly accounted in the Trust Fund Books.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Council to monitor consistently and evaluate regularly the implementation of PPAs in compliance with Rule 18, Section 3 and Rule 4, Section 4(1) of the IRR of RA No. 10121 to ensure that funds are maximized in support to the DRRM activities of the municipality.
We recommended that LDRRM Officer prepare and submit immediately to the AT the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the MSWD to cause the immediate refund of the amount paid to the ineligible beneficiaries by informing them in writing.  Upon receipt of the refunded amounts, remit the amounts to the Bureau of Treasury; and
Ensure that proper verification and validation of the targeted beneficiaries are done in the future to ensure that ESP and other similar benefits will be granted to duly qualified recipients with strict adherence to the prescribed laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the Bids and Award Committee or the MT (in the absence of GSO) to submit promptly to COA copies of contracts, purchase orders and notices of deliveries pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MT to adhere to provisions of the Local Revenue Code on the submission of the required documentation, particularly the ITR, to determine the correctness and completeness of business taxes collected.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% DF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.
We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject JMC pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.   Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
1.7.1. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and  cost segregation of road components for road projects.
1.7.2. For existing road networks, direct the Municipal Assessor to assist the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.
1.7.3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
2.6.1.  Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
2.6.2 Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to conduct reconciliation between the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.
2.6.3.   Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the book value of Road Networks completed prior to the year 2018 in the books of accounts.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
3.7.1. Require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement/overstatement by drawing the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
3.7.2. Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to be diligent in keeping the Municipal Government's records to ensure completeness thereof.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
4.23.1.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian and  Inventory Committee to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs;
4.23.2.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer's Office to cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable items and the Municipal Accounting Office to record the dropping from the books of the items disposed; and
4.23.3.	Instruct the Treasury and Accounting Office to prepare and maintain and update the Property Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and its revenue collectors to remit and report its collections promptly. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant prepares journal entries to record the revenues for the calendar year 2020 to Government Equity accounts and debiting the affected income accounts.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the report of stale checks and its supporting disbursement vouchers and other documents to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of journal entry as an adjustment to the Cash in Bank - LCCA and Accounts Payable accounts. In addition, notify the concerned payees whose checks became stale for replacement of new checks.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Economic Enterprise Development Management Officer (MEEDMO) to prepare the monthly billing summary to be forwarded to the Accounting Office as the basis in the recording of revenue earned from the waterworks system for the period; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the accrual basis of accounting for revenues to present fairly and accurately the financial statements of the Economic Enterprise.

We recommended and agreed that Management adhere to the provisions of the GPPB, particularly on the utilization of the BGCM to attain the degree of transparency and monitoring objectives of the oversight bodies.

We recommended and agreed that Management religiously comply with all the reportorial requirements for the utilization of public funds, such as BGCM, to attain a higher degree of transparency of information and adhere to the oversight committees' full disclosure policy.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the following correcting entries to fairly present the Government Equity account as of December 31, 2020:
To reverse the erroneous entry made for expenditures incurred:
Government Equity				₱8,660,753.88
Due to Special Accounts				₱8,660,753.88
To record unrecognized assets:
Other PPE				₱287,000.00
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory	  180,000.00
Government Equity				₱467,000.00
To record unrecognized subsidy including the ending cash balance
Due to Special Accounts		          ₱8,710,156.85
Government Equity			            ₱8,710,156.85
Additionally, instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to be familiar with recording receipt and disbursement transactions from funds received from NGAs and/or other LGUs pursuant to the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to issue Inventory Custodian Slip and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for inventory and PPE items issued to prevent losses of government properties.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct:
12.6.1 The Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the supporting documents and signatories for claims against government funds before certifying as such;
The Municipal Treasurer promptly and completely submit all the paid DVs for audit and examination; and
To submit paid DVs held in its Office. Otherwise, these completed transactions will be suspended in the audit.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection efforts on delinquent Real Property Taxes and institute appropriate administrative and judicial remedies, if necessary, to enforce tax compliance from delinquent taxpayers, and update the listing of real properties within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.

We recommended and agree that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of using expense method in recording supplies and materials purchases, maintain an inventory account using the perpetual inventory system, and prepare supplies ledger card per stock number;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain supplies and materials stock cards per stock number, regularly prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to present its inventory accounts in the financial statements fairly; and
Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count pursuant to the provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

observations.managementAction:
1. Demand letters were already sent to officials with unliquidated cash advances.




2. Start deducting from salaries of employees who cannot refund or liquidate their outstanding cash advances.

3. No additional cash advances were granted to elective officials other than their own official travels.

4. Will institute legal remedies and sanctions to accountable officers who are no longer in the service.

5. Will strictly comply with the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

6. Updated accounting records like subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive exert further efforts to establish the ownership of 38 parcels of land with total carrying value of ₱18,481.53 for proper recognition in the books pursuant to Paragraphs 26 and 27 of IPSAS 17.




We recommended that the Local Chief Executive   (a) direct  the GSO and  the Municipal  Accountant to identify the items for disposal and perform the required procedures to derecognize the carrying amounts of the unserviceable properties in the Accounting and Property Records; (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer as concurrent GSO and the Municipal Accountant  to establish the cost of items which are part of a set or recorded in bulk to facilitate proper recognition in the books; and 

(c) require the officials concerned to (i) locate the PPE items which were not accounted for during the conduct of the physical inventory; and (ii) if these cannot be located anymore, require the accountable officers and end-users to request for relief of property accountability for the lost items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to confer with the Municipal Treasurer as concurrent GSO, to recognize each PPE item using its correct account classification in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of each PPE account at year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify all the items which were incorrectly recognized as PPE in the books; (b) accordingly prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances of the individual PPE accounts; and (c) draw adjusting entries to reflect the corresponding prior year expenses to the Government Equity.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant be more prudent in identifying and classifying the items to be recognized in the books as PPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to  prioritize the conduct of a complete physical inventory and the reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; and (b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/ GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
a) All efforts to locate available records on the whereabouts, manner of acquisition of these 38 lots proved futile and still, no record was found. Hence,  Management is looking for other possible ways to correct the carrying cost of these in the books, like using their fair market values.  Since it is no longer possible to establish the ownership of the 38 parcels of land, they are looking for legal ways on how they can secure certificates of titles of these lots in favor of the Municipality of San Miguel.

b) On-going identification of the items for disposal and derecognition of the carrying amounts of the unserviceable properties in the accounting and property records. 




Unaccounted PPE items are now being located and request for relief of accountability will be prepared and submitted in case of failure to locate the same.




c) Some of the PPE items which were misclassified were already re-classified to their correct account classification in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.




d) The Municipal accountant will prepare the necessary Adjusting Entries to reflect the correct balances of each PPE account.





e) Will prioritize the conduct of a complete physical inventory and maintain appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the officials in-charge of the development projects charged under the 20% Development Fund to supervise and monitor closely the timely and effective implementation of the development projects under their respective offices.
observations.managementAction: Will maximize utilization of the 20% Development Fund by implementing fully all the programmed development projects in the Annual Investment Plan.

a) Detailed LDRRMIP was prepared for CY 2020.




b) If the situation permits, various training will be conducted to capacitate the DRRM personnel and volunteers with adequate skills and training.

c) Management is in the process of establishing a system that would address post disaster activities and rehabilitation.


d) Management provided DRRM facilities for COVID 19 quick response teams.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive : (a) provide priority attention to the remaining barangays which have not established a fully-functional MRF; (b) embark on more effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program through re-use, recycling, and composting activities for an effective solid waste diversion as embodied in its SWM Plan; (c) formulate strategies to educate and encourage the constituents to practice waste segregation at source; and (d) enforce strictly the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/ laws/ regulations pertaining to solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: MENRO focused on the implementation of the audit recommendations and undertook various steps, programs and projects to implement the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended that (a) the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the HRM Officer to continue working back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱849,312.20; and

(b) the Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with the timely remittance of contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the HRMO are working back on prior year’s transactions to reconcile the prior years’ balance of ₱849,312.20.
b) Monthly contributions and loan amortizations are being remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue tracing the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱85,865.91 and to strictly observe the full remittance of the amount due to PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: While the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are still looking for previous years’ records to be able to reconcile the prior years’ unreconciled balance of ₱85,865.91, the Municipality continued to remit Philhealth contributions monthly and on a timely basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to work back on the unreconciled prior years’ balance of ₱137,936.90; and (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the withholding and timely remittance of the amount due to the Pag-IBIG.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are continuously looking for available records to be able to reconcile the prior year’s unreconciled balance of ₱137,936.90.

The withholding and remittance of PAG-IBIG contributions is being done monthly and on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit; and (b) require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is processed and approved as required under existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance to ensure efficient and effective control of the cash advance transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the issuance of Principal and/or Supplementary Receipts/Invoices in the ordinary course of business and the consequent examination of evidence of receipt of payments thereof as referred under existing BIR rules and regulations.
We recommended that the above Accountable Officers be relieved from their respective post until they will render accounting on the above accountable forms.

We recommended that the LGU thru its GAD Council convene, draw out the calendar of activities for the information drive to the different sectors of the Municipality and implement its plan to include the LGU’s employees including the Casuals, Job Orders and the Contract of Service.

We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prepare the GPB strictly in accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01. Ensure that organization-focused PPAs do not undergo HGDG. In case of attribution of PPAs budget to GAD Budget, use HGDG sectoral checklist for specific sector PPA, if not applicable, use the HGDG generic checklist. The attribution must be duly supported with means of verification per element of the HGDG checklist.

We recommend that the Accountant be directed to make the necessary adjustments in the books, the source of fund cannot and will not be a factor in the classification of accounts, and proper coordination of the Accounting Office and Treasury to avoid recurrence of similar error in the future.

We recommend that: 

The Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures;

 Concerned officials with government vehicles and the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit regularly thru the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets; and

 The Municipal Accountant be required to look into the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the DVs. Claims with incomplete supporting documents must be returned to the claimant before payment be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a follow-up on the demand letters issued to all concerned officers and employees to expedite liquidation of cash advances already due. Also, in the event of plain disregard of settlement of cash advances by the concerned employee despite due demand, we recommend the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 & 2018 AARs | No unliquidated cash advances were recorded as of December 31, 2020 except for the cash advance of former Municipal Mayor, Claudio Bonior for intelligence and confidential expenses for which, liquidation reports were already submitted to COA Central Office, awaiting issuance of Credit Notice.
observations.reason: There were no unliquidated cash advances for travel and time-bound purpose as of December 31, 2020 per submitted financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reclassification entries recognizing the accounts Due to Other Funds in the SEF and TF books and Due from Other Funds in the GF books, for taxes withheld in the SEF and TF but paid in the GF. We also recommended that the accounting office personnel shall be more prudent in monitoring payment of withheld taxes. Lastly, we recommended that management instruct the concerned MTO personnel to locate the document that would facilitate remittance of paid penalty to the BIR so as to avoid incurrence of additional penalties and/or surcharges.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers were prepared by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: There were no unreconciled items as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all department heads implement and monitor prompt submission of job order DTRs in order to facilitate timely preparation of payroll and on-time remittance of PAGIBIG remittances, especially loan amortization remittances, to avoid charging of penalties to the personal account of the job order worker. We also recommended that the concerned department head make their concerned job order workers understand the implications of their non-compliance therewith.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | DTRs are submitted on time. But there are still employees with no PAGIBIG number thus, deductions were not yet remitted.
observations.reason: There were no reported or noted unnecessary charging of penalties to personnel account of the job order worker.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the MPDC, rigorously monitor the on-going works undertaken by the concerned contractors in order to achieve a fast-track implementation of projects and immediately apply appropriate measures in order to address other issues that could possibly have the effect of extending the duration of project implementation.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | Of these projects, three are already 100% completed, one at 90% and one at 80%. LGU also has a control of SWAs and a staff monitors such. If with delays or deadline is approaching, the attention of the contactor will be called, warning notice will be sent and progress meeting will be called. Completion is also hampered with the effects of suspensions brought by the pandemic and unworkable conditions.
observations.reason: Rigorous monitoring were already undertaken by the management in order to address the issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC immediately furnish the Audit Team an authenticated copy of the final and approved Annual Procurement Plan and observe the provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of the Government Procurement Law or R.A.9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.2012-001 to ensure the efficient discharge of governmental functions and adoption of fiscal discipline measures.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR | APP is now submitted to the Audit Team as recommended. APP is also posted on time in the DILG website. An official website is now created and active where APP is also posted.
observations.reason: Management already submitted the APP to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer implement strategic measures, such as scheduling of days in the preparation of CTC and RPT share remittances, taking into consideration the off-peak use of computer units in the MTO.  Also, we recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer cause the remittance of barangay shares of real property taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to the provisions of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 to avoid unnecessary accumulation of funds in the municipal treasury and to enable the concerned barangays to timely implement beneficial barangay projects through the use of such funds.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | Management already regularly released the barangay RPT shares.
observations.reason: Barangay RPT shares were already released religiously.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct all department heads to observe Section 141 of NGAS Manual in taking up and recording of payable accounts, so that only valid payable accounts are recognized. It is also recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from booking up payables which still lack incomplete documentation so as to fairly present the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Management made sure that payables are booked up with proper supporting documents.
observations.reason: Management has already practiced requiring the attachment of pertinent documents. However,manage-ment admitted that not all procured goods were already delivered as at yearend.

observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | Donated properties are booked up in CY 2020.
observations.reason: There were a few transactions which were found wanting of some necessary documents to vouch full ownership over those PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide explanation on the: (a) issuance of 13 Suspension Orders equivalent to 155 calendar days due to the same reason of unworkable field condition caused by heavy rains in the locality and (b) non-inclusion of the estimated number of rainy/unworkable days which was not made known to all participating bidders before the bidding date. 

Likewise, we recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer and MPDC observe Item 3.J of Annex A and Item 1.3 of Annex E of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184, respectively, by preparing a more comprehensive study of the program/project so as to come up with a Program of Work already which reflects the most accurate contract duration date possible, covering all the necessary items of work, to expedite project implementation in accordance with the terms of the contract and timely render the services and benefits that can be derived from the programs to the public.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | These 3 infrastructure projects are now 100% completed. The issuance of suspension orders and time extensions were appropriate considering circumstances. Preparation of succeeding Program of Works are now are now being carefully studied.
observations.reason:
Still, there were noted delays in physical completion of some infrastructure projects.

This audit finding is reiterated and included in Part II of CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Local Finance Committee and Motorpool In-Charge focus on the afore-cited income accounts and formulate schemes and apply dynamic and systematic measures to maximize revenue generation efficiency rate in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019              &          2017 AARs | Intensive efforts were made to meet targeted deadlines including sending of demand letters and constant follow up. With the efforts made, actual collections were improved this year.
observations.reason: Some collection targets were sufficiently met in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and PPE of the LGU. Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommended that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

observations.reason: Management has not yet submitted the RPCPPE and RPCI to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct all the department heads who have had programs and projects implemented in CYs 2016 to 2019, with fund source other than LGU funds, to facilitate representation to their complementary national government agencies, and ask confirmation on the presence of fund transfers which have remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2019, being the person directly spearheading the program/project implementation. Only in the absence of confirmation, after exertion of all possible efforts to establish the transfer of funds from a specific government agency or entity, can the management opt to record and declare these unaccounted funds as income, which may then be used to finance beneficial programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 & 2018 AARs | A letter was sent to LBP, requesting them to furnish the agency with the necessary credit advice last November 2019, but no reply to date.
observations.reason:
There were still book reconciling items not yet recorded as of December 2020.

This audit finding was reiterated in Part II of this                     CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor continue to prioritize the processing of titles so as to acquire adequate and legal proof of ownership over these parcels of land and in order not to disrupt normal LGU operations and prevent loss of structures already built thereon. It is also recommended that management provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of certificates of title.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 & 2018 AARs | Necessary steps has been continually done to secure certificate of title over these lots and fully comply with the audit recommendation. As of March 2021, seven (7) titles are already transferred in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: Copies of the seven (7) land titles still have to be submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management initiate the conduct of an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Huge amount of this account pertains to the disallowance of previous municipal treasurer which is under litigation. The LGU will just have to wait for the judgment in order to act on this matter. For those accounts of employees, salary deductions are imposed and demands were made. Thus, there is a decrease of this account
observations.reason: There were still accounts which remained uncollected as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for: (1) debtors who are still alive and within the locality and (2) debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. Consequently, we recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommended that management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write-off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | The MSWDO has exerted efforts to send demand letters. There is also a collection from other debtors in CY 2019. The MSWDO is yet to submit list of debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility on or before April 30, 2020.
observations.reason: Accounts already due from the previous years remained uncollected as at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the MAO to gather data on the whereabouts of the animals comprising the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and verify existence thereof thru conduct of physical inventory. Result of physical count shall be incorporated in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant be also furnished with the same report in order to reconcile with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Inventory was conducted and management submitted documents last February 2020.
observations.reason: The submitted documents still have to contain the required data. Moreover, balance of the account Agricultural Produce for Distribution for CY 2019 and 2020 are still the same at ₱1,112,860.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, henceforth, observe the timeliness set forth in Annex C of the R-IRR of R.A.9184 to avoid delays in the implementation of programs and projects. Also, we recommended that the BAC meticulously review the Procurement Monitoring Report before uploading it in the LGU website to provide accurate data to the public.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Management has already reviewed the Procurement Monitoring Report before uploading it in the LGU website to provide accurate data to the public.
observations.reason: There were instances when the procurement timeliness were not faithfully observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit formal explanation on the low revenue realization of Sales Revenue - Hospital account. Also, we recommended that management promptly act on communications involving hospital operations which demand compliance of documentary requirements so as to have a smooth processing of Philhealth refunds and avoid delays or non-collection thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | Proper analysis in the declaration income shall be observed in order not to set very high targets during the budget year.
observations.reason: Hospital income in CY 2020 was higher compared to CY 2019 due to collections from services rendered in connection with COVID-19 related activities such as conduct of rapid testing and PCR tests.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify excess funds which are due for remittance to source agencies; (b) identify excess of fund transfers which are to be requested with approval to be used for other useful programs/projects and; (c) prepare the necessary reclassification entries to properly identify those accounts which are due to LGUs and NGAs and those which are to be recorded using the account “Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share”.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2018 AAR | There are Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs account which were utilized but the Construction in Progress account were not yet adjusted. Adjusting entries are prepared in the first quarter of 2020 to effect the utilization of these accounts.

Liquidation are submitted and updated. However, liquidation depend so much on the completion of the project
observations.reason: There are some excess funds which still have to be remitted to source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA Circular No.2012-001. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC observe Section 23 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 in the submission of eligibility documents of bidders, for COA review.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR | BAC Secretariat has assigned personnel to transmit the POs and contracts to COA on time
observations.reason: Management has submitted copies of perfected contracts/ purchase orders, for auditorial and technical review, but some were submitted only after the project has been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management find means to facilitate the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the spirit of transparency of procurement process.
