observations.reason: APP and PMR is posted in the DILG website. The LGU is still on process of reactivating the website. | The LGU website can be searched in the internet. However, only the APP can be found in the LGU’s official website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, management observe the provisions of COA Circular 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other LGUs so as to ensure that funds received are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and those services and benefits due to the public are not delayed.
observations.managementAction: CY  2019 & 2017 AARs | Management tried its best to accomplish programs/projects and liquidate the same. Liquidation reports have been submitted to NGAs. Constraints in the implementation were due to the fact that the municipal engineering office lacks personnel to assist him in the supervision of projects.
observations.reason: Management was still not able to fully implement and liquidate the transferred funds from National Government Agencies (NGAs).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide reasons as to the partial or non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, management is enjoined to fast track the implementation of programs/ projects/ activities identified in the budget and ensure inclusions of the projects enumerated under RA 9003 to serve the purpose of the created funds.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR | The implementation of Solid Waste Mgt. Program for CY 2021 is scheduled in the second quarter as per AIP in order to give ample time to implement in case of failure in the procurement process which was the main cause of this finding.
observations.reason: Accomplishment Report for Solid Waste Management Program still have to be submitted to and evaluated by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC coordinate with the Local Finance Council to evaluate and assess the actual necessity of these unimplemented PPAs. Otherwise, management may consider                 re-programming the funds so as to include only those PPAs really found viable and responsive to the actual and urgent need of the constituents, in order to ensure full attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes pursuant to the intents of Item 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the BAC to fast track implementation of on-going PPAs. We also recommended that henceforth, management include in the formulation of the Annual Budget, only those PPAs which are actually deemed significant and doable, and avoid inclusion of PPAs with generic description.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019           &                2017 AARs | The management is now more prudent with regard to appropriation for Barangay Development. Among the specific action taken is to ask for the barangay concerned for the Program of Work of the project they wish to implement before granting them the financial assistance and at the same time verifying whether the said project is among those identified in their barangay development plan. This is in addition to requiring them to liquidate previous financial assistance before granting another.
observations.reason: Some identified programs/ projects/ activities were not implemented during the year since most of them are focusing in implementing projects which are funded by national government agencies, as seen in the submitted Report of Government Projects.

observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | Inventory was conducted. Individual SL is created although the recognition of assets was not completed as of year-end.
observations.reason: Submitted Subsidiary Ledgers of the Accounting Office still have to be matched to the records of Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the provisions of Section 32 of Manual on NGAS in order to prevent misappropriation or loss of public funds.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR | We will establish a system of collection of remittance daily every 4 o’clock in the afternoon from all collectors to be captured by our system daily and to be deposited twice a week or daily depending on the amount of cash on hand involved.
observations.reason: There were instances when collections were not immediately deposited the next banking day.

observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | GSO is directed to propose an inventory committee for the conduct and finalization of inventory count.

It is also noted that some deliveries and infrastructure projects are delayed due to pandemic and rainy days during the third quarter of the year.
observations.reason:
There were still delayed deliveries and project completion in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate new pricing/costing schemes to come up with more relevant rates and fees for Masanao Hilltop Resort and Restaurant to ensure that goods and services are offered to the public at competitive prices without sacrificing the target operating income that can be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 AAR | The Ordinance fixing the rates is still under review by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was not yet satisfactorily complied with by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of properties and continue to identify in the other departments/offices those PPEs and inventories which are already unserviceable or non-functional and facilitate disposal thereof in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting and property records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the assets of the municipality in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR | Inventory reports were prepared by the General Services Officer (GSO). Copies thereof were given to each department head for review. Management still has to create inventory committee and appraisal committee.
observations.reason:
The inventory reports prepared by the GSO still cannot be relied upon as some PPE/inventories were no longer in the custody of the department head indicated in his report. Moreover, the GSO still needs to conduct a complete count of the PPE/inventory in all the department offices, and not just merely rely on the old reports with updates for current year purchases. 

This audit finding was reiterated in Part II of this                CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record purchases of supplies and materials to the corresponding inventory account and compute inventory balances as prescribed under Sections 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office  (a) maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; (b)furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances. Furthermore, we recommend that the Accounting Unit record issuances based on the SSMI submitted. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: CY              2012 AAR | GSO is taking the control of the receipts and release of supplies to different departments. Starting the year 2020, inventory is being recorded upon purchase and the corresponding departments submitted inventory balances as of period end to correct inventory accounts and take up expense. Inventory for agricultural produce was adjusted on January 2021.
observations.reason: Maintenance of Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock is still not followed. Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials, were also still not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and not by the Municipal Assessor and make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR | The Municipal Treasurer’s Office in coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, are now starting to extract copies of Assessed Value per Property Owners to set the lists of Accounts Receivables beginning for CY 2021 plus tax delinquent of previous year.
observations.reason:
Balances of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable accounts are still rendered inaccurate since the set-up amounts were only  estimates and are not representing the actual amount receivable from all of the taxpayers.

This audit finding was reiterated in Part II of this CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same

Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries, until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

 The Municipal Accountant be required to look into the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the DVs. Claims with incomplete supporting documents must be returned to the claimant before payment be made.

observations.reason: Fees for the use of tombs in the municipal cemetery is being amended in the New Municipal Tax Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the refund of the financial assistance it granted to the 66 Job Order employees in the total amount of P334,000.00 for lack of legal basis.

We recommended that the total amount of P106,000.00 be refunded by the Disbursing Officer, duly receipted by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding cash deposited to the account of the Municipality. We finally recommend that the former Municipal Mayor, Municipal Agriculturist, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and the Municipal Social and Development Officer, as project implementors, to submit a monitoring report on the Livelihood Programs implemented.

We recommended that management provide reasons as to the non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management implement programs/projects/activities on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1 in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public or to enjoy the comfort that the municipal employees of officials could have experienced therefrom.
observations.managementAction: CY The management is now more prudent with regard to appropriation for Barangay Development. Management has taken all the necessary measures in implementing the programs which is focusing in implementing projects which are funded by national government agencies, as seen in the submitted Report of Government Projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate new pricing/costing schemes to come up with more relevant rates and fees for Masanao Hilltop Resort and Restaurant to ensure that goods and services are offered to the public at competitive prices without sacrificing the target operating income that can be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: CY Management still has to propose amendment of Municipal Ordinance for the pricing/coding of Masanao rates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) institute appropriate action  to accountable officers with long overdue cash advances such as: (i) requiring the refund/liquidation of the cash advance; (ii) withholding the payment of salary/benefits of the concerned accountable officers,  (iii) desist from the practice of granting cash advances to  elective officials other than their own official travels; and (iv) institute  other legal remedies and sanctions that may apply; (b) strictly comply with the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain up-to-date subsidiary records as a tool to reconcile the records against the progress reports to reflect the accurate balance of the accounts in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive exert further efforts to establish the ownership of 38 parcels of land with total carrying value of ₱18,481.53 for proper recognition in the books pursuant to Paragraphs 26 and 27 of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management   (a) direct  the GSO and  the Municipal  Accountant to identify the items for disposal and perform the required procedures to derecognize the carrying amounts of the unserviceable properties in the Accounting and Property Records; (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer as concurrent GSO and the Municipal Accountant  to establish the cost of items which are part of a set or recorded in bulk to facilitate proper recognition in the books; and (c) require the officials concerned to (i) locate the PPE items which were not accounted for during the conduct of the physical inventory; and (ii) if these cannot be located anymore, require the accountable officers and end-users  to request for relief of property accountability for the lost items.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant confer with the Municipal Treasurer as concurrent GSO, to recognize each PPE item using its correct account classification in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant  draw adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of each PPE account at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) identify all the items which were incorrectly recognized as PPE in the books; (b) accordingly prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances of the individual PPE accounts; and (c) draw adjusting entries to reflect the corresponding prior year expenses to the Government Equity. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant be more prudent in identifying and classifying the items to be recognized in the books as PPE.

We recommended that Management  (a) prioritize the conduct of a complete physical inventory and the reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; and (b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively.

We  recommended that Management   (a) instruct the Municipal  Accountant to  analyze  and  ascertain  the  validity  of  long  outstanding  liabilities  in  the  books, otherwise, request the write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;  and (b)  revert to Government Equity the recorded  obligations  without  valid  documentation  and/or  which  have  been  outstanding  for  two  years  or  more  and  against  which  no  actual  claim  has  been  filed.

4. We recommended that management assign  personnel  to  specifically  to
      reconcile the balances in the books and that the balances in the  bank
      with Account Account:0522-1114-30.

5. We recommended that the General Services  Officer/Municipal  Treasurer
      of the municipality maintain and update stock cards and the  Municipal
      Accountant maintain ledger cards to  facilitate  the  conduct  of  the
      inventory taking and render the Report on the Physical  Count  of  the
      Property, Plant and Equipment (PRCPPE) not later than  January  31  of
      each year.

3.4  We  recommended  that  the  OIC  Municipal  Accountant  reconcile   the
   reciprocal accounts and effect the adjustments  to  reflect  the  correct
   account balances to present  reliable  data  on  the  agency’s  financial
   position as of a given period of time.

5.  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
      Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and look into  the
      matter in order to determine the causes of the delay and to direct the
      prompt submission of the required reports  in  compliance  with  their
      duties and responsibilities and as prescribed by  existing  rules  and
      regulations.  Unjustifiable failure on the part  of  the  official  or
      employee concerned to  submit  the  documents  and  reports  mentioned
      herein shall be considered a ground for the  automatic  suspension  of
      payment of their salaries, until they shall  have  complied  with  the
      aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to  any  disciplinary  action
      that may be instituted against them (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).

5.4. We recommended  that the Municipal  Accountant  issue  a  certification
that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in  the
books before a new cash advance is processed and approved as required  under
existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation  of
cash advance to ensure efficient and effective control of the  cash  advance
transactions.

6.8. We recommended  that  Management  prepare  and  submit  the  Report  on
Sources and  Utilization  of  DRRMF  to  the  Office  of  the  Auditor  duly
certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th  day  after  the
end of each month pursuant to Item No. 5.1.5 of COA  Circular  No.  2012-002
dated September 12, 2012.

4. We  recommended  that   the  Municipal  Treasurer  strictly  observe  the
   issuance of  Principal  and/or  Supplementary  Receipts/Invoices  in  the
   ordinary course of business and the consequent examination of evidence of
   receipt of payments thereof as referred  under  existing  BIR  rules  and
   regulations.

4. We recommended that the above Accountable Officers  be  relieved  from
      their respective post until they will render accounting on  the  above
      accountable forms.

4. We recommended that management require  the  accountable  officers  to
      liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to
      avoid possible misuse and losses of funds.  On  the  other  hand,  the
      Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every  time  collections  are
      turned over to him, all accountable forms whether issued or  not  must
      be presented and/or accounted for, and issuance of another accountable
      forms  must  be  made  only  after  liquidation/remittance   of    all
      collections.

   5. To ensure strict  adherence  to  rules  and  regulations,  appropriate
      disciplinary action shall be taken against  accountable  officers  who
      fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules  and
      regulations.

10.5 We recommended that the BAC prepare the PMR and submit the same to  the
   GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14)  calendar  days  after
   the end of every semester, as required under Section 12.2 of the  Revised
   Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act,  to
   allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies in compliance
   with  the  provisions  of  the  Government  Procurement  Act  and  ensure
   transparency on government procurement.

10.6 Further, we suggested that COA be furnished with a printed copy of  the
   said monitoring report as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

11.5 We recommended that the LGU thru its GAD Council convene, draw out  the
   calendar of activities for the information drive to the different sectors
   of the Municipality and implement its plan to include the LGU’s employees
   including the Casuals, Job Orders and the Contract of Service.

12.4 We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council  establish  sex  disaggregated
   data for its own personnel and from  there  conduct  gender  analysis  to
   identify gender  issues  and  appropriate  organization-focused  PPAs  to
   address the gender issues and incorporate them in the GPB for costing.

13.4 We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prioritize the  attendance  to
   seminar on GMEF to capacitate itself in the conduct of gender  audit  for
   CY 2020.

14.12 We recommended that the GFPS/GAD Council prepare the GPB  strictly  in
     accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  JMC  No.  2016-01.  Ensure  that
     organization-focused PPAs do not undergo HGDG. In case  of  attribution
     of PPAs budget to GAD Budget, use HGDG sectoral checklist for  specific
     sector PPA, if not applicable, use  the  HGDG  generic  checklist.  The
     attribution must be duly  supported  with  means  of  verification  per
     element of the HGDG checklist.

We recommended that the LCE require all concerned personnel to comply with the preparation of records/reports for local access roads prescribed under COA Circular 2015-008.  Also, the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct actual inventory count of all local road networks under the Municipality’s jurisdiction pursuant to Section V.1of COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the implementing departments to confirm whether projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds.  The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise determine if the unexpended balances accrue to the National Government or the LGU in case of counterpart funds.  If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, the LGU shall take the following actions:
For funds accruing to NGA’s, return immediately the unexpended balance to SA.  If the SA cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury; or
For counterpart funds, the Local Finance Committee shall conduct the necessary evaluation and thereafter give recommendations on the proper disposition of the funds in relation to the priority projects of the Municipality.
Lastly, initiate the immediate closure of the bank accounts once the balance is returned to the Bureau of Treasury (for Due to NGAs) or transferred to the general fund (for counterpart funds).

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:
enforce strictly the full settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2019;
stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled to avoid accumulation of the same; and
institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
continue incessantly the conduct of reconciliation of balances of the concerned receivable and liability accounts against relevant records and effect proper adjustments for deficiencies noted and errors uncovered to reflect the correct account balances;
exert extra efforts in obtaining evidences from prior years’ financial records to validate the Due from NGAs account.  Otherwise, require the Municipal Accountant to initiate request for write-off of the dormant account in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular 97-001 dated February 5, 2015.  The request shall be supported with documents required under the unnumbered memorandum dated July 21, 2014 of the Director IV, COA Regional Office No. V, Legaspi City; and
For those accounts under Due to NGAs, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and identify the source agencies. If projects were already completed, return the identified account balances to the source NGAs; and
if source cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to exert extra efforts to work back on the transactions charged to the LDRRMF and update the subsidiary ledgers of trust funds per year of transfer; and
thereafter, include in the Notes to financial statements the required disclosure in the prescribed format.

We recommended that the LSB strictly comply with the provisions of the Section 4.1 of PD 1445 and Section 155(a) of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.
Likewise, to avoid illegal expenditures, agency officials should be guided by the principle of “availability of funds” which provides that no money shall be disbursed unless there is an existing approved appropriation and there is available fund for the purpose.

We recommended that the LCE direct strictly the BAC Secretariat to prepare a consolidated and approved APP to facilitate the procurement process and delivery of goods, services to end-users for the benefit of intended beneficiaries.
Likewise, the MHO, as the end-user, shall ensure the timely submission of PRs and shall be aware of the procurement schedule to be undertaken by the Bids and Awards Committee relative to all requests for purchase of drugs and medicines.  The MHO shall also coordinate closely with the BAC to ensure the procurement will commence on time.

We recommended that the LCE cause the immediate closure of all existing open and controlled dumpsites both at the Municipal and Barangay levels and gear its efforts toward the construction of a sanitary landfill.  In this connection, we suggested that Management coordinate with the Provincial Government and District Representative and even venture with local private business establishments to cooperate financially to raise additional funds for the purchase of land and construction thereto of sanitary landfill.
We also recommended that the LCE require all Barangays to maintain MRFs or drop off centers.  Likewise, IEC shall be intensified to communicate the Municipality’s overall solid waste management program, more particularly the importance of segregation at source at the household level.
Also, burning of residual wastes shall be stopped.

We recommended that the LGU adopt a policy setting a limit on the allowable communication expenses considering the affordable promotional rates and plans offered by telecommunication companies to promote economy in the use of government funds.
The LGU should also consider installing a centralized internet connection that could serve all Departments instead of providing internet allowances to each Department Head.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to procure fuel, oil and lubricants through public bidding or any alternative mode of procurement as provided under R. A. 9184.  The BAC shall also evaluate all transactions pertaining to procurement, and prepare corresponding resolution to be attached to DV.  Henceforth, comply strictly with the procedures, controls, forms and reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant:
Not to process disbursement vouchers relative to fuel, oil and lubricant transactions without the necessary documents evidencing the conduct of proper procurement process as provided under RA 9184 and the forms and reports required by COA Circular 77-61;
Record said transactions using the perpetual inventory method in accordance with the NGAS manual; and
Compute the necessary withholding tax and remit the same to the BIR.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the LCE direct the Bids and Award Committee or the General Services Officer to submit promptly to COA copies of contracts, purchase orders and notices of deliveries pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001. She should ascertain that all needed documents are attached to the disbursement voucher before processing, approval and preparation of check to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the motor vehicles owned by the agency be permanently marked with “For Official Use Only” (three inches) under which should be written the name and logo of the office or the agency using the same.

We recommended that the LCE construct a garage to secure the vehicles and heavy equipment from burglars, and prevent them from direct exposure to natural elements such as rain and sunlight that hasten their deterioration as provided under paragraph 5 COA Circular No. 75-6 and Section 2 of PD 1445. Garage or motor pool will also provide a place to conduct repair and maintenance of heavy equipment.
We recommended that Management should strive for 100 per cent implementation of all projects embodied in the 20% Development Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% DF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.
We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject JMC pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.   Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Recommendations


   We strongly recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  all  officials
   concerned:


   1. Require the Municipal Property Inventory  and  Disposal  Committee  to
      complete physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned
      by the Municipality and to facilitate preparation  and  completion  of
      the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
      for submission to the Office of the Auditor.

   2. Prepare and maintain the prescribed PPELC to be  reconciled  with  the
      RPCPPE to establish the correctness of the PPE accounts  amounting  to
      P83.445 million.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
 to:


   1.   Provide allowance for depreciation on depreciable assets regularly;


   2.  Record immediately the depreciation expense in the books of  accounts
      in order to present fairly  the  value  of  the  Property,  Plant  and
      Equipment in the financial statements; and


   3.  Effect  prior  years’   adjustment   for    depreciation   equivalent
      to   the  amount  that should have been provided from the date of  its
      acquisition/construction.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


  1. Strictly enforce the settlement of unliquidated  cash  advances  as  of
     December 31, 2019 and thereafter;


  2. Stop granting additional cash advance to officers and employees  unless
     the previous one had been fully liquidated to avoid accumulation of the
     same;


  3. Initiate appropriate actions against erring officials and employees, if
     warranted;

  4. Require the Municipal Accountant  to  send  written  demand  letter  to
     officers/employees with accountability that is no longer connected with
     the municipality.

Recommendation

   We recommended that the Municipality refrain from granting cash  advances
   other than travelling expenses to elected municipal officials pursuant to
   COA Circular No. 97-002.

Recommendation

   We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct   the   designated
   Disbursing Officer to refrain from  transferring  her  cash  advances  to
   other municipal personnel in accordance with COA Circular No.  97-002  to
   prevent misuse of government funds.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct:


   1. The  concerned  implementing  offices/departments  and  the  Municipal
      Engineer to implement immediately the various PPAs funded by NGAs  and
      other LGUs;


   2. The Municipal Accountant  to  exert  extra  effort  in  resolving  the
      reconciling items in the subsidiary ledger balances.

   3. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal  Treasurer  to  immediately
      return or remit the excess amount to the source agency.

Recommendation

   We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  together  with  the  Municipal
   Engineer and OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Officer maximize  the
   utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the  implementation
   of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to  help
   in the attainment of social, economic and  environmental  development  so
   that optimum benefits can be availed by the constituents.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


   1. Instruct the General Services Office and other  offices  concerned  to
      refrain from using unregistered vehicles owned by the Municipality and
      regularly monitor the expiration of all  motor  vehicles  registration
      and  its  (registration)  timely  renewal   to   avoid   invalidation,
      fines/penalties for delinquency and additional losses.

   2. Direct the General Services Office to dispose  the  unserviceable/non-
      functional  properties  following  the  guidelines  on   disposal   of
      unserviceable properties.

Recommendations


   We strongly recommended that:


   1.  The  Municipality  exercise  due  diligence   and   care   over   the
      government properties  owned  and  entrusted  to  them  to  avoid  any
      liability occasioned by negligence in the safekeeping or use  of  such
      property;


   2.  The  immediate  filing  of  the  notice  and   Request   for   Relief
      from Accountability of the totally damaged Municipal Ambulance by  the
      person/s responsible with a written satisfactory  explanation  or  the
      reason(s) for such delay to the Regional Director thru the audit  team
      to facilitate proper accounting of the property.   Hereunder  are  the
      documents for the Request for Relief from Accountability:


     a. Basic notice of loss/damage
     b. Affidavit or Sworn Statement of the proper accountable  officer  on
        the facts  and  circumstances  surrounding  the  said  loss/damage,
        supported by the affidavit of two  (2)  disinterested  persons  who
        have personal knowledge of such fact/loss/damage
     c. Comment and/or recommendation of the Agency Head concerned  on  the
        request
     d. Exact or accurate amount of government cash or book  value  of  the
        property subject of the request for relief
     e. Property  Acknowledgment  Receipt  (PAR)/Memorandum  Receipts  (MR)
        covering the properties subject of the request
     f. Report of Damaged/Destroyed Property


   3. The  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  apply
      accounting policies relative to the  subject  matter  and  record  the
      accountability of the accountable officer.


   4. The Municipality hold the person/s concerned liable for the value  of
      the damaged property.

   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
   Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-09 dated February 27, 2020.
   Management Comment


   The Municipality did not comment  on  the  above  audit  observation  and
   recommendations.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipality ensure  the  implementation  of  all
   PPAs identified under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction  Management  Fund
   Investment  Plan  (LDRRMFIP)  and  Budget.   Likewise,   prioritize   the
   acquisition  of  disaster  preparedness  equipment   and   infrastructure
   projects for calamity-stricken areas.

   We also recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
   Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to ensure that plans,
   programs and activities in the LDRRMFIP are  feasible  and  that  regular
   monitoring is conducted on the implementation of the Projects, Plans  and
   Activities of the Municipality.

Recommendations


   We strongly recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require/instruct  the
   Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to  submit  immediately  the
   concerned disbursement vouchers with all  its  supporting  documents  for
   post-audit.


   We likewise recommended that internal control be strengthened by ensuring
   that the Municipality strictly complies with the afore-cited implementing
   laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal  Treasurer
   to demand  from  the  concerned  suppliers  issuance  of  valid/unexpired
   official receipts in lieu of the issued invalid/expired ORs.


   We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer verify the  validity  of
   the Official Receipts before releasing the checks to suppliers to  ensure
   adherence by the latter to Revenue Regulation No. 18-2012  dated  October
   22, 2012 and to prevent imposition of sanction/s relative thereto.

Recommendations


   We strongly recommended that the management observe properly  procurement
   planning and process in accordance with RA 9184.


   Likewise, we  recommended  that  the  Municipality  draw  check  payments
   directly in  the  name  of  the  supplier  in  conformity  with  existing
   regulations and withheld the appropriate taxes due the government.

Recommendation

   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Solid
   Waste Management Board maximize the utilization of  the  funds  allocated
   for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities  that
   ensures the protection of public health and environment.

We recommend that henceforth, management observe the provisions of COA
Circular 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other LGUs so as to ensure that
funds received are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and those services and
benefits due to the public are not delayed.

We recommend that the MPDC coordinate with the Local Finance Council
to evaluate and assess the actual necessity of these unimplemented PPAs. Otherwise,
management may consider re-programming the funds so as to include only those
PPAs really found viable and responsive to the actual and urgent need of the
constituents, in order to ensure full attainment of socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes pursuant to the intents of Item 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC
No.2017-1. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with
the BAC to fast track implementation of on-going PPAs. We also recommend that
henceforth, management include in the formulation of the Annual Budget, only
those PPAs which are actually deemed significant and doable, and avoid inclusion of
PPAs with generic description.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer provide explanation on the: (a)
issuance of 13 Suspension Orders equivalent to 155 calendar days due to the same
reason of unworkable field condition caused by heavy rains in the locality and (b)
non-inclusion of the estimated number of rainy/unworkable days which was not
made known to all participating bidders before the bidding date.
Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer and
MPDC observe Item 3.J of Annex A and Item 1.3 of Annex E of the 2016 R-IRR of
R.A.9184, respectively, by preparing a more comprehensive study of the
program/project so as to come up with a Program of Work already which reflects
the most accurate contract duration date possible, covering all the necessary items
of work, to expedite project implementation in accordance with the terms of the
contract and timely render the services and benefits that can be derived from the
programs to the public.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make a follow-up on the
demand letters issued to all concerned officers and employees to expedite liquidation
of cash advances already due. Also, in the event of plain disregard of settlement of
cash advances by the concerned employee despite due demand, we recommend the
withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance
therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the
MPDC, rigorously monitor the on-going works undertaken by the concerned
contractors in order to achieve a fast-track implementation of projects and
immediately apply appropriate measures in order to address other issues that could
possibly have the effect of extending the duration of project implementation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct all the department heads
who have had programs and projects implemented in CYs 2016 to 2019, with fund
source other than LGU funds, to facilitate representation to their complementary
national government agencies, and ask confirmation on the presence of fund
transfers which have remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2019, being the
person directly spearheading the program/project implementation. Only in the
absence of confirmation, after exertion of all possible efforts to establish the transfer
of funds from a specific government agency or entity, can the management opt to
record and declare these unaccounted funds as income, which may then be used to
finance beneficial programs and projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Local Finance Committee and
Motorpool In-Charge focus on the afore-cited income accounts and formulate
schemes and apply dynamic and systematic measures to maximize revenue
generation efficiency rate in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444
(a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160.
The audit recommendation was well-taken by the management.

We recommend that the:
(a) Municipal Accountant review the composition of subsidiary ledgers
for all Construction in Progress accounts so as to provide accurate
supporting data for the corresponding general ledger account
balances;
(b) Municipal Engineer assist the Municipal Accountant by providing
copies of Program of Works, Statement of Work Accomplished,
Certificates of Completion and Turn-over of all infrastructure
projects in the current and prior years, when needed, so as to supply
data required in the reconstruction of the CIP account SLs;
(c) Municipal Accountant conduct a periodic reconciliation of inventory
of completed projects with the records of the Municipal Engineer so
as to promptly recognize completed projects and drop the
corresponding amount in the related CIP accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine
the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize
and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of
inventories and PPEs of the LGU;
(b) require the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI
and submit copies thereof to the Municipal Accountant, immediately
after the conduct of physical count, for reconciliation with accounting
records, and furnish similar copy thereof to the audit team order to
verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
(c) instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to trace the Property
Acknowledgment Receipts (PAR) issued to accountable
officers/employees, in order to pinpoint property accountability,
should the result of the physical count be less than that of the
accounting records, due to missing or unserviceable PPE items and
inventories.
(d) direct the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to submit
copies of proof of reconciliation between GSO and accounting records
to the audit team, for validation purposes, in order to fully and
properly document adjustments to be taken up by the Municipal
Accountant in the LGU books, if warranted.

We recommend that:
(a) the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Municipal
Agriculturist, make representation with DARFO7, and ask for
copies of donation papers and turn-over documents, duly signed and
acknowledged received by concerned parties, in order to provide
proof of concrete ownership and control over these donated PPEs, in
accordance with IPSAS 17, 23, 29 and 33 and Section 63 of
P.D.1445;
(b) the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of proper and complete
copies of donation papers and turn-over documents, record the
donated PPEs in the LGU books at its fair value pursuant to Item 42
of IPSAS 23.

We recommend that the Municipal Assessor continue to prioritize the
processing of titles so as to acquire adequate and legal proof of ownership over these
parcels of land and in order not to disrupt normal LGU operations and prevent loss of
structures already built thereon. It is also recommended that management
provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of certificates of title.

We recommend that:
(a) the concerned requisitioning officer or department head, in
coordination with the BAC, monitor the delivery of procured
goods in accordance with the delivery term as stated in the
Purchase Order, to avoid delays in future procurement of goods;
(b) the Municipal Engineer apply strict monitoring of project
accomplishment in accordance with the terms of the contract, to
avoid further delays on-going contracts, and prevent incurrence
thereof in the future;
(b) the BAC ensure giving emphasis on the importance of timely
delivery of goods as well as project completion to the participating
bidders during the entire procurement process, as can be
documented in the BAC minutes of meeting, so as to improve their
consciousness on complying with the terms of the Purchase Order
and contracts and give the public reasonable assurance of timely
attainment of benefits that can be derived from these
programs/projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer implement strategic measures,
such as scheduling of days in the preparation of CTC and RPT share remittances,
taking into consideration the off-peak use of computer units in the MTO. Also, we
recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer cause the remittance of
barangay shares of real property taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after
the end of each quarter pursuant to the provisions of Section 271 of Republic Act
7160 to avoid unnecessary accumulation of funds in the municipal treasury and to
enable the concerned barangays to timely implement beneficial barangay projects
through the use of such funds.

We recommend that all department heads implement and monitor prompt
submission of job order DTRs in order to facilitate timely preparation of payroll
and on-time remittance of PAGIBIG remittances, especially loan amortization
remittances, to avoid charging of penalties to the personal account of the job order
worker. We also recommend that the concerned department head make their
concerned job order workers understand the implications of their non-compliance
therewith.

1.7 We recommended that Management require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to -
 Record adjustments corresponding to credit memos after it have
been identified and documented;
 Analyze and verify the details of the one closed account and
record appropriate adjustments duly supported by documents;
 Submit for audit the Bank Reconciliation Statements on a
regular basis pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996; and
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to make
concerted efforts to review and analyze their respective records to come
up with reconciled and correct balances.

2.9 We reiterated our CY 2018 audit recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer be directed to comply
with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management
System and to their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VI
and VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, respectively.

3.6 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
 Record adjustments to RPT Receivables, which is the difference between
the taxes due (per certified list of the MT) and the recorded balance of
RPT Receivables as at December 31, 2019;
 Set up RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2020 based on the Certified List
of Taxpayers;
 Investigate or trace the negative balances posted in the subsidiary ledgers
of the Receivable accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries in
order to present the correct amount of receivables in the financial
statements; and
 Monitor status of liquidation of fund transfers and demand/follow-up
with concerned Barangay officials for the prompt liquidation of past due
accounts and/or refund of unused/excess fund transfer.

3.7 Moreover, Management should not allow another fund transfer to Barangay
with unliquidated balances.

4.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Re-appropriate for eligible development projects the savings from the
implementation of projects under the 20% DF;
b. Evaluate whether the 24 PPAs funded from prior years’ budget are still
relevant otherwise revert the fund to other programs and projects
which are more needed and applicable upon recommendation of the Local
Chief Executive and approval by the Sanggunian concerned (Section 322 of
RA No. 7160); and
c. Require a close coordination among offices involved in the implementation
of projects under the 20% DF in order to achieve completion targets
indicated in the AIP. Make sure the timeline set for every project is strictly
followed.

5.7 We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office
(LDRRMO) be required to ensure the optimal use of the LDRRM Funds by
implementing the planned programs and activities under the four thematic areas for
Mitigation Fund, which are necessary for the attainment of the goals and objectives
of the LGU’s LDRRM Plan.

6.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be
required to remit in full the taxes withheld on income payments to BIR, as well as
the employees’ and employer’s contributions and loan amortizations to GSIS and
Pag-IBIG Fund, within the period prescribed in Revenue Memorandum Circular
No. 23-2012, RA No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997, and RA No. 9679 or HDMF Law
of 2009.

7.9 We recommended for the creation and operation of the IAS as required under
RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177, to assist in the achievement of an efficient
and effective fiscal administration and performance of LGU’s affairs and functions.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare the
financial statements of the Barangays within sixty (60) days after the close of the year
as required in Section 10.4 of the Manual on Financial Management of Barangays.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of its Loans Payable in the face of the LGU’s financial statements to provide users of the fairly presented financial statements.
We recommended that Management implement as scheduled, the development projects programmed under the 20% development fund to ensure timely delivery of services/benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended strict adherence to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that Management will fast track the hiring of Local DRRM Officer and create the other two mandatory positions as set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CS Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO's organizational structure and manpower to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide/submit to COA Office the lacking documentary requirements for further evaluation.  Further, strictly adhere the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of the funds released to NGOs/POs as provided under COA Circular 2007-001 to ensure that government funds are spent in accordance with the purpose.

We recommended Management to submit justification on the excess of the Contract Cost to the COA Evaluated Cost in the aggregate amount of P2,449,270.02 for further evaluation. We also recommended Management to ensure compliance with the existing rules and regulations in the preparation of Approved Budget Cost (ABC) and that cost estimates be in accordance with the project’s plans and specifications.

We recommended that Management, thru the Sangguniang Bayan, expedite the revision and updating of the Municipal Economic Enterprise Development Code in order that fees and rates will be adjusted and/or updated to meet the financial needs of the LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to focus on ways of improving the collection efficiency of the Municipality in order to generate enough funds to augment its limited resources.
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provision of RA 9679 in observing the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG contribution government share at P100 per month per employee.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned accountable officers to cause the immediate submission of all lacking DVs with original supporting documents to the Auditor's Office and to follow strictly the regulations of the submission of accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately the required documents for the Technical evaluation and inspection of the completed infrastructure projects. Failure to do so, may compel the Audit Team to issue Notice of suspensions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the daily and intact remittance of collections from the revenue collectors. Stop the practice of issuing additional accountable forms while collections out of the previously issued forms were not yet remitted. Moreover, impose disciplinary actions/sanctions to those collectors who repeatedly commit the same violation.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks amounting to ₱192,419.88 and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation; and (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payee of the availability of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made adjustments for the staled checks under JEV Nos. 101-1902-011 and 101-1903-0021 dated February 28, 2019 and March 29, 2019, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) adhere strictly to all applicable laws, rules and regulations affecting cash advances; (b) require the Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned officials and employees whose cash advances remained unliquidated as of year-end for the proper recording of expenses in the books; and (c) comply strictly with the guidelines and principles of imprest fund system wherein regular, recurring, and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier pursuant to the regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances.  Out of the cash advance of ₱4,717,234.06 which was outstanding as of December 31, 2018, a total of ₱2,780,110.00  was already liquidated, leaving a balance of ₱617,124.06 as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 28.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Planning Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to implement and regularly monitor all  developmental projects embodied in the  local development plan within the targeted time; (b) ensure that the MPDO incorporate only eligible projects chargeable against  the fund pursuant to the regulations; and (c) direct the Municipal and Development Council to re-appropriate balances of the fund for the ensuing year to finance only those development programs, projects or activities identified under the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation (c) to be implemented in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 4, page 46.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) provide attention to the nine remaining barangays for their compliance relative to the establishment of their own MRF and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program through re-use, recycling, and composting activities for an effective solid waste diversion; (b) strictly implement at the household level the no segregation no collection policy in accordance with the Act; and (c) deputize Solid Waste Management Officers and require their attendance to  training and capacity building at the National Ecology Center to enforce the requirements of laws, rules and regulations concerning environment.
observations.managementAction: Solid Waste Management Officers were deputized and trained.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 6, page 56.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the Fund following the guidelines under the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the physical inventory and monitoring of all procured supplies, materials, equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5, page 52.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE strengthen efforts with the help of the GFPS to comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal person partly complied with the needed processes and mechanisms pursuant to the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Also discussed in Observation No. 7, page 58.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require (a) the Municipal Accountant and the HRM Officer to work back on the unreconciled prior years balance; (b) the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment amounting to ₱10,146.89; and (c) the HRM Officer to immediately notify the Accounting Office of any modification in the payroll to adjust recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation by the Accounting Office and the Human Resources Management Office for unreconciled prior year’s balance is still on-going. The erroneous recording was already adjusted in the books per JEV No. 101-1901-001 dated January 31, 2019.
observations.reason: Also discussed in Observation No. 8, page 60.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the rules and regulations on the utilization of SEF, as prescribed per DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and to make necessary corrections on the above noted deficiencies
We recommend that the concerned officials/offices see to it that disbursements vouchers are supported with complete documentation as required in existing regulations.
We recommend that management should be mindful of the guidelines set forth in the circular and submit immediately the documents required in the cited circular
We require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to update his cashbooks and record immediately all transactions for the day in consonance with COA Circular No. 2002-003.

We recommend to the collectors/accountable officers to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer who in turn shall consolidate the reports in the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

recommendation that
Management:

full settlement of all
unliquidated cash
advances as of December
31, 2018;

b. Stop the granting of
additional cash advances
to officials and
employees unless the

settled, to avoid

same; and

legal actions/sanctions
against those erring
accountable
officers/employees for

with existing laws, rules
and regulations on the
granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash
advances, if warranted.

We recommended that the |LCE, Municipal Planning 
and Development Officer,
Municipal Budget Officer
explain why the
transfers of
appropriations were not

appropriation ordinance

budget.

We also recommended that
Management ensure that

which are considered as

ordinance covering a

Sangguniang Panlalawigan
for review.

We recommended that
Management submit

appropriated projects

We recommended that
Management appropriate

of SWM programs,

as required under

We also recommended

20% EDF for SWM
programs/projects/activi
ties incorporated in the

We recommended that the
