We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned to: 1. Create a new set of inventory committee considering that there were members who already had retired from service in the existing inventory committee under EO No. 2 series of 2010. 2. Require the committee to conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality to facilitate the completion of inventory of PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment. 3. Prepare and maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish the correctness of the PPE accounts amounting to P158,897,313.40.

Require the committee to conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality to facilitate the completion of inventory of PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. Finding no. 1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Effect prior years’ adjustment equivalent to the amount that should have been provided from the date of its acquisition/construction;
2. Provide allowance for depreciation on depreciable assets regularly; and
3. Record immediately the depreciation expense in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements for calendar years 2011 to 2016 and 2018 and to record immediately any book reconciling items that require adjustments and corrections in the general ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to prove the correctness and existence of the cash in bank balances as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: Strictly enforce the settlement of unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018 and thereafter; Stop granting additional cash advance to officers and employees unless the previous one had been fully liquidated to avoid accumulation of the same; Initiate appropriate actions against erring officials and employees, if warranted; Require the Municipal Accountant to send written demand letter to officers/employees with accountability that is no longer connected with the municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. Finding no. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers for post-audit. We likewise recommended that internal control be strengthened by ensuring that the municipality strictly complies with the afore-cited implementing laws, rules and regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount of P697,132.23 to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and LDRRM Officer - Designate follow the four (4) thematic areas reflected in the National DRRM Plan in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP of the municipality so that disaster risk will be adequately addressed.

We recommended that Municipal Officials must work closely with the MDRRMO to ensure effective formulation, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the Local DRRM Plan and the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan. We also recommended that the MDRRMO include in their plan projects, programs and activities relative to the above mentioned standards especially those on not yet conducted, available, existing and operational in the municipality to ensure disaster preparedness.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prepare a budget proposal for the siting of sanitary landfill and see to it that PPAs relative thereto be provided with a budget and included in the succeeding year’s annual budget for proper implementation.

We recommended that unexpended LDRRMF amounting to P3,552,235.81 as of December 31, 2014 be transferred to a special trust fund pursuant to Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the MSWMB, the Municipal Treasurer and the MENRO to strictly impose and collect garbage fees not only to business establishments but also to every household and fines/penalties be imposed to violators of the above mentioned ordinances. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the members of the MSWMB to prepare SWM plan to facilitate assessment and monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a new set of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to perform mandated functions in accordance with laws, rules and regulations relative thereto. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality strongly implement the segregation of garbage at source and see to it that PPAs in the Solid Waste Management Plan are included in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in all financial transactions of the Municipality and attach the same to disbursement vouchers and journals pursuant to Section 31 of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume II.

Likewise, the General Services Officer-Designate prepares an Inventory Report on excess materials taken from various projects and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) or Inventory Custodian Slip, if applicable.

We strongly recommended that the MSWMB strictly enforce the “No segregation, No collection” Policy as mandated by Environmental Code of the Municipality. Likewise, provide containers for each type of waste from all sources. The solid waste container depending on its use shall be properly marked or identified for on-site collection as “compostable”, “non-recyclable”, “recyclable” or “special waste”, or any other classification as may be determined by the Board in accordance with laws, rules and regulations required under RA 9003 and the Environmental Code of the Municipality of San Miguel.

We recommended that the OIC – Municipal Accountant submit the following documents (a) approved scholarship program including the detailed qualification and criteria of selection and the amount of scholarship to be granted to each Municipal scholars/beneficiaries (b) Sangguniang Bayan resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with SAMODA, (c) notarized memorandum of Agreement/contract between the Municipality and SAMODA, (d) approved Individual Social Case Sturdy Report and (e) Certificate of Indigency signed by the Punong Barangay pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor work for the provision of a sufficient garage or motor pool where all motor vehicles will be kept when not in use in order to prevent them from rapid deterioration. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor program the construction of a bodega for unserviceable properties and dispose those beyond repair according to the guidelines on Disposal.
observations.reason: A motor pool for serviceable/unserviceable motor vehicles had been provided/constructed for heavy equipment only as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that unexpended LDRRMF amounting to P3,552,235.81 as of December 31, 2014 be transferred to a special trust fund pursuant to Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipality of San Miguel through the Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of LDRRM Officer and three (3) Technical Staff following the position titles, qualification standards and salary grades set by Joint Circular No. 2014-1 to be able to properly perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer/Technical Staff.
observations.reason: The Municipality had already hired a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) II as of December 31, 2019 and intends to open 2 permanent positions to augment the MDRRM office of the municipality in the CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and MPDC submit to this Office at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going government programs/projects/activities (“P/P/A”) of the Municipality and those that are to be implemented during the year.

We recommended that the driver be required to properly accomplish a Driver’s Trip Ticket before using a government vehicle and a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a monthly Report of Official Travels for audit purposes. Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption shall likewise be submitted to the Auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.reason: No report was submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unexpended CY 2013 DRRMF to the General Fund so as to make this available for other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 reverted in February AAR 2020 including the balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality, by immediately closing the account Due from LGUs in the year the funds are already expended and liquidated by the recipient barangays. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, otherwise, additional fund transfers shall not be released.
observations.managementAction: CY 2018 Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Accountant to the Brgy. Captains and additional assistance is granted to those with appropriate unliquidated assistance

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the grant of additional cash advances for catering expenses to any LGU employee unless the previous cash advance has already been liquidated. Moreover, it is recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately send demand letters to those LGU employees who still have unliquidated cash advances for catering expenses to avoid withholding thereof from their respective salaries pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. Likewise, it is recommended that the Masanao Manager ensure the inclusion of storable food supplies in the Annual Procurement Plan to avoid haste in the procurement process. Lastly, cash advances specifically for catering expenses of Masanao shall be granted only to the Masanao Manager, only for those emergency and perishable food supplies, in order to properly monitor the timely liquidation and avoid overlapping of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: CY Procurement of food supplies were done through public bidding and alternative mode of procurement. Only the masanao manager is allowed to draw a cash advance for perishable food items/goods up to the accountability limit on her fidelity bond. No cash advance shall be granted unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the imposition of surcharges/penalties on unpaid water bills pursuant to Section 31 of Municipal Ordinance No.C-116 Series of 2013, in the absence of any ordinance ordering otherwise, so as to generate additional income to the municipal funds.
observations.managementAction: CY Ten per cent penalty have been charged to delinquent consumers since last year 2019 to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management be more mindful of the timeliness in project implementation, so as to deliver its intended purpose and benefits to the public the soonest possible time.
observations.managementAction: CY The project is already completed but subject to compliance with the findings in the punchlist.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a justification as to the failure to obtain prior approval or authorization by the sanggunian before incurring expenses or entering into a contract. Henceforth, we recommend that management obtain and attach approval or authorization by the sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: CY Only the Brgy. Development Program was the generic appropriation but appropriate SB resolution/ordinance was required before implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop the amount representing SMSHIP premium collections from the account Due to Philhealth so as to facilitate utilization of this fund for other meaningful programs/projects.
observations.managementAction: CY The SMSHIP collection from indigent members in the Due to Philhealth was already declared / removed from account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw reclassification entries for transactions which were improperly recorded in the books to attain fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY The over deducted amount has been refunded to employees and reclassifying entries have already been prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to properly and completely identify those obligations which are considered undocumented and confirmed non-existent regardless of the year they were incurred, as well as all payables which have remained outstanding for more than two (2) years, be reverted to the Surplus Account, pursuant to Executive Order No. 109, and Sections 141 and 157 of GAAM, in order to clear the books of these non-existent obligations and fairly present the actual current liabilities in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY Corresponding journal vouchers were prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management always ensure the availability of funds in order to sustain sufficiency of computer units installed with e-TRACS system. Likewise, familiarization of e-TRACS by the concerned collecting officers is encouraged to facilitate timely preparation of Report of Collections and Deposits, pursuant to Section 29, Chapter 3 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Moreover, it is recommended that San Miguel Municipal Hospital be provided with a computer unit installed with e-TRACS and coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) as to its viability and sustainability to reduce the volume of ORs which need to be captured manually into the computer system.
observations.managementAction: CY Computers were already installed with e-TRACS. Reports of Collections and Deposits were already generated on time, according to the MTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the root cause of the negative balance of the Government Equity account under the sub-fund 20% Development Fund, in order to fairly present the account in the individual financial statements. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Government Equity account, especially that adjustments effected thereto are substantial in amount, for transparency and easy monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: CY The negative balance was eliminated as of December 31, 2019, according to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, enforce immediate and regular remittance of daily collections by the Collecting Officer to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS in order to fairly present collections of the LGU in a given period and avoid possible loss or misuse thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY Collectors are now remitting their collections intact, immediately and regularly to LO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.
observations.managementAction: CY The MDRRM officer has taken all the necessary measures in implementing the programs and activities of the DRRM Office, it can be evidently reflected to the current performance of the office with the present leadership and guidance of the present Administration. As to CY 2015, there were minor lapses done as to the preparation of PPMPs and procurement matters due to the newly appointed MDRRM Officer, adjusting on the LGU process specifically MDRRM PPAs and studying and implementing, at the same time, the DRRM laws, regulations and mandates.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant regularly submit all the DM/CM indicated in the BRS and attach the paid and cancelled checks thereto pursuant to the pertinent Paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011 in order to facilitate a comprehensive review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.
observations.managementAction: CY According to the Municipal Accountant, the LGU was already enrolled in the We-access of Land Bank in order to get the updated bank statement necessary for the BRS. However, for Debit and Credit Memos, she already tried to get copies thereof from the bank, but to no avail. Bank statements and returned check images are submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer, adhere to the requirements in the collection, remittance, liquidation and deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction: CY LO does no longer receive remittances without the required RCDs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and PPE of the LGU. Likewise, we recommend that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommend that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY GSO is directed to propose an inventory committee for the conduct and finalization of inventory count.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Account to draw the necessary journal entries to record the transactions which have long been reflected in the BRS as reconciling items in order to fairly present the Cash in Bank and the related income and expense accounts, as well as PYA accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY A letter was sent to LBP, requesting them to furnish the agency with the necessary credit advice last November 2019, but no reply to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Assessor to make representation with the concerned owners/relatives/representatives of the owners of the private lots, in order to process documents requiring their signatures and eventually secure Certificates of Title over these lots. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Assessor immediately claim the Certificates of Title and furnish the Municipal Accountant copies thereof for proper recording in the LGU books. Finally, we recommend that management ensure first that necessary locational clearance and other requirements imposed under existing laws, rules and regulations can be smoothly processed before deciding to purchase a certain lot so as to avoid legal disputes over the lot ownership and guarantee eventual issuance of Certificate of Land Title in the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: CY Processing of title is on-going according to the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all concerned officers and employees, requiring immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, we recommend the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: CY Demand letters were sent but as of December 2019, there was a balance from CY 2018 cash advances. Another demand letter shall be issued to the concerned employee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management initiate the conduct of an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.
observations.managementAction: CY The MSWDO has exerted efforts to send demand letters. There is also a collection from other debtors in CY 2019. The MSWDO is yet to submit list of debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility on or before April 30, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for: (1) debtors who are still alive and within the locality and (2) debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write-off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY The MSWDO has send demand letters. There is also a collection from other debtors in CY 2019. The MSWDO is yet to submit list of debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility on or before April 30, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the MAO to gather data on the whereabouts of the animals comprising the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and verify existence thereof thru conduct of physical inventory. Result of physical count shall be incorporated in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant be also furnished with the same report in order to reconcile with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: CY The MSWDO submitted documents last February 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee, henceforth, observe the timeliness set forth in Annex C of the R-IRR of R.A.9184 to avoid delays in the implementation of programs and projects. Also, we recommend that the BAC meticulously review the Procurement Monitoring Report before uploading it in the LGU website to provide accurate data to the public.
observations.managementAction: CY Procurement Monitoring Report was already submitted and posted in the website. However, up to now, management is still not able to create its own official LGU website.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit formal explanation on the low revenue realization of Sales Revenue - Hospital account. Also, we recommend that management promptly act on communications involving hospital operations which demand compliance of documentary requirements so as to have a smooth processing of Philhealth refunds and avoid delays or non-collection thereof.
observations.managementAction: CY Proper analysis in the declaration of hospital income targets shall be observed in order not to set very high targets during the budget year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct all department heads to observe Section 141 of NGAS Manual in taking up and recording of payable accounts, so that only valid payable accounts are recognized. It is also recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from booking up payables which still lack incomplete documentation so as to fairly present the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY Payables are booked up with proper supporting documents. This year, a GSO is designated to supervise the issue and release of supplies. Thus, this finding might be complied this CY 2020 in coordination with GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify excess funds which are due for remittance to source agencies; (b) identify excess of fund transfers which are to be requested with approval to be used for other useful programs/projects and; (c) prepare the necessary reclassification entries to properly identify those accounts which are due to LGUs and NGAs and those which are to be recorded using the account “Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share”.
observations.managementAction: CY There are NGAs and LGUs account which were utilized but the Construction in Progress account were not yet adjusted. Adjusting entries are prepared in the first quarter of 2020 to effect the utilization of these accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reclassification entries recognizing the accounts Due to Other Funds in the SEF and TF books and Due from Other Funds in the GF books, for taxes withheld in the SEF and TF but paid in the GF. We also recommend that the accounting office personnel shall be more prudent in monitoring payment of withheld taxes. Lastly, we recommend that management instruct the concerned MTO personnel to locate the document that would facilitate remittance of paid penalty to the BIR so as to avoid incurrence of additional penalties and/or surcharges.
observations.managementAction: CY Corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers were prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA Circular No.2012-001. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC observe Section 23 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 in the submission of eligibility documents of bidders, for COA review.
observations.managementAction: CY BAC Secretariat has assigned personnel to transmit the POs and contracts to COA on time for auditorial and technical review, but mostly only after the project complete set has been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management find means to facilitate the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the spirit of transparency of procurement process.
observations.managementAction: CY APP has been posted on time in the DILG website since the LGU website is set to be reactivated this year 2020. The LGU is still on process of reactivating the website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide explanation on fund transfers not yet fully implemented and liquidated as year-end. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management be more mindful of the urgency of project supervision of implementation and observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 94-013 in order to fully maximize funds received and deliver the expected benefits from the intended projects effectively and efficiently.
observations.managementAction: CY Management tried its best to accomplish the programs/projects and liquidate the same. Liquidation reports have been submitted to NGAs. Due to National Agencies (NGAs).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide reasons as to the partial or non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, management is enjoined to fast track the implementation of programs/ projects/ activities identified in the budget and ensure inclusions of the projects enumerated under RA 9003 to serve the purpose of the created funds.
observations.managementAction: CY The implementation of Solid Waste Management Program for CY 2021 is scheduled in the second quarter as per the AIP in order to give ample time to implement in case of failure in the implemented procurement process which was the main cause of this finding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC immediately furnish the Audit Team an authenticated copy of the final and approved Annual Procurement Plan and observe the provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of the Government Procurement Law or R.A.9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.2012-001 to ensure the efficient discharge of governmental functions and adoption of fiscal discipline measures.
observations.managementAction: CY APP has been posted on time in the DILG website since the LGU website is set to be reactivated this year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the provisions of Section 32 of Manual on NGAS in order to prevent misappropriation or loss of public funds.
observations.managementAction: CY We will establish a system of collection of remittance daily every 4 o’clock in the afternoon from all collectors to be captured by our system daily and to be deposited twice a week or daily depending on the amount of cash on hand involved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.
observations.managementAction: CY These machines have become operational and earning starting 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of properties and continue to identify in the other departments/offices those PPEs and inventories which are already unserviceable or non-functional and facilitate disposal thereof in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting and property records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the assets of the municipality in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY Inventory reports were prepared by the General Services Officer (GSO). Copies thereof were given to each department head for review. Management still has to create inventory committee and appraisal report. Moreover, the GSO still needs to conduct a complete count of the PPE/inventory in all the department offices, and not just merely rely on the old reports with updates for current year purchases.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish an accounting and operating system in running Masanao operations to have a system of internal control and proper administrative practices installed, to ensure compliance with laws and regulations and to provide for efficient, economical and effective operations.
observations.managementAction: CY Management is still awaiting for a meeting of the tourism council as to the organizational structure.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant conduct a review on the collectability of the dormant receivable accounts, as well the validity and complete documentation for the payable accounts, and take appropriate action pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2016-005, in the event that write-off of such accounts are warranted, in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY Management finds it hard to collect some accounts which no longer have complete documentation, thus, some accounts were not collected by the management. This matter has to be verified yet as to the entries and whether an authority has to be asked first before this payable/receivable can be written off due to its nature and the fact that this is an inherited account. These are cash shortages of the Municipal Treasurer way back late 1970’s. Management can no longer locate the heirs of the municipal treasurer who came from other municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record purchases of supplies and materials to the corresponding inventory account and compute inventory balances as prescribed under Sections 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office (a) maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; (b) furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances. Furthermore, we recommend that the Accounting Unit record issuances based on the SSMI submitted. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: CY Inventory are booked up and adjusted based on the actual inventory as of year end. Considering that there was no GSO in the prior years, nobody can supervise the issue and release of these supplies which could be the basis in recording the expense. This year, a GSO is designated to supervise the issue and release of supplies. Thus, this finding might be complied this CY 2020 in coordination with GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and not by the Municipal Assessor and make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance. Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: CY Balances of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable accounts are still inaccurate since the set-up amount was only an estimate and is not representing the actual amount receivable from all of the taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide clarification on the low revenue realization of some income accounts. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management formulate schemes and exercise measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160, relative to municipal budgetary functions.
observations.managementAction: CY Management still adopted its old strategy of estimating target incomes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the auditor. Likewise, reconciliation of accounting and property records should be done. Moreover, property custodian designate shall update and renew the PAR; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with accounting ledger card.
observations.managementAction: Conduct inventory count
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Inventory reports submitted was not in correct format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year which would be the basis of the latter to set-up the RPT/SET receivable at the beginning of the year. Further, we also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the detailed/updated list of delinquent taxpayers every year and reconcile their records with the Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Submit certified list to accounting & COA office

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer shall strictly require the daily and intact remittance of collections from the revenue collectors.

Stop the practice of issuing additional accountable forms while collections out of the previously issued forms were not yet remitted.

Impose disciplinary actions/sanctions to those collectors who repeatedly commit the same violation.

Not Implemented

During our cash examination on cash and accounts of the accountable officers still disclosed significant amounts that were not remitted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal remedies against the contractor and impose/collect the necessary liquidated damages for the failure to complete or deliver the said projects.  Management may consider the termination of the contract due to the default of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on the program/projects/activities (PPAs) requiring the highest degree of priority in terms of urgency or impact on the four thematic areas of DRRM. Likewise, ensure efficient and effective implementation of the programmed PPAs during the year to the utilization of the fund, so that desired goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be optimally achieved in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management incorporate the prior years' unexpended LDRRMF in the current year's Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan.

There still some DV & supporting documents that are not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly observe the implementation of the Ordinance and strengthen the collection to generate more income.
observations.managementAction: The MEEDMO is mandated to submit plans and programs to meet the targeted revenue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the detailed list of delinquent taxpayers and impose the legal remedies on the collection of delinquent taxes to increase/improve revenue collections.

observations.managementAction: Harmonized Gender & Development Guidelines is being used in the formulation of the 2020 GAD plan and budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide reasons as to the non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management implement programs/projects/activities on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1 in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public or to enjoy the comfort that the municipal employees of officials could have experienced therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that they are doing their best to implement identified programs/ projects/ activities. However, still, they were not able to implement all of them in CY 2018 due to time constraints and workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant   draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and not by the Municipal Assessor and make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance. Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did set-up an amount in CY 2018 per amount submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide clarification on the low revenue realization of some income accounts. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management formulate schemes and exercise measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160, relative to municipal budgetary functions.
observations.managementAction: Management was not successful in formulating schemes and measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC immediately furnish the Audit Team an authenticated copy of the final and approved Annual Procurement Plan and observe the provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of the Government Procurement Law or R.A.9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.2012-001 to ensure the efficient discharge of governmental functions and adoption of fiscal discipline measures.
observations.managementAction: Management did not furnish the Audit Team, a copy of the APP.

We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Government Equity account, especially that adjustments effected thereto are substantial in amount, for transparency and easy monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Still, no adjustments were made by the Municipal Accountant in CY 2018.
