However, for Debit and Credit Memos, she already tried to get copies thereof from the bank, but to no avail.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, adhere to the requirements in the collection, remittance, liquidation and deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction: Management still continued its practice of receiving collections from collectors despite the absence of report, thus, the incurrence of earlier dates of deposit than the date of remittance of collections by the COs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the provisions of Section 32 of Manual on NGAS in order to prevent misappropriation or loss of public funds.
observations.managementAction: Still, the LO did not immediately deposited collections intact daily or on the next banking day

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.
observations.managementAction: As of now, management still has not employed personnel to operate the machines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks amounting to ₱192,419.88 and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation; and (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payee of the availability of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale.

We recommended that the LCE (a) adhere strictly to all applicable laws, rules and regulations affecting cash advances; (b) require the Accountant to send demand letters to the above-named officials and employees whose cash advances remained unliquidated as of year-end for the proper recording of expenses in the books; and (c) comply strictly with the guidelines and principles of imprest fund system wherein regular, recurring, and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier pursuant to the regulation.

We recommended that the LCE (a) prioritize the conduct of a complete inventory and the reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; and (b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively and to strictly adhere to existing regulations on inventory process.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Accountant to work back on the previous year’s records to trace the balance of the idle funds amounting to ₱987,439.27,otherwise, request the write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; (b) require the Accounting Office to adjust the erroneous recording of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs  accounts for accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the books;  and (c) direct the concerned department/s to hasten the implementation of the projects and to submit the liquidation of the amount granted as well as to return to the concerned NGAs the unexpended balance of project funds to SA pursuant to COA Circular No. 94- 013.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Planning Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to implement and regularly monitor all  developmental projects embodied in the  local development plan within the targeted time; (b) ensure that the MPDO incorporate only eligible projects chargeable against  the fund pursuant to the regulations; and (c) direct the Municipal and Development Council to re-appropriate balances of the fund for the ensuing year to finance only those development programs, projects or activities identified under the existing regulations.

We recommended that the LCE (a) provide attention to the nine remaining barangays for their compliance relative to the establishment of their own MRF and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program through re-use, recycling, and composting activities for an effective solid waste diversion; (b) strictly implement at the household level the no segregation no collection policy in accordance with the Act; and (c) deputize Solid Waste Management Officers and require their attendance to  training and capacity building at the National Ecology Center to enforce the requirements of laws, rules and regulations concerning environment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the Fund following the guidelines under the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013
The LCE agreed to comply with the recommendation.
We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the physical inventory and monitoring of all procured supplies, materials, equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the LCE strengthen efforts with the help of the GFPS to comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the Municipal Accountant and the HRM Officer to work back on the unreconciled prior years balance; (b) the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment amounting to ₱10,146.89; and (c) the HRM Officer to immediately notify the Accounting Office of any modification in the payroll to adjust recording in the books.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to work back on the unreconciled prior years balance and to strictly observe the full remittance of the amount due to PhilHealth.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to work back on the unreconciled prior year’s balance of ₱137,936.90.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit; and (b) require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory.
We recommend management to assigned personnel specifically to reconcile the balances in the books and that the balances in the bank with Account Account:0522-1114-30.

We recommend that the Accountant be directed to make the necessary adjustments in the books, the source of fund cannot and will not be a factor in the classification of accounts, and proper coordination of the Accounting Office and Treasury to avoid recurrence of similar error in the future.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and designated Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and look into the matter in order to determine the causes of the delay.  He should direct the prompt submission of the required reports in compliance with their duties and responsibilities and as mandated by the prescribed rules and regulations.  Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of their salaries, until they shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).

We recommend that management strictly observe the rules and regulations on the utilization of SEF, as prescribed per DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and to make necessary corrections on the above noted deficiencies.
We recommend that the LGU will investigate the matter, make the necessary adjustments in the book, and liquidate the funds received to the DOST.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommend that Management prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the BAC prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required under Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies in compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency on government procurement.
Further, we suggest that COA be furnished with a printed copy of the said monitoring report as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Exert extra efforts in obtaining evidences from prior years’ financial records to validate the Receivables; and
Otherwise, require the Municipal Accountant to initiate request for write-off of the dormant accounts in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular 97-001 dated February 5, 2015.  The request shall be supported with documents required under the unnumbered memorandum dated July 21, 2014 of the Director IV, COA Regional Office No. V, Legaspi City.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to conduct reconciliation of balances of Liability accounts against relevant records and effect proper adjustments for deficiencies noted and errors uncovered to reflect the correct account balances.
For those accounts under Due to NGAs, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and identify the source agencies.  If projects were already completed, return the identified account balances to the source NGAs; and
if source cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct reconciliation of trust accounts with the journals and ledgers. Afterwards, make representation with the concerned government agencies with regards to the discrepancies noted in the books of the LGU, and adjust accordingly;
Remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations to the concerned government agencies; and
Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of balances.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference in their books, and thereafter make the appropriate adjustments and reclassification of accounts.  Ascertain regular reconciliation of records are made by the concerned officers in order to have the same correct balances in their respective records in consonance with Section 181(c) of GAAM, Volume I.
We also recommended that Management initiate a claim proceeding against the estate of the subject Municipal Treasurers, if the circumstances warrant.  In addition, the Municipal Accountant account for unreconciled cash amount of ₱129,772 based on available records.  Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant initiate the process of requesting for write-off of the dormant cash accountabilities in view of the death of the accountable persons and unavailability of records considering the prolonged period of dormancy in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.  Also, include in the Notes to FS the actual status of long outstanding cash accountabilities and disclose that the collectability of the receivables was uncertain because of the death of the accountable officers and unavailability of records.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Strictly enforce the full settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018;
Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and
Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer explain why the transfers of appropriations were not covered by an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget.
We also recommended that Management ensure that future transfer of funds which are considered as realignment be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.

We recommended that Management submit program of works, municipal resolutions, Local Development Council resolutions and the revised Annual Investment Plan to analyze further the appropriated projects vis-à-vis actual expenditures.  We also recommended that for lump sum appropriation, authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained by way of appropriate ordinance.

We recommended that Management appropriate separate funds intended for the implementation of SWM programs, projects and activities as required under Section 50 of RA No. 9003.
We also recommended deferment of future disbursements charged to 20% EDF for SWM programs/projects/activities incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the LCE require the RCOs to strictly remit daily and intact all collections received by them to the Municipal Treasurer in compliance with Section 29 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Section 26 of COA Circular 92-382 and Section 69 of PD 1445.  In turn, the MT shall deposit with the proper government depository the full amount of the collections not later than the following banking day.  Also, we enjoined that the MT monitor regularly the accountable forms in the possession of the RCOs to determine official receipts already issued and account for correct and actual balances and demand timely remittance of accurate collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% EDF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.
We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% EDF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are classified as developmental projects pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.  Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% EDF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.
We recommended that the LCE require the Local School Board to strictly follow the approved budget for SEF in consonance with Section 84(1) of PD No. 1445 to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation for all depreciable assets and prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to record the corresponding expense in the books.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Accounting Office all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls and official receipts. Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that Management comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010 on the prompt submission of contracts, purchase order and notices of deliveries.  Also, we recommended that concerned officials and/or employees submit written justification on their failure to comply with the requirements imposed by the Circulars.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator provide the Auditor with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year.  Also, the aforementioned officials submit written notice to Auditors on compliance with posting requirement of government PPAs.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of projects as provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the implementation of the programs/projects of the 20% EDF.  However, we recommended that Management should strive for total implementation of all projects embodied in the 20% Development Plan.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Recommendations


  We strongly recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  all  officials
  concerned to:


  1. Create a new set of inventory committee considering  that  there  were
    members who already had retired from service in the existing  inventory
    committee under EO No. 2 series of 2010.


  2. Require the committee to conduct physical count of all  the  property,
    plant and  equipment  owned  by  the  municipality  to  facilitate  the
    completion of inventory of PPE and prepare the Report on  the  Physical
    Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.


  3. Prepare and maintain the prescribed PPELC which should  be  reconciled
    with the RPCPPE to  establish  the  correctness  of  the  PPE  accounts
    amounting to P158,897,313.40.


  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-03 dated February 23, 2019.

Recommendations


 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:


 1. Effect prior years adjustment equivalent to the amount that should have
   been provided from the date of its acquisition/construction;


 2. Provide allowance for depreciation on depreciable assets regularly; and


 3. Record immediately the depreciation expense in the books of accounts  in
   order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant and Equipment in
   the financial statements.


 We also recommended that the following  adjusting  entry  be  made  in  the
 recording of depreciation:


                       Depreciation Expense- PPE account          Pxxx
                       Prior Years Adjustment                      xxx
                             Accumulated Depreciation- PPE Account  Pxxx


 The aforementioned audit observation was communicated  to  management  thru
 Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-08 dated February 22, 2019.

Recommendation


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
   to prepare and submit the bank  reconciliation  statements  for  calendar
   years  2011  to  2016  and  2018  and  to  record  immediately  any  book
   reconciling items that require adjustments and corrections in the general
   ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 3.3 and 3.4  of
   COA Circular No. 96-011 to prove the correctness  and  existence  of  the
   cash in bank balances as of year-end.


  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-01 dated January 21, 2019.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


  1. Strictly enforce the settlement of unliquidated  cash  advances  as  of
     November 30, 2018 and thereafter;


  2. Stop granting additional cash advance to officers and employees  unless
     the previous one had been fully liquidated to avoid accumulation of the
     same;


  3. Initiate appropriate actions against erring officials and employees, if
     warranted;


  4. Require the Municipal Accountant  to  send  written  demand  letter  to
     officers/employees with accountability that is no longer connected with
     the municipality.

  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-02 dated January 21, 2019.

Recommendations

  We strongly recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require/instruct  the
  Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to  submit  immediately  the
  disbursement vouchers for post-audit.

  We likewise recommended that internal control be strengthened by ensuring
  that the municipality strictly comply with the  afore-cited  implementing
  laws, rules and regulations.


  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-07 dated January 21, 2019.

Recommendation


  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
  and Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount of P697,132.23 to
  the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

  Henceforth, taxes withheld from payments to  suppliers  and  salaries  of
  employees be remitted promptly  and  regularly  in  accordance  with  the
  provisions of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005.


  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-04 dated February 22, 2019.

Recommendations


  We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  LDRRM  Officer  -
  Designate follow the four (4) thematic areas reflected  in  the  National
  DRRM Plan in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP of the municipality so  that
  disaster risk will be adequately addressed.

  We also recommended that the  LDRRM  Officer-Designate  include  the  two
  thematic areas namely Disaster Response and Disaster  Rehabilitation  and
  Recovery in the programs/projects/activities stated in  the  LDRRMFIP  in
  accordance with the National DRRM Plan.

  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-05 dated February 22, 2019.

Recommendations


  We recommended that Municipal Officials must work closely with the MDRRMO
  to  ensure  effective   formulation,   implementation,   monitoring   and
  evaluation of the Local DRRM Plan and the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan.


  We also recommended that the  MDRRMO  include  in  their  plan  projects,
  programs  and  activities  relative  to  the  above  mentioned  standards
  especially  those  on  not  yet  conducted,   available,   existing   and
  operational in the municipality to ensure disaster preparedness.

  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-05 dated February 22, 2019.

Recommendation


  We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prepare  a
  budget proposal for the siting of sanitary landfill and see  to  it  that
  PPAs relative thereto be provided with  a  budget  and  included  in  the
  succeeding years annual budget for proper implementation.


  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2019-06 dated February 22, 2019.

Recommendation


    We strongly recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  MCPC  to
    enforce the formulation of Councils Annual  Work  and  Financial  Plan;
    Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC) and the Local Investment Plan
    for Children (LIPC) to ensure that PPAs relative  thereto  are  properly
    funded and implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer
      to deposit on time all collections  to the authorized  depository  bank
      and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445  and
      Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require concerned accountable
      officers to immediately settle all cash  advances after  the  purpose
      for which they were granted had served pursuant to Section 89 of PD
      No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise impose proper
      sanction such as the withholding of salaries of those who are still
      in service for failing to comply with the Circular.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
      Accountant to cause the transfer of unexpended balance to the special
      trust fund - MDRRMF immediately to facilitate funding of PPAs
      relative thereto.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
      Accountant and officials concerned to ensure that financial
      assistance extended to the barangays, Schools and individuals
      participating in the socio-cultural/socio-civic activities be
      supported by the above-mentioned documentary requirements to provide
      basis for monitoring the proper use of funds.

We also recommended that intended financial assistance for private
      persons/employees be granted as a Cash Advance to a Disbursing Officer
      so that disbursements can be monitored.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other local officials
      concerned to:

     1. Fully utilize and  accomplish  timely  implementation  of  projects
        funded from LGSF in the year it was granted to achieve the  optimum
        benefits and purpose for which the fund was created.
      2.    Adequately plan so as to eliminate possible problems  that  will
        potentially delay projects implementation.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the utilization of
      the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various
      projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the
      attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that
      optimum  benefits  derived  therefrom   can   be   availed   by   the
      constituents.

We also recommended that the concerned municipal officials enhance
      the monitoring and evaluation of the status and implementation of the
      20% development fund in pursuance to Municipals mandate and faithful
      compliance of pertinent laws, rules and regulations.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the
      MSWMB, the Municipal Treasurer and the MENRO to strictly impose and
      collect garbage fees not only to business establishments but also to
      every household and fines/penalties be imposed to violators of the
      above mentioned ordinances.


We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the members
      of the MSWMB to prepare SWM plan to facilitate assessment and
      monitoring.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a new set of the
      Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to perform mandated functions
      in accordance with laws, rules and regulations relative thereto.

We also recommended that the  Sanggunian  Bayan  of  the  Municipality
      strongly implement the segregation of garbage at source and see to it
      that PPAs in the Solid Waste Management Plan are included in the AIP.


We strongly recommended that the   management   observe   properly
      procurement process in accordance with RA 9184.

      Likewise, we  recommended  that  the  municipality  shall  draw  check
      payments directly in the name  of  the  supplier  in  conformity  with
      existing regulations  and  withheld  the  appropriate  taxes  due  the
      government.


We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry
      Voucher (JEV) in all financial transactions of  the  Municipality  and
      attach the same to disbursement  vouchers  and  journals  pursuant  to
      Section  31  of  the  New  Government  Accounting  System  for   Local
      Government Units, Volume II.

We  recommended  that  the  General  Services  Officer-Designate  prepares an
      Inventory Report on excess materials  taken  from  various  projects
      and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) or  Inventory
      Custodian Slip, if applicable.


We strongly recommended that the MSWMB strictly enforce the  No
      segregation, No collection Policy as mandated by  Environmental  Code
      of the municipality.

      Likewise, provide containers for each type of waste from all  sources.
      The solid waste container depending  on  its  use  shall  be  properly
      marked or identified for on-site collection  as  compostable,  non-
      recyclable,  recyclable  or   special   waste,   or   any   other
      classification as may be determined by  the  Board  in  accordance  to
      laws,  rules  and  regulations  required  under  RA   9003   and   the
      Environmental Code of the Municipality of San Miguel.

We recommended that unexpended LDRRMF amounting to P3,552,235.81  as  of
    December 31, 2014 be transferred to a special  trust  fund  pursuant  to
    Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of COA
    Circular No. 2012-002.


We recommended that Municipal Officials responsible for the  processing,
    approval and payment of the disbursements  be  required  to  refund  the
    amount totaling P551,250.00.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipality  of  San  Miguel  through   the
    Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to  occupy  the  position  of
    LDRRM Officer and three  (3)  Technical  Staff  following  the  position
    titles, qualification standards and salary grades set by Joint  Circular
    No.  2014-1  to  be  able  to  properly  perform  their  functions   and
    responsibilities as LDRRM Officer/Technical Staff.

We recommended the refund of unnecessary  and  extravagant  expenditures
    totaling P558,250.00 incurred  by  Municipal  Officials,  Employees  and
    Punong Barangays.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and  OIC  
    Municipal Accountant strictly adhere  to  the  above-cited  implementing
    laws, rules and regulations.


We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor,  Municipal  Treasurer  and
    OIC  Municipal Accountant responsible for the processing, approval  and
    payment of disbursements refund the total amount of P14,910.00 spent for
    religious purposes such as Patronal Town  Fiesta,  Barangay  Fiesta  and
    Christmas Basketball Tournament.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management  Board make  a
    representation to the Legislative body of the LGU to ratify a  Municipal
    Ordinance pertaining to the collection garbage fee.

We  likewise  recommenedd  that  the   Sangguniang   Bayan   enact   the
    corresponding Municipal Ordinance imposing fees for the burial tombs.

We recommended that the Municipal  Engineer  and  MPDC  submit  to  this
    Office at the beginning of the year a list of  all  on-going  government
    programs/projects/activities (P/P/A) of the Municipality and those
    that are to be implemented during the year.

We recommended  that  management  cause  the  refund  of  the  financial
    assistance it granted to the 66 Job Order employees in the total  amount
    of P334,000.00 for lack of legal basis.


We recommended that the total amount of P106,000.00 be refunded by  the
    Disbursing Officer, duly receipted by the Municipal Treasurer  and  the
    corresponding cash deposited to the account of the Municipality.

We finally recommended  that  the  former  Municipal  Mayor,  Municipal
    Agriculturist, Municipal Planning  and  Development  Officer,  and  the
    Municipal Social and Development Officer, as project  implementors,  to
    submit a monitoring report on the Livelihood Programs implemented.

We recommended the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash  advances.
    The OIC    Municipal  Accountant  should  send  letter  of  demand  to
    officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the driver be required  to  properly  accomplish  a
    Drivers Trip Ticket before using a government vehicle and a summary of
    which shall be made at the end of the month  in  a  monthly  Report  of
    Official  Travels  for  audit  purposes.   Monthly   Report   of   Fuel
    Consumption shall likewise be submitted to the Auditor for verification
    purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel  consumed  during  the
    period.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and PPE of the LGU. Likewise, we recommend that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommend that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Account to draw the necessary journal entries to record the transactions which have long been reflected in the BRS as reconciling items in order to fairly present the Cash in Bank and the related income and expense accounts, as well as PYA accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Assessor to make representation with the concerned owners/relatives/representatives of the owners of the private lots, in order to process documents requiring their signatures and eventually secure Certificates of Title over these lots. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Assessor immediately claim the Certificates of Title and furnish the Municipal Accountant copies thereof for proper recording in the LGU books. Finally, we recommend that management ensure first that necessary locational clearance and other requirements imposed under existing laws, rules and regulations can be smoothly processed before deciding to purchase a certain lot so as to avoid legal disputes over the lot ownership and guarantee eventual issuance of Certificate of Land Title in the name of the LGU.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all concerned officers and employees, requiring immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, we recommend the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unexpended CY 2013 DRRMF to the General Fund so as to make this available for other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian.

We recommend that the management initiate the conduct of an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.
Consequently, we recommend that demand letters be sent to those debtors whose accounts are still collectible, such as those employed by the LGU. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that the management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability. Finally, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant apply Section 37 of P.D. 1445 to municipal employees with long overdue accounts.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality, by immediately closing the account Due from LGUs in the year the funds are already expended and liquidated by the recipient barangays. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, otherwise, additional fund transfers shall not be released.

We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for: (1) debtors who are still alive and within the locality and (2) debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write-off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability thereof.

We recommend that management instruct the MAO to gather data on the whereabouts of the animals comprising the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and verify existence thereof thru conduct of physical inventory. Result of physical count shall be incorporated in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the audit team. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant be also furnished with the same report in order to reconcile with the accounting records.

We recommend that management stop the grant of additional cash advances for catering expenses to any LGU employee unless the previous cash advance has already been liquidated. Moreover, it is recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately send demand letters to those LGU employees who still have unliquidated cash advances for catering expenses to avoid withholding thereof from their respective salaries pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
Likewise, it is recommended that the Masanao Manager ensure the inclusion of storable food supplies in the Annual Procurement Plan to avoid haste in the procurement process. Lastly, cash advances specifically for catering expenses of Masanao shall be granted only to the Masanao Manager, only for those emergency and perishable food supplies, in order to properly monitor the timely liquidation and avoid overlapping of cash advances.

We recommend that henceforth, management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee, henceforth, observe the timeliness set forth in Annex C of the R-IRR of R.A.9184 to avoid delays in the implementation of programs and projects. Also, we recommend that the BAC meticulously review the Procurement Monitoring Report before uploading it in the LGU website to provide accurate data to the public.

We recommend that management implement the imposition of surcharges/penalties on unpaid water bills pursuant to Section 31 of Municipal Ordinance No.C-116 Series of 2013, in the absence of any ordinance ordering otherwise, so as to generate additional income to the municipal funds.

We recommend that management formulate new pricing/costing schemes to come up with more relevant rates and fees for Masanao Hilltop Resort and Restaurant to ensure that goods and services are offered to the public at competitive prices without sacrificing the target operating income that can be derived therefrom.

We recommend that management submit formal explanation on the low revenue realization of Sales Revenue - Hospital account. Also, we recommend that management promptly act on communications involving hospital operations which demand compliance of documentary requirements so as to have a smooth processing of Philhealth refunds and avoid delays or non-collection thereof.

We recommend that management instruct all department heads to observe Section 141 of NGAS Manual in taking up and recording of payable accounts, so that only valid payable accounts are recognized. It is also recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from booking up payables which still lack incomplete documentation so as to fairly present the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements.
We recommend that management be more mindful of the timeliness in project implementation, so as to deliver its intended purpose and benefits to the public the soonest possible time.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify excess funds which are due for remittance to source agencies; (b) identify excess of fund transfers which are to be requested with approval to be used for other useful programs/projects and; (c) prepare the necessary reclassification entries to properly identify those accounts which are due to LGUs and NGAs and those which are to be recorded using the account “Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share”.

We recommend that management submit a justification as to the failure to obtain prior approval or authorization by the sanggunian before incurring expenses or entering into a contract. Henceforth, we recommend that management obtain and attach approval or authorization by the sanggunian.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to drop the amount representing SMSHIP premium collections from the account Due to Philhealth so as to facilitate utilization of this fund for other meaningful programs/projects.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw reclassification entries for transactions which were improperly recorded in the books to attain fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reclassification entries recognizing the accounts Due to Other Funds in the SEF and TF books and Due from Other Funds in the GF books, for taxes withheld in the SEF and TF but paid in the GF. We also recommend that the accounting office personnel shall be more prudent in monitoring payment of withheld taxes. Lastly, we recommend that management instruct the concerned MTO personnel to locate the document that would facilitate remittance of paid penalty to the BIR so as to avoid incurrence of additional penalties and/or surcharges.

We recommend that the management prioritize the creation of the inventory committee, prepare the RPCPPE and to reconcile with the accounting records.
We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the auditor. Likewise, reconciliation of accounting and property records should be done. Moreover, property custodian designate shall update and renew the PAR; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with accounting ledger card.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year which would be the basis of the latter to set-up the RPT/SET receivable at the beginning of the year. Further, we also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the detailed/updated list of delinquent taxpayers every year and reconcile their records with the Accountant.

We recommend that:
The Municipal Treasurer shall strictly require the daily and intact remittance of collections from the revenue collectors.
Stop the practice of issuing additional accountable forms while collections out of the previously issued forms were not yet remitted.
Impose disciplinary actions/sanctions to those collectors who repeatedly commit the same violation.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer ensures that collection made during the month/year be reported and submitted to the accounting unit in the same month/year to avoid understatement of collection and income.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all legal remedies against the contractor and impose/collect the necessary liquidated damages for the failure to complete or deliver the said projects.  Management may consider the termination of the contract due to the default of the contractor.

We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on the program/projects/activities (PPAs) requiring the highest degree of priority in terms of urgency or impact on the four thematic areas of DRRM. Likewise, ensure efficient and effective implementation of the programmed PPAs during the year to the utilization of the fund, so that desired goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be optimally achieved in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We recommended that the Management incorporate the prior years' unexpended LDRRMF in the current year's Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of all paid DVs with original supporting documents to the Auditor's Office and to follow the regulations of the submission of accounts strictly.
We reiterated our recommendation that Management ensure the completeness and propriety of the required documents for all contracts submitted to COA for review under COA Circular No. 2009-001.
We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the required documents to effect the necessary Technical evaluation and inspection of the completed infrastructure projects.
We recommended that the Management strictly observe the implementation of the Ordinance and strengthen the collection to generate more income.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the detailed list of delinquent taxpayers and impose the legal remedies on the collection of delinquent taxes to increase/improve revenue collections.

We recommended that the Management through its GFPS administer the gender analysis using the HGDG on its GAD PPAs during the preparation of the GPB as well as on the GAD AR to determine the actual expenditures that can be attributed to GAD budget pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JM No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require: a) the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to record the reversion of stale checks totaling ₱44,211.40 in the Cash in Bank and the related Payable and Government Equity accounts; and b) the   Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to update the list of Outstanding Checks on a monthly basis to clear the Bank Reconciliation of   stale checks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted/reversed the stale checks as discussed in the observation.
observations.reason:
Although the noted deficiencies during prior years observation were adjusted, we still noted various and additional reconciling items for CY 2018 that were unadjusted in the books.

Similar deficiency is discussed in Observation and Recommendation No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to a) issue demand letters for the unliquidated cash advances which are long   overdue; and b) stop the granting   of additional cash advances to the accountable officers which still have unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters to those accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances.

Strict implementation of granting cash advances pursuant to the regulation was not yet observed.

Reiterated in Observation and Recommendation No. 2, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) require the concerned implementing officials to closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the projects to ensure their effective and efficient completion within the target duration; b) conduct detailed engineering of projects in order to avoid realignment of funds involving change in type of projects /transfers of project locations.

observations.reason: Other deficiencies noted were discussed in Observation and Recommendation No. 6, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; and (b) refrain from accommodating payment of expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  (a) the GAD Focal Person to  carefully identify and  include only PPAs that are gender responsive  in the GAD Plan and Budget to address the  issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men stakeholders, clients and constituents; (b) the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant  ensure that only expenditures which correspond to the identified gender issues and concerns in the GAD  Plan  are charged against the  GAD Budget and actually spent; and  monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs to prepare the accomplishment report  in the prescribed format.
observations.reason: Other deficiencies noted were discussed in Observation and Recommendation No. 9, Part II of this report.
