We recommend to Management that after approval of the SEF budget by majority of its members, it will pass to the legislative body for authorization thru appropriation ordinance, since the SEF cannot do away with the fundamental principle of disbursements that no money shall be paid out of the local treasury except in pursuance of an appropriations ordinance or law

We recommend that management stop charging expenses against the SEF and strictly observe the mandate of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1.  The Accountant and OIC Treasurer are required to see to it that only those expenses which fall under the allowed expenditures should be processed and paid.  Further, the amount given is to be restored

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and designated Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and look into the matter in order to determine the causes of the delay.  He should direct the prompt submission of the required reports in compliance with their duties and responsibilities and as mandated by the prescribed rules and regulations.  Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of their salaries, until they shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that LDRRM Officer to prepare annual LDRRMFIP (using the Annex “A” format) with details as to particulars, projects, and activities to be funded and implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Sometimes the LDRRMFIP was not updated due to multiple task assigned to the concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officials/offices see to it that disbursements vouchers are supported with complete documentation as required in existing regulations.
observations.reason: There are times that the payee would promise to comply with the lacking documents upon return to the office, but failed to do so.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to issue the notice to liquidate to the concerned AOs of all the unliquidated cash advances and enforce the suspension of salary of the concerned active AO until the compliance thereof. In the event that the AO is already separated from the LGU, the notice be send to the last known address of the concerned AO. Lastly, for AOs who were already deceased, prepare the list of the deceased AOs with the corresponding Death Certificate for a request to COA for write-off of the accounts.
observations.reason: Management already sent demand letters to the concerned individuals but some of them are already separated from the service, thus, unliquidated cash advances remain unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU to come up with the necessary plans as enumerated above, and employ a full time Local DRRM Officer then subject itself for reevaluation for further improvements, also to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the disbursement made
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management: 

(a) require the Revenue Collectors to liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible misuse and losses and (b) require the Collectors to attach the original copy of accountable forms which have been cancelled to the stub and (c) require the AOs to liquidate their collections before issuance of another accountable forms.
observations.reason: The AOs were accustom to have it reported upon exhaustion of a given stub.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that: 

The Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures;

 Concerned officials with government vehicles and the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit regularly thru the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets; and

 The Municipal Accountant be required to look into the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the DVs. Claims with incomplete supporting documents must be returned to the claimant before payment be made.
observations.reason: There was misinformation in the instruction, it was only submitted to the Accounting as supporting documents for disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to update his cashbooks and record immediately all transactions for the day in consonance with COA Circular No. 2002-003.
observations.reason: Due to other functions assigned to the ICO-MT, the balances in the cash book were not keep updated.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor and verify the issuance of the accountable forms  issued to the accountable officers and check the number of issuances and number of the remaining balances of the accountable forms and fill-up properly Sections C and D and the VERIFICATION AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT column of the RCD by indicating the amount in words and in figures received by him for easy monitoring of the movement of the accountable forms and the remittances of collections;

 The accountable officers, upon the conduct of cash examination of all his cash and accounts shall render an accounting thereof.

We recommend to the collectors/accountable officers to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer who in turn shall consolidate the reports in the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.reason: Management failed to continue the submission of the report.

observations.recommendation:
Officials/employees of the NGAs  shall submit a certification that the official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned one and certificate or evidence of service rendered or approved DTR; and

OIC-Municipal Accountant shall strengthen control on the disbursement of funds. Require the submission of the documents mentioned on COA Circular 2012-001 for the grant of RATA to NGAs before it shall be forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for payment.

An inventory committee should be created to conduct the annual physical inventory of all assets under PPE to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.  In the preparation of the report, the prescribed format should be used and that the different columns should be properly filled-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for.  Assignment of property number should be properly done so that each of the items can be easily identified and properly classified. On the other hand, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards; Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card and Real Property Ledger Cards.  Once the physical inventory of PPE is already available it shall be reconciled with the (a)  PPE ledger card, (b)  the control accounts and (c)  property cards.  If any discrepancies exist, it should be immediately verified and adjusted.  Further, reclassification of obsolete and unserviceable assets, as well as assets no longer used by LGU-San Miguel to “Other Assets” account should be made.
observations.reason: Management conducted actual physical inventory taking of all its assets but the report was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
2.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Joint Circular No. 1-90 of DOF and DBM.

2.2  Assign or hire additional staff to augment the personnel of the accounting office who shall handle the preparation of bank reconciliation statements.

2.3  We also recommended that deficiencies noted and   errors uncovered in the reconciliation process be immediately adjusted and corrected.

We recommended the following courses of action to the Management:

Strictly enforce compliance to Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 governing the grant and liquidation of cash advances.  Likewise, ensure not to approve or allow additional cash advances to LGU officers and employees as well as other officials of National Government Agencies who are allowed to charge cash advances to the LGU fund with outstanding cash advances.
 
Demand for the immediate liquidation from those who have already transferred, retired or separated from the government service,  and to file request for write off for the unliquidated cash advances of those employees who are already deceased.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant confirmed that the balance of cash advances were substantially settled as of March 29, 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a. 	The Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual and monitor the progress of the inventory taking. 

b. 	Thereafter, the Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts. 

c. 	Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.
observations.managementAction: The LCE assured the audit team that he will enjoin all the concerned officials to comply with the recommendations
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% EDF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject Joint Circular pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The LCE assured the audit team he will enjoin all the concerned officials to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the submission of Results of Test Analysis or a Status of Quality Control Test for the construction materials used particularly G.I. sheets, reinforcing steel bars and concrete blocks otherwise related transactions may be suspended in audit.
Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to include in the project inspection test of materials used in the infrastructure projects to ensure project quality.
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant not to pass in pre-audit or process transactions particularly during final payments Disbursement Vouchers on infrastructure projects which are not supported with Results of Test Analysis from DPWH-BRS/S\DOST/DTI accredited testing laboratories for construction materials, in order to ensure the quality of the construction materials used for the safety of the general public.
observations.managementAction: The LCE assured the audit team he will enjoin all the concerned officials to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the procuring entity ensure that the project for the construction/upgrading of the RHU building will be completed in accordance with the approved plans and specifications the soonest possible time so that the constituents can avail of the services it will provide. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer should intensify project monitoring on the project site. Contractors incurring negative slippage should be given warning and be required to submit detailed schedule of work undertaken to mitigate slippage. The procuring entity should require the payment of liquidated damages for the delay in the contract completion.
observations.managementAction: The LCE assured the audit team he will enjoin all the concerned officials to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC strictly comply with the procedures for adopting shopping mode of procurement as provided under Section 52.1(b) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 wherein the entire process of procurement will not be completed within one (1) or two (days).

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the conduct of an in-house meeting/briefing on the correct procedures of shopping mode of procurement with the BAC members and the Heads of Offices to correct the erroneous practice.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned LGU Officials effect the full payment of terminal leave benefits to Mr. Ricardo I. Lumbao.

We recommended also compliance with Section 5 of IRR, RA No. 10154 which provides for the timely and expeditious release of retirement benefits to all retiring government employees.

We recommended that all transactions pertaining to procurement be evaluated by the Bids and Awards Committee, and a corresponding resolution thereto be prepared and attached to the corresponding disbursement voucher in consonance with the provision of Section 12 of RA No. 9184.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the heads of offices to refrain from adopting the reimbursement scheme and observe/follow the procurement plans of their offices or the Annual Procurement Plan of the Agency.


Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process disbursement vouchers relative to procurement transactions without the necessary documents and BAC resolutions duly approved by the HOPE so as to ensure that all transactions have undergone BAC evaluation.

We recommended that the Local School Board ensure that the programs/projects to be funded by the SEF are allowable expenditures under the DEPED-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, S2017, and will support the supplementary budgetary needs of public schools.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from approving disbursement vouchers for the purchase of fuel, unless the required documentary requirements are submitted.  Also, require the drivers to accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets and the GSO/Officer-in-Charge to prepare the Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption in compliance of the above regulation.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of Section 84 of PD No. 1445 and Section 155(a) of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I, which emphasized that current year’s budget is available only for current expenditures.

Likewise, to avoid excess expenditures, agency officials should be guided by the principle of “availability of funds” which provides that no money shall be disbursed unless there is still an existing appropriation that has been legally made and there is an available fund for the purpose.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to provide clarification on the non-liquidation of social pension for Senior Citizens as of year-end despite the one-month period within which the funds were delivered directly to the respective barangays of concerned senior citizens, contrary to COA Circular No.94-013 on the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of funds transferred to implementing agencies.
observations.managementAction: An explanation was already submitted by the Municipal Treasurer addressing the matter at hand. The non-liquidation was due to the failure of beneficiaries to claim the benefit since this was during Christmas vacation. In 2018, release of benefits was strategized to avoid the same incident.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairperson, instruct the BAC Secretariat to observe Section 37.1.6 of the 2016R-IRR of R.A. No.9184 in its posting requirement of the Notice of Award, within three (3) calendar days, in the PhilGEPS, the website of the municipality and any conspicuous place in its premises, in the spirit of transparency of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management has already observed posting of Notices of Award in the PhilGEPS, the website of the municipality and any conspicuous place in its premises, within three (3) calendar days from its issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the recorded account balances per fund and review the recorded transactions for possible erroneous recording in order to make the necessary adjustments, if warranted, and furnish the Audit Team copies of the adjusting entries and supporting documents therefor. It is recommended that henceforth, management ensure compliance with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on withholding of taxes and timely remittance of the same to avoid incurrence of penalties/surcharges and fines. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant review the recorded transactions for possible erroneous recording.
observations.managementAction: Management already complies with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on withholding of taxes and timely remittance of the same to avoid incurrence of penalties/surcharges and fines. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant already tries her best to review the recorded transactions to avoid erroneous recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the remittance to concerned barangays of their shares of real property taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to the provisions of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 to avoid unnecessary accumulation of funds in the municipal treasury and to enable the concerned barangays to timely implement beneficial barangay projects through the use of such funds.
observations.managementAction: Management already remitted to concerned barangays, their shares of real property taxes, on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management expedite the purchase and installation of a safety vault for the MTO in order to properly safeguard undeposited collections from risk of loss of theft.
observations.managementAction: Management already acquired a safety vault for the MTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer evaluate the necessity of maintaining funds with FCB. If found justified, we recommend that management adhere to the guidelines set forth in DOF Circular No.001-2015 to uphold its purposes, in order to prevent sanctions that may be imposed by DOF.
observations.managementAction: The LGU complied with the provision of Department of Finance (DOF) Circular No. 001-2015 on the required P4.5M average daily balance for 4th class municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the unreconciled difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to make an immediate review and tracing of differences between the two accounts once financial statements are generated to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to trace the reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor reconcile their records to determine the cause of the noted discrepancy, in order to fairly present the Land account in the financial statements and submit the documents to the Audit Team, for review.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor already conducted a reconciliation of their reports. They found the difference to have been caused by the recognition of the accounting office of the taxes paid in line with the purchase of such land as addition to the cost of the land, which the Municipal Assessor, does not record in her reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the necessary documents to prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the social pension. Likewise, it is recommended that management explain the payment of social pension even though the period to which it pertained had not yet lapsed.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted the necessary documents to prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the social pension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation as to the delay of the supplier in posting the Performance Security. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the BAC observe the posting requirements of Performance Security pursuant to Section 39 of RA 9184 in order to guarantee the faithful performance by the winning bidder of its obligations under the contract in accordance with its Bidding Documents.
observations.managementAction: Management has already observed posting requirements of Performance Security pursuant to Section 39 of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee abide by the provisions of R.A.9184 by requiring the hospital management to prepare ahead of time, a comprehensive list of medicines and medical supplies to be used for hospital operations for its inclusion in the Annual Procurement Plan, in order to avoid the repetitive use of Shopping as the mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Management already procures its medicines and medical supplies thru public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit an explanation as to the excessive grant of PEI in excess of the allowed amount of P5,000.00 per employee. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management comply with the requirements of DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3 dated December 2, 2014 in the grant of Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) to LGU officials and employees particularly on the observance of the 55% Personal Services cap/limitation in the LGU’s Annual and Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 325 (a) of R. A. 7160 in order to avoid the payment of personnel benefits not in accordance with the provisions of the Local Government Code and DBM guidelines which will result to disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management has already complied with the 55% Personal Services cap/limitation in the LGU’s Annual and Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 325 (a) of R. A. 7160 in order to avoid the payment of personnel benefits not in accordance with the provisions of the Local Government Code and DBM guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry to write-off the corresponding amount which have been dormant for years in consonance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and Section 04 (f), Chapter 2 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, Volume I, in order to fairly present the payable accounts in the financial statements. In the event of claims by the concerned electricians in the future, we recommend that the latter present valid documents to prove first rightful claim for actual services rendered, before the Municipal Accountant restores the payable account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already drew a JEV to reclassify the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA Circular No.2012-001. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC observe Section 23 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 in the submission of eligibility documents of bidders, for COA review.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders, for auditorial and technical review, but only after the project has been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to properly and completely identify those obligations which are considered undocumented and confirmed non-existent regardless of the year they were incurred, as well as all payables which have remained outstanding for more than two (2) years, be reverted to the Surplus Account, pursuant to Executive Order No. 109, and Sections 141 and 157 of GAAM, in order to clear the books of these non-existent obligations and fairly present the actual current liabilities in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant tried to revert some accounts while others are still not due to lack of supporting documents to do so.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management always ensure the availability of funds in order to sustain sufficiency of computer units installed with e-TRACS system. Likewise, familiarization of e-TRACS by the concerned collecting officers is encouraged to facilitate timely preparation of Report of Collections and Deposits, pursuant to Section 29, Chapter 3 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Moreover, it is recommended that San Miguel Municipal Hospital be provided with a computer unit installed with e-TRACS and coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) as to its viability and sustainability to reduce the volume of ORs which need to be captured manually into the computer system.
observations.managementAction: Management has acquired new computer units for installation of e-TRACS. However, the MTO still believes that they lack the personnel to handle the e-TRACS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management find means to facilitate the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the spirit of transparency of procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The APP and PMR were already submitted and posted in the website. However, up to now, management is still not able to create its own official LGU website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide explanation on fund transfers not yet fully implemented and liquidated as year-end. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management be more mindful of the urgency of project implementation and observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 94-013 in order to fully maximize funds received and deliver the expected benefits from the intended projects effectively and efficiently.
observations.managementAction: Management tried its best to accomplish programs/projects and liquidate the same. Liquidation reports have been submitted to NGAs. Constraints in the implementation were due to the fact that the municipal engineering office lacks personnel to assist him in the supervision of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of properties and continue to identify in the other departments/offices those PPEs and inventories which are already unserviceable or non-functional and facilitate disposal thereof in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting and property records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the assets of the municipality in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Inventory reports were allegedly prepared by the General Services Officer (GSO). Copies thereof were given to each department head for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish an accounting and operating system in running Masanao operations to have a system of internal control and proper administrative practices installed, to ensure compliance with laws and regulations and to provide for efficient, economical and effective operations.
observations.managementAction: An accounting system was initially introduced for evaluation and comment of the manager and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant conduct a review on the collectability of the dormant receivable accounts, as well the validity and complete documentation for the payable accounts, and take appropriate action pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2016-005, in the event that write-off of such accounts are warranted, in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management finds it hard to collect some accounts which no longer have complete documentation, thus, some accounts were not collected by the management. This matter has to be verified yet as to entries and whether or not an authority has to be asked first before this payable/ receivable can be written off due to its nature and the fact that this is an inherited account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the root cause of the negative balance of the Government Equity account under the sub-fund 20% Development Fund, in order to fairly present the account in the individual financial statements. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Government Equity account, especially that adjustments effected thereto are substantial in amount, for transparency and easy monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still has to trace the root cause of the negative balance of the Government Equity account under the sub-fund 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, enforce immediate and regular remittance of daily collections by the Collecting Officer to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS in order to fairly present collections of the LGU in a given period and avoid possible loss or misuse thereof.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Treasurer, she has already advised her collectors to immediately and regularly remit daily collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide reasons as to the partial or non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, management is enjoined to fast track the implementation of programs/ projects/activities identified in the budget and ensure inclusions of the projects enumerated under RA 9003 to serve the purpose of the created funds.
observations.managementAction: Management has continued its seeming practice of hasty procurement in the later part of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.
observations.managementAction: Management has tried to implement all the planned programs/ projects/ activities in the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting Department conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. 

Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that the management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability. Finally, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant apply Section 37 of P.D. 1445 to municipal employees with long overdue accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management has exerted collection efforts, but only to the extent of known debtors. In addition, the huge amount of this account is the cash shortage of Mrs. Trinidad R. Millare, previous Mun. Treasurer, which still remains unclassified until now, to its proper account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record purchases of supplies and materials to the corresponding inventory account and compute inventory balances as prescribed under Sections 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office  (a) maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; (b)furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances. Furthermore, we recommend that the Accounting Unit record issuances based on the SSMI submitted. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: Management has made monitoring only on the medicine and medical supplies, considering the huge amount involved.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendations that Management prioritize the creation of inventory committee and conduct the physical inventory count. Likewise, prepare the Report on Physical Count of PPE and to reconcile with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Review the Inventory by office
observations.reason: Not Implemented, reiterated in CY 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of prior years be incorporated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management appropriate one percent of the Local Government Unit’s Internal Revenue Allotment as required in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and to prepare Local Development Plan and Investment Plan for Childers with programs, projects and activities that promote and protect the rights of the children and disadvantaged sector of society.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the concerned offices to submit to the Office of the Audit Team Leader the reports of all on-going projects at the beginning of each year. Likewise, to submit the Quarterly Report on Government Projects every end of the quarter and include all infrastructure projects implemented by the Municipality regardless of the fund source and regardless whether by contract or implemented by administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure completeness of documents for contract review in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to strictly adhere to the provision of RA 9184 and other related law, rules and regulation on procurement.  For future similar transactions be sure to state specific description for each item to be procured, likewise indicate in the purchase orders complete information pertaining to the procurement necessary to impose penalty/liquidated damages in case of delay and/or non-delivery and non-compliance of the terms and conditions of the contract.
observations.managementAction: To adhere the RA 9184 for future purchases

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Members of the SB consider enacting appropriate ordinance on the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyance to prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated proliferation of tricycles along the Municipality’s highways, which may pose hazards and imminent danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties.
observations.managementAction: The SB has not enacted yet an ordinance allowing the operation of three-wheeled public conveyances along the Municipality’s highways.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to assigned personnel to specifically to reconcile the balances in the books and that the balances in the bank with Account Account:0522-1114-30.
