observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should be mindful of the guidelines set forth in the circular and submit immediately the documents required in the cited circular
observations.reason: Due to the voluminous of the transactions, the in charge forgets to submit the documents.

observations.recommendation:
Officials/employees of the NGAs shall submit a certification that the official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned one and certificate or evidence of service rendered or approved DTR; and

OIC-Municipal Accountant shall strengthen control on the disbursement of funds. Require the submission of the documents mentioned on COA Circular 2012-001 for the grant of RATA to NGAs before it shall be forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for payment.
observations.reason: Management prepared their RATA on an individual basis rather than on payroll thus it would not been properly monitored by the accountant.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to book-up the cost of work/breeding/other animals purchased/acquired/produced. Also, the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist together with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Accountant should conduct an actual inventory on the status of Work/Breeding/Other Animals. The Municipal Treasurer, on the other hand, being the Property Officer should prepare the Property Card for Work/Breeding/ Other Animals and Breeding Stocks.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant informed that for CY 2015 still they failed to book-up the cost of work/breeding/ other animals because of their failure to conduct actual inventory on their Work/Breeding/ Other Animals. The Municipal Agriculturist on the other hand, admitted his failure to conduct the same due to voluminous workloads because there are other projects downloaded by the national agency that made them busy Furthermore, the Municipal Mayor instructed again his Municipal Agriculturist to give priority to conduct actual inventory on the status of Work/Breeding/Other Animals for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should instruct the Supply Officer to submit copies of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Auditor for evaluation  within  five (5) working  days  from  execution  of a contract  or issuance of a purchase order so that any defect and/or deficiencies of government transactions could be pointed out immediately and gives management the opportunity to correct the same.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant regularly submit all the DM/CM indicated in the BRS and attach the paid and cancelled checks thereto pursuant to the pertinent Paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011 in order to facilitate a comprehensive review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.
observations.reason: Copies of credit and debit memos are not available from banks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive including the BAC strictly adhere to the guidelines in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract under the DPWH Department Order No.22 and require the collection from the winning bidder of the over-computed cost of P783,868.75 brought by non-compliance to the aforesaid regulation.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the LCE upon the advice of the DILG-RO8 informed the LGU to proceed with the procurement and implementation of the sub-project, and the consultant of the LGU regarding the preparation of the revised DED explained that the VAT rate per BIR regulations is 12%, and the government (LGU) will withhold 5% during the billing process from the contractor, and the remaining 7% will be remitted by the Contractor to the BIR. Thus, if the LGU will only charge a 5% VAT, who will pay/assume the remaining 7% VAT as mandated by law.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to require the submission of the revised DED showing the refund of the over-computed amount because there was no document showing that a refund of the said amount was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and other provisions of law.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that upon the advice of the DILG-RO8 there were no other comments or revisions required by the DILG-RO8, thus, the said DED was approved and said DED was advised the LGU to proceed with the procurement and implementation of the sub-project, which the LGU complied. The audit team was informed last January 5, 2018 on the status that there is a pending CIAC Arbitration Commission case between the LGU and CGGA Builders and Enterprise in CIAC Case No. 46-2017.
observations.reason: Intervention on the part of the LCE to require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents and settle the Notice of Suspension issued regarding the said transaction was not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management appropriately provide the potable water supply to targeted beneficiaries within the designated time frame. We also recommend that management, if necessary, rescind the contract without prejudice to the collection of liquidated damages in view of its non-completion despite the time elapsed.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that there is a pending case brought before the Construction Industry Arbitration Commission (CIAC) between the LGU and CGGA Builders and Enterprise in CIAC Case No. 46-2017. Further, there was also no document submitted by management showing the current status of the project and the collection of liquidated damages from the defaulting contractor.
observations.reason: Incomplete documents were submitted for the settlement of the Notice of Suspension issued for the progress billings. There was no submission of documents showing collection of liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of the LDRRMF. Specifically, we require the submission of the distribution lists and Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) for the in-kind donations to facilitate verification of the distribution of relief goods and list of in-cash donations for review.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit the required documents to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.reason: No records and documents were submitted to comply with the audit deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from granting CNA Incentive Bonus to its personnel and follow strictly pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the disbursement of public funds.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the municipality to recover the CNA Incentive.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions embodied in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and transfer the unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account.
observations.managementAction: The balance of LDRRMF as of year-end was not yet transferred to a special trust fund account.
observations.reason: The same audit finding is re-iterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the operations of economic enterprises. The Municipal Treasurer should also prepare separate reports for economic enterprises, such as the water system.
observations.managementAction: No special account in the General Fund was maintained for the operations of the San Miguel Waterworks System (SMWS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the conduct the physical inventory taking of all its property, plant and equipment and submit a complete report thereon and reconcile the property and accounting record.

We recommend to management to direct the LDRRM Officer to have a close coordination with the local development council in crafting of Local Risk Reduction and Management Plan in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction.

We recommended the creation of inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties, prepare the Report on the Results of Physical Count of PPE to be reconciled with the accounting records and investigate any discrepancy.  Also advise the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards for PPE and update the ARE for easy identification of the condition and whereabouts of the properties
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No inventory report submitted to the office

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. The Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual and to regularly monitor the progress of the inventory taking; b. Thereafter, require the Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.
observations.managementAction: The LCE created the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the purchase of supplies and materials be taken up first as inventory by the Municipal Accountant and later charged to expense upon issuance to the end users following the procedure set forth under Section 121 of NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the accountant to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LCE assured compliance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from approving disbursement vouchers for the purchase of fuel, unless the required documentary requirements are submitted. Also, require the drivers to properly accomplish the Driver’s trip tickets and the GSO/Officer-in-Charge to prepare the Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption in compliance of the above regulation.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Municipal Accountant to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the TWG of the BAC be made to explain of their failure to post-qualify the bidder despite the non-compliance with the legal and technical requirements to qualify and be awarded the contract on the supply of motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the BAC/TWG to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator provide the Auditor with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Mun. Engr and MPDO to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 on the prompt submission of contracts, purchase order and notices of deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the acting GSO to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the accounts to the accounting office on time and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required journals. Submit the required reports and the accounts to COA on time and strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 for the timely verification of transactions/ accounts and to avoid possible sanction that may be imposed for failure to do so.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the concerned personnel to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended that  the Local Chief Executive require: a) the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to record the reversion of stale checks totaling ₱44,211.40in  the  Cash in Bank  and  the related Payable and Government Equity accounts; and b)  the   Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to  update  the  list  of Outstanding Checks on a monthly basis  to clear  the  Bank  Reconciliation of   stale  checks .

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the  Municipal Accountant  to a) issue  demand  letters  for the  unliquidated  cash  advances which are  long   overdue;  and b) stop the granting   of additional  cash  advances to  the  accountable  officers which  still have unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all PPE and expedite the submission of the physical inventory report not later than January 31 of each year; and (b) require the MTO to maintain Property Cards and the Accounting Office to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards   for easy reconciliation of records to settle the discrepancy noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) require the concerned implementing officials to closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the projects to ensure their effective and efficient completion within the target duration; b) conduct detailed engineering of projects in order to avoid realignment of funds involving change in type of projects /transfers of project locations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO thru the Liga ng Mga Barangay and Solid Waste Management Committees a) encourage active participation of barangay officials in the strict implementation of waste segregation at the barangay level; and (b) provide technical and financial assistance to the remaining 23 barangays in the establishment of their MRF.

We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; and (b) refrain from accommodating payment of expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Budget Officer and the LDRRMO  continue to allocate 5% of the budget for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and follow utilization of the fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth in R. A. 10121.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures to the LCPC Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  (a) the GAD Focal Person to  carefully identify and  include only PPAs that are gender responsive  in the GAD Plan and Budget to address the  issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men stakeholders, clients and constituents; (b) the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant  ensure that only expenditures which correspond to the identified gender issues and concerns in the GAD  Plan  are charged against the  GAD Budget and actually spent; and  monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs to prepare the accomplishment report  in the prescribed format.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Accountant faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and full remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant fully comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to fully comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Accountant fully comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly Philhealth contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive (i) instruct the officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit; and (ii)require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and (iii) the Municipal Accountant comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.
We recommend management to assigned personnel specifically to reconcile the balances in the books and that the balances in the bank with Account Account:0522-1114-30.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant:
To   strictly   adhere   with   the   provisions   of the abovementioned    guidelines and   record   all   transactions   in the period it occurs.  The  Accountant    shall recognize   the  assets/expenses  on  the  year  for  these  were  incurred  following   the   matching  principle  of  accounting.  At   the   end   of  the   year         where
payment cannot be effected yet, said obligations shall be recorded as expenses and set-up the corresponding Payable account on the basis of complete supporting documents; and
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect the following adjusting entries to correct the previous erroneous entries:
Account				Debit		Credit
PPE Account
Information and Communication
Technology Equipment	      P 243,085.00
Furniture and Fixtures 	           25,600.00
Building		                     424,380.00
Infrastructure assets	                     797,442.55
Construction in Progress		         240,000.00
Prior Year’s Adjustment					P1,739,507.55

We recommend to Management that after approval of the SEF budget by majority of its members, it will pass to the legislative body for authorization thru appropriation ordinance, since the SEF cannot do away with the fundamental principle of disbursements that no money shall be paid out of the local treasury except in pursuance of an appropriations ordinance or law.

We recommend that management stop charging expenses against the SEF and strictly observe the mandate of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1.  The Accountant and OIC Treasurer are required to see to it that only those expenses which fall under the allowed expenditures should be processed and paid.  Further, the amount given is to be restored.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and designated Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and look into the matter in order to determine the causes of the delay.  He should direct the prompt submission of the required reports in compliance with their duties and responsibilities and as mandated by the prescribed rules and regulations.  Unjustifiable failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of their salaries, until they shall have complied with the aforesaid requirement, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommend that Management prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommend that LDRRM Officer to prepare annual LDRRMFIP (using the Annex “A” format) with details as to particulars, projects, and activities to be funded and implemented during the year.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the BAC prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required under Section 12.2 of    the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies in compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency on government procurement.
Further, we suggest that COA be furnished with a printed copy of the said monitoring report as basis for auditor’s action, if any.
We recommend that the concerned officials/offices see to it that disbursements vouchers are supported with complete documentation as required in existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
  deposit on time all collections to the authorized depository bank and  to
  strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32
  of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned accountable
  officers, officials and employees  of  the  municipality  to  immediately
  settle/liquidate their cash advances after  the  purpose  for  which  had
  already been served, pursuant to Section  89  of  PD  No.  1445  and  COA
  Circular No.  97-002,  otherwise  impose  proper  sanction  such  as  the
  withholding of salaries for failure to comply with the said Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
  to immediately prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements  for
  calendar years 2011 to 2016  pursuant  to  Section  74  of  PD  1445  and
  Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to prove the  correctness
  and existence of the cash in bank balances as of year-end.

We strongly recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  all  officials
  concerned to:


  1. Create a new set of inventory committee considering  that  there  were
    members who already had retired from service in the existing  inventory
    committee under EO No. 2 series of 2010.


  2. Require the created inventory committee to conduct physical  count  of
    all the property, plant and equipment  owned  by  the  municipality  to
    facilitate the completion of inventory of PPE and prepare the Report on
    the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.


  3. Prepare and maintain the prescribed PPELC which should  be  reconciled
    with the RPCPPE to  establish  the  correctness  of  the  PPE  accounts
    amounting to P141,261,880.47.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
  adhere to pertinent sections of the RA 7160 to  enforce  payment  of  tax
  delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
  and Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount of P295,042.15 to
  the Bureau of Internal Revenue.


  Henceforth, taxes withheld from payments to  suppliers  and  salaries  of
  employees be remitted promptly  and  regularly  in  accordance  with  the
  provisions of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
  to cause the transfer of unexpended balance to the special trust  fund  -
  MDRRMF immediately to facilitate funding of PPAs relative thereto.

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive,  Sangguniang  Bayan  and
   Officials concerned comply with RA No. 10121’s statutory ceiling for  the
   LDRRMF so as to accomplish all planned projects and  activities  relative
   to disaster management.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  LDRRM  Officer  -
   Designate follow the four (4) thematic areas reflected  in  the  National
   DRRM Plan in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP of the municipality.

   We also recommended that the  LDRRM  Officer-Designate  include  the  two
   thematic areas namely Disaster Response and Disaster  Rehabilitation  and
   Recovery in the programs/projects/activities stated in  the  LDRRMFIP  in
   accordance with the National DRRM Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRM  Officer
  – Designate to religiously observe the timely submission of  the  monthly
  Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF (Annex B) to the COA  Auditor
  copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the  LDRRM  Officer-
  Designate  to  utilize  the  said  fund  strictly  for   calamity-related
  activities, supported by the required documents, so as to accomplish  all
  planned projects and activities.

We strongly recommended that:


 1. The Local Chief Executive  instruct  the  GAD  Focal  Point  System  to
    carefully study PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.
    2016-01 and follow the procedural guidelines in the formulation  of  GAD
    Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report.


 2. The GAD Focal Point System see to it that GPB is submitted to DILG  for
    review and approval in order that the  same  will  be  incorporated  in
    their Annual Budget to be enacted by their Sanggunian.


 3. The Sanggunian Bayan pass an ordinance concerning  the  adoption  of  a
    Gender and Development Code to enforce the formulation of GAD  Plan  and
    Budget.

We strongly recommended the following:


 1. That the Municipal Mayor create/organize  the  Local  Council  for  the
    Protection of Children (LCPC) to facilitate the  implementation  of  the
    above-cited  provisions  for  the  protection  of   children   in   that
    municipality.


 2. The provision of fund based on the required allocation of 1% of its IRA
    to strengthen LCPC’s programs, projects and activities and  include  the
    same in Annual Budget to be enacted  by  the  Sanggunian  Bayan  of  the
    Municipality of San Miguel.

 3. That the Sanggunian Bayan pass an ordinance concerning the adoption  of
    the Local Code for Children (LCC) to  enforce  the  formulation  of  the
    Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan (prioritized according to needs
    for integration in the local development plan); Local  Development  Plan
    for Children (LDPC) and the Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC).

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct  the  Municipal  Accountant
    and officials concerned to ensure that financial assistance extended  to
    the barangays,  School  and  individuals  participating  in  the  socio-
    cultural/socio-civic activities be   supported  by  the  above-mentioned
    documentary requirements to provide basis for monitoring the proper  use
    of funds.


    We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant make sure  that  fund
    transfers be recorded in the Due from LGUs account instead of  Donations
    so that the municipality can monitor the implementation of the  proposed
    PPAs.


    We  also  recommend  that  intended  financial  assistance  for  private
    persons/employees be granted as a Cash Advance to a  Disbursing  Officer
    so that disbursements can monitored.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  other  local  officials
concerned to:

   1. Fully utilize and accomplish timely implementation of projects  funded
      from LGSF in the year it was granted to achieve the  optimum  benefits
      and purpose for which the fund was created.


   2. Adequately plan  so  as  to  eliminate  possible  problems  that  will
      potentially delay projects implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the utilization of  the
    20%  Development  Fund  allocated  for  the  implementation  of  various
    projects, programs and activities that are envisioned  to  help  in  the
    attainment of social, economic and  environmental  development  so  that
    optimum benefits derived therefrom can be availed by the constituents.

    We also recommended that the concerned municipal officials  enhance  the
    monitoring and evaluation of the status and implementation  of  the  20%
    development fund  in  pursuance  to  Municipal’s  mandate  and  faithful
    compliance of pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the MSWMB,
    the Municipal Treasurer and the MENRO to  strictly  impose  and  collect
    garbage fees not only to  business  establishments  but  also  to  every
    household and fines/penalties be  imposed  to  violators  of  the  above
    mentioned ordinances.

    We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  members
    of the MSWMB to prepare SWM plan to facilitate assessment and monitoring.
The recommendations are embedded under each sub-item a. to ad. above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU. Likewise, we recommend that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommend that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of salaries or any money due to concerned employees with unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002, in the event of non-liquidation as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to properly and completely identify those obligations which are considered undocumented and confirmed non-existent regardless of the year they were incurred, as well as all payables which have remained outstanding for more than two (2) years, be reverted to the Surplus Account, pursuant to Executive Order No. 109, and Sections 141 and 157 of GAAM, in order to clear the books of these non-existent obligations and fairly present the actual current liabilities in the financial statements.

We recommended that management submit an explanation on the practice of allowing rental of municipal heavy equipment and facilities on account and henceforth, allow the use thereof only on cash basis pursuant to Item 3 of Section 51 of Municipal Ordinance No.C-90. Likewise, we recommend that management formulate means to intensify monitoring and collection of those long overdue accounts to boost the income of the municipal government. Also, we recommend that management revisit the relevant provisions of Municipal Ordinance No.C-90 on the proper imposition of rates for municipal heavy equipment and facilities as well as its administrative provisions. Lastly, we recommend that management observe Section 47, Volume III of the GAAM, and Section 111 of P.D.1445 on the clear and adequate documentation of financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the remittance to concerned barangays of their shares of real property taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to the provisions of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 to avoid unnecessary accumulation of funds in the municipal treasury and to enable the concerned barangays to timely implement beneficial barangay projects through the use of such funds.

We recommended that management always ensure the availability of funds in order to sustain sufficiency of computer units installed with e-TRACS system. Likewise, familiarization of e-TRACS by the concerned collecting officers is encouraged to facilitate timely preparation of Report of Collections and Deposits, pursuant to Section 29, Chapter 3 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Moreover, it is recommended that San Miguel Municipal Hospital be provided with a computer unit installed with e-TRACS and coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) as to its viability and sustainability to reduce the volume of ORs which need to be captured manually into the computer system.

We recommended that management provide reasons as to the non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management implement programs/projects/activities on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1 in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public or to enjoy the comfort that the municipal employees of officials could have experienced therefrom.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording, pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume 1. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant   draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and not by the Municipal Assessor and make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance. Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to provide clarification on the non-liquidation of social pension for Senior Citizens as of year-end despite the one-month period within which the funds were delivered directly to the respective barangays of concerned senior citizens, contrary to COA Circular No.94-013 on the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of funds transferred to implementing agencies.

We recommended that management provide clarification on the low revenue realization of some income accounts. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management formulate schemes and exercise measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160, relative to municipal budgetary functions.

We recommendedthat the BAC Chairperson, instruct the BAC Secretariat to observe Section 37.1.6 of the 2016R-IRR of R.A. No.9184 in its posting requirement of the Notice of Award, within three (3) calendar days, in the PhilGEPS, the website of the municipality and any conspicuous place in its premises, in the spirit of transparency of the procurement process.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) provide an explanation on the delayed execution of contracts. Henceforth, we recommend that the BAC facilitate the execution of contracts between the municipality and the winning bidder within ten (10) calendar days from receipt by the latter of the Notice of Award pursuant to Section 37.2.1 and 37.2.2 of the 2016 R-IRRA of R.A. No.9184 in order to ensure expeditious procurement process.

We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA Circular No.2012-001. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC observe Section 23 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 in the submission of eligibility documents of bidders, for COA review.

We recommended that the BAC immediately furnish the Audit Team an authenticated copy of the final and approved Annual Procurement Plan and observe the provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of the Government Procurement Law or R.A.9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.2012-001 to ensure the efficient discharge of governmental functions and adoption of fiscal discipline measures.

We recommended that management find means to facilitate the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the spirit of transparency of procurement process.

We recommended that management require the MDRRMO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to facilitate the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRM to a special trust fund for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years pursuant to paragraph 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommended that management provide explanation on fund transfers not yet fully implemented and liquidated as year-end. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management be more mindful of the urgency of project implementation and observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 94-013 in order to fully maximize funds received and deliver the expected benefits from the intended projects effectively and efficiently.

We recommended that management provide explanation on fund transfers from LGUs not yet fully implemented and liquidated as year-end. Also, we recommend that henceforth, management observe the provisions of COA Circular 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other LGUs so as to ensure that funds received are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and those services and benefits due to the public are not delayed.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the recorded account balances per fund and review the recorded transactions for possible erroneous recording in order to make the necessary adjustments, if warranted, and furnish the Audit Team copies of the adjusting entries and supporting documents therefor. It is recommended that henceforth, management ensure compliance with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on withholding of taxes and timely remittance of the same to avoid incurrence of penalties/surcharges and fines. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant review the recorded transactions for possible erroneous recording.
We recommended that management expedite the purchase and installation of a safety vault for the MTO in order to properly safeguard undeposited collections from risk of loss of theft.

We recommended that management require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate and conduct meeting with the different LGU offices and national government agencies involved in the implementation of the planned GAD programs and activities; make an evaluation as to the clear-cut causes of the non-implementation of these projects and; formulate strategies in order to ensure the complete implementation of GAD programs and activities in the future.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee be required to submit
the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Their
reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be
properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted
onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445
and COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit
immediately the paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls and its supporting documents
for calendar year 2017, as well as the collection accounts for the month of December
2017. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should submit the monthly reports
together with all the vouchers, payrolls, official receipts and their supporting
documents in accordance with Paragraph 7.2.1 COA Circular 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.

We recommend that management undertake the procedures prescribed
under Sections 254 and 256 of RA 7160 relative to the enforcement of collection of
real property taxes, to lessen the accumulation of tax delinquencies. Likewise,
require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt measures and strategies to improve
collection efficiency such as vigorous information campaign addressed to the
taxpayers and deputizing the barangay treasurers to collect all taxes on real
property located in the barangay, as provided under Section 247, Chapter VI of RA
7160.

Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit the
respective shares of the barangays and the province on the real property tax
collected within five (5) days after the end of each quarter in compliance with
Section 286 of RA 7160.

We recommend that the officials and employees concerned be required to
immediately settle/refund in full the unliquidated travel advances or enforce the
withholding of their salaries or any money due them if necessary, pursuant to
Section 4.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002. In no case, shall additional
travel advances be granted unless the previous cash advance is first settled as
required in Section 4.1.2 of the same Circular.

We recommend that Management review the hiring procedure and
personnel management of Job Orders and set a standard policy and guidelines
relative thereto in accordance with the regulations set forth in Section 76 and 77 of
RA 7160 and the CSC Resolution No. 020790. Also, we recommend that
Management require concerned officials to specify in the Contract of Service the
specific nature of work or duties and responsibilities of the JO personnel, and to
submit copies of these contracts to COA within five (5) days from its issuance as
required in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to inform COA
within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start
of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or
public notices are already posted at the project site. Likewise, enjoin concerned
officials to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to
uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board prepare an Annual School
Board Budget that would include programs, projects and activities that are aligned
with Section 100(c) of RA 7160, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series
of 2017. Likewise, strictly observe the limitation on the use of Special Education
Fund (SEF).

We recommend that all fund transfers to barangays be supported with a
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) duly signed by both parties. Likewise,
require the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to reclassify the
Subsidy to LGUs account to Due from LGUs account and require the recipient
barangay to liquidate the funds received. Henceforth, strictly observe Section 3.1.1
of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 on the
documentation requirements and recording of fund transfers.

We recommend that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized in
implementing development projects indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.
Likewise, the Local Development Council should prioritize and incorporate in the
Annual Investment Plan of the LGU those projects that are aligned with Section 3 of
the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017.

We recommend that management enjoin all department heads involved in the
implementation of disaster prevention and mitigation and disaster preparedness
PPAs to monitor its implementation and to ensure that funds allocated for LDRRM
be optimally utilized during the year in accordance with the timeline of projects and
programs accomplishment set in the LDRRM Plan. Likewise, require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balances of
LDRRMF totaling P2,609,853.16 to a Special Trust Fund account. Moreover,
require the Municipal Accountant and other department heads concerned to submit
the required reports under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No.
2014-002. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions set forth under said
regulations, particularly on the prescribed period of submission of reports.

We reiterate our recommendations that Management prioritize the creation of inventory committee and conduct the physical inventory count. Likewise, prepare the Report on Physical Count of PPE and to reconcile with the accounting records.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up the CIP and the appropriate asset accounts. Further, completed projects in the trust fund account shall be transferred to the General Fund.

We recommended that the appropriate asset and expense accounts be recognized in the Trust Fund book for transactions charged against the previous years’ Quick Response and Mitigation Funds and to draw the necessary year-end accounting entries in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommend to the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit all the collections promptly and intact to safeguard against misuse and loss of government funds.

We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) take the approved NDRRMC as the basis in the preparation of LDRRMP and strictly adhere on the guidelines set by the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of prior years be incorporated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management appropriate one percent of the Local Government Unit’s Internal Revenue Allotment as required in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and to prepare Local Development Plan and Investment Plan for Childers with programs, projects and activities that promote and protect the rights of the children and disadvantaged sector of society.
We recommended strict compliance to the provision of Section 4.6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 on providing complete documentation of claims against government funds.
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere the rules and regulations on the Fund transfer to NGO/PO as provided in Item 2.1 of COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned offices to submit to the Office of the Audit Team Leader the reports of all on-going projects at the beginning of each year. Likewise, to submit the Quarterly Report on Government Projects every end of the quarter and include all infrastructure projects implemented by the Municipality regardless of the fund source and regardless whether by contract or implemented by administration.
We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that Management ensure completeness of documents for contract review in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the use of Sanitary Landfill for the people of the Municipality to enjoy the benefits for which this Act was created.

We recommended strict adherence to the requirement of Section 24 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the provision of cover of the waste compartment of the vehicle used for the collection and transport of solid wastes to ensure its containment while in transit and to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended to management to strictly adhere to the provision of RA 9184 and other related law, rules and regulation on procurement.  For future similar transactions be sure to state specific description for each item to be procured, likewise indicate in the purchase orders complete information pertaining to the procurement necessary to impose penalty/liquidated damages in case of delay and/or non-delivery and non-compliance of the terms and conditions of the contract.
We recommended that Management immediately implement the projects/activities for an efficient and economical utilization of government funds and to attain the desired objective of the projects.

comply with the provisions of Section 84 of PD No. 1445 and Section 155(a) of

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to (a) speed up the preparation of documents necessary for the registration of the lots in the Municipality’s name with the Register of Deeds; and (b) exert best efforts to make the necessary research for the retrieval of documents supporting the untitled parcels of land including the basis for the valuation of the lots.
observations.managementAction: (a & b) Efforts are underway   in locating the whereabouts of the titles of said lots owned by the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.
observations.managementAction:
a) The Municipal Mayor explained that the funds for the other insurable properties of the municipality will be included in their budget for CY 2018. 

(b) The updated list   of inventory of insurable properties was prepared and submitted by the agency to the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take immediate steps to dispose of all unserviceable properties to prevent possible loss and avoid further deterioration in accordance with the guidelines provided by the COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
observations.managementAction: According to the Chairman on Disposal Committee, the inventory of unserviceable properties by department will be conducted upon completion of the documents needed for the disposal           of unserviceable properties and upon completion of the inventory. The said body will request the SB to pass a resolution for the proper disposal of the said properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution establishing specific guidelines on the rates or amounts of financial assistance being extended by the Municipality and the criteria for the entitlement for each type thereof to rationalize disbursements under the Donations account and other MOOE account.
observations.managementAction: No Sangguniang Bayan Resolution was submitted to support the disbursements of financial assistance/ aid to individuals in crisis situation.  Instead, the management granted the rates or         amounts granted to indigent individuals based on Administrative Order No. 75 series of 2003 dated September 30, 2003 re: Amended Implementing Guidelines on the Management of DSWD Operated Crisis Intervention Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply strictly with the aforementioned regulation and require the Municipal Engineering Office and other officials concerned to disclose all transactions involving PPAs for transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the information and publicity of Projects/Program/ Activities by posting of signboards on their on-going projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on the invitation of observers for all stages of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman already invited observers in writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs; (b) direct the other responsible officials concerned to address the issues that impede the full implementation of priority projects; (c) continue to invest in high–impact or developmental projects and include in the Annual Investment Plan; and (d) cause the re-appropriation of the balance of the fund in the ensuing year to finance development projects for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction:
(a & b) The Municipal Engineer and  Municipal Planning and Development Officer closely monitored the effective and efficient implementation of the different projects under the 20% Development Fund.

c) The Management continued to invest in high impact or developmental projects.

d) The unutilized balance of the fund was re-appropriated for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and (b) continue to allocate 5% of the budget for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and utilize it in accordance with the guidelines on the matter.
observations.managementAction:
a) The Reports            on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF from January to October 2017 were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

b)  The agency continued to allocate 5% budget for the LDRRMF

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO  (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes in every household; (b) follow-up from the Sangguniang Panglalawigan office on the status of the review of the Municipal Environment Code to expedite the enactment of its “No Littering” policy; and (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 43 barangays which have remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
The office of the MENRO is constantly conducting extensive information campaign through dialogue/meetings/ assemblies/ seminars and trainings on        the segregation of wastes and oriented the barangay government on their responsibilities to properly implement the provision of RA No. 9003 on the segregation of wastes and source.

The Sangguniang Panlalawigan enacted KapasiyahanBlg. 039-T’17 dated February 9, 2017 re: “Kautusan na Pinagtitibay ang Kautusang Bayan Blg.10 ng Sangguniang Bayan ng San Miguel, An Ordinance Enacting the Municipal Environment Code   of the Municipality   of San Miguel, Bulacan.”

Per interview with the MENRO,    the municipality extended financial assistance of P100,000.00 to each of the 49 barangays and already included in the CY 2018 budget.  Also, the number of barangays with MRF increased from six in CY 2016 to 21 this year as follows:

1.  Barangays 
with operational 
MRF – 2	

2.  Barangays with
partially operational
MRF -  3

3.  Barangays with MRF but not yet operational – 16

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Out of the            ending balance of P543,290.07 as of December 31, 2016, a partial remittance amounting to P539,401.87 was made in January 12, 2017 while the remaining balance was remitted in February, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the (a) Accounting Office and the Human Resource Office to tally their records of premiums withheld and remitted; and (b) Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of RA No. 8291.
observations.managementAction:
a) The Municipal Mayor instructed the Accounting and HR Department to exert their best efforts to find the missing premiums. 

b)  The Accounting Office and Treasury Office complied with the full remittance of the withheld premiums for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to issue the notice to liquidate to the concerned AOs of all the unliquidated cash advances and enforce the suspension of salary of the concerned active AO until the compliance thereof. In the event that the AO is already separated from the LGU, the notice be send to the last known address of the concerned AO. Lastly, for AOs who were already deceased, prepare the list of the deceased AOs with the corresponding Death Certificate for a request to COA for write-off of the accounts.
observations.reason: Management already sent demand letters to the concerned individuals but some of them are already separated from the service, thus, unliquidated cash advances remain unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Accountant be instructed to make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of Loans Payable account. An adjusting entry be made whose purpose is to reflect the correct balance of Loans account. Books pertaining to CY 2015 had already been closed; any adjustment thereof should have been reflected on CY 2016

We recommend that the LGU to come up with the necessary plans as enumerated above, and employ a full time Local DRRM Officer then subject itself for reevaluation for further improvements, also to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the disbursement made
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management: 

(a) require the Revenue Collectors to liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible misuse and losses and (b) require the Collectors to attach the original copy of accountable forms which have been cancelled to the stub and (c) require the AOs to liquidate their collections before issuance of another accountable forms.
observations.reason: The AOs were accustom to have it reported upon exhaustion of a given stub.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: There are still delays in the submission of the FS and the related vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that: 

The Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures;

 Concerned officials with government vehicles and the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit regularly thru the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets; and

 The Municipal Accountant be required to look into the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the DVs. Claims with incomplete supporting documents must be returned to the claimant before payment be made.
observations.reason: There was misinformation in the instruction, it was only submitted to the Accounting as supporting documents for disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to update his cashbooks and record immediately all transactions for the day in consonance with COA Circular No. 2002-003.
observations.reason: Due to other functions assigned to the ICO-MT, the balances in the cash book were not keep updated.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor and verify the issuance of the accountable forms issued to the accountable officers and check the number of issuances and number of the remaining balances of the accountable forms and fill-up properly Sections C and D and the VERIFICATION AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT column of the RCD by indicating the amount in words and in figures received by him for easy monitoring of the movement of the accountable forms and the remittances of collections;

 The accountable officers, upon the conduct of cash examination of all his cash and accounts shall render an accounting thereof.
observations.reason: There are still accountable forms that were not immediately presented during the cash examination

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the collectors/accountable officers to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer who in turn shall consolidate the reports in the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.reason: Management failed to continue the submission of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC furnish the Auditor a printed copy of the PMR to provide basis for monitoring the status of the procurement activities of the municipality.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat started to tabulate the procurement status, however, it was not keep updated.

observations.recommendation: Management should give priority attention on the preparation and cause the immediate and regular submission of the bank reconciliation statements pursuant to Sec 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 dated October 02, 1996 and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: An inventory committee should be created to conduct the annual physical inventory of all assets under PPE to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.  In the preparation of the report, the prescribed format should be used and that the different columns should be properly filled-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for.  Assignment of property number should be properly done so that each of the items can be easily identified and properly classified. On the other hand, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards; Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card and Real Property Ledger Cards.  Once the physical inventory of PPE is already available it shall be reconciled with the (a)  PPE ledger card, (b)  the control accounts and (c)  property cards.  If any discrepancies exist, it should be immediately verified and adjusted.  Further, reclassification of obsolete and unserviceable assets, as well as assets no longer used by LGU-San Miguel to “Other Assets” account should be made.
observations.reason: Management conducted actual physical inventory taking of all its assets, but the report was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit on time the RCI together with the Disbursement Vouchers to the Municipal Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant on the other hand, upon receipt of the reports should prepare the trial balance monthly together with the required supporting documents as provided in Section 70 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (Local Government Units), Volume I, to facilitate verification by this Office. Should there be any corrections and/or unaccounted items that may be noted in the process, the necessary adjustments should be effected immediately. for proper documentation. Section 4 of PD 1445.
On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer shall also ensure that the Municipal Accountant had already certified the DVs before making any payment pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of properties and continue to identify in the other departments/offices those PPEs and inventories which are already unserviceable or non-functional and facilitate disposal thereof in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting and property records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the assets of the municipality in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Adjustments shall be made after the General Services Officer (GSO) conducts the physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer evaluate the necessity of maintaining funds with FCB. If found justified, we recommend that management adhere to the guidelines set forth in DOF Circular No.001-2015 to uphold its purposes, in order to prevent sanctions that may be imposed by DOF.
observations.managementAction: The LGU complied with the P4.5M average daily balance for 4th class munici-pality

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Account draw the necessary journal entries to record the transactions which have long been reflected in the BRS as reconciling items in order to fairly present the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entry reverting the payable and cash in bank accounts for fairness of presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant work on the tracing the items which caused discrepancies of Cash in Bank balances in the two separate records to fairly present a reliable amount in the financial statements.

We recommend that management be compliant more compliant with the provisions of Joint Circular No.2015-01 of COA-DBM-DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND relative to the release of confidential fund.
We recommend that management submit an explanation on the foregoing noted deficiency.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, enforce immediate and regular remittance of daily collections by the Collecting Officer to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS in order to fairly present collections of the LGU in a given period and avoid possible loss or misuse thereof.
observations.reason: Sometimes, some collectors are plainly not mindful and compliant with the rules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, adhere to the requirements in the collection, remittance, liquidation and deposit of collections.
observations.reason: Those seemingly “advance deposits” were still conducted due to accommo-dation reasons by the LO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the provisions of Section 32 of Manual on NGAS in order to prevent misappropriation or loss of public funds.
observations.reason: At times, deposits are done when convenient to the LO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the unreconciled difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to make an immediate review and tracing of differences between the two accounts once financial statements are generated to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.
observations.reason: Some accounts are still for tracing/ verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management demand the immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality, by immediately closing the account Due from LGUs in the year the funds are already expended and liquidated by the recipient barangays.
observations.managementAction: No additional financial assistance is granted unless previous assistance is liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the necessary documents to prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the social pension. Likewise, it is recommended that management explain the payment of social pension even though the period to which it pertained had not yet lapsed.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the processing of land titling. It is also recommended that the management provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of titles and inform and furnish the Audit Team, any data and documents as to the status of land titling. Moreover, it is recommended that management ensure that the purpose for every land acquisition has been met for the benefit of the general public.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor will only be able to focus on the processing for titling of the properties once relieved as BAC Secretariat Head.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor reconcile their records to determine the cause of the noted discrepancy, in order to fairly present the Land account in the financial statements and submit the documents to the Audit Team, for review.
observations.reason: The difference was mainly due to the recognition of the accounting office of the taxes paid in line with the purchase of such land as addition to the cost of the land.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion. Likewise, corresponding liquidation report for the construction of evacuation center is requested for submission, for verification.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were delayed due to unfavor-able weather condition and failure to secure the COMELEC exemption during the election ban.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the MPDC, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and concerned department heads to immediately submit copies of the perfected government contracts, purchase orders already issued/executed, together with the complete supporting documents, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof, as well as ocular inspection of implemented projects. Otherwise, reviewed fund utilization reports which are deficient in documentation shall be suspended in audit.
observations.reason: Instructions are given to concern personnel to comply with submission of contracts. However, it is a fact that at times they tend to forget to do it considering that BAC membership is additional load on their regular duties. But the BAC this year are creating a monitoring tool in order to lessen the possibility of missing the submission requirement and documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the root cause of the negative balance of the Government Equity account under the sub-fund 20% Development Fund, in order to fairly present the account in the individual financial statements. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Government Equity account, especially that adjustments effected thereto are substantial in amount, for transparency and easy monitoring purposes.
observations.reason: The root caused was traced and it was due to the erroneous classification of accounts and the non- forwarding of assets of the following year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide reasons as to the partial or non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, management is enjoined to fast track the implementation of programs/ projects/activities identified in the budget and ensure inclusions of the projects enumerated under RA 9003 to serve the purpose of the created funds.
observations.reason: Implementa-tion was delayed due to long process of procurement, follow the project implementa-tion plan in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant regularly compute and provide for the corresponding depreciation for all depreciable assets in order to present accurate balances of the assets in the financial statements of the SEF.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant conduct a review on the collectability of the dormant receivable accounts, as well the validity and complete documentation for the payable accounts, and take appropriate action pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2016-005, in the event that write-off of such accounts are warranted, in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: This matter has to be verified yet as to entries and whether or not an authority has to be asked first before this payable/ receivable can be written off due to its nature and the fact that this is an inherited account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management, in coordination with the Local School Board, revisit the provisions of R.A.5477 in order to arrive at identification and implementation of projects/activities which are more fruitful and reflective of the intents of R.A.5477, to fully serve its purpose.

We recommend that management provide reasons as to the huge increases in other hospital expenses which resulted in increasing net loss. Likewise, management has to be more prudent in its disbursements in order to lessen expenditures and arrive at a net income for hospital operations, so as not to deplete the much needed fund in the General Fund-Proper.
observations.reason: Matters still have to be done such as follow up Philhealth on billing submitted for reimbursement, monitoring of turnover on the remittance against the billing submitted in order to increase the revenue of the facility. Hospital Mgt. Board is also reconstituted to help in the monitoring of income and expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide reasons as to the huge increases in other Masanao Restaurant expenses which contributed to the increasing net loss. Likewise, management has to be more aggressive in its marketing strategies in order to sufficiently promote Masanao Restaurant, not only in its locality, but to neighbouring towns as well. It is also recommend that management be more prudent in its disbursements and make periodic monitoring and review of Masanao operations, for quality assurance purposes.
observations.reason: Monitoring on income was done vis a vis the expenses incurred that was why in CY 2017, Masanao has significantly increased its income which shows a net income after deducting the expenses, according to the Municipal Accountant but the matter still has to be verified due to the absence of a separate financial statement for MASANAO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish an accounting and operating system in running Masanao operations to have a system of internal control and proper administrative practices installed, to ensure compliance with laws and regulations and to provide for efficient, economical and effective operations.
observations.managementAction: An accounting system was introduced for evaluation and comment of the manager and employees.
observations.reason: The accounting system still has to be submitted to the Audit Team for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management, specifically the Bids and Awards Committee, review the Manual on Negotiated Procurement-Community Participation in order to use it only on applicable transactions in cooperation with the properly identified community groups, so as not to evade or defeat the purposes of R.A.9184.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to adhere to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the regular submission of LDRRM report to facilitate timely review thereof.
We recommend that management submit an explanation to provide clarification on the foregoing noted deficiency.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Person to ensure the allocation of at least five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU in compliance with DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Section B, Part 5 of the Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant as to the status of funds received and monitor utilization thereof in observance of Section 84 (2) of P.D. 1445 to avoid overpayment to contractors, based on funds received, which is detrimental to the implementation of other projects.
