We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation as to the delay of the supplier in posting the Performance Security. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the BAC observe the posting requirements of Performance Security pursuant to Section 39 of RA 9184 in order to guarantee the faithful performance by the winning bidder of its obligations under the contract in accordance with its Bidding Documents.

We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.
observations.reason: Contract Sonologist to render duty once a week for the operation of Mobile Ultrasound. Conduct of massive campaign still to be done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.
observations.reason: Sometimes, there appears conflicts on time management and prioritization of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry to write-off the corresponding amount which have been dormant for years in consonance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and Section 04 (f), Chapter 2 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, Volume I, in order to fairly present the payable accounts in the financial statements. In the event of claims by the concerned electricians in the future, we recommend that the latter present valid documents to prove first rightful claim for actual services rendered, before the Municipal Accountant restores the payable account.
observations.reason: This matter has to be verified as to entries and whether or not an authority has to be asked first before this payable can be written off due to its nature and the fact that this is an inherited account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting Department conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility.

Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that the management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability. Finally, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant apply Section 37 of P.D. 1445 to municipal employees with long overdue accounts.
observations.reason: The huge amount of this account is the cash shortage of Mrs. Trinidad R. Millare, previous Mun. Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for: (1) debtors who are still alive and within the locality and (2) debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that the management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write-off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability.
observations.reason: The management plans to request the write off of these receivables. The Municipal Accountant will be coordinating with COA on the possible steps to take to write these receivables as these are deemed uncollectible.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee abide by the provisions of R.A.9184 by requiring the hospital management to prepare ahead of time, a comprehensive list of medicines and medical supplies to be used for hospital operations for its inclusion in the Annual Procurement Plan, in order to avoid the repetitive use of Shopping as the mode of procurement.

We recommend that management submit an explanation as to the excessive grant of PEI in excess of the allowed amount of P5,000.00 per employee. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management comply with the requirements of DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3 dated December 2, 2014 in the grant of Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) to LGU officials and employees particularly on the observance of the 55% Personal Services cap/limitation in the LGU’s Annual and Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 325 (a) of R. A. 7160 in order to avoid the payment of personnel benefits not in accordance with the provisions of the Local Government Code and DBM guidelines which will result to disallowances in audit.

We recommend that management require the concerned municipal driver to comply with Section 361 (e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and pertinent section of COA Circular No.75-6 in accomplishing the Driver’s Trip Ticket in order to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.  A repetition of the failure will subject subsequent transactions to possible disallowances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant install measures to facilitate the timely submission of the disbursement vouchers within the prescribed period of submission as required under COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommend that management observe the provisions of COA Circular                    No.94-013 in recognizing its accountability over funds received and recording the release to recipient barangays, as well as in monitoring the proper utilization of funds transferred and eventual reporting thereof. Likewise, we recommend that the municipality and barangays follow the provisions of the approved MOA, so as to avoid technical problems in the future. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Engineer be more proactive in exercising its technical supervision over the identified projects for implementation by the barangay in order to ensure the proper utilization of funds. 	We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer make a follow-up on the status of projects implemented by barangays, in accordance with the provisions of the MOA and DPWH Department Order No.137 dated June 30, 1999. Lastly, we request that the Memorandum of Agreement executed between the Municipality of San Miguel and DPWH, as well as a copy of the PDAF SARO No. SARO-BMB-A-12-T000001508 dated May 8, 2012, be submitted to this Office, for proper evaluation and monitoring purposes.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to send billings and/or demand letters to the individuals concerned who are still in the locality for possible collections. If collection is futile for valid reasons, request authority from the Commission on Audit for the write off of these accounts. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant take the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality based on the result of the request for write off of the accounts.
observations.reason: Action still to be made to possibly write off these amount as these are deemed uncollectible.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately send demand letters to the concerned officers/ employees specifically those whose cash advances are past due for 31 days and over, otherwise instruct the accountant to deduct the amount for whatever is collectible from these personnel. Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the liquidation of all cash advances and see to it that travel advances are liquidated within thirty (30) days after return to permanent official station in accordance with the guidelines issued under COA Circular No. 96-004, implementing the afore-cited Executive Order No. 248, as amended. Follow up the liquidation of cash advances for Intelligence Fund to COA Central Office.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring and follow up were done especially for those cash advances which were not liquidated within the reglamentary period.
observations.reason: Some employees just don't bother liquidating their cash advance until he/she will make again another cash advance which will not be signed or released unless the previous cash advance is liqudated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record purchases of supplies and materials to the corresponding inventory account and compute inventory balances as prescribed under Sections 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office  (a) maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; (b)furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances. Furthermore, we recommend that the Accounting Unit record issuances based on the SSMI submitted. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 AAR
observations.reason: Only the medicine and medical supplies are controlled and monitored considering the huge amount involved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to delegate the preparation of the BRS to competent accounting personnel and if he is too busy to update and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to facilitate prompt submission to COA in order for us to validate the accuracy of cash in bank balance as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Bank Reconciliation Statements for depository accounts under the General Fund, Trust Fund and SEF covering the period January to October 2017 were already submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Bank Reconciliation Statements for the depository accounts under the General Fund Trust Fund were not yet submitted as of audit date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary correction/adjustments for the use of the proper account classification on the Trust Fund as prescribed in the Chart of Accounts for Local Government to ensure its correct presentation and valuation in the financial statements at year end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made the adjusting entry per Unnumbered JEV under the Trust Fund as prescribed in the Chart of Accounts for Local Government to ensure its correct presentation and valuation in the financial statements at year end. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer submitted the required adjusting entry per Unnumbered JEV (Annex-10) of Management Comments submitted by Management last May 17, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the required financial reports of the Local Government Unit as of December 31, 2016 and thereafter, to comply with the prescribed period pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160, PD 1445, COA Circular 95-006 and EO 292.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted reports of accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant on a weekly basis to facilitate prompt submission of accounts and financial reports to the audit team. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer still delayed in the submission of accounts and financial reports for CY 2017 due to lack of personnel and the delay in the submission of accounts to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of accounts to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer require the Liquidating Officer to monitor daily the remittance of the RCCS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already issued the AAR addressed to all RCCs and Water Collectors requiring them to remit their daily collections to the Liquidating Officer at every two o'clock in the afternoon.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer require the Revenue Collecting Officers, Deputized Barangay Treasurers and other Accountable Officers to prepare a Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms issued to them and prepare and submit a Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability of forms issued to them together with the Individual Reports to the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive already issued a memorandum addressed to all RCCs and Water Collectors requiring them to prepare a Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms issued to them and to prepare and submit a Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability of forms issued to them together with the Individual Reports to the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OSCA and the MSWDO conduct the necessary verification and home visitation in determining the final list of the Social Pensioners. Further, immediately after scheduled payout, if the social pensioner did not appear, the MSWDO should conduct home beneficiaries visitation to facilitate delisting and replacement and inform the Disbursing Officer of the status so that he/she can immediately liquidate the unpaid cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO and the OSCA conduct regular monitoring of beneficiaries and conduct home visitation on a monthly and quarterly basis to update the beneficiaries of the social pension prior to submission to DSWD – RO.8.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to prepare and submit the required Schedule with the subsidiary records for the individual loan grant and repayments to determine the accuracy and validity of the account balance as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The LCE already ordered the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required schedule for the Loans Receivables – Others. The Municipal Treasurer had submitted the required schedule for the Loans Receivables – Others to determine the accuracy and validity of the account balance as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and to submit the reports on a monthly basis.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund for the whole year of CY 2016 but not on a monthly basis as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, there was no monthly report submitted by management for CY 2017.
observations.reason: The Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund covering the period January 2017 to December 2017 was submitted late and not on a monthly basis as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned to maintain as current as possible and updated the actual status of work accomplishment of projects as of a given date.
observations.managementAction: Management furnished the audit team with photos of billboards maintained in the site of infrastructure projects for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government fully procure the necessary machines and equipment for its full utilization by the necessary MRF machines and equipment for its full utilization.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already procured the necessary machines and equipment for the necessary MRF programs but the machines and equipment for the solid waste management are necessary because the same were not yet utilized during the year due to lack of power supply.
observations.reason: The MRF of the LGU is not yet fully utilized because the machines and equipment for the solid waste management are available but not yet utilized due to lack of power supply.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the cause of the discrepancy of the unliquidated cash advances balance and make necessary adjustment in the book of accounts of the municipality, which should be properly supported.
observations.managementAction: The balance of unliquidated cash advances in the financial statements as of December 31, 2016 was reconciled with the schedules. The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary journal entries and supporting schedules to reconcile the unliquidated cash advances and the corresponding schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Agency comply with the Memorandum of Agreement and the Guidelines in the disbursement of ESA and explain/justify the discrepancies/deficiencies noted in its implementation of the program.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the required supporting documents and justifications as stated in the Memorandum of Agreement to DSWD-RO8 and the said disbursements were already liquidated with the DSWD-RO8.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and the IRR of RA 9184 and require the submission of the required documentation to warrant the awarding/payment of this project.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required documents and the required documentation to support the procurement and disbursement for the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to submit the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those to be implemented during the year and inform the audit team on the posting of projects signboards and/or public notices within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit a list of on-going projects with evidence of signboards and photos of the site of the infrastructure projects for CY 2017.
observations.reason: There was no complete list of all on-going projects/programs/ activities and those to be implemented during the year submitted by management at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Agency instruct the concerned Officials of the Local Government Unit to conduct an inventory of the land owned by the Local Government Unit and submit documents such as tax declaration and deed of conveyance.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor submitted a list of parcels of land owned by the LGU with supporting documents such as tax declaration and deed of conveyance.
observations.reason: The physical inventory of properties on respective department owned by the LGU as of year-end was not yet submitted as of audit date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97 - 002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 96 - 004 dated April 19, 1996 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 specifically on the following: 1. Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees if their previous cash advances are not yet fully liquidated. 2. Require the officers and employees concerned to liquidate cash advances within thirty (30) days for local travels and sixty (60) if travel abroad after his return to his permanent official station as required under Section 16 of EO 248 as amended. Likewise, other cash advances must, as a rule, be liquidated within the prescribed periods depending upon the nature and purpose of the cash advance as stated in COA Circular No 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. 3. Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the head of agency or his duly authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his cash advance for travel and for other purposes. This is to preclude complaints arising from suspension of salaries due to non-liquidation of their cash advances. 4. Suspend the salaries and delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time cash advances are fully liquidated. 5. Institute appropriate legal action for those who have no salaries that can be withheld.
observations.managementAction: Only the unliquidated cash advances of the retired Municipal Treasurer was liquidated as of end of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Agency of the Municipality of San Miguel, Leyte instruct the concerned Officials of the Local Government Unit to conduct an inventory of the land owned by the Local Government Unit and submit documents such as tax declaration and deed of conveyance.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor implemented the inventory and submitted supporting documents such as title certificates and deeds of conveyance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the accountable officer concerned to comply with the Implementing Guidelines for the Implementation of the Cash for Work Project and paragraphs 6 and 7, Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445 on the submission of complete documents to support payment of any claim.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the Fundamental Principles provided under Section 4 of PD 1445 and the guidelines under COA Circular 2012-001 in their payment of goods and other claims.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to ensure all claims are supported with complete documentation before payments are made pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management follow pertinent provisions of laws, rules and regulations in the grant of personnel benefits. Further, direct all concerned officials and employees to refund the excess amount of PEI received, otherwise all those involved who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable therefore.
observations.managementAction: Management had complied with pertinent provisions and all concerned officials and employees were instructed to refund the excess PEI to the treasury. However, no refund was made by some concerned officials and employees.
observations.reason: No refund was made by concerned officials and employees on the excess PEI received.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit withheld taxes on or before the 10th day of the month following the month of collection as required in Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.
observations.managementAction: The reflected amount of Due to BIR in the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2016 pertained to taxes withheld for the month of December.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Budget Officers to identify the actual expenses reflected in the Obligation Request for the purpose of facilitating the proper classification of expenses by the Municipal Accountant, to provide accurate data for decision making. Henceforth, management should avoid charging to MOOE the payments for job order employees which should be properly charged to Personal Services (PS), in order to avoid circumventing the PS limitation provided in Section 325 (a) of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Proper charging of Job Order wages was implemented by the LGU and the Budget Officers were instructed to identify the actual expenses reflected in the Obligation Request for the purpose of facilitating the proper classification of expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Officer in Charge of the San Miguel Water System to strictly adhere to the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 2006-03 especially in the collection of water bills to generate income.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was able to comply in full compliance with the ordinance and submitted records showing collection efforts.
observations.reason: The audit team cannot validate full compliance due to pending submission of some records and documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require officials concerned to formulate written Standard Operating Procedures or Guidelines in the implementation of the Livelihood Programs, and strictly enforce collections so that more constituents will benefit therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive issued directives to formulate standard operating procedures and strictly enforce collections.
observations.reason: The audit team cannot validate compliance due to pending submission of required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct officials concerned to comply with DBM/NEDA/NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 and incorporate the 5% budget requirement for GAD to ensure full implementation of projects and activities intended to address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the GAD Plan for CY 2016 and CY 2017. However, the GAD Plan and the GAD Accomplishment Report were not yet submitted as of audit date.
observations.reason: The GAD Plan and GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2016 and CY 2017 were not yet submitted as of audit date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to strictly comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 particularly on the timely submission of year-end financial reports and the related schedules for our timely audit and preparation of the annual audit report.

We recommend that management re-visit the guidelines set forth under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 particularly on the identification of programs/projects chargeable to Peace and Order Program, since this is the basis in the computation of the 30% allocation for confidential fund of the LGU

We recommend that the Local School Board be guided by the provisions of Sec. 100 (c) of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 of DECS-DBM and DILG in the identification of priority expenditures chargeable to SEF and in the preparation of its annual budget.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant follow strictly the rules and regulations on the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta benefits to Public Health Workers based on their Daily Time Records and/or actual number of workdays rendered to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommend that management:

Direct the BAC to strictly adhere with the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of Republic Act (RA) 9184 and to stop the practice of splitting Contracts/Purchase Order.

Require the BAC to submit explanation/justification on the splitting of contracts/POs.

We recommend that management stop the practice of transacting with the San Miguel Development Planners Cooperative (SMDPC) pursuant to the Non-Policy Matter 142-2014 on Government employees Cooperative issued by the GPPB.

We recommend strict compliance to the provisions of Section 21 of RA No. 10121 on the proper utilization of LDRRMF with reference to Item 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 for the projects and activities chargeable against the LDRRMF.
We recommend that management adhere to Section 6.3 of NDRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the utilization of the LDRRMF 30% QRF of the Municipality.

We recommend strict adherence to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization. Refrain from charging labor payrolls for job order personnel performing regular and administrative functions.

We reiterate the recommendation to recognize both the asset and liability accounts of the Municipality in connection with the purchase of heavy equipment acquired thru credit financing with the Land Bank of the Philippines.

We recommend to recognize both the asset and liability accounts of the Municipality in connection with the purchase of heavy equipment acquired thru credit financing with the Land Bank of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order to the Accountant directing to record the transaction
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipality to record the income and charges due from/to the depository banks. Moreover, close the depository account maintained with a private bank and transfer the account balance to any authorized government depository banks.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor issued office order to the Accountant directing to record the transaction
observations.reason: Partially implemented, reconciling items were already adjusted, however, account from UCPB still not yet closed

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections intact and on time in accordance with the requirements of the DOF and DBM JC No. 1-81 to protect government funds from unwarranted losses.
observations.managementAction: Send letter and office order to the Municipal Treasurer
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to limit the hiring of job order employees to what is necessary in the normal operation of the Municipality to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenditures. Stop the practice of hiring casual/job order employees for the sake of accommodation and maximize the man-power available in the office.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance to the provisions of RA 9433 for payment of Hazard Duty Pay to MSWD personnel.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the BAC Secretariat, thru the BAC Chairperson to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report of the Municipality and submit to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the BAC chair to prepare & submit the PMR to GPPB
observations.reason:
Not Implemented

Verification with the GPPB website

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management advise the Municipal Engineer to properly supervise the implementation of infrastructure projects to avoid delay and to attain the desired objectives of the project. Also, adhere to the guidelines in the granting of extension of contract time duration in the issuance of suspensions order.
observations.managementAction: Send letter and office order to the Municipal Engineer
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended to advise the BAC Secretariat, through the BAC Chairman strict compliance to the prescribed period of submission of purchase order   and   its   supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2009 – 001 dated Feb. 12, 2009. Further, also advise the Municipal Treasurer to furnish us with the delivery receipt and inspection report within 24 hours from date of delivery.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate submission of the bank reconciliation statements for verification in audit. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to always ensure that monthly bank reconciliation statements are prepared within ten days from receipt of the bank statements and thereafter submit to COA for verification. This is to ensure that deficiencies and errors in the previous months are immediately detected and eventually corrected in the succeeding month. We also recommended that deficiencies and errors uncovered in the reconciliations be immediately adjusted and corrected to bring the Cash in Bank account to correct balance as of the year end.
observations.managementAction: Hired JO and partially completed the conduct of bank reconciliation. Promised to comply with the audit observation.
observations.reason: Reason given is lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation to Management: a. Strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2016; b. Stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same. Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers for continued non-compliance with the existing rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the settlement of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended an optimum and efficient utilization of the 20% EDF and achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes by ensuring that one hundred percent of the programmed projects and activities funded by the 20% EDF are given priority in its implementation to ensure that the ultimate beneficiaries can avail of the projects once completed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Solid Waste Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention in the full implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU, once they received a revised copy of the plan, to be able to comply with the requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission. We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint a permanent employee who will be in-charge of the operation of the solid waste management board.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the 2016 MPDO to follow-up the approval of the Solid Waste Mgt Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to convene and determine the annual supplementary budgetary needs for the operation and maintenance of public schools within the municipality, and correspondingly prepare and approve the annual school board budget giving priority to the: 1) construction, repair, and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities; 2) establishment and maintenance of extension classes; 3) and sports activities. We also recommended that the Local School Board observe strictly the provision of Section 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry to revert back the amount of 1,498,181.52 to the Government Equity account by reversing the original entry made and transfer the unexpended amount of LDRRMF from general fund to trust fund by issuing a check payable to trust fund and to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund books under Special Trust Fund Account 'Trust Liability-DRRM (code 204-01-020)'.

We recommended that the motor vehicles owned by the agency be permanently marked with “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) under which should be written the name and logo of the office or agency using the same.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the acting GSO to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC refrain from splitting of government contracts and ensure compliance with the requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the BAC to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to Section 138 (f) of GAAM, Volume 1. Ascertain that all the needed documents are attached to the disbursement voucher before processing, approval and preparation of check to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Municipal Accountant to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation:
Officials/employees of the NGAs  shall submit a certification that the official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned one and certificate or evidence of service rendered or approved DTR; and

OIC-Municipal Accountant shall strengthen control on the disbursement of funds. Require the submission of the documents mentioned on COA Circular 2012-001 for the grant of RATA to NGAs before it shall be forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for payment.
observations.reason: Management prepared their RATA on an individual basis rather than on payroll thus it would not been properly monitored by the accountant.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to book-up the cost of work/breeding/other animals purchased/acquired/produced. Also, the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist together with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Accountant should conduct an actual inventory on the status of Work/Breeding/Other Animals. The Municipal Treasurer, on the other hand, being the Property Officer should prepare the Property Card for Work/Breeding/Other Animals and Breeding Stocks.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant informed that for CY 2015 still they failed to book-up the cost of work/breeding/ other animals because of their failure to conduct actual inventory on their Work/Breeding/ Other Animals. The Municipal Agriculturist on the other hand, admitted his failure to conduct the same due to voluminous workloads because there are other projects downloaded by the national agency that made them busy Furthermore, the Municipal Mayor instructed again his Municipal Agriculturist to give priority to conduct actual inventory on the status of Work/Breeding/Other Animals for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should instruct the Supply Officer to submit copies of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Auditor for evaluation within  five (5) working  days  from  execution  of a contract  or issuance of a purchase order so that any defect and/or deficiencies of government transactions could be pointed out immediately and gives management the opportunity to correct the same.
observations.reason: The agency still failed to submit timely the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the created inventory committee conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment. Likewise, we recommend that the Accounting Office maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish the correctness of the PPE accounts amounting to P104,388,658.24.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the bank reconciliation statements and to comply with Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to prove the correctness and existence of the cash in bank balances stated in the consolidated year-end balance sheet.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene the Municipal Development Committee to plan the improvement and promotion of the new Public market Building. We also recommend that the Municipal Development Committee design marketing strategies to attract prospective tenants to avail the occupancy of the new Public Market Building.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials, co-terminus employees and those not connected to the municipality pursuant to Items 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a Memorandum to the concerned employees with unliquidated cash advances per Schedule/Aging of Cash Advances as of December 31, 2014 and to submit to the OIC - Municipal Accountant their liquidation documents otherwise, failure to refund and/or submit the liquidation shall be a valid cause for suspension of the payment of their salaries. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Mayor pursue/take legal action against all parties who fail to liquidate their cash advances.

We recommended that all Municipal Officials and Employees refund the excess amount of PEI for CY 2014 totaling P1,660,000.00. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned Municipal Officials see to it that payments for allowances and other personnel benefits should be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing such payments to avoid the suspension and disallowance of the amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Disposal Committee charged to dispose unserviceable properties. We also recommended that the Disposal Committee follow the procedures and accomplish the forms to be used in reporting disposable supplies and property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive work for the provision of a sufficient garage or motor pool where all of motor vehicles will be kept when not in use in order to prevent them from rapid deterioration. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive program the construction of a bodega for unserviceable properties and dispose those beyond repair according to the guidelines on Disposal.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer- Designate prepare and maintain the four (4) sets of records and ledgers for controls. We also recommended that PPE Ledger Card and Property Card be reconciled with the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). We further recommended that the Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Card be reconciled with actual physical stock on hand.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provision of Section 1, Rule 18 of the IRR of Republic Act 10121 and Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1, dated March 25, 2013. We also recommended that Municipal officials responsible for the processing, approval and payment of the disbursements be required to refund the amount totaling P551,250.00.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer – Designate religiously submit to the COA Auditor copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO the annual LDRRMFIPand monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund certified correct by the OIC - Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that the LDRRM Officer - Designate follow the four aspects reflected in the National DRRM Plan in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality. We also recommend that the LDRRM Officer-Designate include the Rehabilitation and Recovery aspect in the programs/projects/activities stated in the LDRRMFIP of the municipality in accordance with the National DRRM Plan.

We recommended that the Municipality of San Miguel through the Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of LDRRM Officer and three (3) Technical Staff following the position titles, qualification standards and salary grades set by Joint Circular No. 2014-1 to be able to properly perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer/Technical Staff. We further recommended that the wages of Job Hires not actually assigned to LDRRM Office be transferred to the appropriate office for the proper charging of the expense account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive convene the GAD Focal Point System who will perform the functions provided in PCW, DILG, DBM and, NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and follow the guidelines in the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget and Annual Accomplishment Report observing the prescribed schedule and activities in C.5 and C.8 of the Joint Circular.

We recommend that the provisions of COA Circulars Nos. 77-55 and 85-55-A dated March 29, 1977 and September 8, 1985, respectively as updated by COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG MC No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 be strictly adhered to and that the Local Chief Executive and Punong Barangays exercise prudence on the use of Municipality and barangay resources and optimize the same for more projects that are worthwhile. We further recommended the refund of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures totalling P558,250.00 incurred by Municipal Officials, Employees and Punong Barangays.

We recommend that the OIC - Municipal Accountant see to it that all approved daily time records and approved applications for leave, if any, be attached to the duly paid payrolls. We further recommend that all payrolls should be signed before submission to this Office to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prepare budget proposals for the development of Sanitary Landfill and Waste Treatment Facility and see to it that PPAs relative thereto be provided with a budget and included in the succeeding year’s annual budget for proper implementation. We also recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board include in the budget the improvement of facilities such as acquisition of equipments for its MRF like plastic shredder and bio-reactor.
We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board make a representation to the Legislative body of the LGU to ratify a Municipal Ordinance pertaining to the collection of garbage fee.

We recommend that the OIC - Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer - Designate prepare and maintain the four (4) sets of records and ledgers for controls. We also recommend that the PPE Ledger Card and Property Card be reconciled with the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). We further recommend that the Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Card be reconciled with actual physical stock on hand.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned department heads to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on each findings and recommendations, as presented in Annex III, and discussed in detail in Part II-Detailed Findings and Recommendations portion of the CY 2014 Annual Audit Report, in compliance with Section 88 of the General Provisions of RA No. 10633, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2014.

We recommend that the total amount of P106,000.00 be refunded by the Disbursing Officer, duly receipted by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding cash deposited to the account of the Municipality. We also recommend that the Disbursing Officer submit an explanation in writing why the amounts granted per payroll were not received in their entirety by recipients despite their signatures on the payroll acknowledging receipt of the total amount indicated therein. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit an explanation in writing why the payrolls attached to the Disbursement Vouchers did not tally with the amount per DV and checks. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist be held liable for the inclusion in the payroll of the persons no longer existing in the barangays and for including twice in the payroll the same grantees.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the cause of the discrepancy and make necessary adjustment in the book of accounts of the municipality, which should be properly supported.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to present JEV and other supporting documents for validation. Per verification of FS as of December 31, 2016 the Advances to Officers and Employees amounted to P3,201,612.37 for General Fund while schedule submitted only amounted to P306,959.25, for Special Education Fund amounted to P176,834.91 while submitted schedule amounted to P183,236.18. Further, submitted schedule for Advances to Special Disbursing Officer amounted to P2,606,510.00 per Trust Fund FS while schedule submitted amounted to P2,614,500.00.
observations.reason: No complete journals, general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers presented; discrepancies remain between ledger balances and submitted schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive comply with the Memorandum of Agreement and Guidelines in the disbursement of ESA and explain/justify the discrepancies/deficiencies noted in its implementation of the program.
observations.managementAction: Documents presented to comply with our audit recommendation were not submitted.
observations.reason: No documents submitted to support disbursement; unable to validate compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the provisions of DPWH D.O. 2, Series of 2015 in the preparation of project plan and cost estimates and use the prescribed form for the Approved Budget for the Contract and implement the purpose of pre-bid conference as cited in Sec. 22.3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act.
observations.managementAction: They have not complied with our audit recommendation because the purpose of pre-bid conference as cited in Sec.22.3 of IRR of RA 9184 was not fully observed.
observations.reason: No justification/document submitted; equipment rental per item work not discussed in pre-bid conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to strictly adhere to the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The AAR was not submitted on time. This is included in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Prescribed period for submission of the report was not complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to comply with the provisions of law on the conduct of physical inventory and the submission of the inventory report.
observations.managementAction: No report/document was submitted for our verification and validation as well as documents to support the recorded amount such as Certificate of Title.
observations.reason: No inventory report submitted; no supporting documents like Certificate of Title presented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management follow pertinent provisions of laws, rules and regulations in the grant of personnel benefits and direct concerned officials to refund excess amounts received.
observations.managementAction: Management contends compliance but supporting documents remain to be submitted; issues under review.
observations.reason: No full compliance demonstrated; documentation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from granting CNA incentive and follow pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the disbursement of public funds.
observations.managementAction: Management contends compliance but did not provide required supporting documents.
observations.reason: No documentation provided to substantiate compliance; issue outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to recognize both the asset and liability accounts of the Municipality in connection with the purchase of heavy equipment acquired thru credit financing with the Land Bank of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order to the Accountant directing to record the transaction
observations.reason: The accountant refuse to record.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the creation of inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties, prepare the Report on the Results of Physical Count of PPE to be reconciled with the accounting records and investigate any discrepancy.  Also advise the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards for PPE and update the ARE for easy identification of the condition and whereabouts of the properties
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No inventory report submitted to the office

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to (a)
speed up the preparation of documents necessary for the registration of the lots in
the Municipality’s name with the Register of Deeds; and (b) exert best efforts to
make the necessary research for the retrieval of documents supporting the untitled
parcels of land including the basis for the valuation of the lots.

2.5 We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Finance
Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties
of the Municipality; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission
of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the
necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.

3.8 We recommended that Management take immediate steps to dispose of all
unserviceable properties to prevent possible loss and avoid further deterioration in
accordance with the guidelines provided by the COA Circular No. 89-296 dated
January 27, 1989.

4.9 We recommended that Management request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass
a resolution establishing specific guidelines on the rates or amounts of financial
assistance being extended by the Municipality and the criteria for the entitlement
for each type thereof to rationalize disbursements under the Donations account and
other MOOE account.

5.5 We recommended that Management comply strictly with the aforementioned
regulation and require the Municipal Engineering Office and other officials
concerned to disclose all transactions involving PPAs for transparency and
accountability.

6.9 We recommended that the Members of the SB consider enacting appropriate
ordinance on the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyance to
prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated proliferation of tricycles
along the Municipality’s highways, which may pose hazards and imminent danger
to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties.

7.4 We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent
provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on the invitation of observers for all stages of the
procurement process.

8.5 We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient
implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely
necessary interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the
programs; (b) direct the other responsible officials concerned to address the issues
that impede the full implementation of priority projects; (c) continue to invest in
high–impact or developmental projects and include in the Annual Investment Plan;
and (d) cause the re-appropriation of the balance of the fund in the ensuing year to
finance development projects for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.

9.8 We recommended that Management (a) require the LDRRMO, through the
LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant
and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month; and (b) continue to allocate 5% of the budget for the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and utilize it in accordance with
the guidelines on the matter.

10.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO (a)
through the Liga ng mga Barangay encourage active participation of barangay
officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by
educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or
recyclable materials from compostable wastes in every household; (b) follow-up
from the Sangguniang Panglalawigan office on the status of the review of the
Municipal Environment Code to expedite the enactment of its “No Littering” policy;
and (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 43
barangays which have remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A.
9003.

11.3 We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue
Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed
period.

12.4 We recommended that Management direct the (a) Accounting Office and the
Human Resource Office to tally their records of premiums withheld and remitted;
and (b) Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full
remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the
following month in compliance with Section 6 of RA No. 8291.

13.2 We recommended that Management appeal or pay the disallowances of
P575,319.30.
We recommend management to assigned personnel to specifically reconcile the balances in the books with the balances in the bank with Account: 0522-1114-30.

We recommend management to issue the notice to liquidate to the concerned AOs of all the unliquidated cash advances and enforce the suspension of salary of the concerned active AO until the compliance thereof. In the event that the AO is already separated from the LGU, the notice be send to the last known address of the concerned AO. Lastly, for AOs who were already deceased, prepare the list of the deceased AOs with the corresponding Death Certificate for a request to COA for write-off of the accounts.

We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Accountant be instructed to make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of Loans Payable account. An adjusting entry be made whose purpose is to reflect the correct balance of Loans account. Books pertaining to CY 2015 had already been closed; any adjustment thereof should have been reflected on CY 2016.

We recommend that the LGU to come up with the necessary plans as enumerated above, and employ a full time Local DRRM Officer then subject itself for reevaluation for further improvements, also to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the disbursement made.
We recommend that management should be mindful of the guidelines set forth in the circular and submit immediately the documents required in the cited circular.

We recommend that the management: (a) require the Revenue Collectors to liquidate/remit their collections as provided in the above Circular to avoid possible misuse and losses and (b) require the Collectors to attach the original copy of accountable forms which have been cancelled to the stub and (c) require the AOs to liquidate their collections before issuance of another accountable forms.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend that:
The Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures;
Concerned officials with government vehicles and the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit regularly thru the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets; and
The Municipal Accountant be required to look into the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the DVs. Claims with incomplete supporting documents must be returned to the claimant before payment be made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the created  inventory
committee  to  conduct  physical  count  of  all  the  property,  plant  and
equipment  owned  by  the  municipality  to  facilitate  the  completion  of
inventory of PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count  of  Property,
Plant and Equipment.

Likewise, we recommended that the Accounting Office maintain the  prescribed
PPELC  which  should  be  reconciled  with  the  RPCPPE  to  establish   the
correctness of the PPE accounts amounting to P118,905,001.25.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  OIC  -  Municipal
 Accountant to immediately prepare the bank reconciliation statements and to
 comply with Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA  Circular
 No. 96-011 to prove the correctness and  existence  of  the  cash  in  bank
 balances stated in the consolidated year-end financial position.

We recommended that the municipality refrain  from  granting  cash  advances
other than travelling expenses to elected officials pursuant to  the  above-
mentioned laws, rules and regulations.

We also  recommended  the  immediate  submission  of  Activity  Designs  and
liquidation report pertaining thereto.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require concerned  accountable
officers to immediately settle all  cash  advances  after  the  purpose  for
which they were granted had already been served pursuant to  Section  89  of
PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise  impose  proper  sanction
such as the withholding of salaries of those who are still  in  service  for
failing to comply with the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to  it  that  all  approved
daily time records or certificate of service and approved  applications  for
leave, if any, be attached to the duly paid payrolls.

We further recommended that all payrolls should be signed before  submission
to this Office to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that  Acknowledgment  Receipt  for  Equipment  and  Inventory
Custodian Slip be issued on property and  equipment  purchased  pursuant  to
the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations.  The ARE and ICS should  be
in the name of the official who is in actual possession of the  property  to
pinpoint responsibility and accountability.  The person entrusted  with  its
custody and control shall be responsible for its proper  use  and  care  and
shall exercise due diligence in the utilization and safekeeping thereof.

We recommended that the  municipality  submit  the  supplemental  budget  or
appropriation  ordinance  relative  to  the  release   of   P306,599.08   to
facilitate further validation of the  claim  in  accordance  to  the  above-
mentioned laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that the municipality demand an  Official  Receipt  from
the concerned supplier  to  support/prove  settlement  of  the  account  and
submit the same as supporting document of the concerned transaction.

We  strongly  recommended  that  management  observe  properly   procurement
process in accordance with RA 9184.

Likewise, we recommended that the municipality  shall  draw  check  payments
directly  in  the  name  of  the  supplier  in  conformity   with   existing
regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee instruct  BAC  Secretariat
to post all procurement in the Portal as prescribed in  the  above-mentioned
laws, rules and regulations to promote transparency and achieved  efficiency
in the procurement process of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant to submit the above-mentioned  documents  to  facilitate  further
review of the concerned transactions.

We strongly recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  instruct  the  GAD
Focal Point System  to  carefully  study  PCW,  DILG,  DBM  and  NEDA  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and follow the procedural guidelines in  the
formulation of GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report.

We likewise recommended that the GAD Focal Point System see to it  that  GPB
is submitted to DILG for review and approval in order that the same will  be
incorporated in their Annual Budget to be enacted by their Sanggunian.

We  further  recommended  that  the  Sanggunian  Bayan  pass  an   ordinance
concerning the adoption of a Gender and  Development  Code  to  enforce  the
formulation of GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer - Designate follow the  four  thematic
areas reflected in  the  National  DRRM  Plan  in  the  preparation  of  the
LDRRMFIP of the municipality.

We also  recommended  that  the  LDRRM  Officer-Designate  include  the  two
thematic areas namely Disaster  Response  and  Disaster  Rehabilitation  and
Recovery in the programs/projects/activities stated in the LDRRMFIP  of  the
municipality in accordance with the National DRRM Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal  Entry  Voucher
(JEV) in all financial transactions of the Municipality and attach the  same
to disbursement vouchers and journals pursuant to  Section  31  of  the  New
Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume II.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive convene the Municipal
Development Committee to plan the improvement and promotion of the new
Public market Building.

We also recommend that the Municipal Development Committee design
marketing strategies to attract prospective tenants to avail the
occupancy of the new Public Market Building.

We recommended that the OIC – Municipal Accountant submit the
following documents (a) approved scholarship program including the
detailed qualification and criteria of selection and the amount of

Agreement/contract between the Municipality and SAMODA, (d) approved
