signed by the Punong Barangay pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a Disposal
Committee charged to dispose unserviceable properties.

We also recommended that the Disposal Committee follow the procedures
and accomplish the forms to be used in reporting disposable supplies
and property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive work for the provision of
a sufficient garage or motor pool where all of motor vehicles will be
kept when not in use in order to prevent them from rapid deterioration.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive program the
construction of a bodega for unserviceable properties and dispose those
beyond repair according to the guidelines on Disposal.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and General Services
Officer - Designate prepare and maintain the four (4) sets of records
and ledgers for controls.

We also recommended that PPE Ledger Card and Property Card be
reconciled with the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant, and
Equipment (RPCPPE).

We further recommended that the Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Card be
reconciled with actual physical stock on hand.

We recommended that unexpended LDRRMF amounting to  P3,552,235.81  as
of December 31, 2014 be transferred to a special trust fund pursuant to
Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer-Designate prioritize the
procurement of early warning systems, search, rescue and retrieval
equipments and other equipage useful for disaster preparedness and
disaster response other than administrative equipments and assess the
adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipments in
the LDRRM Office.

We likewise recommended that the LDRRM officer-Designate submit the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment charged
to LDRRM Fund which include the newly procured items amounting to
P1,026,151.50.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to adhere strictly with the provision of Section 1, Rule 18
of the IRR of Republic Act 10121 and Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG
Joint Circular No. 2013-1, dated March 25, 2013.

We also recommended that Municipal officials responsible for the
processing, approval and payment of the disbursements be required to
refund the amount totaling P551,250.00.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer – Designate religiously submit
to the COA Auditor copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO the annual
LDRRMFIP and monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund
certified correct by the OIC – Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipality of San Miguel through the
Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of
LDRRM Officer and three (3) Technical Staff following the position
titles, qualification standards and salary grades set by Joint
Circular No. 2014-1 to be able to properly perform their functions and
responsibilities as LDRRM Officer/Technical Staff.

We further recommended that the wages of Job Hires not actually
assigned to LDRRM Office be transferred to the appropriate office for
the proper charging of the expense account.

We recommended that the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 77-55 and 85-55-A
dated March 29, 1977 and September 8, 1985, respectively as updated by
COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG MC No. 2011-59
dated April 25, 2011 be strictly adhered to and that the Local Chief
Executive and Punong Barangays exercise prudence on the use of
Municipality and Barangay resources and optimize the same for more
projects that are worthwhile. We further recommended the refund of
unnecessary and extravagant expenditures totaling P558,250.00 incurred
by Municipal Officials, Employees and Punong Barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board make a
representation to the Legislative body of the LGU to ratify a Municipal
Ordinance pertaining to the collection of garbage fee.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement the priority
projects of the Municipality strictly in accordance with the Program of
Works and the Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates. Any
deviation thereof, shall be reported in a Revised Program of Works and
a Revised detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates in order to
monitor the status or percentage of completion of the project.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct regular
monitoring of priority projects being implemented by the Municipality.

We likewise recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan enact the
corresponding Municipal Ordinance imposing fees for the burial tombs.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer submit the
request for demolition prior to demolition of public structures and
prepare the Report of Waste Materials detailing the materials taken
from the demolished structures of Barangay San Jose and other future
demolitions.

Also, we recommended that the General Service Officer- Designate prepare
the Acknowledgement receipt for Equipment (ARE) for various carpentry
tools purchased by the Municipality and the Inventory Report on excess
materials taken from various projects.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the  concerned
department heads to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on each  finding  and
recommendation, as presented in Annex III, and discussed in detail  in  Part
II-Detailed Findings and Recommendations  portion  of  the  CY  2015  Annual
Audit Report, in compliance with Section 93 of the General Provisions of  RA
No. 10651, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU, prepare the inventory reports and submit a copy thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Likewise, we recommend that the accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, thus, fairly presenting these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of properties and continue to identify in the other departments/offices those PPEs and inventories which are already unserviceable or non-functional and facilitate disposal thereof in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting and property records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the assets of the municipality in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer evaluate the necessity of maintaining funds with FCB. If found justified, we recommend that management adhere to the guidelines set forth in DOF Circular No.001-2015 to uphold its purposes, in order to prevent sanctions that may be imposed by DOF.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant regularly submit all the DM/CM indicated in the BRS and attach the paid and cancelled checks thereto pursuant to the pertinent Paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011 in order to facilitate a comprehensive review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.

We recommend that the Municipal Account draw the necessary journal entries to record the transactions which have long been reflected in the BRS as reconciling items in order to fairly present the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entry reverting the payable and cash in bank accounts for fairness of presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant work on the tracing the items which caused discrepancies of Cash in Bank balances in the two separate records to fairly present a reliable amount in the financial statements.

We recommended that management continue the practice of issuance of demand letters to all concerned officers and employees, requiring immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, we recommend that management withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management be compliant more compliant with the provisions of Joint Circular No.2015-01 of COA-DBM-DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND relative to the release of confidential fund.
We recommend that management submit an explanation on the foregoing noted deficiency.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, enforce immediate and regular remittance of daily collections by the Collecting Officer to the Liquidating Officer, pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS in order to fairly present collections of the LGU in a given period and avoid possible loss or misuse thereof.
We recommend that management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, adhere to the requirements in the collection, remittance, liquidation and deposit of collections.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer comply with the provisions of Section 32 of Manual on NGAS in order to prevent misappropriation or loss of public funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the unreconciled difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to make an immediate review and tracing of differences between the two accounts once financial statements are generated to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.

We recommend that management demand the immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality, by immediately closing the account Due from LGUs in the year the funds are already expended and liquidated by the recipient barangays.

We recommend that management make the necessary representation with the DBM in order to expedite the release of the Authority to Purchase for the rescue vehicle and begin the procurement process therefor. It is also recommended that henceforth, management accelerate the preparation and submission of required documents for the fund transfer, as well as develop a closer monitoring and communication and coordination with the source agencies of fund transfers so as to speed up the release and utilization of funds, for a more timely delivery of services to the public.

We recommend that management submit the necessary documents to prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the social pension. Likewise, it is recommended that management explain the payment of social pension even though the period to which it pertained had not yet lapsed.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the processing of land titling. It is also recommended that the management provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of titles and inform and furnish the Audit Team, any data and documents as to the status of land titling. Moreover, it is recommended that management ensure that the purpose for every land acquisition has been met for the benefit of the general public.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor reconcile their records to determine the cause of the noted discrepancy, in order to fairly present the Land account in the financial statements and submit the documents to the Audit Team, for review.

We recommend that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion. Likewise, corresponding liquidation report for the construction of evacuation center is requested for submission, for verification.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the MPDC, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and concerned department heads to immediately submit copies of the perfected government contracts, purchase orders already issued/executed, together with the complete supporting documents, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof, as well as ocular inspection of implemented projects. Otherwise, reviewed fund utilization reports which are deficient in documentation shall be suspended in audit.

We recommend that management provide reasons as to the non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management implement programs/projects/activities on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2011-1 in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public or to enjoy the comfort that the municipal employees of officials could have experienced therefrom.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the root cause of the negative balance of the Government Equity account under the sub-fund 20% Development Fund, in order to fairly present the account in the individual financial statements. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Government Equity account, especially that adjustments effected thereto are substantial in amount, for transparency and easy monitoring purposes.

We recommend that management provide reasons as to the partial or non-implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, management is enjoined to fast track the implementation of programs/projects/activities identified in the budget and ensure inclusions of the projects enumerated under RA 9003 to serve the purpose of the created funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant regularly compute and provide for the corresponding depreciation for all depreciable assets in order to present accurate balances of the assets in the financial statements of the SEF.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant conduct a review on the collectability of the dormant receivable accounts, as well the validity and complete documentation for the payable accounts, and take appropriate action pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2016-005, in the event that write-off of such accounts are warranted, in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the management, in coordination with the Local School Board, revisit the provisions of R.A.5477 in order to arrive at identification and implementation of projects/activities which are more fruitful and reflective of the intents of R.A.5477, to fully serve its purpose.

We recommend that management provide reasons as to the huge increases in other hospital expenses which resulted in increasing net loss. Likewise, management has to be more prudent in its disbursements in order to lessen expenditures and arrive at a net income for hospital operations, so as not to deplete the much needed fund in the General Fund-Proper.

We recommend that management provide reasons as to the huge increases in other Masanao Restaurant expenses which contributed to the increasing net loss. Likewise, management has to be more aggressive in its marketing strategies in order to sufficiently promote Masanao Restaurant, not only in its locality, but to neighbouring towns as well. It is also recommend that management be more prudent in its disbursements and make periodic monitoring and review of Masanao operations, for quality assurance purposes.

We recommend that management establish an accounting and operating system in running Masanao operations to have a system of internal control and proper administrative practices installed, to ensure compliance with laws and regulations and to provide for efficient, economical and effective operations.

We recommend that the management, specifically the Bids and Awards Committee, review the Manual on Negotiated Procurement-Community Participation in order to use it only on applicable transactions in cooperation with the properly identified community groups, so as not to evade or defeat the purposes of R.A.9184.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to adhere to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the regular submission of LDRRM report to facilitate timely review thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfer the remaining balances of 30% standby fund and 70% mitigation fund in CY 2016, pursuant to par.5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002, to have a systematic and organized determination of standby fund available for use without delay, in case of emergency or calamities.
We recommend that management submit an explanation to provide clarification on the foregoing noted deficiency.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Person to ensure the allocation of at least five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU in compliance with DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Section B, Part 5 of the Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units.
