We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) cause strict compliance by the Agency with the DOF Circular for purposes of promoting better fund management; and (b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer the immediate transfer of cash balances from the existing private bank depository accounts to an authorized government depository bank within the prescribed deadline to avoid the imposition of sanctions by the DOF/Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) as mentioned in the aforementioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: (a) and (b) The Internal Revenue Allotment of the Municipality have been transferred to Land Bank of the Philippines. However, a memorandum letter was issued by the DOF stating that Philippine Veterans Bank is included as one of the authorized depository bank, so they didn’t close their account in the said bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) continually support initial effort put forth by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) to smooth down records of taxpayers; (b)  provide additional manpower and equipment to the Office, whenever necessary, so that the outputs/reports vital to bookkeeping of receivables are ready before the end of CY 2016; and (c) direct the MTO and the Municipal Assessor to coordinate closely in the cleansing of property assessment and tax records and consider for analysis the impact of the restricted properties on the  determination of the appropriate assessed values for purposes of computation of taxes due.
observations.managementAction:
(a) and (b) The Municipality provided additional manpower and equipment to update the records of the taxpayers.

(c) The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor are coordinating on their tax records.  In addition, the Municipality, as of this date, has two barangays left for updating the record of their delinquent taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management (a) strictly enforce the COA rules and regulations on the granting, monitoring, liquidation, and reporting of cash advances; (b) properly plan all disbursements at the start of the calendar year to avoid rush spending thru cash advance; and (c) cause the immediate submission to the ICFAU, COA Central Office of the liquidation reports covering all unsettled cash advances for Confidential Fund, considering that the term of the Mayor will expire on June 30, 2016.  A copy of the transmittal letter stamped received by the COA Central Office should be furnished to the Agency Accountant for recording of the liquidation and to the Audit Team for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Management enforced the COA rules and regulations in granting, monitoring, liquidation and reporting in cash advances.

(b) The various offices prepared a plan for their calendar year activities to minimize the need for spending thru cash advances.

(c) The liquidation reports of the cash advances for Confidential Fund of the past mayor have submitted to the ICFAU, COA Central Office. The transmittal letter stamped received by the ICFAU was provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the conduct of charity programs/activities which are consistent with the PCSO line-up of services under the Lotto Charity Fund of R.A. No. 1169 to become more effective partner of the National Government in the extension of services to the marginal sector in the community.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted charity programs/activities which are consistent with the PCSO line-up of services under the Lotto Charity Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the Chart of Accounts in the recording of the LGU share from PCSO Charity Fund for better control purposes; and (b) ensure that income from all sources are properly appropriated, allotted, and obligated only for duly approved programs, projects and activities during the budget year.
observations.managementAction:
Receipts of the LGU share from PCSO Charity Fund have been recorded in conformity with the Chart of Accounts. The required correcting entry was entered in the books in the month of February 2016.

The Municipality has properly recorded the income and expenses of the PCSO Charity Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team a complete set of documents supporting the distribution of goods from the Municipal offices concerned to the intended beneficiaries; and (b) ensure utmost compliance with the provisions of Section 4 of P.D. 1445 to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances in future transactions.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipal Accountant submitted the necessary supporting documents for the distribution of goods to the beneficiaries. 

(b) Management complied on attaching complete supporting documents prior to approving the claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) continue to invest in high-impact undertakings to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development fund and refrain from venturing into non-capital investment projects under the Fund; and b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within the target duration through close monitoring and supervision by the municipal officials in-charge; thereby taking full advantage of the developmental benefits.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipality continued to invest in high-impact undertakings to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

(b) The Municipal Engineer closely monitored and supervised the implementation of the different projects out of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to align the existing five-year Plan with the four thematic areas prescribed under R.A. 10121 and allocate available funds in accordance with such categorization to avoid mingling of funds;  (b) advise the MDRRMO to strictly follow the LDRRMFIP based on the four thematic areas and categorization under the 30% QRF-70% MF rule to ensure smooth implementation of activities; (c) properly account for disbursements made, specifically inventory and equipment acquisition and issuance, for purposes of promoting transparency and accountability; and (d) instruct the Municipal Accountant/Municipal Budget Officer/Municipal Treasurer to promptly transfer any fund balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund to ensure availability of funds for unforeseen events in the ensuing years.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Available funds were allocated.

(b) The MDRRMO was advised to follow the LDRRMFIP.

(c) Management has conducted an inventory of the acquired assets.

(d) The balance of the LDRRMF was transferred remaining at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; (b) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (c) request for inputs or feedbacks from the DepEd officials/School District Supervisors/ Schools Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures and ensure that PPAs are in accordance with the DepEd criteria and the provisions of laws, rules, and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; (d) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its primary goals and objectives; and (e) monitor closely the implementation of the PPAs and take appropriate remedies, such as realignment or re-programming of funds, if necessary, thereby retaining in the Annual Budget only those relevant and priority activities in consultation with the school heads/project proponents.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Local School Board prepared a budget in accordance with the legal and regulatory requirement on the budgeting, allocation, and utilization of the SEF.

(b) Management refrained in accommodating expenditures on activities on which the DepEd have a direct mandate to execute.

(c) Management requested inputs from the District Supervisors.

(d) Management maximized the utilization of SEF.

(e) The Municipal Budget Officer closely monitored the implementation of the PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) abide strictly with the rules and regulations of R.A. No. 9184 with the end view of promoting transparency and competitiveness in the procurement of consulting services; and (b) exercise prudence in determining the best options in the outsourcing of services by putting weight on the inventory of capabilities existing within the Agency to save on cost and promote efficiency among the personnel responsible for a particular task.
observations.managementAction:
We strictly abide the rules and regulations of RA No. 9184 for the end view of promoting transparency and competitiveness in the procurement of consulting services.

We exercised prudence in determining the best option by putting weight on the inventory to save on cost and promote efficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO (a) continually involve the participation of the barangay officials in all environmental concerns; (b) strictly enforce the implementation of the existing environmental laws and encourage constituents to abide and cooperate for the success of the program; (c) intensify  information campaign on the mandatory segregation of wastes in every household; (d) make immediate representation from the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the review of the Municipal Environment Code and the enactment of ordinance on “No Littering” to provide legal backbone on MENRO’s solid wastes programs, projects and activities for the ensuing years; and (e) exert effort to look for possible resources to adequately finance the Solid Waste Management Plan during the ten-year period.

We recommended that management (a) cause the stoppage of payment of membership dues and contributions to all organizations/ associations which are not recognized as local leagues under the Local Government Code; (b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate funds strictly for the leagues of municipal mayors, vice mayors, and other local elective officials as explicitly provided for in Section 258 of  R.A. No. 7160; and (c) as an alternative, develop an in-house human resource development program catering to the training needs of all department  heads/key personnel.  In such manner, their professional growth could be assessed fairly in terms of the goals and objectives of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Management stopped the payment of membership dues and contribution to all organization which are not recognized as Local League under the Local Government Code.

(b) The Municipal Budget Officer obligated funds strictly for the Municipal Leagues.
observations.reason: (c) Unimplemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) promptly prepare the remittance vouchers based exactly on the amounts withheld to avoid retention of substantial balances under the Due to BIR account and prevent possible imposition of penalty; (b) exert effort to work back and analyze prior years’ credits of P335,179.11 and prepare remittance voucher, if warranted; (c) obtain confirmation receipts from the BIR for remittances made thru the BPI; and (d) ensure safe remittance either thru online payment direct to BIR or thru government depository banks authorized to accept remittances under existing schemes offered by the BIR.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipal Accountant promptly prepared remittances vouchers.

(b) Municipal Accountant is reconciling the prior year balance of P335,179.11.

(c) Municipal Accountant obtained confirmation receipts from BIR for the remittances made thru Bank of the Philippines (BPI).

(d) The Municipal Accountant enrolled on the online payment direct to BIR.

observations.recommendation: Management should give priority attention on the preparation and cause the immediate and regular submission of the bank reconciliation statements pursuant to Sec 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 dated October 02, 1996 and other pertinent rules and regulations
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the collections for the day should be deposited/remitted intact daily to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer. On the other hand, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that every time collections are turned over to him, all accountable forms whether issued or not must be presented and accounted for.

	To ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to update his cashbooks and record immediately all transactions for the day in consonance with COA Circular No. 2002-003.
observations.reason: Due to other functions assigned to the ICO-MT, the balances in the cash book were not keep updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate shall issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is processed and approved as required under existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance to ensure efficient and effective control of the cash advance.

Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor and verify the issuance of the accountable forms  issued to the accountable officers and check the number of issuances and number of the remaining balances of the accountable forms and fill-up properly Sections C and D and the VERIFICATION AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT column of the RCD by indicating the amount in words and in figures received by him for easy monitoring of the movement of the accountable forms and the remittances of collections;

 The accountable officers, upon the conduct of cash examination of all his cash and accounts shall render an accounting thereof.
observations.reason: There are still accountable forms that were not immediately presented during the cash examination

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the collectors/accountable officers to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer who in turn shall consolidate the reports in the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.reason: Management failed to continue the submission of the report.

observations.recommendation:
Tthe concerned officials observe the limitations prescribed under Section 5.0 of COA Circular 2012-013 dated October 29, 2012 and EO 298 and refrain from claiming excessive  travelling expenses including incidental expenses plus transportation expenses from the hotel to the venue and vice versa; and 

the concerned officials submit documents as proofs of their taxi hired on the seminar proper.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor or the member of the Sangguniang Bayan in charge of the Education Committee shall establish a criteria or a well-defined policy, guidelines and internal control in determining or granting a student the scholarship program as basis for evaluation.
We recommend that the BAC furnish the Auditor a printed copy of the PMR to provide basis for monitoring the status of the procurement activities of the municipality.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat started to tabulate the procurement status, however, it was not keep updated.

observations.recommendation: Management should give priority attention on the preparation and cause the immediate and regular submission of the bank reconciliation statements pursuant to Sec 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 dated October 02, 1996 and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
The accountable officers concerned shall liquidate their cash advances so that proper accounting could be made thereof to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.

The Municipal Accountant as an internal auditor should strictly implement the rules and regulations on cash advances to avoid accumulation of huge amount of unliquidated cash advances. Lastly, limit the granting of cash advance to those provided for in Section 03 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: An inventory committee should be created to conduct the annual physical inventory of all assets under PPE to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements.  In the preparation of the report, the prescribed format should be used and that the different columns should be properly filled-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for.  Assignment of property number should be properly done so that each of the items can be easily identified and properly classified. On the other hand, the Accounting Unit shall prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards; Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card and Real Property Ledger Cards.  Once the physical inventory of PPE is already available it shall be reconciled with the (a)  PPE ledger card, (b)  the control accounts and (c)  property cards.  If any discrepancies exist, it should be immediately verified and adjusted.  Further, reclassification of obsolete and unserviceable assets, as well as assets no longer used by LGU-San Miguel to “Other Assets” account should be made.
observations.reason: Management conducted actual physical inventory taking of all its assets but the report was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
Management shall: 

Require the collectors/designated collectors to incorporate all the accountable forms issued to them in the Report of Collections and Deposits upon remittance; and

Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand the presentation of all unused accountable forms issued to the accountable officers, together with the collections made during each remittance.

The Municipal Budget Officer through the Municipal Mayor shall provide, allocate or include a position of a Municipal DDRM Officer and three (3) statutory positions as required  under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01 dated April 04, 2014. They should create the positions mandated under the law to man the Office of the LDRRMO who is responsible for setting the direction, development, implementation and coordination of disaster risk reduction and management programs.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit on time the RCI together with the Disbursement Vouchers to the Municipal Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant on the other hand, upon receipt of the reports should prepare the trial balance monthly together with the required supporting documents as provided in Section 70 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (Local Government Units), Volume I, to facilitate verification by this Office. Should there be any corrections and/or unaccounted items that may be noted in the process, the necessary adjustments should be effected immediately. for proper documentation. Section 4 of PD 1445.

On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer shall also ensure that the Municipal Accountant had already certified the DVs before making any payment pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We require the Hon. Municipal Mayor to act assertively on the disallowances issued and enforce the immediate refund of the said disallowances considering the loss of government funds was already incurred. Tolerating delayed refunds would further contribute to additional loss of government funds. In addition, Municipal Accountant shall prepare Subsidiary Ledger for this account for future transactions to facilitate sending of demand letters to individual concerned pursuant to Section 64, Volume I of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.

All persons liable for the disallowed transactions totaling P7,538,588.83 should make an appeal in writing within six (6) months from receipt of the notices of disallowance based on the procedures set forth in the 20009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the OIC – Municipal Accountant submit the following documents (a) approved scholarship program including the detailed qualification and criteria of selection and the amount of scholarship to be granted to each Municipal scholars/beneficiaries, (b) Sangguniang Bayan resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with SAMODA, (c) notarized memorandum of Agreement/contract between the Municipality and SAMODA, (d) approved Individual Social Case Study Report and (e) Certificate of Indigency signed by the Punong Barangay pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that unexpended LDRRMF amounting to P3,552,235.81 as of December 31, 2014 be transferred to a special trust fund pursuant to Section 21, paragraph 3 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We likewise recommend that the LDRRM Officer - Designate and the OIC - Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents enumerated above for our evaluation in the hiring of job order personnel charged to the LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer-Designate prioritize the procurement of early warning systems, search, rescue and retrieval equipments and other equipage useful for disaster preparedness and disaster response other than administrative equipments and assess the adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipments in the LDRRM Office. We likewise recommended that the LDRRM officer-Designate submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment charged to LDRRM Fund which include the newly procured items amounting to P1,026,151.50.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and OIC - Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the above cited implementing laws, rules and regulations. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and OIC – Municipal Accountant responsible for the processing, approval and payment of disbursements refund the total amount of P14,910.00 spent for religious purposes such as Patronal Town Fiesta, Barangay Fiesta and Christmas Basketball Tournament.

We recommend that the OIC - Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of documents supporting the disbursement of salaries and wages of Job Orders to facilitate verification. We also recommend that each JO personnel prepare their individual accomplishment report noted by their respective Department Head. We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor refrain from assigning collection function to JOs assigned in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer implement the priority projects of the Municipality strictly in accordance with the Program of Works and the Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates. Any deviation thereof, shall be reported in a Revised Program of Works and a Revised detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates in order to monitor the status or percentage of completion of the project. We further recommend that the Municipal Engineer conduct regular monitoring of priority projects being implemented by the Municipality. We likewise recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan enact the corresponding Municipal Ordinance imposing fees for the burial tombs. We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer submit the request for demolition prior to demolition of public structures and prepare the Report of Waste Materials detailing the materials taken from the demolished structures of Barangay San Jose and other future demolitions. Also, we recommended that the General Service Officer- Designate prepare the Acknowledgement receipt for Equipment (ARE) for various carpentry tools purchased by the Municipality and the Inventory Report on excess materials taken from various projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer post a notification to the public and other forms of announcement and/or publicity for or otherwise relating to the PPA being implemented by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer and MPDC submit to this Office at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going government programs/projects/activities (“P/P/A”) of the Municipality and those that are to be implemented during the year.

We recommend that management cause the refund of the financial assistance it granted to the 66 Job Order employees in the total amount of P334,000.00 for lack of legal basis. We further recommend Job Order employees to refund the amount claimed for travel expenses as shown in the attached schedule since this is not allowed. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to revisit the guidelines prescribed in DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, especially Sections 2.3 and 4.0, to ensure the proper utilization of funds and to stop the practice of charging regular operating expenses to the 20% Development Fund. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a separate accounting for the Economic Enterprise for Community Seed Banking Program and Solid Waste Management Program.

We recommend Job Order employees to refund the amount granted to them for which there is lack of legal basis. We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Agriculturist, Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Social and Development Officer, as project implementors, to submit a monitoring report on the Livelihood Programs implemented. We recommend also that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant immediately stop the practice of charging regular operating expenses to the 20% Development Fund in order that the fund would not be unnecessarily depleted and to avoid the incurrence of disallowances.

We recommend that employees shall be allowed only the reimbursement of actual fare and reasonable cost of meals and incidental expenses from official station to destination within the 50-kilometer radius not exceeding Four Hundred Pesos (P400.00) pursuant to Section 5 of Executive Order No. 298, dated March 23, 2004. We further recommend that employees travelling outside Iloilo City or those attending conventions shall be allowed only the equivalent travel expenses pursuant to Section 4 of Executive Order No. 298, dated March 23, 2004, and transportation expenses per Section 3.1.1.2 of COA Circular No.96-004, dated April 19, 1996. We also recommend that claim for taxi fare shall be allowed only after presentation of Official Receipt issued by the driver of the taxi service. Failure to submit the required Official Receipt shall be allowed only the maximum amount of Seventy Five Pesos (P75.00).

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant in order to ensure the proper utilization of funds should charge expenses aligned with the identified activities found in the approved Annual Investment Plan. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a separate accounting for the Economic Enterprise for Community Seed Banking Program and Solid Waste Management Program of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw an adjusting entry recognizing the account Construction Materials Inventory in order to fairly present in the financial statements, the procured goods and materials under the custody of the LGU which are due for issuance upon start of the construction period. This practice shall properly inform the users of financial information on the correct status of project implementation. Likewise, we recommend that COA be furnished a copy of the correcting journal entry voucher.
observations.managementAction: A letter explaining the alterations made on the Charge Sales Invoice, by the Supplier, was already submitted to the Audit Team. Delivery Receipts for the 2nd and final delivery of construction items, as a proof of delivery of the lacking items per 1st partial delivery, were already submitted to the Audit Team on March 2017. An adjusting Journal Entry to recognize construction in progress has already been made. It would be difficult now to recognize the materials inventory because the waterworks administrator does not maintain record of receipt and issuance of materials delivered and issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Engineer closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant as to the status of funds received and monitor utilization thereof in observance of Section 84 (2) of P.D. 1445 to avoid overpayment to contractors, based on funds received, which is detrimental to the implementation of other projects.
observations.managementAction: Close monitoring was done for the next payments and follow ups to the Provincial Gov't. for the speedy release of funds especially if the contractor has complied already with all the requirements and has sent/demanded for billing/payment already. Liquidation of funds was fast tracked (assign staff in the acctg. dept to monitor funds and liquidation) for the speedy release of funds considering that no release shall be granted unless previous funds is liquidated.
observations.reason: There were still some projects implemented with a negative fund balance per submitted liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adhere to the provisions of Sections 15 and 25 of NGAS Manual by enforcing remittance of collections by the collecting officers, intact and daily, to the Liquidating Officer and that the latter demand the collecting officers of the same, in case of noncompliance therewith, in order to properly recognize collections and income in the period of occurrence.
observations.managementAction: Enforced remittance of collections intact for the period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, MPDC and Municipal Engineer cause the submission of the required documents, for proper evaluation of LGU compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No.2015-45. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management observe the provisions of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No.2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Documen-tary require-ments has been already complied in last quarter of 2016 and ready for submission as to date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Waterworks Administrator and Supplier to provide explanation as to the noted deficiencies on varying quantities of delivered items per Charge Sales Invoices and Delivery Receipts. 
We recommended that management determine and require the concerned party to submit explanation on the presence of alterations on the Charge Sales Invoices.
We recommended that management require the Waterworks Administrator to submit explanation as to the noted deficiency in the Delivery Receipt. Likewise, we recommend that the Waterworks Administrator attach a proof of delivery of the afore-cited lacking items per 1st partial delivery, upon submission of the 2nd partial payment liquidation report. Furthermore, we recommend that the concerned personnel in-charge of the preparation and issuance of the subject Purchase Order provide an explanation as to the absence of delivery terms therein. Lastly, we recommend that henceforth, management ensure that procurement activities of the LGU is properly monitored and executed by providing clear-cut terms with the supplier as to the delivery of ordered items in order to safeguard government funds against possible loss due to payment of undelivered items and avoid delay in project implementation due to erroneous delivery of items.
observations.managementAction: An explanation letter with attachment of proof of delivery of the afore-cited lacking items per 1st partial delivery was already made and for submission before December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation as to the delay of the supplier in posting the Performance Security. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, the BAC observe the posting requirements of Performance Security pursuant to Section 39 of RA 9184 in order to guarantee the faithful performance by the winning bidder of its obligations under the contract in accordance with its Bidding Documents
observations.managementAction: Sometimes during the serving of Purchase Order the materials were completely delivered by the supplier. The posting of perfor-mance security was tolerated as per RA 9184,but as of this time the BAC agreed delayed posting of performance security shall not be granted.
observations.reason: There were still a few transactions with delayed posting of performance security.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Hospital Administrator, in close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Management, immediately find means to hire the appropriate and competent personnel to operate the Ultrasound and ECG Machines to make it operational and income-generating in order to satisfactorily serve the purpose of the loan and provide new health services to the public.
observations.managementAction: For the ultrasound, coordina-tion with the midwives of the munici-pality and the midwives of catchment area is yet to be made. The use thereof is recom-mended to pregnant women for ultrasound to provide the midwives honoraria for every referred patient.              For the ECG, it is recom-mended to be utilized by the newly-hired chief nurse to operate the ECG machine as she was operating such machine as an intensive care unit nurse.
observations.reason: No report has been submitted to the Audit Team to prove receipt of income generated by the equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant refrain from certifying OBRs and recognizing payables of transactions not supported with adequate and valid documents.
observations.managementAction: This practice has been corrected starting 2016 and onward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Hospital Administrator to stop the practice of splitting requisitions and award of contracts and that the LGU Bids and Awards Committee faithfully observe the provisions of Section 54.1, Rule XVI of the R-IRR of RA No.9184 and prohibit splitting of requisitions.  Furthermore, we recommend that management require the Hospital Administrator to submit sworn justifications for practicing splitting of requisitions.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendation was well-taken and complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit an explanation as to the cause of the noted deficiency, for further evaluation. Henceforth, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive, cause the installation and implementation and monitoring of a sound internal control in order to safeguard issuance of bloated Purchase Orders and wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: We made a survey of prices so to avoid the same circumstances in the future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant cause the remittance of barangay shares of real property taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to the provisions of Section 271 of Republic Act 7160 to avoid unnecessary accumulation of funds in the municipal treasury and to enable the concerned barangays to timely implement beneficial barangay projects through the use of such funds.
observations.managementAction: Remittance of barangay shares  of real property is already done on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, BAC members and TWG submit the necessary documents to support the propriety of the BAC honoraria payment. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant, ensure completeness of supporting documents, especially the computation of savings, before effecting payment thereof, pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No.2004-5A dated March 23, 2004 and DBM Budget Circular No.2007-3.
observations.managementAction: The BAC agreed to submit required documents to support the BAC honoraria payment as per provisions of Budget circular ,but for the next transaction, the BAC shall refrain from receiving the BAC honoraria as to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO agreed to closely monitor the utilization  of LDRRMF & insure that planned programs/ projects/activities are fully implemented
observations.reason: Not all PPAs under the DRRMF were fully implemented in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the municipal employees and general public pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal monitored that some of the GAD activities were taken from GAD related budget. For 2016, GAD Focal see to it that all GAD activities will be charged to GAD budget in order to fully maximize GAD funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant recognize as payables only those valid obligations /indebtedness to contractors/suppliers arising from the purchase of goods and services, pursuant to Section 141 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume III. Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Account credit the Due from LGUs account only when the corresponding financial aid has been liquidated by the barangay to fairly present the receivable and payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Some adjustments were already effected as claimed by the Municipal Accountant, though the Audit Team is still awaiting copies of the corresponding JEVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant recognize as payables only those valid obligations /indebtedness to contractors/suppliers arising from the purchase of goods and services, pursuant to Section 151 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume III to fairly present the payable accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary correcting entries to adjust the accounts Due to Officers and Employees and Advances to Officers and Employees to their correct balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant no longer included cash advances for confidential expenses under the account Due to Officers and Employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No.94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely and proper liquidation of all implemented projects and remittance of the remaining balances thereof to source agencies or seek authority for the utilization of the excess funds.
observations.managementAction:
Management tries it best to fast track implementa-tion of the projects in accordance with the purpose per signed MOA. 

Management still tries to prepare liquidation reports upon completion of the project and remit the balances of the funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to restore the account Due from Officers and Employees to recognize the amount due from the cash shortage of the former Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, we recommend that management provide COA an update on the status of the said shortage as well as the collection effort made to recover the loss of funds.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry to write-off the corresponding amount which have been dormant for years in consonance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and Section 04 (f), Chapter 2 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, Volume I, in order to fairly present the payable accounts in the financial statements. In the event of claims by the concerned electricians in the future, we recommend that the latter present valid documents to prove first rightful claim for actual services rendered, before the Municipal Accountant restores the payable account.
observations.managementAction: Transact-ions are no longer processed should these be without the complete necessary documents.
observations.reason: The long outstanding payable account was still not written off in the books as of December 31, 2016 due to lack of time in verifying the collectability of the account by the concerned payees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting Department conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for debtors who are still alive and within the locality and debtors whose accounts are initially deemed by management as unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. 

Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that the management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability. Finally, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant apply Section 37 of P.D. 1445 to municipal employees with long overdue accounts.
observations.managementAction: No management action was communicated to COA as having been conducted by the management relative to this matter.
observations.reason: Some debtors are still not able to settle their accounts due to unavailability of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer conduct an assessment and inquiry as to the whereabouts of the debtors and prepare separate lists for: (1) debtors who are still alive and within the locality and (2) debtors whose accounts are initially deemed unrecoverable, either due to death or inaccessibility. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Officer thru the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to those debtors with overdue accounts which are still collectible. On the other hand, for accounts deemed unrecoverable, we recommend that the management make representation with the Commission on Audit and request for authority to write-off accounts, with all the supporting documents attached thereto necessary to prove non-collectability.
observations.reason: Long outstanding Receivables still appeared in the year-end Balance Sheet.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee abide by the provisions of R.A.9184 by requiring the hospital management to prepare ahead of time, a comprehensive list of medicines and medical supplies to be used for hospital operations for its inclusion in the Annual Procurement Plan, in order to avoid the repetitive use of Shopping as the mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Management has started to adopt public bidding.
observations.reason: Management found it more convenient to apply Shopping as the alternative mode of procurement, although bidding has already been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit an explanation as to the excessive grant of PEI in excess of the allowed amount of P5,000.00 per employee. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management comply with the requirements of DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3 dated December 2, 2014 in the grant of Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) to LGU officials and employees particularly on the observance of the 55% Personal Services cap/limitation in the LGU’s Annual and Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 325 (a) of R. A. 7160 in order to avoid the payment of personnel benefits not in accordance with the provisions of the Local Government Code and DBM guidelines which will result to disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has now become more prudent in the grant of employee benefits and strictly observed the P.S. limitation in the grant of other benefits.
observations.reason: No written explanation pertaining to this audit issue was submitted by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned municipal driver to comply with Section 361 (e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and pertinent section of COA Circular No.75-6 in accomplishing the Driver’s Trip Ticket in order to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.  A repetition of the failure will subject subsequent transactions to possible disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is also more cautious in verifying the completeness and accuracy of the supporting documents. Drivers were already directed to properly fill-up the Trip Tickets.
observations.reason: Some Trip Tickets were still found to be lacking in data such as the distance travelled due to malfunction-ning odometer of service vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant install measures to facilitate the timely submission of the disbursement vouchers within the prescribed period of submission as required under COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office tried its best to submit the vouchers to COA the soonest possible time.
observations.reason: Most of the Disbursement Vouchers were not submitted to COA due to limited space and  lack of bodega.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management observe the provisions of COA Circular                    No.94-013 in recognizing its accountability over funds received and recording the release to recipient barangays, as well as in monitoring the proper utilization of funds transferred and eventual reporting thereof. Likewise, we recommend that the municipality and barangays follow the provisions of the approved MOA, so as to avoid technical problems in the future. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Engineer be more proactive in exercising its technical supervision over the identified projects for implementation by the barangay in order to ensure the proper utilization of funds.  	We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer make a follow-up on the status of projects implemented by barangays, in accordance with the provisions of the MOA and DPWH Department Order No.137 dated June 30, 1999. Lastly, we request that the Memorandum of Agreement executed between the Municipality of San Miguel and DPWH, as well as a copy of the PDAF SARO No. SARO-BMB-A-12-T000001508 dated May 8, 2012, be submitted to this Office, for proper evaluation and monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Allegedly, manage-ment has been more mindful of the provisions of COA Circular No.94-013.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineering Office was not able to closely monitor the projects implemented by some barangays. The subject MOA was also not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in recognizing its accountability over funds received and recording its release to recipient barangays by debiting the account Due from Other LGUs and crediting the account Due to NGAs only upon submission of liquidation reports by the respective barangays in order to provide monitoring on the proper utilization of funds transferred and timely reporting thereof.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to send billings and/or demand letters to the individuals concerned who are still in the locality for possible collections. If collection is futile for valid reasons, request authority from the Commission on Audit for the write off of these accounts. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant take the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality based on the result of the request for write off of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already coordinated with the DSWD and sought its assistance in sending demand letters to debtors since the DSWD is the implementing agency of this program.
observations.reason: The Management is not yet done with the sending of demand letters to debtors. Moreover, request for write-off of uncollectible is yet to be substantiated with documents to prove such condition.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the municipal treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the municipal accountant for recording. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant   draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the municipal treasurer and not by the Municipal Assessor. Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers  with the amount due and collectible and submit to the municipal accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Treasurer’s Office are currently working on the preparation of list of delinquent taxpayer.
observations.reason: Necessary adjustments have still to be prepared by the Municipal Accountant pending issuance of final list of delinquent taxpayer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately send demand letters to the concerned officers/ employees specifically those whose cash advances are past due for 31 days and over, otherwise instruct the accountant to deduct the amount for whatever is collectible from these personnel. Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the liquidation of all cash advances and see to it that travel advances are liquidated within thirty (30) days after return to permanent official station in accordance with the guidelines issued under COA Circular No. 96-004, implementing the afore-cited Executive Order No. 248, as amended. Follow up the liquidation of cash advances for Intelligence Fund to COA Central Office.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters were already sent as claimed by the Municipal Accountant but still subject for verification by the Audit Team.

Strict monitoring of cash advances is already implemented by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Substantial amount of cash advances and Due from Officers and Employees representing the cash shortage and cash advance of the former Municipal Treasurer Antero G. Simila still appeared in the books as of Dec.31,2016.

observations.managementAction: The LGU is now more prudent in using the 20% DF and is looking into more developmental projects which could be funded out of this fund.
observations.reason: No report was received by the Audit Team relative to the usage of 20% DF in CY 2016,but only the SAAOB.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record purchases of supplies and materials to the corresponding inventory account and compute inventory balances as prescribed under Sections 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity stock. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office  (a) maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and SSMI, showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; (b)furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances. Furthermore, we recommend that the Accounting Unit record issuances based on the SSMI submitted. Consequently, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: Inventory accounts were already established.
observations.reason: Supplies Ledger Cards were still not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the AAR submit the accounts and financial reports of the Local Government Unit as of December 31, 2016 and thereafter, to comply with the submission within the prescribed period pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160, PD 1445, COA Circular 95-006 and EO 292.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to submit within the prescribed timelines. However, the LGU have provided a temporary storage room, for them to facilitate submission of accounts and other reports. This is also an audit observation in 2014 and 2013. This finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to submit within the prescribed timelines due to being undermanned; temporary storage room provided to facilitate submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer on the proper reporting of collections in the Monthly Report of Collections and Deposits; b.) sequential issuance of numbers for official receipts; c.) daily remittance by the RCCs to the liquidating officer and d.) complete submission of report of accountability of accountable forms and complete submission of report of collection and deposit with official receipts/deposit slips to accounting office.
observations.managementAction: They have not complied with our audit recommendation because the Revenue Collecting Officers are not making their daily remittance to the liquidating officer and there is no individual RAAFs being prepared. This observation is incorporated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Revenue Collecting Officers are not making their daily remittance; no individual RAAFs prepared; Accounting Office undermanned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the updated Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for the bank accounts maintained by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is not updated. This observation is reiterated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not prepare and submit the updated BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials/employees to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and RA 9184 and require the submission of the required documentation to warrant the awarding/payment of this project.
observations.managementAction: Documents submitted to comply with our audit recommendation were presented to the audit team for validation.
observations.reason: Some documents were submitted but validation is pending to determine compliance with requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials/employees to submit the list of on-going projects and those to be implemented during the year and to post signboards and/or public notices on the site of the projects and inform the audit team on the posting.
observations.managementAction: They were able to submit the list of on-going projects but it was not submitted at the beginning of the year and the posting of projects signboards the audit team was not informed as required by law. We were able to validate posting of billboard for expansion of SMWS and it was completely filled-up and updated.
observations.reason: Submission of on-going projects was not made at the beginning of the year; posting of billboards not communicated to audit team timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive explain in writing why there are erasures/alterations on the dates of the duplicate ORs and require accountable personnel to render explanations and institute disciplinary action where necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the personnel to explain in writing why there are alterations. Continued disregard by the Revenue Collecting Officers upon sixty (60) days upon receipt of this memorandum said officers should be relieved from their duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and require Municipal Accountant to send reminders and institute appropriate actions.
observations.managementAction: Only the retired Mun. Treasurer was liquidated as of end of Dec. 2016. Other unliquidated cash advances remain. Management contends they complied but documentation is pending.
observations.reason: Some advances liquidated; others pending; documentation and full implementation of reminders and sanctions not evident.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned to submit documents such as the Certificate of Title to determine validity of the recorded amount.
observations.managementAction: The AAR submitted the list of properties but no supporting documents such as Certificates of Title were submitted for verification.
observations.reason: Some documentation provided but not the required titles; verification still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require submission of distribution lists and Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) for the in-kind donations to facilitate verification of the distribution of relief goods and list of in-cash donations for review.
observations.managementAction: Documents submitted were not complete; some distribution lists and RIS not available.
observations.reason: Documentation incomplete; distribution lists and RIS not consistently provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable officer to ensure full compliance with the Implementing Guidelines for the Cash for Work Project and ensure submission of complete documents to support payment of any claim.
observations.managementAction: Documents submitted are subject to validation.
observations.reason: Some documents submitted; others still for validation; incomplete support remains.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality strictly adhere to the Fundamental Principles provided under Section 4 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 in their payment of goods and other claims.
observations.managementAction: Documents submitted are partially for evaluation to determine compliance with requirements.
observations.reason: Some documents presented; final validation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality ensure all claims are supported with complete documentation before payments are made pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Documents submitted are partially for evaluation to determine compliance.
observations.reason: Incomplete supporting documents; some submissions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the official in-charge to submit documents on the implementation of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program and the Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted documents and thereafter complied with the recommendation; strict adherence was advised.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and transfer the unexpended balance to the Special Trust Fund account.
observations.managementAction: The LGU contends they complied and the amounts were transferred.
observations.reason: Transfer to Special Trust Fund claimed but documentation for full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to remit withheld taxes on or before the 10th day of the month following collection as required in Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.
observations.managementAction: Municipality contends that records and documents were submitted and that remittances were made; verification pending.
observations.reason: Records and documents for remittance submitted but validation pending; some unremitted balance recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct Budget Officers to identify the actual expenses and provide accurate data for decision making and avoid charging to MOOE payments for job order employees which should be charged to Personal Services.
observations.managementAction: The LGU contended they have identified actual expenses reflected in Obligation Requests; accounting classification to be adjusted.
observations.reason: Adjustment of expenses pending; reclassification underway.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with Section 313 of RA 7160 and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for operations of economic enterprises and prepare separate reports for economic enterprises.
observations.managementAction: Management contended that the San Miguel Water System’s income is insufficient to cover operating cost and that separate reports are to be prepared.
observations.reason: Economic enterprise reporting not fully established; water system not yet self-sustaining.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Officer in Charge of SMWS to strictly adhere to Municipal Ordinance No. 2006-03 especially in the collection of delinquent water bills to generate income.
observations.managementAction: Management contends they initiated actions but enforcement of ordinance needs strengthening; validation pending.
observations.reason: Enforcement of collection ordinance not strictly applied; some collections remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive formulate written Standard Operating Procedures or Guidelines in the implementation of Livelihood Programs, and strictly enforce collections so that more constituents will benefit.
observations.managementAction: LGU contended they have already implemented project collections and some beneficiaries have repaid loans.
observations.reason: SOPs/guidelines not fully documented; collections ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require officials to incorporate the 5% budget requirement for GAD to ensure full implementation of projects and activities intended to address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Management contends that the 2016 GAD Plan and accomplishment was submitted but supporting documentation was not provided for audit.
observations.reason: GAD Plan submitted but accomplishment report and evidence not fully provided to audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipality to record the income and charges due from/to the depository banks. Moreover, close the depository account maintained with a private bank and transfer the account balance to any authorized government depository banks.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor issued office order to the Accountant directing to record the transaction
observations.reason: Partially implemented, reconciling items were already adjusted, however, account from UCPB still not yet closed

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections intact and on time in accordance with the requirements of the DOF and DBM JC No. 1-81 to protect government funds from unwarranted losses.
observations.managementAction: Send letter and office order to the Municipal Treasurer
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to limit the hiring of job order employees to what is necessary in the normal operation of the Municipality to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenditures. Stop the practice of hiring casual/job order employees for the sake of accommodation and maximize the man-power available in the office.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance to the provisions of RA 9433 for payment of Hazard Duty Pay to MSWD personnel.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under Section 4.2.1 of the COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in granting cash advances.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the Joint Memo Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of COA, DBM, DILG, GOCC and DND on the granting and reporting of confidential funds.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the BAC Secretariat, thru the BAC Chairperson to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report of the Municipality and submit to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the BAC chair to prepare & submit the PMR to GPPB
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
Verification with the GPPB website

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advise the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under Section 2.78 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998 and Part III of Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 dated June 20, 2014, all of the Bureau of Internal Revenue in the withholding of taxes from municipal officials and employees in any form of compensation and personnel benefits.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in payment of honoraria to officials and employees assigned to special projects. Also require the Municipal Accountant not to approve claims for honorarium in the absence of documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: Stop the payment of honoraria to municipal employees assigned to government special projects
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management advise the Municipal Engineer to properly supervise the implementation of infrastructure projects to avoid delay and to attain the desired objectives of the project. Also, adhere to the guidelines in the granting of extension of contract time duration in the issuance of suspensions order.
observations.managementAction: Send letter and office order to the Municipal Engineer
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended to advise the BAC Secretariat, through the BAC Chairman strict compliance to the prescribed period of submission of purchase order   and   its   supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2009 – 001 dated Feb. 12, 2009. Further, also advise the Municipal Treasurer to furnish us with the delivery receipt and inspection report within 24 hours from date of delivery.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented
